reviews.comment: He is a nice guy who cares about his students. His lectures were dull, and they were out of sync with the recitations. Graded by homeworks, 3 midterms and 1 final exam. Level of work expected is high, but still manageable. Wouldn't take him again for peace of mind
Teaching for the second half of the semester was very hard to follow. The exams do not reflect the teaching content or the knowledge, the curve is unreasonable which I think do not encourage learning
Nice guy. Useful OH. Lectures and homework are basic. Exam difficulty fluctuates a lot. Expect zero partial credit. Notes not recorded or posted anywhere. Would recommend another teacher for 21-228.
ok lectures, tests vary in difficulty, same for homeworks

lectures are confusing, pushes heavy usage on textbook and youtube lectures. features extremely tough tests and assignments with minimal grading leniency, making it hard to gauge standing. 
avoid at all costs
Does not teach the best

Writes lecture on board, but it's hard to follow. Relied on textbook and YouTube lectures (search for "cmu 21-228" on YT). Homeworks and tests are very hard and there is no partial credit. I had no idea if I was learning the right things or how well I was doing in the class. Expect pain and suffering

Confusing lectures, the textbook is clearer when explaining difficult concepts. Difficult exams and homework, very little partial credit given. Class is curved but prof is vague about specifics so hard to know where you stand.
abdioi;beaoie

One of my favorite professors ever. I'm not sure why other students gave him such a low rating. That was NOT my experience at all. Professor Reinhart is fantastic and incorporates humor into his lectures. I learned a lot from him and I would highly recommend his classes.

This professor does not care about the students at all. The notes are completely useless with 0 explanation. Most of the students never go to class cuz u learn nothing lol. A student asked on piazza why a function equals to X and his response was indeed it is equal to X. How is he still teaching? I am honestly confused.
He absolutely does not care about his students or their success. Worst professor I had during my time at CMU.

Negative experience overall. His lectures/notes are full of expressions that are not clearly defined mathematically. Hand waving explanations often led to confusions. I don't see a passion for teaching and students' learning in general.
I love her. She is bestie westie fr.

Prof. A shares detailed notes and always explains examples step-by-step in lectures. Weekly quizzes with no midterms average the workload over the semester. She's also very approachable in office hours.

Genuinely poor self awareness for students. Took exceptionally long to respond to any student requests in general. Very poor communication in general from her and it doesn't seem like she realizes it. Would not recommend taking her for concepts. Did cancel lecture a few times as well.

Abdelghany is one of my favourite professors of all time. She really cares about her students, really passionate while teaching, and very willing to help (if you are able to find her in her OH). Homeworks imo are really hard, but you learn a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: doesnt reply to piazza posts and quite hard to reach outside very small windows of time, lectures are messy too and expects students to learn material for a midterm not discussed in lecture?
Outside of lectures, generally unavailable and uninterested in her students. Seems nice but often absent from her own classes and office hours.
Generally has a lack of awareness for her students

she doesn't care about her students. canceled lecture so many times and was rude to students. she was so unaware of so many things about the course and it seemed like she did not want to teach the class.

Good lecturer. Expect long and difficult homeworks. DO NOT try to do this class alone, especially if this is your first real proof based math class. Her OH is useful and her TAs will not let you drown. Her tests are challenging, but fair.
She is a tough grader and is not lenient at all, but she explains course material very well. This class (21102) has very interesting topics and I would recommend it.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This guy is hilarious. He loves making as many pop culture references as possible in his lectures. Overall an amazing professor, and likes keeping in touch with students even from past colleges. Take him if you can, and acknowledge he is a good professor.
Great guy, but the course was so painful, and the recitations were the most awkward 80 mins of my life.
I like his humor, i vibe with him
would give 10 starts if I could
my goat but drop the gen z slang
super cool guy and very funny. if u need help succeeding in his classes just talk to him he will try his very best to help u
Very nice guy

Hicks the goat is the best! clearest, most enthusiastic, most approachable professor I ever had, and yet he does it well and also no nonsense and is to the point all the times! content is hard but very doable and he gives bonuses! take advantage of them and PARTICIPATE! he loves when you participate and will help you! totally 6/5!

Professor Hicks fostered an engaging learning environment and clearly communicated both expectations and the essential course content. While he doesn't curve tests, he includes bonus questions that often reference unique points from his lectures, which encourages attendance and attentiveness. Overall, I would gladly take another class with him!

Dr. Hicks was one of the best professors I've had the privilege of taking a class with at CMU, and one of the best teachers I've had throughout my life. He had not only the ability to convey class material to us in an approachable and digestable manner, but also was able to connect with students on a personal level through his sense of humor.

Excellent professor who spend time answering any questions you have! Very enjoyable lectures and very fun extra credits on exams! He is so good at math that he can give you a super-duper clear overview of the entire undergraduate mathematics curriculum in just 2 hours 🤩
Really easy exams/HW and fun lectures that include more than just math.
good guy! will spend time answering any questions you have, and is overall an enjoyable lecturer as well.

Dr. hicks is first and foremost a professor that cares about his students. He's willing to help whenever and his office hours are more than helpful. His explanations are straightforward and clear and his grading is fair; if you lock in then getting an A is really doable.

Professor Hicks is goated - explains everything super clearly, great lecture notes with examples of types of problems (which come up in exams). Would definitely take again, if you can take him for any class he's the best.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: if you can take 259 with hicks, do it
Professor was very kind and informative. He would often be very understanding of different learning styles and try to accommodate everyone to the best of his ability.
W professor. One of the best math classes I've taken so far. He made calc 3 far easier than other professors would.

Hicks the GOAT! The only professor that made me love math more at CMU! 
The only professor who loves students and cares about them and gives 10+ extra credit points!
I love him so much! Made me inspired by his lectures! totally A+
Hicks the GOAT

OMG guys! Take his class bro! Prof. Hicks is my goat!! 10/5 rating! he will literally lift you up and help you not just pass, but excel in math A++ in calc III and vector calc. So inspirational, best 10AM lecture I ever had, so inspirational, lots of extra credit, makes this class much more fun than the CMU university itself! love you Dr. Hicks!

Hey guys!!! I really love Prof. Hicks so much! He explains things very clearly and is so passionate about teaching, gives nice pop culture bonuses and math bonuses, clearly a man who cares about your success! 10/5! I love you so much Dr. Hicks and you made my excel at calc III much more! Best math prof. I ever had! I will miss you soon! A++ prof!

AYO! Prof. Hicks is literally the best human being on the planet!!!!! He is literally so caring when he teaches calc iii, a content heavy course but makes it so easy and engaging. 
I will always love you Dr. Hicks, amazing lectures, provides extra practice problems, very clear on grading, just so great!! 6/5!!! Gave you bonus back! OMG I Love you!

Dr. Hicks is a dedicated professor who cares deeply about his students. He's passionate, enthusiastic, and funny. While he faced challenges with the course material and time management, his effort and commitment to teaching are commendable. With refinement in grading and test design, he'll excel.

exams aren't really hard, they're all just from problems we practice in class or from the practice set he gives us. just focus on pop culture references or any mathematical symbols he uses in class and you're all set to get an A, maybe even A+

I genuinely looked forward to every class with Dr Hicks. His teaching style is not only engaging but fun and relatable. One of the highlights of his classes is the clever pop culture references that keep you attentive. He puts a lot of effort into making the lessons both informative and entertaining.

Dr. Hicks is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about his students' learning and well-being. He's patient, understanding, and encouraging. Math isn't my strength, but he spent hours helping me understand the material. He's devoted to your success and goes above and beyond.

Do plenty of practice problems to succeed on his exams!

Professor Hicks is very approachable after class. He helps me intuitively understand concepts and provides proofs or geometric explanations beyond the teaching material. He responds quickly to emails and adjusts exam review sessions and office hours to accommodate students' schedules. I enjoy the course with Professor Hicks
if you take his class, brush up on some sitcoms and good movies. Lookout for random movie quotes in the middle of his lectures as they make up some of the bonus questions on the test.
She is an amazing lecturer. She is also really funny. She is enthusiastic, passionate, extremely knowledgeable, and very kind to her students.

Absolutely phenomenal lectures and will do anything to help her students in any way possible. Always answers questions in the best sense for her students to understand the concept of that week if it means to stay after lecture for any amount of time. She also gives guidance of how to do well mentally with the programming assignments.
Excellent lectures. Always patiently answering questions. The course is certainly difficult even you have a lot of prior knowledge, but the professor is always helpful.

Anne is an awesome lecturer. She answers any questions you have and is very respectful (she once stayed back after lecture for nearly 40 minutes answering questions and explaining advanced topics). While the course is very difficult, she does a phenomenal job of making the concepts easier to understand.
Absolutely one of the greatest professors at CMU.
This guy made me like physics. I did not like physics and now I do. Taking him again this semester just because it's him. Please if you have the chance, take Dr. Anderson's classes.
Absolute amazing at lectures

He is one of the best professors at the university. He cares for both the content of the lectures and the well being of students. He makes time to help you with concepts if you need and finds ways to keep lectures engaging. He is also always eager to point you to other resources and follow up with you. A true example.
Lecture-heavy course with Friday quizzes, but genuinely love his lecturing style and would highly recommend taking!
He is a very good lecturer and his OH are very helpful

I loved Professor Anderson's class. His lectures are captivating and were worth getting up for at 8am. He truly was passionate about his teaching, and I only wish I could have learned from my mistakes earlier on.

we love Dr Anderson! Good lectures (goes through many examples) and his office hours are really useful. Exams are fair and homework is more difficult but doable. Recitation attendance matters and homework is submitted by hand. He is a really great professor and passionate in lecture. He goes off textbook sometimes so lectures are helpful
We love Dr. Anderson
Super nice, and engaging as a lecturer. Not always the most fun labs, as they can sometimes get monotonous, but his lectures are incredible.

BEST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER HAD. He literally made me consider switching my major to physics. His lectures feel like TED talks. He is so helpful and makes class so interesting. Kept me engaged at 8am which says A LOT!The class is also extremely organized with so much support. Even if you aren't in CIT like me, I would take this course over the other

literally the best professor. He is clearly passionate about physics and cares a great deal for his students, He teaches well while still being kind and understanding which is hard to find. 
Class is fine if you have taken any beginning physics class but you might struggle if you're coming in without any physics knowledge.

The only man that would make me excited for a 7AM online class is David Anderson. An excellent professor and human in general who kept lectures engaging, even for people who are allergic to physics like myself. Simultaneously understanding and helpful as well as stern, as he has high expectations for us. He was my first professor crush at CMU, haha

Attendance mandatory for recitation, not for lecture. Fantastic professor. Very excited and very clear. It is obvious that he enjoys teaching. Makes difficult concepts easy to understand. Will give incredibly easy quizzes each week - don't be fooled! Tests are 10x harder. Can be pretty funny. Go to lectures, ESPECIALLY if you don't read the book.

Very enthusiastic & entertaining lectures with clear explanations! Office hours are helpful and individualized - he walks you through problems and explains each step. Reading not needed but go to lectures. Whatever downsides this course has are easily outweighed. This class made my semester so much better, and that's definitely owed to Anderson.

Fantastic lecturer, spends time with you on anything and ensures you understand it thoroughly and beyond. Would generally describe him as rigorous, but never punitively so. The expectations for tests are high; the amount of physics covered is comprehensive. Very supportive in getting you there. Will always work with you. One of my favorite profs!

Took his Exp Phys and Intro Phys classes which were the BEST Physics classes of my life. Cares about his students, learning, and is just an awesome teacher. not taking this class is such a missed opportunity.

Fantastic professor. He explains things very clearly and really cares about students. Very humorous during lecture time. Easy to ask for extension of homework. Workload is manageable and predictable. I genuinely likes this course because of him.

Very passionate about physics and teaching, and his class is organized. The homework recitation problems are usually harder than the tests. Take advantage of his office hours, where he freely goes through any problem step by step and takes the time to make sure you understand. Overall a great fella, who is also surprisingly amazing at Mario Kart
Tests can be very time-crunched, around 9 pages to be done in 50 minutes. Quizzes are significantly easier than exams. Weekly homework and quizzes. All the practice given is useful for studying.

Think of what a kind version of Draco Malfoy would be like as a man in his mid fifties, then make him a college physics professor. Thats Dr. Anderson for ya. Just an absolute legend and hard not to like.

Prof. Anderson is so cool! He's the only one of my professors that doesn't make me fall asleep right away. You can tell he cares, and his explanations of difficult concepts make them seem super easy. Highly recommend.

Anderson reminds me of the Doctor from Doctor Who, but like, less pretentious. His lectures are super interesting (as much as 33141 can be interesting lol) because you can tell he's passionate about the subject, and he's really nice. The accent's also a plus!

Course was insanely easy online, just a TON of busywork (finding equations, filling in variables/values, solving algebra equations, etc. Went to lecture approximately 0 times, used the book for the quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: The man's a legend. What can I say

David Anderson is a great teacher. He is passionate about his job and physics and loves his students. He is a very nice guy and I would like to have him again. His quizzes are fair and tests are also not bad. If you do your work and do not make silly mistakes it is not very hard to get an A. You just gotta be constant! Assignment every week!

An amazing professor. For the most part, his lectures are engaging even at 9:30 in the morning. Recitation attendance is required, but the recitation problems are very helpful for preparing for the midterms. One of the four midterms is dropped. There are weekly quizzes and the lowest is dropped. Homework should take about an hour. Would recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I cannot emphasize enough how much I love this man, he's one of the best professors. He's very helpful at office hours and genuinely cares for his students. Work hard and study, and you can get the A

Absolutely fantastic professor. His lectures were very engaging and exciting because he was always so passionate about the topic. Also, the class was super organized--we always knew when everything was due, and recitations were guided by papers with practice problems that he put together. It's difficult--but students have the resources to succeed.

His lectures are sometimes heavy, but he manages to keep students' attention with cool/funny demonstrations. HW is moderate and gives many practice problems for recitation. You need to work hard to get a good grade, but he also drops grades. Get ready for Friday quizzes. He explains hard concepts very well. Note: Writes his Qs backwards.
Professor Anderson is one of the best lecturers CMU has to offer. Despite the fact that the lectures were at 9:30, he finds a way to hold your attention. Get ready for those weekly quizzes.
He's super enthusiastic and pulls your interest to the lectures. Decently challenging material but definitely manageable

This class could have been great if I worked harder.  Do not slack off.  Read the material, do the work, and you'll do fine.  Lot of lectures, and they're fast, so rather than copying everything down make sure you understand the fundamentals and the concepts.  He focuses on the concepts more than the numbers anyways.  Get extra help if you need to.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: A good guy. He'll really help you out if you go to office hours. I like how he focuses on the concepts instead of the numbers. Gets you ready to derive it yourself when you forget.

Anderson is like the inspiring British father you wish you had at CMU.  GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS, lectures are good but no one is tryna get up at 9 am MWF so use his hours for extra help.  He gives quizzes on Fridays so be ready for that, but otherwise he is super fair and the way he approaches physics is very new and creative; not focused on numbers
Ayyy cool teacher. Tests are okay just study and attend recitation since its mandatory as is 5% of your grade. You can skip 1 or 2 and your good.
It's always exciting to be in his class
He is awesome

Experimental physics with him has been very well taught and fairly graded. The lab reports are very low key and can be finished within the class period, so there is no outside time commitment, plus it never took more than two hours

Great professor.  He's hilarious, easy, and helpful.  Just make sure to pay attention during lectures and write down everything he says.  He literally tells you exactly what to do during the lab and if you follow his instructions you'll be done early and get an easy A.
Experimental physics. EASY grading, lots of time to get all work done, and usually doesn't use the entire 3 hour block. Fine professor.
She is great at explaining things and I can tell her enthusiasm for the materials in her lectures.

This professor forced you to attend in-class discussion. If she saw you not speaking, she would call you out. Also result of discussion will be written down in notes for grading.
Her slides are useless. You have to look at her write-up on the board and handwritting is messy and chaotic. 
Her exam is difficult. Heavy point deduction is expected.

This professor consistently expects you to be able to incorporate nuanced information into your answers without her specifically asking for it. Very unclear 90% of the time, which is a shame because the material is interesting. Often gets frustrated/annoyed when students ask clarifying questions. Writes info on board, so ppts are very unhelpful.

Work itself isn't bad. email communication w/her is frustrating+unhelpful. She failed to clarify/write the expectations for the neurotransmitter project+took a month (since we already submitted work for project) for her to claim these expectations. Many ppl have insufficient time to redo work to meet the new expectations nearing finals season.

Her course was probably the most difficult one for this semester. She doesn't take attendance but there are group exercises after each lecture that are graded. Also her slides do not have any info, you need to go to actually get any notes. Tests were all take home but were so difficult. Wanted to go to oh before a test and she wasn't there
I spent a summer apprenticing with Andrew, and he was awesome. Really knowledgeable, witty, and pushed me to think out the box in bold ways.
Inspirational.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is easily the most inspiring, well read, and enthusiastic art teachers I have ever had. Out of the box with a super duper loose curriculum. Any day he can just decide "its too stale in here, we're gonna go outside and draw weird things. One thing, dont ask him for clarification on his thought process, artistically he moves a mile a minute.
Best prof I have ever had, a little disorganized but incredibly intelligent and knowledgeable about art. Took me in directions I never expected but I never felt pressured. The GA was also fantastic.

Often loses temper on students and gets too close, acts as if he knows what is best for them without actually thinking about other people's taste.  I wanted to learn photoshop and it was the main topic, but the course lost focus and everyone just did random projects.
Definitely a great teacher to take if you want a bit of a challenge. I wish I listened more to what he had to say-he pushed me hard and I fought when I should've listened!

Class: Concepts 1(disregard if you got him for drawing) If you want an easy class, dont take Johnson, if you want to push the boundaries of what you thought you could do,take Johnson's. He's crazy, disjointed, we don't follow a lesson plan we learn alot and think even more. He will help if you ask. I recommend him for anyone thinking art

If you expect boring, traditional classes where the professor tells you exactly what to do - dont take him!  if you want to learn things in a different way and make self discoveries, he is the professor for you.
nasty, negative -- helpful if you let him be, though. he's tough, so you gotta be tough too.
Great guy who gave difficult but fair exams. Study hard and you will do well.

Prof. Ada is is awesome in providing inspiring lectures. He cares about individual students a lot (respond the majority of piazza/pandanote post, nice instructor OH). 

Many exam questions are slight modification from textbook practice and homework (so do go over all of them!). 

Overall one of the best CS profs I've had in my life.
THE best prof Ive had at CMU so far
Awesome
Best professor ever T T

There are definitely plenty of hype men for Professor Ada. He is very charismatic and caring especially in a school where most professors are dry as paper and only care about research. People are confusing his likability with his actual teaching skill. It was neither memorable nor beyond what other professors at CMU can provide.
Ada is fantastic. 15-251 is a difficult course without a doubt, but he motivates the learning extremely well.

Prof Ada is a real gem. I respect the effort he put into making 15-251 a great remote learning experience (buying recording equipment, implementing TA-student one-on-one mentorships, getting rid of the final, etc...). His lectures are also extremely well thought out.

Ada is really good professor and person. He's really invested in 251 and works his best to make it the best experience for the students. Even with the pandemic, he modified the class so that the students would benefit the most and more stress wasn't placed on them (changed the grading scheme, removed the final etc.). He's a real gem.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Anil was the best professor I have ever seen. He gave amazing, inspirational lectures. The course was extremely hard, as you need to understand the materials deeply to get good grades on the exams, but Anil was the major reason that I enjoyed this course so much. I would recommend this course to all CMU students interested in CS and math.
Great professor. His 251 is really worth taking

Best professor in my life ever, period. I would honestly say that Ada was born to teach 251. Absolutely amazing lecture, he knows what he's doing, genuinely cares about teaching and students learning. I would take 251 over and over again just because of him. I would go as far to say that his lectures are life-changing.
Simply amazing.
He's a really great lecturer.
Very good prof

Ada is the best professor ever. Amazing teacher, really knows his stuff and really knows how to teach. Any negative things you may have heard about the course have nothing to do with him (he only makes it better). It's hard - but super fun and rewarding. If you read the notes and prepare well you'll get an A.

Hes a hard-working professor! He's easily one of the best professors I've had. He transformed a really hard class where everyone gets a C and they just curve it into a hard class that you learn in and you work hard for that B. My personal opinion but I think hes really charming (heart eyes)
I really liked him. Very clear, good lecturer. His classes require some work, but are worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The course is mainly run by the CA's, but he's not a terrible teacher.  Very boring lectures, though.  If possible, take Kosbie, but Ada isn't the worst by any means.  Prepare to do tons of homework in the course (not just Ada's class), and your weekends are gone, but it's not that hard.  Time-intensive.  Put in the work, and you'll be fine.
Great teacher and an even cooler guy. Taught the class very well and made the effort to get to know students that attended office hours (at least from the times I went).
Amazing professor. Truly wants kids to learn, and will meet you more than halfway. If you put the effort in, you will succeed in this class.
Great lecturer
Very self involved and definitively pretentious.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: My experience in Beck's printmaking class was extremely disappointing. When questions were asked, responses often felt dismissive rather than helpful, which created an unwelcoming environment for learning. I left the course feeling frustrated and under-supported.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I do not follow all the negative reviews, and I found her to be quite sweet and caring. Above that, I did not feel like she wasted my time.

What a disappointment to be around faculty like this. Impossible to follow, rambling and incoherent style. Not sure if this was a class or just someone taking up time. I spent 4k to take a class with Kim and learned nothing.

She is very reminiscent of an aunt who doesn't really 100% know what your homework will be at any time but she is a nice person and as long as you showed up and work in your sketchbook (at least for foundations: drawing) you should have an A+

A decent teacher, but very disorganized and classes were mostly improvised. She often didn't give out important class-related information on time, and the grades she put in for my projects differed greatly from what she told me about them in class. Most of my work was digital, so she didn't fully understand the medium either.
She took alot of time to help me personally. The class was tough, but she was alot of fun. I'd take another class with her!

Cell bio was the hardest CMU bio class I have experienced. We moved through material quickly and needed to memorize the lectures verbatim. Grading criteria was often unclear. One semester later, I can also say this class was the single most helpful class that I have taken at CMU. Ask questions, Dr. McCartney is kind and thorough in her explanations

Worst prof I've had at CMU, condescending (if you're struggling "just study more"), uncompromising (no makeup exams period), and overloads slides with textbook content verbatim, only to explain it even worse. Exams aren't a test of knowledge but rather a guessing game trying to figure out what she's put on the rubric (last exam best grade was a 76)

Teaches as if she's trying to prove how smart she is.  Her (mandatory) lectures would be helpful if you didn't know how to read; they are just copied directly from a textbook which she tries to explain, but worse.  The textbook is the extent of her knowledge- if you ask for more depth or clarification, she does not know.

Assigns two (2) homeworks to be due the class before an exam.  5 minutes left in class before an exam, and she is excited to show us an entirely new mechanism that we are expected to know for the exam.  Often gets defensive when students ask questions.

Frequently answers student questions with "I don't know the answer to that."  Teaches as if her class is the only course that students have to manage in the semester.  She will introduce a weeks worth of material the class before the exam.  Mandatory lectures where she just regurgitates a textbook that someone else wrote.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very knowledgable in the subject matter and does a great job of illustrating the materials importance. The class can be very demanding and its easy to fall behind . Homework should be started as early as possible. Grading can be a little tough but the rigor of the homework forces you to absorb the material and prepares you well for the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Dr. McCartney is probably an expert in her field but she is an awful teacher. If you take her course prepare to essentially teach yourself. 

You'll be overwhelmed. The material covered in one semester in her class is what other universities cover in two.

Her classes are geared to only the most prepared students. If you're not, you're out of luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Amazing professor, lectures are wonderful, lecture notes are super good. Actually tried to make the course better for students by looking at their feedbacks. Respects and cares about students.
One of the best lecturers here at CMU. Makes a lot of efforts to improve teaching quality. Definitely recommended.

He is super understanding and wants to help and support you even if you're doing really badly. I took a class with him a few years ago during a really hard time in my life and he did his best to work with me. He tries to set up the class with as much support as possible, both from him and also from classmates.

Professor Branson is an angel from heaven itself. He was an amazing lecturer and took the time to learn his student's names without even talking to them first. He also really listened to student feedback and incorporated it into his teaching. A real gem.

This is the worst policy class I've taken. She is strongly biased and dismissive of views that differ from her own. She fails to create an open environment, and students feel afraid to speak up if their opinions don't match hers.
A great Professor. Highly recommended.
Great knowledge about international policies and politics.

Silvia is a phenomenal instructor - best I've had in this subject area. I considered myself a good writer prior to this class, but her assignments have led to visible improvement. I disagree with some of the other posts - she welcomes opposing views but has little tolerance for poor analysis. You need to properly support your arguments.

Silvia is an incredible professor. She practically knows everything, and she's incredibly sweet. There's a lot of reading but you never have to do it, since she comes to class with handouts on everything you need to know. But her course material is super interesting, as are the class discussions. Add anything to those and she'll be pleased.

Her lectures are boring, but her assignments are easy. She likes you to spit back her opinions verbatim. She is EXTREMELY stubborn and views herself as superior to students. A good grade is not worth the stress of her personality.
lots of reading. useless class that is part of required core courses.
a little boring, but its not too hard if you do the reading..the class discussions can be very interesting
clean delivery of material, interesting reading
Very Funny. Really likes Diet Dr. Pepper. Exams are (imo) fair but HEFTY. Homework is kinda tedious.

great guy, fast-paced class if you're not ready for it. his lecture notes(doesn't tolerate skipping class w/o reason)and **practice exams** are super helpful. study for the midterms, he broadcasts whats on those. final exam is essentially made for geniuses, i dropped a letter grade after the final because it's impossible. he likes boba & dr pepper.
HE IS SO NICE!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Often makes sarcastic or rude comments when asked questions he thinks are beneath him, that I felt were wildly unhelpful and inappropriate. Clearly he is extremely smart and can be nice sometimes, but he will get very frustrated & raise his voice if you don't understand his way, and often refused to even try explaining things another way.
Dickhead
Hard tests, decent lecturer, but grading scale is completely unknown (he doesn't tell you how grades are calculated)
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The lectures are better than average, but the course design is excellent. The course exceeds the sum of its content.
Good professor. I like his way of explaining things and presenting the material, very practical less formal perhaps but his methods just works.

Is clearly very smart but doesn't always try to explain things in ways everyone can understand. Makes occasional obnoxious comments. Goes very fast. Is very confusing. Office hours are important but if you don't think exactly the way he does, makes you feel dumb. Basically, the class is curved and most people struggle a lot.
Not a bad teacher, but condescending. If you don't have much of a physics background you'll have to work hard to keep up, but it's not impossible.
Incredibly smart and knows a lot about the subject but is extremely condescending and sarcastic. Makes you feel dumb whenever you ask a question

Definitely challenging even though Ive taken multiple physics classes before. Briere isnt openly arrogant, he just has a low tolerance for incompetence and tests you such that you cant rely on memorizing the same repeated setup of problem solving. You need to understand and apply the concepts. CMU also provides an overload of resources for help.
Lectures are clear and pretty well explained, but he thinks he's better than the rest of the world, his students especially.  One of the more condescending people I've encountered in my life.

VERY SMART GUY. There is no doubt that he is knowledgable on the subject of physics. But brains only completes half of the teaching equation. He is not affable nor personable and believes that he is too good to teach his students.
He knows his stuff, but he's a pathetic teacher.  His exams intend to hurt your grade.  Overall, he's just not helpful either.  In order to do well hope to get a good TA.
Very interesting man, definatly enjoy his class and his lectures are not boring

Had him for physics of musical sound.  Not a difficult class, but lectures were very clear.  Occasionally dry, but always made sense.  Generally personable and humorous as well, but I can see why some people might be put off by his style.

He's one of those professors that knows physics up and down but can't teach it in a way that others will understand. If you don't know much about physics in the first place you probably shouldn't take him, even if it's a 100 level class.To understand the material you pretty much have to live in his office.
Knows his stuff, but lectures are not interesting at all.  Many people seem to doze off.  Doesn't make much of an effort to keep students engaged.

Not a good professor, he lectures clearly and knows his stuff, but acts like physics is the easiest thing in the world and doesn't have ANY respect for students, which comes as a first for me, personally.
She's rude.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I couldn't understand her.

Even though they take 4-5 weeks to grade one exam, there's a no late policy for the barrage of homeworks and quizzes they give. Seems like they're more than willing to give themselves accommodations but wont even extend a single deadline, even by a few hours, for you if you're swamped with work and sick.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: Way too much effort for a gen ed
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: She intentionally structures classes so that you learn as much as possible and it stays with you, but without making it super stressful in terms of grading and tests
Great lecturer. Fun professor.
A really, really excellent teacher - one of my best at CMU. If you work with him and take all the opportunities for extra work that he gives, you'll certainly do wonderfully.

Burak is a great professor. Lectures are clear and he gives plenty of practice for exam. He also teaches you to use Matlab for more than plotting, and it's the first core course that does so. However, he could do better at providing outside help, as office hours are very limited.
Dr. Oz is a great teacher and his lectures are very useful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: pretty great teacher!
He is such a good teacher! Super cool and funny
Dr. O is super nice and he taught DSC really well. Most of the class got A's
Not clear
can't teach
No Comments
A very irresponsible person
Taught the ME controls class well, especially considering it was his first class. Pretty cool guy, easy to understand and follow. Not an easy class by any means, but not too difficult either.

Prof. Bockstaller grades very generously, as long as you can finish his homework very closely. He is also very kind in quizzes and exams grading. Even though I was not able to understand the course content completely, probably because he is better at teaching higher level courses, I could easily get a high score. Definitely will take again.
he made it interesting
God bless this prof!
Amazing.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Tests? Not many

reviews.comment: This professor is extremely clear and engaging. His lectures are consistent, but be ready to read the textbook for clarification and studying.
Straightforward
This dude looks like Arnold Schwarzenegger, and totally sounds like him too. It just makes the classes so much more fun
His lectures are very engaging, and he makes the class very enjoyable. Reading the textbook will help a lot in fully understanding the material.
If you read the book before coming to class, you will be fine.
Great professor! Very fun and enthusiastic. Plus, his awesome Austrian accent is entertaining.
Worst final ever

The tough grading is not really a problem if you pay attention in lecture and go to office hours. Sure, the exams are hard but you are provided with a mock exam and you can bring a notes sheet. This class definitely takes a lot of work and requires a significant time commitment, but it is a core class and should be treated with that in mind.
Final is just a joke. He will try his best lower your grade.
Worst professor

I really want to like Prof. Nace because he is a great lecturer: I just think his policy on exams is very unfair. Every 240 exam for me has gone bad. Too long for the 1.33 hour block, too abstract, and you get only one practice exam ~2-3 days before so I feel pressured to cram instead of gradually adjust to the insane time crunch those exams are...
Worst professor ever.

Nace has decent lectures and I learned a lot during the course, though the class can be frustrating at many times (unforgiving course policies, awful points weighting on the homeworks, awful writeups to homeworks and labs, as well as awfully harsh/punishing grading).

Explains topics well during lecture and approachable. However, some of the labs/hw are unnecessarily tedious and time consuming. Also, they are harsh graders. One time I got all the answers to a HW correct and lost 10+ points because of "style". Beware of Labs and make sure you and your partner are actually collaborating.

I'd like to contrast people's ratings here. Nace is tough, but his exams are a good way of knowing if you actually understood what was happening. It is a great university way of teaching and he is always available if you are struggling and though his class is hard it is useful in the long term.

Not enough space to write everything; I will say that his exams are imo not great. I'm not particularly speaking about difficulty - finals had more than enough time - but some questions seemed off topic or unnecessarily specific to not-so-important parts of the course. Also midterm difficulty was more suited for a 2-3 hour exam than a 1hr20min one.

RUNNNNN from this class. He made me hate hardware engineering. He loves what he is teaching, but the lectures are mediocre at best. Lectures don't help you do well on the hw or exams. The lectures are pretty useless TA's help you learn so much. All he cares about is giving u a hard time on the exams. You can view the whole class as a trial by fire.
He is Riddler from Batman.
Not a clear course

Very tough professor and grader who genuinely loves what he teaches. You really earn every point you get in his class. While at times the amount of homework and length of labs may feel unnecessary, if you really pay attention and stick with it, you will finish his class with an incredible fundamental understanding of hardware engineering.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Really cares about his students, and is very clear when teaching. He makes tough quizzes, but you learn a lot.
This class is a mess. Seriously, homework and quizzes are a joke. NMSL
Really good at explaining stuff and very patient with students, especially at office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Amazing lecturer. Lecture slides are posted online but worth coming to lecture to have them explained. Great guy
Professor Nace is a really good lecturer who is very knowledgable. While his class was a lot of work, it was very well organized and I learned a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Absolutely ridiculous, he is unreasonable in assignments, exams, and projects. He  is not very clear on his requirements and grades harshly despite being unclear. This course relied heavily on trivia.  A complete waste of time. There are better courses to take in the same topic especially in the CS department. This is true of most ECE classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: He is RUDE and not clear what he wants!!!

Don't be fooled by the clarity of the lectures, this professor is the definition of inconsistency. The homework assignments are REALLY long, the questions that he asks in the homework and during the examinations are ambiguous. He cares more about giving you a hard time, than helping  you understand the meaning of the material. I hated his class.

Lectures are clear.  I somehow get the feeling though that this person is interested more in his research than teaching. This makes sense given that cmu is a research university. This class was taught by TAs way more than it should have been. He did not communicate very well over email when I have needed help.  He seems nice/has a lot of potential.
Extremely Ambiguous.
Friendly professor. Teaches well.
Extremely heavy handed
Absolutely horrific and unhelpful. Tanked my QPA
Great class.  Set me up to understand a lot of my later courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Tom Cortina masters the art of teaching the first class in such a tricky field as computer programming. Was really a pleasure to be in his class.
Cortina is such a great professor. He clearly cares about his students and is pretty well respected by most students. I would 100% take another one of his classes.
Tough class, but very well structured. The class can be boring at times, but Cortina is pretty good lecturer (if you can wake up for it).
Great lectures, difficult homework and tests
Gives very clear explanation, very funny guy - and after his class I understood that CS in my way to follow
Lectures are well-structured and well-paced. Really knows his stuff. Very clear during lecture. Funny guy, has great presence.
Very clear professor overall. Friendly in person, and exams are fair.
Friendly and knows his code.

Cortina is the man. Easily one of the best professors at CMU. Actually teaches concepts and applications, and does so very well. The class he teaches is very easy if you have already coded in your life, but if you are new to computer science, then Cortina is the guy for you. He will make you learn, and you will enjoy it.
awesome teacher, really cares, entertaining.  Great ability to explain complicated computer ideas to students like myself whom had no previous experience with programming
Great teacher really cares about students and a very entertaining lecturer

He is very funny and provides clear explanations of the concepts. The homeworks are very fun to do, but there is a lot with a written and programming hw every week. The exams are actually a bit difficult since there is so much you have to memorize about the history of computing
Awesome!!!
Hilarious and engaging. Often causes some confusion with his explanations, but a little thought and you get it right away. Takes an understandable pleasure in giving occasionally-hard work.

He's really a great guy. He handles the class like children far too often for him to hold my interest, but his class is very easy and you do learn some interesting things. He gives a quiz every week, but they are a joke so as long as you go you'll get an A. He makes it very easy to get good grades on everything. And he is always open to talk.
A great guy and a great lecturer. Principles of Computation is a unique take on the subject of Computer Science and is definitely worth taking.
I had him as a professor at Stony Brook Univ.  He was the best Lecturer I ever had, was so sad when he left.
No Comments
Very good teacher, although he does assign too much homework. Just go to class and an A is almost guaranteed, and you will learn a lot as well.

This teacher really teaches well. If you go lecture every time and you concentrate you will get A for sure. Test is not that hard. But too many written homework & programming homework (each week you get one written and one programming homework)
He is a good teacher, and explains things well.
go to him for help if you need it
Professor Cortina is a pretty good guy.  If you have to take 15-100, which a lot of people do, I'd recommend him.
Failed to support students in his department and created a hostile culture at the school of art- shouldn't have been allowed to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Couldn't run the department or his own career, just filled in the gaps left by others and pretended to care about art when in reality he clearly just cared about nothing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Carson has a reputation for destroying artists and was one of the worst people at CMU. Petty and aggressive and constantly interfering in other people's careers. Basically a scammer who exploited hundreds of young artists, taking their money and ditching them.
Clearly not qualified for this position, pretty much the type of teacher you'd expect to find at a community college.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The professor struggles to communicate concepts clearly, often jumping between topics without structure. Lectures feel disorganized, explanations are vague, and questions are sometimes dismissed, making it difficult to understand or engage with the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Clayton is amazing. He has taught about every kind of art student there can possibly be, so he immediately knows what you're trying to do and how to help guide you to your goal. He also knows a lot about pretty much every artist ever to feature in the annals of art history, so he always has good recommendations for each student. He's also very kind

I mean I now know more useless color theory facts than any human being should reasonably carry around. Like, thank you so much for teaching me how “warm tones advance and cool tones recede,” Clayton. Super helpful. If I ever need to overthink why blue next to orange is “vibrating,” I'll let you know.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Clayton's class was extremely disappointing. His approach to teaching painting felt disengaged and unstructured, and I rarely received meaningful feedback or guidance that could actually help me grow. For a program that promises artistic development and faculty support, this experience fell far short of expectations.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I honestly don't know why there's so many bad reviews recently, Clayton is probably everybody's favorite professor at CMU lol. Amazing person and teacher, I can tell he really puts his all into the classes and is genuinely one of the kindest teachers I've ever had. He's made such a good impact on so many students, and I would recommend to anybody.

Very nice professor, very understanding and super chill. However, Clayton doesn't give back grades until midterm or final so you have no idea what mark you are getting in the course. But all homework is manageable
very lovely, caring, and helpful. I wish he was a litter tougher in critiques.
Clayton is one of the greatest professors I have ever had. He is friendly, helpful,  and extremely knowledgable. To attend one of his lessons is a great honor and pleasure.
An awesome teacher. Involved with each student, a fair grader, and a talented artist.
I wouldn't be where I am today w/out Clayton. His willingness to help in and out of class is amazing. If you prove yourself to him he'll stop at nothing to help you out.
he's great.  mellow yet tough, helpful.
clayton is good for criticism
Very hot teacher with very hot hands (just look at them!!) and soothing voice...okay, he teaches well too, ya know.

Assigned the same project several times: do a drawing, rip it up, glue it back together, and draw over it again.  This was of limited help in expanding students' skills and appeared to be his personal schtick.  He did not provide strong leadership or structure for the class.
No Comments
Very bad teacher who is also a bad person.
Just don't. Those tests physically hurt me. It's fun when he randomly starts speaking in French or German though.

Pretty good lecturer, but his (four) midterms were impossible to finish in 50 minutes and barely curved. Probably would have been more motivated to learn if I wasn't destroyed by a midterm every 3 weeks. 
Wouldn't have rated this guy this low but his test-taking approach and stubbornness made me hate vector calculus.

Not easy but not awful. I learned a ton. PSETs are hard but exams are OK if your prepared for them. Lectures are clear, he pauses to answer everyones questions and explains things in lots of different ways. I wish there was more CS content but we learned about gradient descent.

He is a nice person but creates super difficult tests that are time crunches. He also grades harshly then refuses to release statistics about the grades to the class. If you have a choice between 259 and 266, choose 259.

Concepts can be a fast moving course and the problem sets really challenge you to your limits. Clive is a very entertaining and intelligence professor who's always open to help, so make sure you take advantage of the course resources and budget your time wisely.

Be prepared to learn beyond just memorization and repeated steps to solve certain types of problems - this course pushes you to truly abstract the concepts you learn. That being said, Clive was an incredible professor whose lectures were funny and thorough. Resources are available to you and Clive is a super smart guy who is always willing to help.
Professor Newstead is a legend. He gives nice course materials and well-organized notes. Tests are fair. Learned a lot in his class. Probably one of the best math classes I have ever taken.

Clive is a fantastic professor. His lectures are amazing. His midterms are fair, but the final was brutal. The problem sets weren't trivial, but the support was great and they were certainly doable. I personally found the textbook to be of very little use, as it often has the same conceptual holes as the lectures and not enough worked examples.
Great professor, very engaging and explains lectures well. Homeworks are not the easiest but lots of accessible office hours and support.

Clive is an amazing prof !!! I thought was going to fail, I got a mid semester grade of C- and almost dropped. I asked Clive to go through my midterms and homework with me and he gave me really useful advice. Finished with an A. It wasn't easy at. all. but in the end I'm proud of my achievement and couldn't have done it without Clive's help.

Clive is a god tier professor who loves math and wants his students to love math too. He gives clear and funny lectures and sets clear expectations for exams. The class was way harder than I expected a calc class to be but I learned a ton and it was my favorite class this semester. Take Clive if you can but be prepared to work for your grade.
Excellent lectures, easy to follow and interesting. Grading was also very fair, tests were in the exact same style as practice tests. Great overall, highly recommend
clive = goat

I really struggled in this class and even though my grade will probably be a C, it would have been an R without Professor Clive's help and I learned a LOT.he really cares but damn if this course is hard

The pace was really fast like we covered all of linear algebra and 3d calc in a single semester but professor Newstead made everything clear and interesting. Exams were difficult and but grading was fair. You could tell he cares about students and wanted us to do well. I didn't like the MATLAB as much but it was useful I guess.

Excellent Prof. Probably the best math teacher I have ever had. The class is extremely difficult, but Prof. Newstead tries to help every student succeed in his class. Make sure to attend lecture and OH. He offers drops on assignments and exams. The problem sheets are extremely difficult, so make sure to find friends to work with. Highly recommend!

he lied to the entire class on multiple occasions especially regarding our midterm. He also provides ZERO feedback as to how well you're doing in the course, resulting in multiple people dropping the course even though they would've ended with As or Bs. Never take a course with him, for your own good.

Super contradictory about guidelines in the course. When asking questions about the course, most of the time he lies. He frequently puts stuff on midterms that haven't been covered yet, and he often lies straight to your face when asked about content that is going to be on midterms.

At first, he seems like a very nice dude. That is, until you take one of his midterms. He will lie straight to your face about content that is going on the midterms, and some of the material on the midterm will be covered in the lecture after the midterm itself. It is safe to assume that if you ask a question, around 7/10 times he is lying to you.

Really great guy when you get to know him. Go to his OH, ask questions in class, he truly cares and wants to help. I heard he was a tough grader/gave hard exams but I think it's better now. One of the best math profs I have had here. He's a hidden gem.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: It's a required course with 5 exams and basically no preparation or resources given besides his impossible to understand textbook, and he is literally censoring students.
Hitler in the classroom

Awful teacher. 
Does not teach material properly but the hw is extremely long and difficult. Avoid him
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: He rambled for the entire class period. I would have taken the class and just read the textbook to learn the material, but he required attendance and didn't use a textbook. Painful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a good lecturer and responses on Piazza are relatively quick, however, he definitely has really high expectations and the homeworks are extremely difficult.

Cosma gives clear, well-paced lectures, and has a dark sense of humor. I get the sense sometimes that he'd like to tell more jokes, but he holds back so as to stay on topic. I learned a lot in this class. Statistical Computing (36-350) is most useful if you've done no or little programming, but I'd recommend it to anyone who needs to learn R.
She's mean
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Extremely bad
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It is not too hard to get an A in the class. Lectures felt really light and comprehensible. The first midterm was kinda difficult but each subsequent one got a lot easier. Her office hours are super helpful and she is really easy to reach.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The professor is a nice guy I guess. He hosts help sessions where he goes over all of the problem sets, probably because its impossible to try and do them by yourself. And he assigns two per week. The class is pretty theoretical as well.

He is too oldschool and hasn't updated his course with the technology available. Refuses to post answers on Canvas and instead prints out a bunch of copies that you can pick up in lecture. Not a single  grade is dropped and the tests are fairly difficult. The only time I actually learned anything was in recitation and the lectures are mostly theory

nice guy but assigns diabolical quantities of homework that barely pertain to the weekly exams. has unreasonably high expectations of students and exams have too difficult of content for people to perform even at a mediocre level. lied to the class about avg grades from past years being better to make them feel bad and study harder. exam avg of 50%
Hael Collins only wants his top 3 students to succeed. He doesn't care about the rest of the class's grades… No sympathy. No curves for super hard tests even though the average is below 50.
Hael Collins is a bad professor, his lectures can be somewhat interesting but he does not prepare you at all for the tests and has a harsh grading policy , does not curve when the average was 56.
Awful professor and makes sure everyone fails the course and tests, the average on each test continues to depreciate and is below 50 percent as the co

Avoid, take Anderson or wait for an alternative. Collins requires more than necessary. He gives very little leniency and requires more work than what is necessary. He is a decent lecturer, but fails at being a good professor. He can be not engaging at times and is not understanding of students other commitments.

Physics II sucks. Doing it with only two lectures a week sucks worse. All things considered, Collins does a pretty good job covering the extremely wide range of material in lectures (albeit a bit confusing sometimes). Turning problem sets in person is only slightly inconvenient. Tests/homework always have reasonable questions and grading. Good guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment:
Goes over mostly theory in lectures, never gives us actual examples to solve through lectures. Physics is a challenging subject without a great instructor and he proved that to be true!
I hope he gives out more useful lectures and my 3rd midterm was horrible since he posted highly theoretical questions in the end. you will need the textbook!
A great prof!

Teaches mostly theory and doesn't go through helpful examples in class. Recitation is mandatory and you will NEED IT. Gives a horrendous amount of homework each week with only a few days to complete it. Doesn't use Canvas - instead, all homework must be turned in by hand. If you are not familiar with ECE, this class will be VERY difficult.

I think Prof Collins is great and kind of underrated. It's very clear how to do well in his class. Spend some time on his homeworks, which are worth a lot of the grade, and you will do just fine in the class. The tests were very doable and the recitation problems were super useful. The course policies could be more lenient though.
Good instructor with reasonable expectations and standards for the class, not the most understanding of students with accommodations.
Very nice and cares about students a lot, but lectures are hard to follow sometimes (quite theory-heavy) and homework/tests are difficult and time-consuming.

Goes off on tangents during lectures, and homework is generally unrelated to lectures. Doesn't teach how to solve problems or how to logic through one. Physics is an incredibly difficult class without a good instructor, and this class definitely demonstrated this. Grade boundaries had to be lowered by 15% just so the distribution could be "normal".
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The professor is personally lovely, caring, and knowledgeable, however, the grading is not transparent throughout the semester. In my case, I was given a B while my grade was higher than the threshold of A (It was finally resolved.) While I believe this is just a mistake, I had never experienced this at CMU and schools earlier.

While I don't mind that his mandatory lectures don't relate well to the material. Why he does not accept virtual submissions baffles me, especially when he has shown a clear track record of losing submissions. 
His lectures do not help, he overloads homework for what is quite frankly a meaningless subject for most engineering students.

I have no idea how professor Collins has such a high rating considering the torture he puts his students through even in physics 1. Man goes through a bunch of useless content (once he even talked about gradients for 20 minutes) and then assigns a massive load of homework every week. His exams don't even relate to the problems he gives for practice

Imagine the worst professor you've ever had x10. That's Professor Collins for you. His lectures are boring 50 minute sessions of him making cringy jokes at 8:00 am in the morning. I redid all the homework and recitation sheets before the exam only to find the exam to be filled with problems completely different from what was taught. F-

attendance quizzes at the end of every 8 am lecture. lecture content does not match the homework and there is not enough time to fully understand the content before he moves on to the next topic leaving you confused and lost.

Can make the lectures entertaining with jokes but the material is still very abstract, theoretical, and at times hard to follow. Lecture material is important for understanding course topics, but it is not very useful for homework problems and many test problems. Recitation is very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Weekly problem sets take a few hours, but are medium difficulty. His tests are similar to the problem sets. Do the homework and you're good. The lectures are enjoyable because of his funny/cringey jokes. He is very nice and caring. His office hours and course center are your best friends. Overall, great professor! I really enjoyed his class!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: A great teacher who's able to keep us awake with his humor even in early morning lectures

I would give 6 stars. Every lecture is got little spur of the moment quips, a joke about the feelings of the variables, a funny remark about his personal life or interests. He doesn't get off topic though. On the days when your eyelids are made of lead, that sort of witty humor keeps you awake I wish he knew how much each student loves this guy.
we love hael <3333

Professor Collins is a very helpful professor. If you ever have issues understanding concepts, he's very willing to teach you. However, homeworks are long and he has mandatory book quizzes.  Overall an amazing teacher - just make sure to do the readings.
Great personality, a really nice professor

Haidar is hands down the worst professor in meche and cmu. she's very cold, unclear, and condescending and is pointlessly harsh and delusional. whoever says she's sweet is lying. she is NOT sweet or good at all, waste of your money and time
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very sweet and understanding professor, does not grade too harsh, and is fairly easy going.

Very cold and hard, fails to provide clear instruction, always blames on students, very unprofessional herself, is not that passionate about engineering herself, sets up a bad example! totally waste of your time
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Seems cold at first but is actually quite nice once you talk to her. Very willing to help if you ask good questions and she also gives a lot of insightful feedback. She cares about her students and does her best to make labs useful and enjoyable without too much difficulty.

Very disorganized and cruel, she's the vain of all professors in CMU! very passive aggressive and doesn't explain things clearly. Clearly, a shame on CMU for those paying $70K tuition, very poorly trained and terrible quality! Should never have come to CMU in first place

Glad I took the course while she only taught the labs, though seemed to me her TAs were doing most of the work. Doesn't understand very simple questions and doesn't even try, would rather give false instructions. Despite being incapable she is quite arrogant and loves telling people what to do. I feel bad for the kids who have to take her lectures.

Very cold, blunt, passive-aggressive and unqualified professor at all! There are never clear directions!!! What a shame on Carnegie Mellon's engineering. I am a prospective engineer in Meche with a bright dream and never expect anybody as incompetent as DIANA would teach 24-101! the tuition is already high emough and she is making it harder! shame!

Haidar made 24101 easier and you can tell that she cares for the students. However, overall the class felt like a worser version of my physics classes back in high school. The FIXATION with unit conversion made me want to gouge my eyes out and you will lose points over the dumbest things in homework/exams. Labs are the only plus for this class.
Useless lectures and terrible TAs

The course was state-of-the-art nonsense. So boring, so underprepared, and so random. Why would I spend time on Excel plotting and email writing in 24101?? Why??

And, the homework and quizzes of this course are so weird. Can't think of a better word to describe their quality except for LOW.

Never would I expect that 60k a year would give me this. There are never clear instructions. The TAs and the professor never seem to be on the same page. The professor can't seem to provide correct answer keys (her errors always get pointed out by students). She says that 4 midterm like quizzes respect students more than regular exams. Do NOT take.

It's a rare talent to explain a thing I fully understand in such a way that I leave confused on the subject. Her examples were long, convoluted, and often incorrect. Also, she gave us broken starter code and then offered no help with the highly technical errors (there was no CS req for the class). PS don't buy the textbook - you won't use it
I loved his lectures! They were very engaging and he explained difficult concepts well. The tests had quite interesting problems and he was a fair grader.
He did a really good job explaining abstract topics. He was also super accessible at his office hours and would make sure you understood the topic.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Although the 80% exam weighting is tough, his notes are always clear and amazing
Teaches really well.

Very good at explaining difficult concepts. At his OH he is very helpful and will answer all of your questions so that you can understand them. However, he does not provide enough examples to practice on so that you can get a grasp of everything.

The mark of a great professor is one who is able to take a complicated subject and turn it into something clear and comprehensible without losing out on any of the content. Quintana does this and more, he makes Linear Algebra and Vector Calculus intuitive and clear like no other instructor I have ever interacted with, online or otherwise. The GOAT.

Quintana is very good and simplifies the content as much as he can. The course is at the right difficulty, but concepts itself is a very hard class. If you go to OH he will try to help you as much as you can, he is very kind and good at explaining concepts from class
Good guy, good teacher. Took his 127 and 254 and really liked both. Haven't had too many math classes here yet, but his are by far the best I've taken.

He's super young but I think he might easily be one of the best professors I've had so far at CMU. His notes are amazingly organized, his lectures are easy to follow, and he's genuinely appreciative of your questions. Great guy.
He's amazing. His classes are so well put together and he has genuine passion for his work. 10/10 recommend him and I am hoping to get him for 21-254 too!

Dylan Quintana was a great professor for my linear algebra and vector calc combined class. While it was scary that exams were 80% of our grade, it all paid off in the end. This class will be rough at times, and you will feel challenged, but Dylan gives you all you need to succeed. If you go to lecture, and leverage his notes, you will succeed.

Hello world!!! I took 21-254 (linalg and vector calculus) with DYLAN QUINTANA, the man, the myth, the legend !!!!
He is a great professor and is very funny and approachable. One thing about this course is that you need to beware of MATLAB assignments, since MATLAB can be very tricky and you need to practice regularly. 

Great professor, very caring

While the homework and tests are difficult, Quintana gives you everything you need to accomplish it. His whole class setup is the most well-put together I have ever seen, and I am forever grateful for his guided notes. He truly cares about his students, and it's certainly worth it to attend every lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: There is no denying 21127 is a hard class, but taking it with him makes it so much better.

Very well organized.  Class website made it easy to find any notes if you had to miss a lecture.  Lectures were also very methodical and easy to follow.  The homeworks were definitely the hardest part of the class, ranging from average difficulty to a handful of tough questions, but never excessively hard.  Overall excellent class, very well taught
Good professor course is tough though
really clear lecture and notes, very caring and reasonable (homework and exams) and gives good feedback :D

He is not the best at explaining concepts, although his note sheets are good. Unfortunately, it is clear that he does not really care about the success of anyone in his class, so much that multiple people this semester had to drop it, including myself, and generally shows disregard when shown class concern.
the way he teaches is so ineffective and genuinely PMO
Such an awesome and chill guy. I highly recommend taking 15-451 with him

An absolutely abysmal lecturer. No idea why he is still teaching, but you want to avoid this teacher at all costs. It would be one thing if it was just his lectures that were terrible, but he also designs bad questions and overall runs the course poorly. If you think a co-instructors will save things, think again.

Not an effective lecturer. When the other professor for the class is lecturing, often interrupts for the sake of arguing a minor mistake that the entire class has already caught and mentally rectified, understanding the intent. More of a hindrance to the class overall.

I immediately regretted showing up for class whenever this man was the one lecturing.  Brilliant at Chess and Splay Trees; total and utter failure at communicating ideas clearly and avoiding excessive detail

On the occasions you stay awake, you will be lost in a sea of hand-waving proofs, obtuse mathematical/CS concepts, and loosely scribbled info on the board. He's definitely brilliant, but his lecturing leaves much to be desired, especially for an undergrad level. Office hours occasionally helpful for understanding some theory though. Good at chess.
Great teacher, and an even better person. She cares a lot for her students if you put in the effort in her classes.

I wonder why people like her become teachers, if only to spread their misery onto others, because she speaks with clear resentment and condescention to her students. I witnessed a classmate crying after Sharon humiliated her during a lecture. I went to her after class because I was struggling - she told me that I was sub-par and to drop the class.

Literally the best professor I ever had at CMU. I took 4 classes with her and can confidently say that all the bad reviews she has are unfounded. Sharon is so kind and genuinely cares about teaching and her students' learning. If you approach her outside of class she is always willing to help. If you are struggling she will help if you communicate!

Sharon's one of the most difficult professor's I've ever had, and that's the best part about her. She expects a lot from you, and if you rise to meet the challenge, that feels VERY rewarding. My strongest piece of advice is to try and genuinely make an effort to connect with her. She's insightful and knowledgeable, but you must put in the effort.

I thought people were exaggerating but this teacher really is the rudest and most unkind person I've ever met. She picks on students and is incredibly egotistical. Her lectures are entertaining when you aren't the butt of her mean jokes. All I've learned about screenwriting is that screenwriters can be bitter + cruel people. Take with someone else.

She could come off as cold and uncaring but she is genuinely a really great teacher who gives good feedback and cares about your ideas. She's not afraid to tell you when something won't work and is strict on certain things but if you abide by her rules she is truly an amazing professor.

I have approved health accommodations of flexible attendance due to various cancer testing and surgeries. Sharon absolutely did not care about these medical accommodations and insisted that attendance was still mandatory despite me being in and out of the hospital. The lack of respect and compassion she showed is truly outstanding. DO NOT TAKE!

Very unempathetic person and professor. You'd think after the negative feedback she's gotten over the long time  spent teaching at CMU that she would try to be better but nope. Class material is sometimes fun but the teaching and feedback is bad. Very unorganized. Also proudly displays the Zi*nist flag in her office right in your face. No decorum.

I dont know why all of the film professors at CMU are the worst but Sharon certainly takes the cake. She is incredibly condescending and expects students to read her mind. Assignments never have instructions until 24-48 til they are due, she gives horrible and honestly nasty feedback, and the syllabus is illegible do not take a class with her.

One of the worst professors I've had. Values her ego over her students and could care less about them. No set office hours, syllabus has no grading criteria, schedule, or policies pertaining to the class. Grades not uploaded online and bare minimum feedback on assignments. I want to ask these kinds of people why even chose to become a professor.S23

I don't understand the bad ratings. Sharon is one of my favorite professors, I took her intro to creative writing class and it's one of the best decisions I've made. She makes class engaging, her activities are fun. She cares greatly for her students and even gave us her phone number, telling us to call her if we're ever in need of help.
