awesome teacher! took her for intro to creative writing and she taught me a lot while being very nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I've had the displeasure of having Sharon 3 times. Disrespectful and unprofessional. Condescending and unkind to students. The guidelines for assignments are unclear (doesn't have a syllabus for papers), and feedback is both nasty and vague. Unable or unwilling to help students better their craft. No extra credit. Will fail you over one late paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Sharon. Sucks. This woman is the type of teacher that asks students an open ended question expecting one answer. If the student isn't able to read her mind, she has some back-handed comment that makes them feel stupid. This professor also believes that the world ends with her class. Miss the min two classes, and she will have a tantrum.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Tough, but fair. Good discussion of screenwriting craft. Relevant films shown and analyzed.
I will never take another class with this professor. She is condescending and unable to explain anything and once you don't understand she does not hesitate to make you feel dumb.

She sounds all fun and humorous until you write/do something that doesn't appeal to her. After one meeting with her she said things like, "You shouldn't be a creative writing major," "Your writing is sub-par for the class," "You should find another professor or drop the class." After hearing that she has done this very often, I dropped the class.

Sharon is an excellent professor, writer, and wonderful person. I took several classes with her and appreciate her sense of humor, honesty, and cut-to-the-chase attitude. If you are serious about becoming a better creative writer, you must take her classes.

Lots of fun, very enthusiastic about the material. If you're interested in the writing, she's very approachable and is willing to spend a lot of time helping you develop your ideas. Her outgoing personality made her one of my favorite CMU English Professors. However, she doesn't give out too many A's, but I wouldn't let that fact scare you away!

He struggles to communicate concepts clearly, often jumping between topics without structure. Lectures feel disorganized, explanations are vague, and questions are sometimes dismissed, making it difficult to understand or engage with the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Duesing's failures as an educator are only trumped by his inadequacies as a mentor and friend. He spent his career at CMU failing to build or develop any substantial program to support students, and left as soon as posssible. It was clear to everyone around him that mentoring, teaching, and even animating were not important to him.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Incredibly toxic professor. Failed to cover even the most basic materials in class. Waste of time and money. Glad he finally retired!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Duesing's Social History of Animation was one of the best classes I took at CMU. It let me see animation as an art form and social artifact. I took 2 studios with him. Art and Tech was hard but great for team building. Our team's animation got me my first job. He has incredible knowledge and was a generous teacher.
Talkative. Too many stories to tell. Trainwreck in lecture delivery

Dr. Donahue is as intelligent as he is funny and light-hearted. He goes off on tangents easily, but is well aware of this tendency and usually manages to incorporate a lot of interesting and relevant content into his side discussions. He knows his content extremely well and is a very highly respected member of his field. Plus, he has great hair.
I met Neil Donahue and  can't wait to tale his class! He is quick as a whip, kind as a doe and plus he has cool hair!

Hippie teacher who loves what he does. challenging subject (quantum mechanics etc.) with little natural intuition but you'll get through. fair grader and tries to accurately represent your work/effort.
Amazing professor! Super sweet and great professor.  Your grade is almost directly correlated to effort, so as long as you're trying your best your grade will probably reflect it.
She is fantastic! Great professor and human being.
Learning is difficult if you don't look at the material beyond its face value. However, if you need help in the course, Parno will make sure you get it.

Great professor. Her lectures are clear and informative. Covers much of the same material as AP Physics C: Mechanics, but with more formality, although you will learn about basic quantum and thermal physics. Don't need to do anything super intense for your semester project to get an A. Programming assignments can be frustrating/unclear at times.
Dr. Parno is awesome. Her lectures are funny and engaging, and she is very accessible outside of the classroom. She is also very kind and willing to help. 10/10 professor.
Sometimes questions are ambiguous and hard to understand on problem sets and other assignments.

Course is tough and accelerated but she is a great professor. There are review questions w/ answers before midterms. I will say the lecture sometimes ending a little early is fine, consider the lecture is 2 hours. Also, the reason why she doesn't show us some calculations bc they are just pages long calculations that derive the formulas....

Great person. Made an effort to answer all of your questions. I think she overestimated our ability to understand on our own. Definitely don't fall behind in this class because stuff builds a lot. Some of the exams weren't that bad; some were really really bad. She's not unfair though. Look at textbook illustrations for confusing homework problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Her first semester teaching this course, but she didn't seem to put effort into well-preparing students for the exams. Almost every lecture ended 5-15 minutes early, but she wouldn't explain many things, telling us to look at the notes when she could've more clearly showed us. Also didn't post review material/answers, saying she didn't have time.
My queen. She put in so much effort and time to make sure students learn and is always patient even when you are genuinely dumb (like me). The subject is tough tough but she is still my queen
He was kind, respected, and a wonderful lecturer with a really positive energy
He was a great lecturer
His lectures were engaging and fun
One of the most enthusiastic and caring professor I had at CMU
He said the final was cumulative and to review earlier content. THE ENTIRE FINAL WAS ON AFTER MIDTERM 2 CONTENT. Midterm 1 was fine but midterm 2 and the final was diabolical.

Course quality is terrible. Lectures are almost useless (Erdmann seems like a good person but dull and overall bad at teaching). Content is disjoint and covered unrigorously (i.e. definitions are often left up in the air, even when necessary). Homework is unnecessarily time-consuming. Grading is incredibly pedantic. Overall very badly structured
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Tests are horrendously unnrepresentative of what you learn, grading is insanely nitpicky. The class is purely a reflection of how much time you invest in it, and Erdmann expects you to devote your life to the class even though the content is dull. 
Also very unaccomidating, refused to allow kids to miss class for highly selective programs (FTTP).
Nice professor
He is enthusiastic and cares a lot about his students. Very engaging and inspirational lectures. Midterms were easy.
He's a pretty good lecturer and knows the material fairly well.
Brilliant, caring professor. You can tell that he really knows the material. He seems to have a lot of fun teaching during lectures

Very engaging lectures though he does tend to go off on tangents. He has an enthusiasm for the subject and is very easy to talk to. Very nice and caring. His midterms are hard though, and the h.w's are written by TA's and tend to be very long. No practice midterm material. Lecture notes are thorough though very technical so difficult to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Erdmann is a legend. Love that man.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof. Schimmerling's lectures are very clear and well-organized, and he always welcomed questions from students. The homework and exams are challenging, but he has been incredibly supportive. He genuinely cares about students' academic progress. He is truly a kind, respectful and helpful professor.

I feel the last reviewers. His lectures are very dry, and he doesn't seem to be able to explain the material well. He uses a "textbook" that he wrote which is horrible. Mid-term and final exams are based on only one single chapter, so luck is a significant factor. I'd recommend 15-317 instead, which is its alternative for most purposes.

Trust me, avoid him at all costs. He will likely be the weirdest professor you ever have to deal with. His quote: "If you haven't looked at the assignment, you are doomed. (*chuckle*) yeah you are doomed." His exam: "Write up proof of a theorem which is three-page long." His exam feedback: "You don't know what you are talking about."

Hard to approach outside of class. His lectures are basically straight out of the textbook, which, by the way, he wrote and he forces you to buy (hw problems were assigned from book). Too many irrelevant tangents in class. Should've listened to the users below, avoid this teacher.

At first I thought he was a complete jerk, but after I talked with him I figured out he just doesn't know how to teach and is probably a little deficient in social skills. It really wouldn't be bad at all, if my grade weren't suffering for it.
schimmerling doest know how to teach and he has something stuck up his ass... he should stop teaching
Reading the book is more helpful

Yeah, so I dropped this course after others did.He is completely unintelligible and unclear when he teaches. He teaches in such a vague manner and says stuff like "you could try this..it's wrong, btw, but in a way its right.." and rambles, he's not organized. He likes guests at office hour, but I just can't understand him.
This guy is unintelligible.  I actually think I'm getting WORSE at calculus.  His tests are TERRIBLE.  Thank god for the TAs, or everyone would flunk.
apparently this is his first year teaching this class although he still blows.  im lucky i know calc and i just bombed the placement test because hes the worst prof ever.
I thank God each class for my laptop or I'd die.

I fall asleep every week in this class and end up learning more, in the recitation than during lecture. He starts every class with, "I am going to review what we did last time." You do the math, but that means you don't move forward, at all.

His class is a nightmare. Thankfully I already know all the calculus concepts we're going to cover: otherwise I would be screwed. It's not that the material itself is hard, but rather that he is completely unintelligible half the time during his lectures. He often makes mistakes during calculations, so more than once I've had to rewrite my notes...
What kind of calculus professor is so lazy that he doesn't use the chalkboard? Be serious, slides?! And I paid HOW MUCH for this?! No matter what he says he's not interested in teaching.
Do yourself a favor... Don't take Calculus in 3-D with him. Based on the FCEs, it seems like he's pretty good for Set Theorty, but who takes that?!

worst teacher ever. i had to teach myself everything, and even then i did horrible on his tests. his tests only cover like 2% of the material he expects us to know for the exam. also, he is really boring.

his test are so random, and no matter how hard you study you need luck to do well on his tests. he teaches by using transparencies, and his lectures are so boring. most of the class is asleep. he just rewrites the examples in the textbook for his lectures
This professor was the worst one I've had yet at Carnegie Mellon...I had to teach myself the material and the curriculum coverage was so much more than the exam coverage. Horrible professor.

The material isn't difficult; I knew it all and aced most of the exams.  At times he asks ridiculous questions, though, and is very unwilling to reward those who actually know the material.  Note: I knew the material b/c I read the book; he doesn't teach.
The most boring man ever to walk the earth.
No Comments
No Comments

His tests only focus on a randomly selected 5% of the material - it's so ridiculous.  Someone who knows virtually nothing on the chapter can walk out with an A while someone who has mastered most of the chapter gets a C.  You have to be lucky to do well.
Good lectures, very nice and kindly when grading the exams and homeworks.

Very nice professor, clearly cares about students' learning, and makes sure to explain the content in a way that is relevant and easy to apply. Also won a teaching award this year for Tepper faculty, proving how great he is
Top professor from CMU. Great lectures and teaching style is great!

Professor Erol is absolutely amazing and one of my favorite professors! He is very knowledgeable, takes the time to explain the why behind concepts, and is passionate about what he teaches (which actually made me like economics!). He is so kind to students and always takes questions after class.
really enjoyed his class! explained concepts very well and was always willing to help
Soooo easy. Practice exams were identical to real exams. Best and easiest course at CMU.
Professor Erol is an amazing person and teacher who cares about his students both in and out of the classroom. One of my favorite teachers ever--he made me so much more interested in Economics.
Reasonable and fair professor - not sure why people had negative things to say.
Amazing Professor!!! Highly recommend him.
Very very hard class. He is a nice guy but is unfair with his homeworks and tests. They often have nothing to do with lecture. Going to his lectures and especially office hours is extremely useless.
One of my favorite professors
He is a horrible professor. Doesn't teach anything and then tests you on random economic theories he came up with during his time at UPenn.
Take Anne instead
dont take it

Iliano is an experienced professor with extremely detailed slide decks. He gets a lot of unwarranted hate for his strict AIV policy, but he has it because he cares about the rigor of the class, and that people truly understand the material. That passion comes through in his teaching, and he's a pretty funny guy when you get past the rough exterior.
the rating score is inflated.

Probably the most overhated professor at CMU. Checkins were fair with respect to course content taught and tons of practice were given before every checkin. Professor was also very approachable in OH. If you understand the practice materials given deeply, you will do well in the class.

Taking this class felt like paying thousands to be gaslit twice a week. The professor doesn't teach—they walk in, read a dated PowerPoint in a monotone, and call it a lecture. When I asked about a grade, I got a blank stare. If you like confusion and teaching yourself via 2 a.m. YouTube, this is the prof for you.

Avoid this professor if you value your GPA and sanityThey are incredibly unorganized, and the syllabus is more of a suggestion than a rule. Assignments are graded based on arbitrary criteria that aren't mentioned in the prompts, and feedback is non-existent.
Very Harsh

Genuinely Prof Iliano needs to read the questions on Ed. Whenever someone asks a completely reasonable question about content on an assignment/upcoming weekly quiz, he always says something like "read it more closely" even when the question is reasonable and it's clear that "reading it more closely" or "thinking about it more" isn't going to help.

I am friends with TAs who have given me info on Iliano's grading system. It's designed to punish students cheating with AI, but it instead makes the course weigh 97% on in-class quizzes and the final exam, with our long homeworks being worth 0%. The average looks good, but it's because so many people already know these topics or dropped the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: No class at CMU has made me feel this terrible ever in my life. Every week, it feels like a boulder is resting on your shoulders but will never fall off unless it just crushes you. This class made me burnout so quickly that I'm rethinking my major and future plans since I've never put in so much work into something just to get nothing back.
I don't want to study cs anymore after taking his 15122 in spring.

Illiano is literally trying not to be a human with his new grading criteria. A lot of people had to drop the class because 72% of your final grade is based on min (weekly quizzes, homework). His class average may seem a little reasonable because all the Cs and some low Bs dropped, so the ones remaining are just As and high Bs competing.

Iliano has designed 122 in an evil manner, taking the minimum grade of your weekly quizzes and your programming projects for 72% of your grade.that means all my programming projects, well over 50 hours of work this sem. will have been for nothing. Iliano is evil and has no regard for his students effort. Good luck if you ever take 122.
anyone who left a good review is lying

I genuinely believe that this professor is evil. He actively searches for students who are cheating, and you can get falsely accused and you will get a NEGATIVE 100 score on that assignment. The line to get an appeal takes over a semester because so many people are accused. Highly highly avoid this professor if you can.
Fantastic lectures, difficult but extremely rewarding class.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Prof Iliano is an effective, clear lecturer, and his class is very well-organized. I believe the negative comments about his personality are outdated. He is nice now and hilarious during lectures.
Iliano lacks any care towards his students
Doesn't respect his students. I never went to his lectures so I don't know so much about his teaching.

Avoid him by all means. He is not understanding of students situations, both financially and emotionally. Personally I did not have issues but so many of my classmates had issues because he required students to get a tablet when they couldn't afford it. In terms of teaching he clearly thinks he has gods gift, but he does not, he is just rude.
Bad prof avoid at all costs
Dont take his course unless you have to

Tough class, will effectively be a part-time job in itself on top of the rest of your courses, I clocked in probably an average of 25 hours a week. But you learn a lot, and the professor is genuinely good at teaching, plus there are abundant resources. I got several 70% grades on check-ins halfway through but turned it around.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: really generous for giving out As, which A stands for AIV
I don't really find him very useful, since the writeups for some of the practice problems are just not worded in a way that an average person can understand them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Just not a good guy nor a professor

He is very understanding and caring. He was extremely helpful and supportive when I had personal issues during the school year. In addition the course materials were certainly very useful - prepares you well for future classes and career in cs.
no bueno para estudiar con iliano. le recomiendo que toca anne
Unnecessarily difficult for what the course is. Wording of the questions are incredibly confusing for no reason. Absolutely tragic.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Probably the worst professor I've had at CMU. If possible, avoid him for 122, he does not care about his students and is completely unapproachable. Decent lecturer but that doesn't make up for how condescending and awful he is in person. Save yourself from a mountain of unnecessary stress if you can.
made me cry in the 6th floor gates bathrooms after oh

He's honestly so bad, please avoid taking him at all costs. He doesn't teach you anything -  you have to teach yourself whatever is to be expected, and everyone in the class agrees upon this consensus. He is also one of the least empathetic people I've ever met. He literally said he doesnt care if your family or pet dies - your hw is still due.
He's honestly abrasive and doesn't care about his students. Sticks out like a sore thumb in the SCS faculty.
Iliano is an amazing lecturer but he is sarcastic to his students. However, I didn't go to OH a lot so it was fine for me.
I heard that he made the course easier where writtens are less valued in the course. However, the past semesters have struggled...
Excellent lecturer but the course is very very hard... You have to do a lot of work for extra credits if you do not do good on exams. Do not take this course unless it is mandatory for your degree :(
This course has been miserable. Do not take 15122 unless you absolutely have to, and if you do prepare to for a semester of pain.
He probably has a low EQ as he always talks in a condescending manner, even in lecture and OH.
Just terrible
Has insanely high standards and is extremely strict
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: He is just so annoying. He thinks he's cool but he's not
Very inconvenient deadlines
So much time and so hard
His tests are so hard and he blames us for not doing well when there are 400 kids and does not take the blame himself.
He is very unrealistic with what he expects from his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Apparently he makes the tests harder each year, which just messes everybody up. He is a terrible professor
Grrrrrr Iliano is the worst
Just bad
His accent is so thick
Just a terrible professor
This was not only conceptually difficult, but also very tedious. It truly was one of the hardest classes I took.
Very rude & condescending
All of these reviews are right. He is not a great professor and is very harsh to students.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He makes me stay up until 2 AM just because of HOMEWORK when I am a athlete and need to wake up early in the morning. Don't even get me started on the exams.
He is cruel in office hours and does not really help much.
Lectures were hard to get through, exams even harder, and homework is really tedious too.
He is cruel to his students. Extremely long exams and homeworks. He expects us to just learn basically everything on our own because he does not do it in lecture.

He is the complete opposite of the tags I chose. I had to leave campus for a sports event (varsity), so it's pretty important. But he chooses to not give us any extensions or any sort of help even though we do not have the same resources as on campus. He needs to treat his students the way he wants to be treated.
You are better off skipping lectures and reading the notes yourself.
this course is so great
Generational trauma @ CMU
Finals was 60+ pages

Illiano's a nice professor! Very responsive over Diderot. I remember looking at this page before I took the class and being very worried by all the reviews here - don't put too much stock into them.

Some of the grading of the writtens/exams often felt pedantic. The midterms are way too much of a time crunch (especially the first!) in my opinion.
Deffo a meme. S-tier material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: The course is fun overall but the lectures are kind of long. The lectures are sleepy, so you need to bring a pillow to the class. Also, you better bring a pillow for your pillow because your pillow may also fall asleep.
His lectures are fun most of the times, although each class being 1.5h is kinda long.
He is very nice
he is no nice
bad professor.
Horrible professor, doesn't care about his students, doesn't have any leniency in grading, and makes his midterms impossibly difficult.
Easy class and easy A. No work. It's not even a class!

This course is amazing. While initially the point-to proofs could take some time to grasp, the overall workload is light. Expect each written assignment to take 1-2 hours and each programming assignment 1-2 hours per week. The labs are Monday are also fun and you get free ducky for doing the optional problems ;)
He cares so much to his students.

I went to one of his office hours in the beginning of the year to ask about material we had just learned and never went again. He was condescending and just referred me to a recitation that had not yet occurred. If you do take the class, I would probably wait for recitations on Friday to clear up any misunderstandings.

I've tried to talk to Prof. Iliano a few times, and every time I did I just felt worse; He was very condescending and never really answered my questions, but just made me feel dumb. Prof. Dilsun is not like this however: she takes time to help and is very caring. The class is hard but just push through and focus on learning and you'll be ok.

If you put in the work, you should be able to get about an A average on your homework. The difficult part is getting good grades on the 16 page long midterms. My best recommendation is to refresh yourself on major topics, and most importantly, to get a good nights sleep before the exam. It is basically a reading comprehension test.
horrible teacher.
he is so nice!

I took 15-122 as the very first CS introductory class at CMU. The assignments had little to no rampups, and the tests were brutal. The "curve is bad for you" mentality invented by Iliano and the merciless grading policies made me question if all CS classes are like this. The answer is no. Just know you are doing great no matter what grade you get.
Too many hws, heavy exams, heavy course
Very good once you get used to him
Really bad professor. He doesnt care about his students at all. Its like he gets an ego boost about his course if the students are struggling. Would not recommend

For the first midterm, he'd rather change the marking rubric and let TA mark all over again than curving. He made the course so hard that it is an emotional damage. I heard from all of my friends that you can spend all time studying and still get a B. Iliano says 15122 is hard because it gets you prepared for 15213 but it is way too hard.
It's not his fault. It's my fault
The class material itself is hard, and he doesn't really make it any better. You will feel like you are constantly humiliated.
Hard and infamous for no curve

This is a very difficult course, however, Illiano is a great lecturer and explains the topics very well. The course has 2 midterms, each worth 12.5%, which are the worst part of the class because they are time-crunch based exams. However, the class is doable because homeworks are worth 50% of your grade. Put in effort, youll be fine.
Run.
Just not a good lecturer. I complete this course by notes and attending recitations.

The most intimidating professor I've had at CMU. Not easy to approach. Randomly accuses people (not me though) of cheating. 
I'd say his lectures were generally good though. I liked the way he explained certain topics. If you skip lecture often, at least try to go to the binary search one, cause it's probably the most interesting lecture.
He is the worst professor I have ever met.
Class content's great, but the lecture, homework, and exams are just not designed for you to understand even you know the concepts and study a lot.
Very difficult class, but very rewarding. Iliano can be intimidating at first, but very nice.

I genuinely liked the material, although I didnt get the best grade I liked what the class was about. However, the Professor made it hell. Where do I start, from telling me I dont know anything about coding and wont get any internships to telling me I have dumb questions. Id get humiliated every time and cry after OH with him. Avoid this class!
RRRRRRUUUUUUNNNNNNN

Quite possibly one of the worst professors I've ever had at CMU. If this class isn't a requirement for you, do not take it. If it is, grind on the homeworks because you will do miserably on all the exams.
Iliano is intimidating. Meeting with him one on one made me a little scared and judged. That being said, he knows his stuff. He is smart and gives interesting lectures. Just not approachable.

DO NOT TAKE HIS COURSE. Iliano was hands down the most uncaring and straight up mean-spirited professors I have ever had. He goes out of his way to humiliate and degrade his students. He caused me to have a full blown panic attack in the bathroom of Gates

Literally the worst and most disgustingly rude Professor at this entire university. Refused to help me during office hours. Course material is also unnecessary and just so entirely dumb. Avoid him at all costs, even if it means taking it during the summer and paying extra. No idea why he hasnt been fired yet.
Literally the worst. Absolute Garbage. Cannot tell you enough avoid his course (15122) at all costs, and only take it if it's absolutely necessary (required by your major).

Iliano is not a terrible professor, but his lectures are pretty boring. I did fine in the class by attending supplemental instruction sessions once a week rather than watching lecture. You were able to see your predicted grade throughout the course. I think the difficulty rating is deceiving; the hw is doable, just needs more time than some give it
He cares a lot, but he's just kinda boring. He's alright.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Avoid at all costs if possible. Really unsupportive and does not really care about students.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I dont think he deserves the grade he gets, hes actually a great professor. The class is just really hard and I think thats why people often score him so low but I dont think the difficultly changes between whichever prof you take it with.

Has completely taken all the fun out of learning computer science. Although the course content is important, it is taught in a way that doesn't show its practicality, which makes it a huge slog to the end. The things we learn at the beginning of the course suddenly become moot when we transition to learning a new language at the end.

I honestly don't understand why people are so critical of this class. I really enjoyed the course, and the grading structure (how much of your grade counts based on exams) imo is very fair. There are many things you can read to help get you to understand the material without the lectures, and OH lines are never long.

Tests do not accurately reflect understanding of the material, but rather just challenges how fast you can read and solve random problems. Not particularly nice to students. Every year he self praises his class and attributes the number of CS graduates employed at google to the fact that his class isn't curved. My least favorite professor
If you want to do well in written homework... you'd better be good at guessing what iliano wants you to do.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Iliano teaches really well, and his lecture flows are pretty good. He leads you from simple ideas and go to more complex ones. If you follow along, his lectures are pretty helpful in understanding the context. I got intimidated by the reviews on this website prior to selecting the course, but he's actually a very nice guy. Encourages learning.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. Does not care about students and gives poor lectures, meaning you have to read the lecture notes to understand the material. Ever since he took over 122, the course became a disaster. Really wish SCS would find a new professor since he clearly has no idea what he's doing.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Resources isn't as helpful as previous programming classes. Classes could be made much more interesting. Explanations are not clear sometimes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Iliano's lectures are a great complement to lecture notes. While lectures notes are ultimately more essential, his lectures give a good reasoning for the material we learn. I have been to his office hours and he tried to help me become an independent learner rather than depending on help. He's a nice guy and a tough professor.

It's so hard to hear what he is saying and his accent is thick. Everyone falls asleep in his lecture which means you need to take time out to read lecture notes in order to know what you were supposed to learn. His TAs are terrible and I can never make his office hours. I wish I didn't have to take this course with him
Course material was good, class was horrible. Written assignments were a guessing game of "What does Iliano want?" Not fun. Did better after not going to lecture. :(
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Not too bad but he could care more about his teaching.

Iliano is the least caring professor on the face of the planet. He thinks that curves are only in place to accommodate for mediocre students. However, there are other factors like tough tests and terrible teachers.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: He thinks his class is useful, but it is very pedantic and not really that practical or relevant. The 'proofs' are not proofs in any sense of the word, and you are docked points for not following their exact solution, even if your proof makes sense.
Does not understand course material and frequently makes mistakes in answering questions.
This man does not care about his students and is unqualified to teach this course. Additionally, he does not care about grades and will not curve the class even if all the test averages are below 70.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Not bad, but his lectures are very hard to understand. Don't worry, though, the lecture notes are quite comprehensive.
Avoid him at all cost. Toxic professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Really nice guy and an okay teacher but the lecture notes are way way wayyyy more helpful than his lecture
One of the worst professors I ever had. Avoid him at all costs.

Prof Evans was very helpful my first year. I give a 4 only because writing is hard for me so the writing assignments took me longer than others in the class. She met with me and made sure I knew what campus resources exist for international students which no one else did.
Take anything she is teaching. She's young and gets it.

Working with her changed my life. Seriously. She helped me find my voice as a writer. She is always willing to meet outside of class time, and goes above and beyond in feedback. Wish I had encountered her sooner - if you can take her in your first year, do it. (Took her for Intro to Gender Studies not 106.)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof Evans genuinely wants to see everyone win, unlike many other people at this hell hole. When I needed a last-minute letter of rec, even after being out of her class for a semester, she came through. I still think about the reading she had us do in class on a regular basis, too.

Everyone has to take first year writing. Not everyone is lucky enough to take it with Prof Evans. I don't care how much more “useful” you think the other topics will be - you're wrong. Believe me. 10/10

Taking this professor's class has been one of the best parts of my CMU experience. Her empathy shines through her teaching, and it is genuinely harder to fail than to pass (with flying colors). All you need to do is try. I would 100,000% recommend taking her classes, especially Writing About Literature, Art, and Culture.
The class was a breeze thanks to this professor.
Professor Evans is the BEST. She is so kind and understanding and the class itself is very enjoyable.
Icon. Would listen to her talk about literally anything.
Love Prof Evans. Super flexible and understanding.
Favorite class first year. 10/10
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super low key class. There is a bit of reading and writing, but it is broken into many weeks and doable. She clearly cares about the content too.

Easy A, smart professor, and interesting content. Of the writing minis I took, Prof Evans was the best. She cared about the students and made sure no one fell behind. The class was at 9 am, but I never missed a class because I wanted to hear what she was going to say and knew she would value my opinion.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Prof Evans is the most caring person I have met at CMU. When I came down with COVID, she checked in with me after each missed lecture and provided extra feedback on rough drafts. Her section focused on graphic literature (not superheros but memoir), and led me to read things I had never encountered before. Do yourself a favor and take her class!

Readings are heavier, especially towards the end of the class, but Fields-Black is remarkably accommodating for late work and/or group conflicts. Speaking of which, if you aren't a fan of group papers, the class might not be your favorite; nonetheless, the class is fascinating, and most importantly, manageable. I

The professor's disorganization creates a stressful environment, with late grading, unclear instructions, and poorly communicated expectations. The overwhelming reading load and lack of communication make the course unnecessarily difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is a wonderful teacher; really knows her subject.  Her teaching is very clear and her class is well-structured.  Lots of reading and the essay prompts are really, really hard.  Since she knows her subject so well, there is no bs-ing the essays at ALL.
Kyle better
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you want a pushover class that glosses over the proofs in a proof-based class, take this course with this professor. You won't be disappointed. It's like she's misread the demographic and the point of the class. She says we go slower to get a more conceptual understanding but we really just cover less content due to her inefficient style.

Pros: by far the easiest math class I've ever taken & prof is enthusiastic. Cons: She treats you like a middle schooler. Attendance is graded. I learned absolutely nothing & it was overall a huge waste of time. Watch a few 3blue1brown videos & save yourself the trouble.

I thoroughly enjoyed learning from Prof. Fatma last Fall. She was always well-prepared in the lectures, very approachable during the office hours, and made learning so easy and fun. I'd love to have the opportunity to take another class from her before my graduation.
Excellent professor! Would definitely take another course from her. She cares about her students and is very knowledgable about the topic she teaches.

50% of your grade is 6 quizzes and 50% is a final exam. What a lazy approach to teaching. Less for her to grade and higher risk for you. You have to prey that emailing her TA will get you a couple more points. Her modules can be hours of her talking but not getting to the point. She is a smart person but a terrible teacher.

The lectures were very engaging and interactive (handouts, live demos, candy!) Many assignments have bonus questions to boost grades, but some problems are "all or nothing" with little partial credit if you don't get the full solution. Overall I really enjoyed this class.
Great teacher and great course
Strong lecturer, connects probability to real world problems. Attending his office hours helped me improve my performance in the class. The tests are hard but fair. Wish we had more time on quizzes.

He goes very fast in lecture and breezes through the fundamentals, and spends too much time on the advanced topics while 90% of the class is lost and confused. The tests do not reflect a good understanding of the material and there are no practice exams. The exam questions are not similar to the homework or available questions in the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: In lecture, he goes over the textbook material way too quickly to understand completely (compared to other lecturer). 10 minute in class quizzes are very difficult to complete unless you read the question and instantly know how to solve it. Little partial credit and the grading feels like it doesn't reflect understanding of material.
He is extremely disconnected from the reality of most students in the course. Grades tests harshly and is not very nice in general.

Very bad at lecturing hes in his own world. He assumes what you already know. Does not get to the bottom or the logic of concepts. It is like telling you to use a calculator or an equation to do derivatives but never tells you what it actually meant and what it implies. He will not connect different ideas together to make it easier to understand.
In general, I would not take his class ever. Homework extremely long, exam questions are hard.

He seems like a caring and generally nice guy, but he doesn't explain anything intuitively. I left the class, knowing how to solve, and compute but not knowing what I was actually doing with those computations. It isn't a particularly hard class, but his homeworks were overly long and I learned very little from the whole class. Never take him.

His tests are graded leniently but my biggest complaint is the long and unnecessary homeworks. They are seriously annoying and take forever, including coding problems. Also the short tests are hard and stressful, and just not necessary, especially since they make tests/exams worth 85% of the final grade.
I actually really enjoyed his lectures, and I think he is super nice and patient in answering questions. He also sends cute emails.
He can be a boring professor 90% of the time, but if you just show up to class and at least try to pay attention the class isn't that hard, gives good test prep resources as well.

Great guy with good lectures. While he is a little plain on the outside if you put your effort into listening during lectures he makes them interesting and worthwhile to attend. I would say that negative reviews from other students could definitely be from students discouraged by difficulty of course if you don't put effort in.
Super boring professor. Does not explain things intuitively. Trying to avoid future classes with this professor.
unclear lectures and gives a midterm the same week as a quiz and homework due date.
Very thorough explanations to all kinds of problems
Homeworks are long, but tests are fairly straightforward as long as you do the practice. Freeman and his TA's are always willing to help. Attendance is extra credit

He employs a very heavy course load, and it is definitely a lot. It is absolutely required to buy a textbook (you can't pirate it). During lectures he does not go over grammar or vocab rules, instead that is all usually outside of class as part of your homework.
Lots of work, lots of learning. My Spanish is better but it cost me great effort. Fun class, though.

VERY Demanding, unforgiving, and puts you on the spot.  Grading is harsh and the online work seems impossible.  He didn't help when I had a problem with my online work when I had over worked myself. Plays favorites, is very strict, and fails at making the class interesting or fun.
Demands a lot from you, but for good reason. Practice everyday and volunteer in class for best results--he'll also like you better for it.
No Comments
Very cool class
Mild mannered and softspoken, but a pretty good teacher.  Assignments tend to be almost too easy, spelling out exactly what you need to do.
No Comments
Contrary to what other reviews may have you believe Golan is indifferent, unavailable, and reluctant to help.

Golan was the professor who had the greatest positive impact on my experience in the school of art. He is a caring, knowledgeable, and highly-involved mentor to students who put in effort and care about art. He makes time for students and is deeply invested in their progress. He's also just fun to be around. I am so grateful that our paths crossed.

Contrary to what other reviews may have you believe, Golan is incredibly passionate, available, and eager to help. I took five classes with him over the course of my BFA, and he is one of the few reasons I didn't drop out. Some of the best projects I produced have been under his oversight, and were only possible thanks to resources he provided.
Would have been good to know he isn't really active in the department before planning to attend.

Probably the number one source of techno elitism on this side of the school. Draws really firm boundaries- will look at you or not- depending on if you can “code”. For me it was a clear line that excluded me from having any value at CMU
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Doesn't actually teach much and just fills the time with guests. Too busy to interact with students. I relocated to Pittsburgh (a horrible place to move to!) to work with him and learn from him and he refused to meet me or mentor me for three years. Later on he went out of his way to significantly interfere in my career.

Really one of the worst professors at CMU when it comes to human-human interaction. Too busy for students and incredibly elitist. Creates a very unpleasant environment in CFA for those who want to integrate tech- really should be in a different department.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects

reviews.comment: Levin's programming class was honestly a mess. I came in excited to actually learn something, but half the time I had no idea what was going on because the explanations made everything more confusing. He talks super fast, throws random slides at us, and then acts surprised when everyone is lost.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: It's great that he was forced out of the studio for creative inquiry. Levin is one of those professors who seems motivated to damage students. Waste of time to try to get his attention. Stay away and avoid at all costs.
Golan's too busy for his students. Not interested in teaching, mentoring, or even pretending to be a part of the School of Art. Should be transferred to School of Design or go back to MIT.

he gives amazing lectures that I always enjoy, not the kind that you would get from stem courses but rather he digs into existing projects online and ties into history and sociology. he gives us mid semester reviews and they are pretty thorough in rating our performance and points out what we could benefit from. he's also very well-connected

;(
don't take her course

I struggled so much with math before this class, and because of Prof Gheorghiciuc's teaching, I'm planning on minoring in it now. Tough class, but you get out what you put in. One of the kindest people I've ever met, and she cares so much about seeing her students succeed. I don't think she values letter grades as much as the process of learning.
she never made lectures hands on or offered questions to understand material for students

She is the worst teacher i have ever had. idk how she is a professor (at CMU)(one of the best schools in the world). id rather drop out of college entirely than take her class. she grades horribly. she doesnt speak english well at all. like omg

Very difficult to pass. Makes it so that if you make a tiny error occurred you received no credit. Even penalized for doing steps correctly. Lost half the credit on a question because I didn't simplify a square root.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Had to drop the class, the week before finals...

Extremely strict grader; even small mistakes cost lots of points. Class average barely passes, yet she makes it feel like students are at fault. I came in well-prepared, never missed lectures,done 1 on 1 tutoring, and still struggled. Passing is tough despite effort.
horrible professor that had below a passing average of 70% for all midterms.....
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Harsh grading, disrespectful, unhelpful lectures, never answers emails, and called me out in front of the whole class for trying to take a picture of the board. Overall very uncaring about students.

Extremely tough grader! Tests are formatted so that if you get 1 thing wrong in the first part of the question, the entire rest of the question is wrong. Makes you feel horrible for doing badly on tests. I studied 15+ hrs for a midterm, and got penalized 50% for 1 error. I went to office hours and she told me that I have no more hope for the course

Just terrible. Everything is made much more difficult than necessary. Her style of teaching is not inviting whatsoever. Genuinely convinced she believes she is above teaching a precalc course. Horrible feeling trying to make an actual attempt to go to lectures and understand what the heck is going on when I walk out more confused every time.

She's a really tough grader, no partial credit, even if your work is mostly right. Tests and assignments are graded very strictly, and it's hard to get a B or A. Wouldn't recommend if you're looking for fair grading or leniency.

Grading is strict and unclear, with poorly explained assignment expectations. The workload feels heavier than expected, and grading doesn't always reflect effort. I wouldn't recommend this course if you want clear guidance or fair grading.

Not caring of the students, no curves were implemented. Lowest midterm got a small percent drop. Other than that 2/3rds of the class are failing a class that was meant to remedy a lack of high math in high school.

Quite literally the worst professor I've ever had. Every time I leave lecture, I'm more confused than I was when I went in. I don't know how she hasn't been fired, respectfully. She teaches in the most confusing way possible and does anything to make the course as hard as possible for students. Blames students for her own failures as a professor.
The most harsh grader I've seen
Not recommended, heavy homework and tough grading policy.
Easy class if you go to lectures. Most people don't go to class though so ...
She is the best
Pretty annoying, not hard class though.
Quiz grading was diabolical
Don't take her courses unless you are good as hell. Tons of homework, awful teaching, irresponsible.

She is great - cares for her students as long as they are serious about the course stuff. Is somewhat strict on the  grading scale (no curve given even if you got like a 89.9%), but that's fair. There are a lot of homework in the second half of the course, but the problems are doable if you pay attention in the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Extremely nitpicky in grading, will take off lots of points for smaller errors and is inflexible with regrade requests. Tests and quizzes are pretty fair in that they are only based on material covered in class, but like I mentioned above are graded extremely harshly. I would not recommend taking Irina whatsoever. Nice person, not a great prof

Class has a serious problem in the way it is structured. A large majority of the grade comes from tests which are ridiculously difficult and graded with no leniency. Averages on tests are low Cs, and she does not curve at all. HW and quizzes are fair, except they barely add anything to final grade. Highly advise students to take with someone else.
Professor Irina is a nice lady who is passionate about teaching. The class is hard, but she does a good job of explaining concepts.

Thinks her class is the most important when in reality, it is not. Home works are much longer than they need to be and lectures are depressing. Better off taking 21-240 or 21-241 with another professor tbh.
Awful.

Very enthusiastic about the material that she teaches. You definitely need to know the material well to do well on quizzes and tests. Don't try to use methods other than the ones taught in class on quizzes or tests. Her office hours are always helpful though so you have ample opportunity to understand the material.
awful
OK Irina is not an easy prof and is not chill at all, but if you take concepts with her you will LEARN and she will help you if you ask

Irina may seem strict, but she cares a lot about all her students. She's also one of the best math teachers I've ever had; her lectures are concise and useful. Her homework are usually challenging, but you learn a lot more from trying to figure out problems yourself instead of having someone hold your hand. Thanks Irina :)

Concepts is a difficult course but Irina's lectures are very clear and she goes over important content multiple times. She also provides numerous resources for students and genuinely cares about their learning.

This professor is not being nice to students, rather being heartless.The worst instructor I have ever had. Although her notes are clear, she frightens students who ask questions. Never reply email. Punish students who unintentionally make nonacademic mistakes. Value rules and order more than intelligence and creativity. Kills all interest of math.
Extremely unclear grading criteria and so disrespectful to students! If you can take concepts with any other professor, do that.

She is genuinely one of the worst instructors I have ever had the misfortune of haven taken. Do not take a course with her if another professor is available. She seems to enjoy in lectures putting down students that ask questions and is inconsistent when grading. Much of the difficulty comes from her more so than the material.

90% of lectures are example problems and 10% usable content and techniques (and no fun corollaries). She ends up teaching specific proofs/answers rather than the techniques and methods to create new proofs. The problems on the exams are practically the same as the hw which are the same as the ones she goes over in class. Study a lot and do all HW.

Was decent in 256 but terrible for 241. Clearly doesn't care about her students and has an extremely harsh grading system. Lecture is impossible to keep up with while taking notes because she goes through her prewritten notes too quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tough class, but she explains the content well. If you show up to class and put in the effort with homework, you should be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She is very strict and non empathetic. Unwilling to be accommodating for students. Doesnt even teach very well. You are better off watching professors on YouTube teach the same subject than attend her classes. She just reads hand written notes.

While the material in this class is difficult, it's definitely doable. However, you can't rely on lectures to do well. Her lectures just consist of her reading off prewritten pieces of paper that are copied almost verbatim from the textbook and often times, her notes include a lot of errors. Because of this, the lectures aren't really that helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures get confusing at times but she's really accessible. Class is hard af but is doable. She's tough but a great person overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: bruh she freaked out during the covid 19 pandemic. No teacher was as anal as her throughout this time. Mustve sent out like 50 emails, ppl stop reading them b/c they realized it was her just going off on some trivial tangent. Made academic integreity declarations ridiculously complicated. plus her class is just her throwing up her notes on the bord

I really love Irina. She can be a little harsh compared to your average professors but she is actually a very caring and adorable professor once you get to know her a little better. I am taking 241 with her as well and it's been a great time. Her class is definitely hard but doable. I just messed up on the final don't make the same mistake
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Concepts get tough pretty fast but Irina has a few patterns in the way she does things. Her homeworks are pretty long but it is much needed practice, and there are always 1 or 2 questions in there that's a trivial application of whatever she covered in class. For exams, you wouldn't be too surprised provided you master the homework, so do your hw!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I honestly still dont know how to feel about Irina's class. I am someone who had no background in discrete math whatsoever so this class was hard for me from the start. I think shes a great teacher for people who already somewhat know the material, but for me, someone who had never seen this material before, this was hard. Think bf taking this lol
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Class was much more difficult than it needed to be, super unsympathetic to student struggles, especially considering so many students dropped. Her lectures were decent, but the grading was so awful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: - Toughest grading I have ever seen. Small mistake on page long proof? 3 /10 points - No curve despite appalling test averages - Over half of the class dropped - No useful resources are ever uploaded online - Overly complicates the simplest proofs or explanations - Youtube videos are more helpful than her lectures - Disrespectful and unsympathetic

Very easily irritated and often jokes about how hard her tests will be, which isn't very helpful in a course as intense as Concepts. She needs to show more respect to her students in general. I had a much better experience taking this course with John Mackey and would recommend his class over Irina's any day.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Great professor, problem sets are not challenging and exams are a lot similar to hws

She's a great professor, and the class was easy as long as you showed up to class and did the homeworks. Super easy quizzes and exams that didn't have too many problems to do but lots of time to do them.
She surely developed a good way to make students learn after teaching this course so many times. Some people think she is mean but she definitely try her best to help after being mean.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: class starts off easy, gets really hard and then becomes a meme.

I've heard great things about her teaching Concepts (21-127), but she was a terrible matrices teacher. She got too much into the proofs for matrices, which made the class way too hard. She also got angry when people asked questions in lectures. Just wanted to put her notes on the board and didn't seem to care about student learning at all.

A very interesting person. She has the kind of chill that you won't normally find on a female professor. Explain everything thoroughly and straightforwardly. She doesn't normally give pep talk but when she does you do feel the sincerity she has. In terms of respecting, come on we're adults here. A disgusting proof is disgusting but she will help.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I don't enjoy her teaching style. Disrespectful even at time.
Irina is so engaging and thorough. Best math prof I've had yet. Concepts with her was hard but very fair on exams
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Hard class, but Irina was an interesting teacher to have.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is a good professor. Good at explaining things, and very caring and hilarious. Definitely would recommend her class.

I took Concepts with Irina (21-127). I had a hard time in this class, but she was always encouraging and gave good lectures. I personally enjoyed her style because she wrote out every step of her proofs. Definitely listened if you asked for help, so if you feel unsure on something don't be afraid to ask her and go to office hours!
It is a hard course and you can expect to work. Irina is okay with explaining things but not great.
Lectures are very clear. Very good at balancing lecture time with questions from students, and is flexible with the course schedule.

Advice to students: attend class and recitation, work to understand the problems in hw. I took Prof. Irina's 21-127 course in my freshman year and if you could do the hw you could crack the tests pretty easily. Write all possible steps for problems in tests, I always lost points on whatever steps I did not mention :-)
Good lecturer. Classes are slow and boring. ATTEND OH! Homeworks are tricky and ATTEND OH to be clear on concepts. Easy exams.
I took 21-122 with her in the past, it was terrible. But now I'm in 21-127, and it's amazing. It is by far the most interesting class I'm taking this semester.
Nice Professor

Lectures sometimes confusing, and sometimes repeated herself a few times as writing, going pretty slowly. At the end of the year, didn't have enough time to finish wave equation, and didn't go through examples so we were left for the test not knowing the material completely. My TA (not a reflection on professor but FYI) was really bad, and wrong.
Not the best, but still a good professor! Straightforward, chill, smart. Lectures helped with assignments, which helped with exams.

Great professor! I like her style of teaching a lot and I find her lecture very easy to follow and understand. HW's are challenging but TAs and Office Hours are extremely helpful. You need quite a bit effort to get a good grade though.
Interesting course. Mostly computational, but there are concepts too. You will need to come to class and take notes. Nice person and good teacher.
Irina is awesome! She's extremely funny and willing to help her students.
Everything is clearly explained and questions are answered willingly

Has a thick Russian accent. Doesn't explain concepts and instead wastes class time on difficult problems that are not relevant to homework at all. Has handwriting that is hard to read if you sit further than the 5th row. Has trouble understanding students' questions and thus her answers are not at all helpful

A pretty difficult teacher.  She likes to dive right into the difficult problems without going over the basics.  If you're not a natural at math, getting outside help is a must.  TA's and other students are usually very helpful.  She likes to grade on a curve so don't be surprised if tests are hard and averages are in the 60's.

Very nice lady, but not the best teacher. If you have her for 121 she is really pretty easy. Just learn from the book and you never have to go to class. Class average on the first test was a 65 so she gave a 10pt bonus quiz and made the rest of the tests easier. Class averages on the next two tests were 88.
Excellent professor. Everything she goes over in class is extremely helpful and she is also very polite.

Irina is the worst professor I have ever encountered. She does not explain anything and is not helpful at all. If you have to take a class that she is teaching, do everything you can to not take it with her. If you have to take it with her, then do everything you can to get outside help as you will need it.

Very enthusiastic, but makes fairly common computation errors and sometimes has difficulty understanding students' questions. Her accent is fairly strong but she's still understandable, and explains concepts competently. A very fun person, even if not the most amazing teacher.
Nice professor. Supposedly much easier than previous semester's prof(Mackey). Mild accent and awful at spelling, but that is not terribly important in math. Overall, not a bad professor.

Shes a nice person, but a terrible teacher. Her handwriting is hard to read, as her g's, 9's, y's and phi's all look the same. She also fails to clearly explain topics, and presents example problems she doesn't solve all the way. Worst of all, she puts questions on homeworks on topics not covered in class, then sends an email to explain the topic.
Not going to class will sink you. She does love all her students though, and if you meet her outside class, you get to see a far more relaxed person (who's a bit evil in a fun way at times).
Two things... She fails to explain important concepts and theorems clearly. She has a thick Russian accent. Not as bad as Adamchik though.
very hard to follow his work and does not tell where in the textbook we are learning so cannot use textbook to help.
He is super nice, and caring if you attend lectures. He's even more helpful in OH! Tests are fair and similar to HW.

flaherty's gpa component requires you to do well on two of the three midterms, generally the first two easier ones. then, u can make room for lower midterm 3 and final exam scores (he curved both of them this semester) his lecture is mid, but u can still learn a lot.

flaherty is a no no. his lectures are extremely boring, and reviewing his lecture slides are impossible bc you can't understand his handwriting and his flow. exams are quite difficult too with one or two questions that come out of the blue, and he does not curve his exams.

Stay away. He is nice, but awful lecturer. His lectures require a lot of pre-requisite knowledge. His exams are difficult and often not curved. He expects you to come into the class with a certain understanding and if you don't he will not invest the time to help you, he will just give you practice problems. Not the case with other CMU profs.
revoke this man tenure
Wait to see his lecture notes
Pointlessly strict on grading tests and fails to actually teach what is important during lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Flaherty's class is challenging, but the course materials and unstuck study can help. Lectures are tough to follow, but the notes are useful. Exams are hard, but the curve saved the day. Overall, a tough but manageable class.

demon 

bros email response time is longer than the USPS base parcel service time from alaska to uganda (4-6 business weeks)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tell me why he makes the most atrocious, most heinous integrals and derivatives to solve. Tell me why I need to do 30 steps minimum for his tests in a time crunch.
The proof part is much harder than calculation. Overall: good

Every was difficult, hw, exams and he claimed the final can only help us then decided to make the final very difficult. please avoid this professor I am basically going to fail the class and he refuses to curve when many people are doing bad

I was told by several people to not take a class with Flaherty and I did. My biggest regret, I ended up vouchering near the end of the course because there was no curve at when several people did bad/failed. He is honestly near retirement and should not be allowed to teach. The TA's carried the class and its obvious they didnt communicate directly.

Difficult lectures to follow and involve irrelevant proofs. HWs+Practice Exams have good questions; unfortunately, Midterms are far more difficult and sometimes seemingly unrelated. Midterm3 was set for 6 days before the "optional" final, defeating its purpose. Unorganized with course material, unapproachable and can be brash in OH. NO CURVE GIVEN.

If you're reading this and you're reading all the bad comments and thinking to yourself "i think i could still get a good grade," please reconsider. This man is an absolute nightmare. I can't stress this enough. His teaching style is archaic at best, and downright incomprehensible at worst. I highly suggest getting taught by another prof.
My time is too valuable to calculate your 30-term integral.

Attending lectures were not really that helpful for me. He is way too interested in showing us the proofs behind each formula rather than showing us how to use it. Also there is barely any communication that occurs between him and his TA's. Overall an unnecessarily hard class, for something that could've been taught in an easier way.
Professor Flaherty is a very nice person, but he's trying to make a paramecium evolve to understand math within one semester. This is unreachable.

This class was such a clown fest. The assignments took 3 months to be graded, and his exams were unreasonably hard. Some of the homework questions were nearly impossible to solve, to the point where even the head TA was not able to get the answer during office hours. Overall just a bad experience.

Professor Flaherty gives a fair amount of homework, but it is often heavy on arithmetic. However, he has since responded to the lack of time on exams appropriately, and that was really not an issue when I took the course. His course nicely aligns with the textbook he recommends and can be adequately used to supplement confusing lecture points.

After about half of the lectures, I left questioning my ability to do math. He is too smart in his attempted "explanations" for the large majority of students and his teaching style is hard to follow.

The class content is really not too difficult and he was really kind with curving this semester. Even as a business student, I managed to pull an A!

I will do my due dilligance and use this platform as a warning to any future students. Flaherty is not a good professor. Lectures are mundane and his exams are unrealistic. He is aware of how difficult his classes are because even the TA's have mentioned student struggles to him and yet, he chooses not to change anything about his class.

His incompetence is unparalleled. Honestly the content isn't that hard he's just a prick. Nothing gets graded in a reasonable timeframe and he talks like he's afraid to move his mouth. He posts all the lecture notes so maybe that's something.

The exam was difficult and class averages were low, professor refuses to curve the exam because it makes students feel good. In my opinion, this is horrible. I know students that are struggling to even pass because of this mentality. Its calc 3 lol.

Nice curved on exam
Nice graded on exam
Give all lecture note for students after class
amazing professor
he is a good professor that posts useful notes for every lecture. sometimes when I can't understand the lecture, I'll turn to the notes for help.
Can be confusing. Go to OH.

Took over Summer 1. Exams were given too much weight as there were 3 exams worth 70% of the final grade. Exams are much harder than homework assignments. Shows too many proofs and goes off on tangents. Only take if there are no other options.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I can see why a lot of people dont love him, but hes been the best teacher of math Ive had at any level. Good dude and excited to have him again.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof Flaherty is a great professor if you are a math major or really like math. He is good at explaining difficult proofs and concepts and is very passionate about the subject. If you're just trying to pass the course, probably best to avoid him as his lectures tend to get very proof heavy and confusing. To each their own.

I saw his rating before taking his class but no other math professors were teaching CALC1 so that sucked. I leaned on Khan Academy the entire semester. I made the mistake of going to drop-in tutoring at Donner, DON'T DO THAT. Go to the actual TA office hours because they're GREAT at explaining things. Especially grad students, who get no visitors.
Tests can be significantly time-crunched, however, he is not a heavy grader. Homeworks are time-consuming. The lecture is slow-paced, with around 4 to 5 examples given.

Lectures are useless, he spends a lot of time going over proofs instead of actually explaining how to solve problems. Seems relatively personable, but for some reason felt it was okay to schedule the 3rd exam and the final exam exactly 1 week apart. Not to mention that the third exam was the Monday after thanksgiving. A bit cruel if you ask me.

Rushes through problems in lecture and spends more time doing proofs than explaining the problem-solving behind it. His homework and quizzes are fine, but his tests are hard, and the practice exam solutions are confusing sometimes. As a piece of personal advice, try not to use Symbolab all the time, as this will only hurt you during tests.
Horrendous. If you're coming into CMU as a freshman and have him, get out of it! I read the bad reviews before my first semester and ignored them thinking he couldn't be THAT bad. But he is.
tough classes taught by an even tougher prof

He just teaches this class to give out bad grades. Exams have nothing to do with what he teaches in lecture. He is a nice guy but lacks social skills and therefore sympathizing with students is difficult.
I'm not sure why this man has a job. He is widely considered the worst professor at CMU.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Worst professor of all time. HE. DOES. NOT. TEACH.
His lectures are not clear enough, and his tests are pretty tough considering his teaching skills.

Tim is a pretty nice person if you talk to him outside of class. However, I would like to note that his lectures are kind of confusing. You will most likely end up teaching yourself the material through the homework and exams, which are way more difficult than his lectures. I only survived because I already had a background in calc.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Flaherty was absolutely awful, I had taken his class before in high school and still had no idea what was going on. My classmates struggled even more than I did. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS!!
Does not teach. During lectures, he goes on rants and shows us proofs instead of teaching.

He's more interested in his research rather than helping his students. He's also a bit condescending. His course is theory-heavy with little to no examples done in class. You're gonna spend a lot of time teaching yourself the content because the lectures are almost useless.

I didn't think he was a bad prof. Homework was assigned every other week, but took up a lot of time. Exams were pretty difficult, but nothing was too different than what we covered in lecture. Didn't study too hard and almost got through with a B, although the final brought it down. Course has a heavy focus on linear algebra and optimization.

Exams are unfair. The final exam took the full 3 hours and had an average score of 65%. Avoid taking this class with Professor Flaherty if you can. He is also just rude in office hours and calls you out for not knowing the material, even though he is terrible at lecturing.

Probably one of the worst professors a this school. Not sure why he has a good job. Maybe he throws some good research at the university or he's tenured. Regardless, widely agreed as a professor you don't want to have.

Terrible. No better way to describe him as a professor. I reported him after he insulted me in class once. I took this class twice with him and failed the first time, got a C the second time. Keep in mind I got a 5 on BC calc and perfect on SAT Math Level 2 exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Beware of this teacher. He is not good at all, I hated his class with a passion. All he does is lecture and not explain anything at all. He writes so quick, and its so hard to grasp the material. His office hours were essentially useless, as it didn't help me learn the material. Everyone seems lost after every lecture with this guy. Ridiculous.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Had to work hard to get an A. Boring lecturer. Taught myself everything. That was difficult though because he provided barely any supplemental materials and those that he did provide were not coherent.

He's a terrible lecturer, and just rude. He told me not to come to his office hours again since I was so confused and was "unprepared with a thorough grasp of the material". Not being good at math, I had to drop the class as I did bad on the first test. Stay away from him if you can.

His lectures are usually fine (except for long proofs), but you can skip lecture and just read the textbook. HW is decently long/difficult so you might want to work with others. First two tests are easy, the last two tests are difficult/ too little time. Not a lot of practice problems though. Not a terrible professor but he can screw you over.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lectures; you can never go to one and get the same grade as if you stayed awake and attentive the entire time. Tests; first two are easy, prepare the water and silicone for the last two. A? good luck, pray you can keep a B. Flaherty? Quirky fellow, would not take his class again however.
Tim Fleherty does not care about his students.

Doesn't listen to student feedback on exam difficulty; caters & teaches primarily to those who plan to be math majors or have already taken the course in another capacity (ie: taken it and dropped, took it in high school, etc). Test diffuculty (specifically exams 3 & 4)
Tim Flaherty does not care about his students. He is simply a professor that is at CMU to do research.
Hard to get an A
