Lectures: he teaches straight from the textbook- making them pretty useless. Homeworks: reasonable. Exams: unreasonably difficult/long (i.e. take a 5x5 determinant on one problem and then apply it). I love his effort- he gives HUNDREDS of slots per week for 1:1 help for students, but it doesn't always equate to successful teaching.

He insults students in class. Doesn't care about the success of his students. Apparently gets almost fired every year. Is banned from teaching other math classes for above reasons. Apparently once upon a time he was a good professor. But he has since lost his touch. Don't listen to other comments praising him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Lecture is all theoretical and focused on proofs. It doesn't teach how to solve problems or do the homework.

Writes very fast and assigns lots of homework that takes a decent amount of time to do. Very test heavy class and the tests are usually much harder than the homework. Practice tests do not represent the actual tests too well, but are worth doing because duplicates of problems from the practice may appear. Most importantly, he is extremely hot.
awful teacher, very awkward
His homework requires some self-study.
Showed up with a picture of Shrek on his vector calculus slides, and that's when I knew he was worth a 5
loves webassign way too much

Calc3D is easy to me. The book was good enough for most of the class. I still went to most lectures, which were helpful until the 3rd unit & he stopped making sense. He teaches from slides that are copied&pasted from the textbook. I was doing well on track for a B until he gave us an impossible final that destroyed my GPA. Very disappointed in him.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: I would call him an inconsistent teacher at best. His lectures are poorly-paced, and he spends excessive amounts of time working through proofs of theorems that are unnecessary and irrelevant to the students' interest. He spends lots of time working through these very theory-heavy proofs, and then skimps on time spent on his actual lecture material

Flaherty is a very decent professor you'll meet. The lectures are decent and helpful but you can get the same knowledge by reading the textbook. The homework is alright but he gives WebAssigns Friday mornings that are due Friday nights. His voice a lullaby and he makes bad jokes in class. You'll be fine, but you don't need to show up to do well.
The textbook is usually plenty to do an okay job in this class. Sometimes he doesn't go into enough detail/clarification in lecture.
Very funny guy. Lectures are pretty rigorous, class was pretty standard calc 3.
Weird sense of humor, kind of a weird guy, but a great lecturer. Very clear explanations and willing to take questions. Has a cute dog.
It is hard to teach to a huge class. Even with that in mind, he is pretty boring, and at times confusing.
Crystal clear explanation of subject, never goes too fast. Eccentric at time, but always in a humorous way. Ask him if math is real.
I actually think he's a pretty good professor.
The professor is good, but he has a monotonous voice. I would recommend the class, but just be warned.
Best teacher in the world!

Course content wasn't so hard, and professor Flaherty teaches really well. Lectures are clear, and interesting. Assignments are partially in textbook and are not difficult. Exams are pretty easy as long as you are careful in lectures and do the homework.
Took summer course. Had only ten people in the class. He was very funny, and always very friendly and helpful.
Studied topology in concepts, walked out feeling ten times as intelligent, 1110 would take again.

Felt like we were running in the class before we could even walk, his lectures were a bit convoluted and not very helpful on HW.  The HW difficulty did not match what was taught to us, which resulted in overcrowded OH.  Also taught material not covered in textbook, thus hard to review the material on your own.  Probably worst class I've taken here
Poorly structured lectures and class in general, things weren't taught in the optimal order in my opinion. The course structure was hard to follow and assumed you already knew a lot of stuff.
Took 21-127. Topology is hard but all the questions he gave us are pretty standard

21-127 is probably the first formal math class for students in CMU. It's definitely hard, because college math is of course harder than high school math. Flaherty is a very interesting professor, and I really enjoy his teaching!

Flaherty is THE man. His lectures are structured like a comedy show. It is virtually impossible to fall asleep in his class (without waking up with him standing in front of your face). He has also helped me to discover my life passion for topology.

Everything was going well until topology, and it was crazy hard. Got a 35% on the topology test but with the curve, managed to get a high D. Good with exam appeals, and homework is definitely much harder than the actual exams. Don't put off homework to the day before it's due. Almost droppedpassfailed the class.
Nice

He has a well planned out lecture that involves some very simple proofs and examples. His hws sometimes have some questions that require some thought but are usually straightforward. His tests are relatively easy I would say.
Good teacher, cares a lot about whether students are learning and has a nice sense of humor.  Gives hard tests, but assignments are brief and painless.

Summer of 09, thought he was hilarious. One day he came in was like "so I woke up at 4 am last night, couldn't go back to sleep, and remembered how to do the proof I wanted to show you guys yesterday, so I got out of bed and wrote it out. so here it is." The man loves his math. Upside: entertaining. Only downside:  spends too much time on proofs.
This man loves his math :)
Oh and his corny jokes make me crack up each time. Easily the best math professor I've ever had. The final is optional!

I don't know what everyone is complaining about. I'm not aware of 21-259 but this guy's 21-256 class is TOO easy. The proofs are not difficult to understand and he provides plenty of examples. Sometimes people ask really stupid questions about how he does certain things which leads me to think they are too slow. Recitation is useless for me.
Flaherty, King of Calculus

Entertaining in a quirky way. Course material was rather difficult conceptually, but tests were mostly a matter of knowing which formula to apply and not messing up. Seems very fair. Recitations were boring re-teachings of lecture, but useful if you didn't understand lecture.

Pretty funny guy, makes calculus fun (or as fun as it can be) with jokes and his general quirkiness. Using the TAs and textbooks will really help; tests aren't too bad as long as you study and know the material. He doesn't grade too hard, our final was curved.

Funny guy, nerdy in all the right ways. Lectures were good and he takes time to comprehensively answer questions. Tests were hard as hell though. The man loves his proofs, but tells you in advance so you can skip if you're disinterested in the formal junk.

Had him over the summer for pre-college. I went in with a really sucky AP calc AB background and came out being freaken awesome at calc. Honestly, he's hard. His tests are hard, and if you fall behind, you're screwed. But he does teach, so if if you can keep up, you should be golden.

Nerdy but funny and seems to care about students. Does waste time on useless proofs and doesn't explain then. Overall, he is very fair and will not put anything on the test that he didn't go over in class and was on the h/w. Final is optional.

Weird guy. Wastes a lot of time on meaningless proofs and writes too quickly in general. Tests are not too difficult and the final is optional. The way the class is weighted it is almost impossible to get below a C and an easy B if you do good on HW and quizzes. Much better option than the other 256 teacher.
Good Professor, usually keeps class interesting.  A little to many proofs.

Flaherty is into the proofs but not into showing what they do or how they can help you on an exam. The TAs have to sometimes "reteach" the material because he sometimes fills the board with useless proofs with no examples. It's a doable course however, just don't rely on lectures to help you much. Bring your clicker!!!
By far one of the BEST professors I had this semester. He'll chase students, wake them up in front of the whole class....it was overall awesome.
Great professor. Genuinely loves what he does, writes *really* fast when pressed for time. Also, he chases students who leave class, lol. =)
z

He's a great professor and is really enthusiastic about the course.  It's useful to stay for lectures because of the quizzes (easy 3 pts) and you get to learn a lot too.  Once he chased someone who left the lecture right after the quiz, so I guess he's pretty funny.  His exams get progressively harder but he curved the final so it was all good.

Nerdy, funny personality. Genuinely enjoys teaching. Hates when people skip or don't pay attention. He ran out of the room to chase a guy who tried to skip out after taking attendance. Good at explaining things, but he does proofs faster than you can think about them. Sometimes, he will expect you to know really obscure integration rules.
Good Teacher, lots of proof, just be respectful and don't fall asleep in class. Do the homework and come to class and you'll do fine. -Calc in 3D
Interesting hand gestures and general movement. Writes really really fast. No joke.
Too many proofs in class. I feel as if I am learning Analysis class...
Really funny guy, love the class.

He is an AMAZING teacher! I had such a hard time in Calc I but with him Calc II was so easy. He is totally open to you coming in for help and he is a really personable guy and loves helping and teaching. One of my favorite prof. by far
He looks like the dad from Honey I Shrunk the Kids! OK teacher, a few too many proofs for me.  Had generous curves at the end, but only after the drop deadline...sneaky.
He's a decent teacher, but as a person, HE SCARES ME.
No Comments
worst teacher i have ever had, excited about math, but not about teaching
LIED to the class and had horrible make up exam policies.
Great teacher, however class is exceptionally hard.  I did more work for these 3 units (mini course) than i have all year in my calc class.
this guy is hard...i dont know why he makes his exam retakes impossible since your maximum grade on the retakes are capped.
Hw looks like Bill Gates. He is hard. Dont take the class.
gives replacement exams that could hurt your grade. condecending during office hours. if the class average on a test is hoovering around 50%, you know something is wrong
he's ok but hard exams
assesments are HARD
His final was impossible
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
"gah-huhhh" (tim laugh)
He was just writing on the board while we copied notes with little effort to explain concepts or engage the class.

I have no idea what to say about this man. He is just a talking textbook. I feel neither hatred nor appreciation for him, and I am sure he has the same to say about me. I will say, though, that the homework solutions are both messy and don't make sense, and his tests do not use practice problems from the homework or recitations.

Absolute worst physics prof ever. and I've taken college physics at other schools. and i'm a physics major, i like physics. I could explain the concepts better. he just spouts the textbook word for word and I haven't shown up to lecture because of it. also puts never-before-covered material on the tests. no recitation or HW problems were on there:(
Not the clearest exposition but proofs easy to follow, homework/exams easy.
little hw, little correlation to textbook. homeworks +tests easy if proofs understood. Decent lecturer.

Took a summer course called Concept of Maths with Rami in the Summer of 2003 at Carnegie Mellon. 8 of us in the class. 6 of us were close friends from ECE. He suspected some of us cheated during our homework and vowed that if we got caught, he will make sure we get expelled. The threat stopped after all 6 of us scored above 90% in the mid term.
Really great prof! Even though his accent is hard to understand at first, it gets better. He really knows his stuff! Higher level classes taught by him are really worth taking.

Tells interesting stories/gives interesting applications/examples for many topics covered in class (Concepts of Math.) I found his accent very hard to understand, on top of that he would tend to stand in front of proofs while writing them (You could only see when he moved on to the next one) and his handwriting would sometimes be near unreadable.

I disagree with an earlier comment; I did not notice an accent, and his presentation was clear. He did tell a few stories but they were brief, relevant, and made the material more interesting. Professor Grossberg was helpful outside of class, too.
I found his accent very difficult to understand (especially at the beginning of the semester)and his lectures could get to be incoherent at times, as he would randomly start telling stories
Great prof.  I specifically looked for other classes he taught.
No Comments
dudes funny
One of the best professors I've had, uses humour well to keep classes engaging
The lectures could benefit from better preparation, as some of the explanations of basic concepts were unclear and occasionally caused confusion.
One of the best professors I've had at CMU. Very enthusiastic and kind, and also makes lectures interesting!
Matt is definitely one of the best professors at CMU. Reached out to him when I struggled with serious anxiety and received a very kind response email.

Matt is a great communicator who is in control of the class. Always friendly and ready to explain concepts. I only took a bootcamp crash course into machine learning with him, but I'd be happy to take more classes

Matt was such a kind professor. He really cared about us learning. You could tell that he practiced his lectures, which were very interesting. The examples/ analogies that he used made complicated prediction techniques quite intuitive. The programming homeworks were challenging but very interesting and cool.
great teacher

He's genuinely so passionate about stats and it's extremely evident in his lively lectures. Extremely laid back and understands his class is usually on the back burner for most students. Definitely recommend!

Genuinely the sweetest professor here at CMU. He is so understanding and impassioned about what he teaches. Although you can easily obtain an A in this class without ever going to lecture(it truly is that easy), I'd recommend going just because of how good of a professor/person he is.
Extremely good professor

Amazingly organized. He has all the materials for the course ready on canvas and truly truly prepares you for your exams. exams are extremely easy, knowledge from courses are useful, and he's just a great guy overall. Never went to lecture and ended up with an A just by studying the course notes and going to lab every week.
Best professor
Great Professor! The course is straightforward, with clear and easy exams. He scans the annotated material and puts it on canvas, making reviewing super convenient.

Weinberg is the absolute best. He's an incredible lecturer and makes the expectations for the class clear. All of the class materials are readily available on his (very organized) Canvas page. The content itself is among the easiest you'll find at CMU. You don't need to attend class to get an A, but I still recommend going because he's so awesome!!
One of the best CMU professors , he actually cares about students!
Makes boring lectures interesting. All in all, a really good professor... strongly recommended for future students.
Professor Weinberg is the best. He is very passionate about what he teaches and clearly cares about the students. The workload for 36220 is manageable and the lecture notes are very helpful.

So I was in my sophomore year of high school when I took his course. To sum it up, he's incredible. His lectures are easy to follow and he checked up on everyone to make sure we were ok with the content. He's so patient, kind, and knowledgeable, and I doubt I'll ever meet a better professor than him.

He also drew smiley faces. Like- who does that?
Weinberg is the best
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He very much cares about the students. He made attending lectures in person worthwhile (even given the walk to the Mellon Institute), but also posts all lecture notes online.
He is so sweet and love his teaching!! I love the structure of the class and how informative his posted notes are.

Professor Weinberg is very passionate about what he teaches and is a super kind individual! He always finds a way to make lectures interesting and informative. He is also quick in responding to emails and gives incredible feedback. For 36200, the homework was very light, and the exams were not too difficult. I would definitely take his class again!
Great guy. Kind, funny prof. with clear lectures. Delivers content well and makes statistics super interesting. Wish I went to lecture more often.

Such a nice guy and easy class too. It got boring at some times and by the end, many people stopped showing up to lecture because he posts lecture notes online and it is easy to review them and teach yourself. Final exam was probably the hardest but the projects and midterms were all easy. It was my easiest class at CMU so far.

Professor Weinberg is very passionate and friendly. He also explains the concepts well. His handwriting is not very well, though, but it doesn't affect his teaching very much. Overall, he is a good professor and his classes are great.

Professor Weinberg is great & 36200 isn't tough. He is very clear in lecture and 36200 is basically all lectures with one weekly recitation (you get a 100 just for showing up, recitation isn't graded on anything except for completion of the lab). He always uploads the notes after lecture too and they are very clear and detailed.

And 36202 Very easy classes, but very interesting. Helped me choose my major. He scans the lectures and puts them online, makes it very easy to learn at your own pace. Most people don't end up going to the lectures, and you can do without them. Projects are challenging, but the TAs are very lenient graders. You get to bring a cheat sheet for exams.
Love Professor Weinberg. Would take his class a thousand times if I could. Best professor on campus hands down!!!!!

Professor Weinberg's is one of my favorite classes! He explains things clearly and the class is well-organized. I haven't really spoken with him personally but in class he always seems very nice and cheery. He also scans a copy of the lecture notes to canvas after each class so if you do miss a day you won't be left confused.
Professor Weinburg is a genuinely nice professor, who is smart and well respected. His course is super doable if you put in the work and attend lectures.

Amazing professor. Incredibly kind and a great teacher, which is a rarity in college. Answers emails super quickly and is always willing to explain concepts more clearly if you are confused. the course is also exceptionally organized, with everything on canvas and fillable notes always posted prior to class. cannot recommended prof Weinberg enough.
pretty easy class if you pay a little bit of attention during lectures, professor is super nice and funny, would definitely take this course again
This class was a breeze and Weinberg made the subject material really interesting. The course load is light and I would recommend to everybody!
Great lecturer, incredibly kind, always understanding of certain issues that arise, responds quickly to emails as well despite having incredibly large classes.

Actually took 24220, but couldn't find course code.

SUPER easy course. I attended 3 lectures and had an exam average of 96, and this is my first stats course. Exams take 25 min and he gives you 80min plus a 3 page note sheet with all the formulae you could ever need. If you can multiply and also passed calc I, you can get an A here.

He is so nice, my friends like to say he is like a father figure always looking out for us. The day he explained skewness as the tail of the graph (which he turned into a dinosaur) I knew he was a professor who is here to teach because he loves it.

Honestly very inspirational and helped me transition from high school to college. Was expecting other professors to be as good as Gordon, but was sorely disappointed. He posts all notes with worked examples, sample midterms/finals/projects to practice and to know what you are doing, and if you are confused you can ask and he will answer concisely.

As someone who had never taken a stats course before, Weinberg made the material extremely easy to understand. He is the best professor at CMU and actually knows how to teach. Attendance isnt mandatory and if you dont attend the majority of classes you should be fine. The days notes are posted after every class which is helpful.
Sweetest human being at CMU.

Gordon is the reason why I didn't transfer out of CMU. I had him as a freshman thinking other professors would be as good as him because he is by far the best professor at Carnegie Mellon. Like he is the type of guy to serve as a friend when you are struggling at CMU. He was that professor and mentor for me when I needed it the most.
I would honestly become his wife.

I really love this professor. Go to his office hours! Not enough people take advantage of them. It helps a lot to have explanations one on one. He is also just the sweetest professor and genuinely wants you to do well. Dont skip lectures and pay attention!
He is awesome. Super nice guy who is really passionate about his work and will help students directly. He knows what he is talking about and is an excellent teacher of the material.

Professor Weinberg is great! I'd love to take another class of his - both 36-200 and 36-202 were clear and honestly, pretty easy. The class organization was fabulous - all the important files were in one place.
the class is especially easy if you've taken AP Statistics in high school
Best professor at CMU without a doubt. That is what he is known for.
Gordon is the best professor at CMU without a question. Everyone on campus knows that as a fact.
Professor Weinberg gives a lot of homework, but they are easy and make you understand the class more. He prepares very well for lectures.
Professor was great, answers all your questions, super lenient with tests. The workload was amazingly light and as long as you somewhat pay attention in class you will do great.

Weinberg is one of the best professors I have ever seen. He is extremely helpful when you have questions. His lecture is also easy to follow. He wants to clarify everything and make sure that everyone understand the concepts. If possible, I would definitely take more courses with him.
The lectures and homework are really helpful but I recommend not underestimating this class, so make sure to study.

Im very bad with stats, and only took this course as a requirement. I watched lectures multiple times/went to office hours whenever I needed help with concepts/assignments. The grading was unfair, and I have compared my assignments with my friends and the sample papers. I tried to talk to him but he was rude and unfriendly & gave vague reasons.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Best professor at Carnegie Mellon without a doubt. He genuinely cares and makes you feel like a person instead of a number, unlike other professors at Carnegie Mellon.

Professor Weinburg sets the bar for being an exemplary professor; he is extremely knowledgeable about his field and is passionate about helping his students. I learned a ton of content and enjoyed being in his class.
Gordon is probably the best professor at Carnegie Mellon. He's extremely intelligent and wants his students to succeed unlike most Carnegie Mellon professors.

Gordon is an angel. Notes are really clear and easy-to-access. has a lot of energy in class and made me want to show up. Gives a sheet of notes for each exam, and 2 sheets for the final. Always finished labs early. The projects are the hardest part of the course, don't procrastinate like I did. Wish he taught more courses!
Might be the nicest professor on campus, and explains this very well. No additional work needed for this class.

This man should be standard of how CMU professors should teach. It doesn't feel like he's here just for research, as he really cares about his students! Wants all students to get A's and succeed. He posts A LOT of material to help you prepare for exams, and invites students to his office hours. Always has a smile on his face! Highly Recommended!

Professor Weinberg is the best instructor I had at cmu so far. He gives clear explanations in lectures and he posts study materials and lecture notes online right no later than the day he gives the lecture. He tries his best to help his students. I highly recommend stats major students to take his class

Gordon is amazing, by far the best lecturer I have had at CMU. He wants everyone to get an A, and he does everything he can to have his students succeed. If I could, I want to take every class he offers.

Terribly slow at grading things so you don't always know how you're doing in the class, but makes lecture materials super accessible and uploads practice exams which are super helpful. He is such a great instructor!
Uploads hand-written lecture notes that are really useful for completing the labs and homework assignments.

Lectures not mandatory and copies of notes posted after every class. Always incredibly quick to respond to questions and more than willing to set up a meeting. Really great teacher (I had him for 201 and 202).
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great professor. 10/10 would recommend
Great notes, never went to class and aced
Great lecturer, clear and concise. Very clear about what is expected on exams and is very helpful with homeworks in office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Best
Literally the best lecturer in my freshman year. He really knows what he's teaching and makes sure the students understand the lecture. Strongly recommend.
HES AMAZING. He's always encouraging students to come to Office Hours, always has a smile on his face even though the material is awfully boring. He's very helpful always and a true gem.
Lecture materials very clear and concise.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Prof. Weinberg is a fantastic lecturer and is incredibly approachable. I took 201 over the summer and it was incredibly easy using his notes and lecture material. Definitely recommend this course to all students, regardless of major.
Such a nice guy. Gets taken advantage of by students

I have never taken stats before, so it was slightly more difficult for me versus other people who HAVE taken stats but he is such a great professor! He is always smiling and super passionate about making sure you learn the material. When I didn't do well on my midterm, he personally emailed me to come to OH to go over what I missed. recommend him
The class is easy. Professor is nice and funny.
Amazing teacher. will make sure you understand what he's taught in a very intuitive way.

Gordon Weinberg is one of the best professors at CMU. He is a very respectable professor. He is super clear with the requirements of the class. His resources on CMU Blackboard are so well classified that you can never be lost. I would take more stats classes with him if he taught them in the future.
This is a very straight-forward class. I think he has been teaching this course for almost 20 years or something. Really nice guy and very helpful.

This class was simple, but interesting. Professor Weinberg covers the typical AP stats curriculum through a series of clear, straight-forward lectures. There is no textbook and he uploads all of the lectures and lecture notes. He is very passionate about the subject and loves helping students who may be confused with the material.
super easy. I literally never go to class, and I have an A.

Had 36-201 with him. Super quirky and energetic professor who laughs at his own jokes. Everything is explained crystal clear. Sometimes I almost feel bad for him when students walk out halfway through lectures.
He is energetic and very clear about all the information.  The class is easy if you just use the lecture notes.

Relatively easy if you pay attention in class and briefly review the material before the exam (and do the homeworks/labs). He's good at keeping your attention, and he has "outlines" of the notes so you only have to write the important stuff down. No textbook needed. Stay awake, take notes, do your work, and you're good. And know how to use Minitab.

He's such a hyper person, though the morning lectures tend to put everyone to sleep haha. He's really helpful with all the homework if you need to ask him something, go ahead. Tests are ok if you pay attention. lecture notes are everything

Super easy class - easier than AP stat.  Good, funny, and very excited about statistics - like little puppy dog excited.  Don't buy the book - he gives lecture outlines of everything. For HW you need one string of numbers from the book - that's it for the whole course. Try to get the free download of MINITAB to avoid running to the library at 2 am.
Easy. Very animated guy. IN love with Minitab. Don't BUY the textbook. SHOW UP TO LECTURE, TAKE NOTES, REVIEW FROM NOTES, and get AN A on the tests.

I appreciate the fact that he prints out the notes and hands them out at the beginning of each lecture. Just observing him give his lectures is entertaining. Great guy! The class, however, is too easy.
Class is almost too easy - hard to pay attention. Great guy, though, and funny. Mannerisms make me laugh. Knows his stuff.

A very enthusiastic lecturer, although sometimes a bit corny. I imagine he is helpful, although I never had to ask for help. He is very good about providing students with notes which makes homework and labs pretty easy. 36-201 should be an easy A for anyone, even without prior stats knowledge.

There's not technically that much work. That being said, if you don't read the textbook/study on your own, you're cooked because the lectures are flipped classroom style, meaning they only go over practice problems. Do yourself a favor, read the notes, do the practice, and don't get behind. It's not that hard to do well if you stay on top of it.

Teaches some material in class, but most is meant to be learned on your own. She cares a lot about students understanding, but due to the difficult nature of the content, it is still a hard class. There is a lot of practice in class with mechanisms which is good.

absolutely loved the way she used lectures to go over problems instead of introducing new content. she made a notoriously difficult class very doable. there were never really any curveballs on the exams. highly recommend!

"Flipped-class" model so you're expected to teach yourself the material and participate in solving example problems in class. She seems to know everyone's name, even if you barely speak to her, which I think shows her dedication to the class. Extremely generous grading and ample extra credit opportunities.

basically hard
in depth review hard
time consuming class
can't rest
help

Great professor, has a lot of content available online so if you miss a class you won't fall too behind, but the second half of the course is TOUGH. Go to class and study, don't put it off before the final. The homework tends to match up well with what's on the exams. No curve but she's really generous with drops (drops 2 exams and 3 homeworks)
Very nice teacher and person. Does make you work for it though, especially the second half of the semester.

Professor Silva is one of the most approachable and kind professor at CMU. It was really easy to ask for help with the course content and she tries her best to help the student. Thank you Professor Silva!

Organic Chemistry is arguably one of the hardest subjects in academia and Dr. Silva is an amazing professor to help students ease their way into this subject and drive them towards success all in one semester. Silva is an amazing lady and I thoroughly enjoyed getting to know her. Her exams are hard so be sure to study in advance, 2nd half is hard!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: So for Silva with OChem I, the first 5 -7 weeks will be pretty easy and you'll get in the habit of not worrying about the class very much. You can do the homework the night before. Then she crams mechanisms and reactions into the last half which works but if you don't step up your game, you won't be prepared.   She has great lecture notes online.

This class was a lot more work than the 6 units labeled on it, but it is a good experience. Silva's lectures can sometimes be hard to get through, but you have to pay attention at all times- her quizzes are based solely on lectures..
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Gave vague feedback/instructions, very petty when you ask questions, felt like her class was less about developing presentation skills and more about catering to her.
obsolete course materials, mandatory attendance, not allowed for computer or phone, low quality teaching

This professor is a fair grader. As long as you actively participate in her conferences and diligently revise your assignments according to her suggestions, I think your final grade won't be low. Moreover, the material she teaches is useful for career development.
这个老师的课需要你花时间，但有付出最终应该都不会差
This is the worst I've ever seen, yes, the very worst. Not deserve to be a teacher at all. And that person who gave a 5-star rating, are you serious? Were you asked to boost the ratings?

This Strategic Presentations course included a required external project tied to the professor's personal ambitions, not disclosed before enrollment and unrelated to the curriculum. Missing it, due to tech issues or otherwise, meant losing 30 points. She shamed struggling students and said, “we all have the same 24 hours.” Very disappointed.
It was just ok. Follow rubric, you'll be graded on it.

I took the Business Writing class which was insightful. The TAs as well as the professor both provide constructive feedback on the assignments which if followed can lead to good grades. The professor is cheerful and keeps the class interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is a strict grader and you need to be on time for her class and not miss a single class since she will cut points for that.
This professor does NOT RESPECT students at all. She is easy to get mad at no reason. She teaches colleges students like a kindergartner.

The Professor is very bad at giving proper feedback. She does not seem interested in helping the student grow in the specific skill that the course is intended to help. As the whole course is subjective, it is very important to be in her good books. If she does not like you, then you will end up doing badly in her course.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: If you want to really learn something in infosec, definitely take Hanan's class! Really inspiring and your work will pay off!
Taught me ways to be a better learnwr
good first class for infosec. hard homeworks initially.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: don't ever let her enter your life!!!

Tough but very fair professor. Lectures are extremely interesting and entertaining. This class is very challenging, but it you go to class, take good notes, and study hard, you will be okay. The subject matter itself is difficult but he is good at explaining it in a digestible way. He goes a fast sometimes though!

For my entire colleg experience, I have never met a professor as bad as Hendrich. He literally doesn't care if you are failing the class. When the class average is a D, he won't even address it. I went to his office hours and he made me wait 30min before answering my question. When I ask him questions, he expects me to know everything when I don't.
He is a very hard professor, especially for this course. He expects you to know things outside of the course to do well. It's supposed to be a math class, yet it's more quantum than anything.

Before I took this class, I didn't have much interest in thermodynamics. This professor is just awesome - he explains things amazingly well, and you can tell he has a genuine passion for the subject. He is very willing to help any student that has questions. Very hard material, but by exam time I felt like I had learned a ton. Keep it up Hendrich!

Hardcore-ness. Kind of ridiculous material and exams. He explains things reaaaallllyy well, but hes more of a concept guy, and the exams are all problems, so...make sure to go to rec and do LOTS of problems.

He is very inspirational - an extremely passionate teacher.  He can rotate a matrix like no one else, when he talked about tensors he almost saw my O-face.  Before I took this class, I thought ENDOR was where the Ewoks lived.  He has completely perturbed my world.

Prof. Hendrich was my favorite teacher, but he's is really hard.  Before I took his class I barely knew what an electrob was and now I've switched my major to Theology.  He's dreamy, he reminds me of an athletic Conan O'Brian, sans the attitude.  Overall, awesome teacher, Old Soul.  Rock on MPH.

Every Thursday of mine would be taken up doing Greggo's hw. The midterms were generally easier than the hw, but a time crunch. The final reflected the difficulty of the hws. The 3-hour final was hard bc because the questions forced you to think on the spot. Lecture notes are extensive and reci problems were good practice for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Genuinely an impossible class, he sets you up to do bad. The 21128 (math major students) were unable to do our final, while we were able to do theirs. He tests you on the most impossible things for a beginners math class. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS unless you absolutely need to. The only thing i got out of this class was friends along the way.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: hard class, lectures were v abstract, and midterms were a time crunch
Oof great lectures hard tests
Very very good lecturer. His notes are really all you need to understand to do well in the course, and they are very thorough, well-organized, and clear. The goat.
Hard course. Be careful about your hw
he is the gregGOAT
tERRIBLE TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE

i visited his mini greggo in office hours and man still refused to give me anything better than a 3-point curve on a 100 point test. this is ridiculous. never take this prof unless u wanna get the unlubricated raw cmu experience all in 1 go
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hard on students
hard homework but very easy exams. Great teaching. love it.
He's lectures were clear, and there are interesting problems that are worth-thinking for a while in psets :)
He delivers great lectures, provides a lot of resources for us to understand the material, and is very active on Piazza answering questions. Very responsible and knowledge prof imo.
good professor, have complete lecture notes so you dont have to go to lectures. hard homework but very easy exams. good teaching. learn lots of new stuff every lecture
My guy explains things in lecture like it's the easiest thing in the world and disregard actual good questions asked in the lecture.

He does not teach to his actual class population but to his ideal class. He could care less if you have never done concepts before. Almost everybody in his current class has either done mathematical proofs before or has done math competitions.
greggoat

Lecture is very content-heavy, but since this is 3D calc and Lin Alg in one class, there is a lot of material. He does go over helpful examples in class, and provides comprehensive review/practice material for exams. Exams (worth 80% of total grade) were brutal, but he did curve final grades for my sem. Overall v tough, but v knowledgeable prof.
Greggo is based

I'm gonna be so honest, he focuses more on the grades and less on the learning of students. He seems frustrated when students don't do well and blames it all on us instead of considering that MAYBE his teaching style is difficult for a lot of students. He also doesn't curve or curves barely even though a large portion of his students are failing.

He is great at giving motivation and explaining things. He also typed up lecture notes, which are super helpful. Exams were more on the calculation side than on the proof side, and I found them to be pretty reasonable. 100% would take again.

Very competent lecturer, fair grader, lenient with deadlines and provides plenty of study material, however he's not always the nicest. Often gave off somewhat condescending vibes to people asking questions, so you don't always feel comfortable admitting you don't understand. Zoom class sucks for everyone, but he didn't exactly make it easier.

I'd say his 127 isn't really that tough as people described. The lecture notes are extremely helpful, especially if you are not paying attention during the lecture. Just make sure you fully understand the materials and the exams will be fine.

The worst Math teacher I have ever had in my life. He was so bad at explaining stuff, and he rushed the course unnecessarily. Please do not take Greggo Johnson for 21-127. Take Ervin for that Class!! The notes were also bad at explaining stuff. On top of that, this is the most useless math class I took. So much useless information in this course!!

Extremely condescending. Asking questions at OH was horrible because he would laugh and get annoyed at me asking questions about things I didn't understand. Goes through the material way too fast and in general isn't a great person.
had him for concepts. went too fast and copied notes onto the board most of the time. didnt explain well. would not recommend. also tests were hard which didnt help.
Great lecturer. Definitely wanna take his classes again in the future.

Professor Johnson is awesome! He puts his lecture notes into a textbook format every day, he gives you around 40 review problems before the test with an answer key showing the work. He also gives you a sample exam with also worked out solutions and answers piazza questions every day to help you study. He cares so much about students, he is awesome!

He must be an angel. His class has the most logical and clear lecture notes for you to use. He responds to piazza question in a very fast speed. Whenever possible hes able to answer your question clearly. Hes absolutely one of the best math professors in CMU.
dont skip class plz
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Difficult class, but he really pushes you to actually understand and apply the concepts. Provides lecture notes online as well
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: His lectures and notes are very student-oriented. Learned a lot and would definitely take his classes again.

Greggo is a fantastic professor who gives clear and informative lectures and is very accessible during office hours. Hes also just a great guy in general. The class maybe tough but he sets clear expectations and does more than enough practice in class to give you a general understanding of what he is looking for. The best professor Ive had so far

Greggo focuses a lot on lectures. In his lectures, he goes over lots of examples that are very helpful for homework and exam questions. His exam problems are similar to lecture materials, and they shouldn't be too hard if you've gone to lecture and done the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: His tests are very straightforward and his lectures are generally very solid. Overall a pretty good professor and as long as you put in the work, you should do well in the class.

Greg is a great lecture and goes over everything that will be on his test; there will be no surprises. The class goes by rather fast, but if you show up to lecture, go over notes, and go to office hours if you need that extra help, the class is manageable.

Greggo's lectures are pretty good and go by quickly. He writes a ton of material on the board, and the examples used in class are normally very helpful. Some material is dependent on learning in recitation, which depends on the quality of your TA (mine was awful so I had to do some self teaching). 3 exams, 6 questions each, not much partial credit.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Very willing to help students learn the material, if you don't understand something, it is your job to go to his office hours, where he can help you grasp topics for the homework. Class notes are informative and concise, but he writes his notes very quickly, so you must pay attention for the entire lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Good office hours
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Hard but worth. His lecture notes are unbeatable. He's very thorough and never fails to leave anything unexplained.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Tough but fair. He responds almost instantly on Piazza and through email, though, so make use of that.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Greggo isn't a bad lecturer, but he tends to make things more complicated than it is. I took Calc 2 in high school, but the calc 2 taught by Greggo was still tough for me even though I learned most of the concepts before. Some of the newer concepts taught that I didn't know before were just confusing. Quizzes are also unnecessarily difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: I worked hard for this class and got nothing at all. He's condescending and bad at explaining tricky concepts. He also seems to think he's still 16 with those clothes and that piercing. Are you not a math professor at a top-ranking university?

One of the best professors I've had here. He is extremely clear in everything. If you go to him, he gives great feedback, but you usually won't need to because there's opportunity for that in recitation and HW is returned with detailed comments of what to improve on. Also, he is genuinely good at communicating concepts, unlike some professors.

Great professor! I took Calc1 and Calc2 in highschool, so I thought it would be easy to transition to Calc3 at CMU. I was wrong and Greggo really made me work hard. I bombed my first test but got a lot better at calculus as the course progressed. He is a very clear professor, and he challenges you on the tests. I ended up loving the class though.
Great professor. Gives clear lectures. Attend them and you'll be on track. Tests are slightly tough but most definitely fair.

Greggo is an excellent professor who thoroughly explains everything. Calculus in 3D is a fast-paced course so the material might feel rushed, but Greggo really takes the time to write extensive exam reviews to prepare his students. Exams can be tough, but definitely doable if you prepare. Go to office hours and he'll like you even more.
Greggo is a hard teacher and he teaches his class tougher than most 21259 teachers do.  You will learn if you put your head to it, but he is not easy.

Lectures are clear, and recitation worksheets are a great supplement to lectures. Homework assignments are most of the time long, but not very hard. Listens to feedback. Three midterms and a final. Gives extra credit on every midterm. The first two were easy, but the third was unexpectedly hard(class avg was 45, and 60+ was an A). Final was easy.

Took calc-3d with Greggo in Fall 2014. Very clear lectures. Listens to student feedback. After the first midterm some students said the homework was a little too much, and he reduced the amount by around  40%. Gives extra-credit problems on every midterm. Exams are not hard and reflects the materials taught. Definitely take his 259. Awesome prof.
lol read the textbook or something cuz lectures are just a bunch of paragraphs

Greggo is one of the few human beings I've seen who can write as fast as he speaks. Multivariable calculus with him made for painful note taking, so don't even try to copy what he writes in time, listen to him instead. He's very theory based, and often leaves you teaching yourself how to actually solve problems, but the concepts will be understood.

Listens to feedback. Very fair. Lots of work, written and online. Works through theorems and basic examples in class and more detailed examples are done in worksheets during recitation or through homework. Definitely need to go to class for this. Has extra credit problems on tests for applying material to past knowledgetheoremscreating proofs.

I won't say that he's a nice teacher; in all honesty, I think he's a bit (read: VERY) arrogant and not fun to e-mail or talk to at all. Regardless, he knows his stuff fairly well, and is one of the clearer teachers I have. I don't know whether or not his grading policies are fair--class is easy, his grading is not--but, education-wise, not too bad.

He's nice but a little intimidating. His lectures are very fast-paced and he is very wordy in his proofs and explanations which can make things confusing. He picked pretty bad TAs but overall you will learn a lot. Exams are hard, but if you take time to understand on your own you will be fine.

Lectures are fast paced and can be difficult to keep up with or understand at times, depending on your familiarity with the subject. Greggo is very knowledgeable, and the tests are not too difficult if you really pay attention and make an effort to understand everything he teaches.

Greggo was not a very good lecturer. His lecture style was very bland, rushed and disinterested. He also did not seem to have much interest in the subject and as a result did not put much effort into communicating to the class. He seems like a nice guy though.
He is a very good teacher and it is very easy to learn with him and his quizzes are relatively fair but his exams are impossible!

Greggo is a great professor. His lectures are very interesting and will give you insights you can't get just by reading the book. His tests can be difficult, but he provides lots of solved practice problems so you can prepare for them. Highly recommend.
Greggo is the man. Very clear and straightforward teaching style, also very helpful if you go to office hours and ask him questions.
Awesome teacher. His tests are kind of long, but if you understand what you are doing, you will be fine.

3D calculus is a HARD class, but Greggo teaches it very well. I really love the class, and did fairly well. Greggo's lectures complement the book fantastically. He explains the material precisely and concisely. Good experience :)
Greggo is one of the better math professors at CMU. I will miss him next year!
Very straightforward, covers material clearly and concisely.  You learn a lot in a short period of time and are never bored.

He's awesome and helpful. He doesn't waste too much time providing proofs and pretty much lays out for you what you need to know for the tests (which aren't easy, but feasible) and how the material he's teaching you is useful. What I liked the most was he was just really straightforward in his instruction style. Could've done better in the class :/

Also 127 Concepts of Mathematics. Has excellent review material available before tests. Friendly guy, and spells out everything about course&material in no uncertain terms. He moves pretty fast, but not too fast that you might have trouble keeping up. Overall, if you can take a class with him, go for it.
He's ok. Not very helpful.

Awesome guy. Great lecturer, gives a few good examples after proofs, and he sky dives. Quiz each week in recitation is easy. Tests are only within one lecture period, very doable if you do homework. Doesn't mind if you show up for a later lecture.  Schedule him!
Greggo is awesome. He's a total badass with a lip ring, pictures of him smoking cigars and skydiving. His class is easy as long as you pay attention and take notes.

Never question Greggo in class, because you are wrong. In addition, he is a total badass, with pierced nipples, tongue, and lip. The material is decently hard, but not impossible. Greggo's explanations are very concise and he's always willing to help you out in office hours.
Cool guy. He basically hand-feeds you the knowledge in class and throws homrwork at you to make sure you get it. As long as you go to class and do the homework, tests and exams are giveaways.

Greggo is AWESOME. He is by far the best out of my past 3 calculus teachers. The way he teaches is very clear and he is NEVER wrong in any way. If you do the homework and study for quizzes, tests are easy. SCHEDULE HIM IN; HE IS SO COOL.
Very helpful. As long as you are doing your work, doing the quizzes, you will be fine.

Jeff is just a real cool dude. I have taken four of his classes. Each one has pushed me and the class to think in bold and interesting ways, and has covered many important and thought-provoking topics. Awesome guy.

Jeff is AWESOME I would absolutely recommend taking a class with him. He's very engaging, knew everyone's names after the first week and called on most people every class. Super interesting and knowledgable guy!
You can tell he really cares about his students. I feel bad I kept falling asleep in class.

Jeff honestly cares about his students. The classes I've taken with him have been my favorite, despite it being nowhere affiliated with my major. His seminars are smaller so participation is essential to get the most out of the class but he makes you feel so comfortable that participation isn't a problem.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: awesome dude. super respectful of my mental health struggles and is overall a great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: What an amazing professor! Jeff was kind and funny and as someone who didn't take Intro when entering the class, he was able to create an environment where I wasn't shy to share my opinions on the films we watched. I highly recommend taking his classes!!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Probably the best professor I've had at CMU. Humorous, engaging, and approachable with just the right amount of intimidation. Would highly recommend taking a class with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: No Comments
No Comments
His reputation precedes him

He's guaranteed to cram your head with knowledge, and you can tell he puts in a lot of work when making the homework assignments. As you go through then, the techniques used and results found ever so gingerly pushes you to the next question's answer. It's brilliant. He's brilliant.

Prof. Tice put in a lot of effort into teaching vector analysis. He comes in well prepared and explains concepts clearly, prepares thorough lecture notes, and crafts assignments that help us learn. He likes challenging students, and is happy to spend time with you after class (or on Piazza) if you want to discuss interesting tangents.
Very knowledgeable and technical. He can give the lectures and OH with no mistake/pause without referring to any notes.

Quite a lot of time needed to be dedicated to excel in continuous time finance. However, Professor Iyer is decently rigorous with the notations and introduction of new ideas. The latex notes are very helpful when reviewing and preparing for exams. He and the TA are always available during office hours to answer questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The continuous time part is just soooooo hard to understand. He always made typos which made the proof even more confusing and the handwriting is awful

21-370 is a discrete class. While I get the materials in the first 3/4 of the semester, I'm destroyed in the last 2-3 weeks when he RUSHED through materials in CONTINUOUS time (beyond 370). He seems to just wanna be done with the class than to let students LEARN about anything. Don't respond well to feedback. Require to code which is not a pre-req.

He is not always prepared for lectures and has very messy handwriting. Sometimes he tends to go all over the place without logistics and order. He's not good at asking for feedback and response to them. Maybe he has a higher expectation for us with good intentions. But I hope he can set a higher standard for himself in teaching FIRST.

Iyer's class is "unnecessarily hard", says a math genius here. Multiple phd students here also think his homework problems (freshmen level class) were made only to be hard but not at all practical or useful. The technicality of his courses takes away the fun of math, they are discouraging instead--difference between challenging and technical.
Awesome professor. Really great at lecturing and has an amazing sense of humor. Homeworks and exams were fairly challenging, but you learn so much from them. Would strongly recommend.

I was taking an honors class but even for that he tends it in a manner than makes it more difficult than usual. Despite making it one of the hardest classes I've taken, Iyer is fun and does a good job teaching.
Hardest professor ever but he's awesome. I got a C and I don't regret taking it at all.
Lots of work for a 9-unit class, but I most certainly learned linear algebra.  Topics at the end (eigenstuff --> spectral theorem) were a bit rushed, though.  Take if you're up for the challenge.
Super laid back guy who talks slow and has a relatively boring class but its really easy with fair assignments and tests

Oppenheim is the least helpful professor, he doesn't do anything that requires him to do any extra work. Probably the worst professor I've ever had. And the slowest talker... but his class is easy though
Simply awesome!
Slow talker, great teacher.
A wonderfully weird professor but in a good way. Class average is like A-. He's awesome.
intolerably boring

Somehow has an occupation where he is not responsible for teaching anything, mentoring anyone, or contributing anything to the university, but still has a salary and a title. His class was literally devoid of any content whatsoever and he provided zero mentorship or support to students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Basically a huckster and scam artist. Rubin wasted everyone's time and made class intolerable.

An incredible professor and person overall. Gave me so much wisdom during my years at CMU. Highly recommend. If you participate lots in discussions, do the readings/watch the videos, and arrive every week on time, you will succeed in her class.

Professor O'Connor's course covers material that is generally left out of lessons in other classes or schools. You absolutely get out what you put into BLE, so be ready to participate and work hard...it'll pay off!
Rubab truly cares about her student's success and pours her heart into her courses!

Professor O'Connor is an amazing teacher and inspirational leader in our school community. I have learned so much from her so far, and I am sure I will continue to. Her class is engaging and encourages active participation from everyone. Her assignments allow you to reflect and challenge yourself. Overall, a fantastic course.

BLE with Professor O'Connor is a course that truly prepares you for post-grad. It is a class where you will get out what you put in, but there are many opportunities for networking with alumni, resume building, interview practice, and public speaking. This class builds your soft skills in a way that is directly translatable to the real world.
Rubab truly cares about helping all of her students in personal and professional growth. Great professor.
Professor O'Connor is an amazing professor who engages her students in every way possible. I would love to have her as a professor again.
It was a great class with a lot of real-world applications that I use daily!
Great Teacher!
Best professor I've ever had - was lucky to experience Prof Rubab during my worst sem of college and she's become a great lifeline :)
Great professor! Super caring. Loved her class

Professor O'Connor is very personable with her students and makes a distinct effort to learn and encourage everyone on their professional journey. Her material is focused on classroom discussion, with a focus on improving soft skills. Do not be surprised if you have a spontaneous classroom assignment where you must speak for five minutes.
Rubab was an awesome professor and I was excited to be in class every time!

The BLE classes are short, but they will push you outside of your comfort zone. There is one assignment a week, and it can typically be completed in less than an hour. Working ahead will make your life much easier.

As a professor, Rubab was always there to help you and was one of the first people I went to when I needed job advice.
Professor O'Connor is a one-of-a-kind professor. She genuinely cares about the development of her students. The class is definitely one where you get out what you put in.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor O'Connor truly cares about students' personal and professional development, rather than simply academic. You will be pushed outside your comfort zone in this class, but it is truly a course where you get out what you put in.

The prof is a nice person, but to be honest, the class (ble 1,2,3) are absolutely useless in my opinion. In fact, I would also actually recommend CMU to just remove this course because it is just a complete waste of time.
Professor Liang truly cares about the topic of accounting, is very passionate during lectures which is appreciated, and you can tell he genuinely wants to see his students succeed!

Very professional and very kind. Best professor in Tepper. If you wanna learn about accounting, Prof Liang has quite a lot of optional accounting exercises. He and his TAs are knowledgeable to answer your accounting questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: The BEST CMU professor ever. Such a nice person and truly cares about learning above everything. Gives feedback on homework and allows you to correct mistakes and turn it back in for full credit (although its not needed, he grades easy). Final is generally take home. Will offer extra credit if you ask. Even will take studenst to lunch on campus
A good professor with a fine knowledge of accounting.  Sometimes he let class discussion get in the way of what he wanted to accomplish, but overall no complaints.  Very little accent.

Love Kats. You can choose to put in minimal effort, but the assignments are manageable enough that you'll be better to just do the readings and watch the films. Very very kind and picks his media well
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took a Russian language class. It seems like he may be better for his history and film classes. I found that he can be judgmental which shouldn't be the case when someone is teaching a language. I wasnt a huge fan of his methods for language learning, which included memorizing long poems and reciting them every class. Tests/homework are easy tho.
BEST PROFESSOR EVER TAKE HIS CLASS WHILE YOU CAN BEFORE HE RETIRES!!! HE IS SO NICE AND THE BEST CLASS EVER!

Best Prof ever! Called us 'sweety' in class, but was always willing and able to help with difficulties in grammar, comprehension, vocab, etc. This course is sure A as long as you show up and do the small amount reading/writing assignments he asks for.
Awesome teacher, one of my favorite people at CMU. Really cares about his students.

Very friendly professor! ie- when student falls asleep he asks if they need coffee. Class trips to concerts and opera were fun, although in class all you do is watch movies and make presentations about books you do not have to read (just sparknote it).

He is an amazing professor. He really wants you to do well, and at the same time is very funny. He however changes the syllabus frequently and all this means is that you have to do a lot of last minute work. But, if you do the work, you will be rewarded with A's easily.
My favorite person at this school.  Just make your work "maximum beautiful"

Dr. Kats is a professor that cares a lot of about the students and wants them to learn by any means. This is why he takes his own time to create classes that go outside in Pittsburgh and finds actually informative and relative videos for us to enjoy. It was more fun than work for this class.
The best professor at CMU, by far, cares the most about his students, makes the effort
best/easiest prof at this school!  Coolest Guy ever. "is good"
teaches really interesting classes, on pretty unique subjects with a very broad topic range
easy
Intimidating at first, but really a good guy
bro just makes the course hard for no reason and cannot lecture

He recently switched to a new textbook, which has you read a chapter that consists of 60-85 pages roughly every week. He rambles quite a bit and it's hard to tell what is important and what isn't for his exams. The grading is not very clear as you are expected to write everything down in relation to a concept even if it seems like common knowledge.

Such a great professor. I chose to take psych just because I wanted a gen-ed course that could fit my fked up schedule (my registration time for the semester was terrible). I always thought I was not great at being in lectures, but his class was just different and fun to attend. Very responsible, knowledgable and ACTUALLY CARES ABOUT HIS STUDENTS.

absolutely amazing professor and lecturer. he's so funny but also a great teacher. super kind and understanding too. if you've taken any sort of psych class before the class will be a breeze, if not you may have to read the textbook

Kody is a great lecturer but very overly enthusiastic to the point where he’s annoying and obnoxious (in my opinion). A little smirky and not very considerate to students’ needs. Has a no laptops policy which is a bit annoying. Just obnoxious in many ways but you’ll definitely learn. If you’re a low energy type of person I would avoid him.

Amazing professor. He teaches the concepts very well. The only thing I would say was frustrating is how he expects reflections to be done. Most people did bad on the first bit which he said was expected, however, the grades were kept which severely brings down your grade.

One of the best professors Ive ever had.I had taken it as a gen ed requirement but I ended up loving psychology. I was really struggling after the transition to online school, and he actually met with me to just encourage me and tell me that Im doing great. As long as you pay attention during lecture, theres not much work to do.

Kody's a fantastic professor. The material can be somewhat boring but his lectures are super interesting as he adds in pop-culture references and external media to make the class more accessible to those studying psych for the first time. The tests are straighforward but can be difficult; go to class, read the textbook, and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very enthusiastic but the jokes are kinda cringe at times. Good lecturer and clearly explains the material. Lectures/recitations involve group discussion, which can get awkward. Not much work but each assignment is worth a lot, so watch out. Tests are hard if you don't remember the studies in depth (a lot). Generally easy class but don't slack off.

He was a really cool & engaging teacher, tried really hard to connect with us by bringing up modern references, which was cringe at times but overall a nice change from the stuffy old professors. Had cool slides and tried to make social psych interesting. Be prepared for a lot of textbook reading everyday, but he did curve the final!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He's very energetic and caring. He's really cute. Taking his class right now and it's very interesting material!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: A fantastic lecturer. He really cares about his students and makes it very clear. Some of the subject mattery may be boring but his lectures make up for that. Very energetic and passionate about psychology. You'll have to read a little but as long as you go to class, you can scrape by without it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Kody's fantastic. Super understanding and very clear standards for his students. Highly recommend him for any class he teaches.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He's a great professor

Will push you conceptually, but incredible lecturer and good problem setter. Show up to class though not mandatory, it's worth it. Ask questions - he goes fast but will stop if even a single person doesn't understand.

After taking two courses (251 and 455) with him, I kinda believe that he is one of the reasons why Algorithms and Theory is an unpopular scs concentration these years. Not only are his lectures confusing, but he also presents the material in an outdated manner which makes it very hard to appreciate the importance and value of the topics.

no. simply. no. lectures are just him reading from slides. he says everything is trivial, but it is not. homework is much harder than questions gone over in lectures or recitations, so I now live in OH. homework takes at least 15 hours on a really good week.
Doesn't explain well in lecture to build the foundation well enough for the homework questions that takes 20 hours plus each week
Exudes sheer awesomeness
Great and inspirational lecturer. Homework can be hard sometimes, but asking for clarification/hints helped a lot. Exam was not hard.
Super cool guy. You can tell that he REALLY knows what he's talking about, and he's helpful about it, too.
Fantastic class. Hard, but fun. Great homework assignments. Overlaps with 15-453. Suggest taking algebraic structures first. Sutner is very generous and approachable, also.
VERY interesting lecture style.  Provided much needed comic relief to straight man Danny Sleator
Klaus is the man.  He says great things, has a great attitude, and is a martial arts master.
He singlehandedly inspired me to go to graduate school. Absolutely the best professor I have ever seen! Caring, clear, logical, and extremely knowledgable.
Good
Extremely smart. Talks like Arnold Schwarzenegger. Not nearly as intimidating as he seems. Unfortunately, his abstract, fleeting lecturing style is difficult to learn from.
Extrememly smart. Extremely clear. Extrememly helpful. Most influential prof for me.
No Comments
Eats freshmen for breakfast.
He is so funny. You can tell he is incredibly smart because he knows just what to say to make the entire class laugh. People without comp math experience will prob find his class harder
Amazing professor, great with students and exams were difficult but fair. TAs were pretty much always available and feedback was plentiful. Can feel like you lose more points than seems fair.
Extremely engaging lectures. I loved every minute I spent in his class, and he is also very compassionate towards his students.
Very engaged and energetic during lectures, the class itself is quite intense but there are enough materials and support so that you can do well anyway. A first-year staple for math majors.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Phenomenal lecturer and very approachable outside of class.

Really nice professor, cares about his students. His homeworks are rather hard and the midterms are quite hard. He is generally hilarious. His lectures are good for a general overview but they are slightly fast-paced.
I didn't find his lectures to be helpful while the homework was extremely difficult.
Best professor I've ever had.
Overall a very good professor. Class was very challenging and homeworks were even harder, but I learned the most out of any class.

Amazing Professor. Inspires freshman to be excited about pure mathematics and holds the class to a high standard while providing excellent support for everyone to succeed given that they are willing to put in the work.
Such a charismatic, personable lecturer--wish I could take 151 again despite the heavy courseload. Gave such a wonderful intro to various important math concepts. A great guy.
Mackey is an amazing prof. he's an excellent teacher and is very kind and respectful towards his students. would highly recommend him
Best professor everr

One of the best lecturers I've ever had. Very helpful faculty member in general; he's good to meet with several times to talk about future plans, particularly ones relating to future CS or math courses. Cares about his students and shows genuine love for mathematics. Tests are fairly hard but you will learn a lot.

One of the top 3 teachers I've had in my entire schooling career. I feel privileged to have been able to take courses with such an inspired individual. If you get this man for any of your classes, consider yourself lucky and don't waste a second in lecture on your phone. You miss out on so much knowledge.

Absolutely outstanding professor - probably the best professor I've had my entire life. He makes a difficult class extremely enjoyable, and inspires me to pursue higher level maths classes. Attend his office hours! They're fun and you get a lot out of it

Amazing professor! I took his class over half a summer semester along with another class so the schedule was pretty rough - but he made the journey fun! Literally one of the best professors/teachers I've ever had! Take his class if you ever get the chance!

Prof MacKey is by far my favorite teacher at CMU. He is amazing at explaining theories, keeps lectures interactive and interesting, and is an inspirational gift to the CMU faculty. In the simplest word, I love MacKey. He truly cares about his students. Some professors prioritize research over teaching at CMU. MacKey prioritizes his students.

He's a great professor, 21-128 is a great class designed to give students a very strong foundation in proof-based mathematics. He's absolutely hilarious, yet also respected and one of the best teachers in the Carnegie Mellon math department.
good good

John Mackey is hands down an amazing professor. Classes and course content are very well planned. Great lecturer, the kind of person you just want to keep listening to. His homeworks and tests are pretty hard, but he gives you enough of a leeway on the curve to make getting a decent grade not too easy, but within reach if you work for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: There was a day that I was laying in bed sick, but still got up to go to Concepts just because I couldn't imagine missing it. Really inspirational guy and always has some diet Pepsi by his side. Good guy and real patient with students. You will really need to try. He expects and believes that his students can grasp difficult material.
Very very inspirational. He is passionate about teaching and he has the magical power to make you love math. I became a math major after taking math128 with him.
He is a very passionate teacher. You can expect to actually learn stuff from him. In general, he will give you not only good knowledge but also good advice for other classes.
Great professor, really hard homework, reasonable grading
Best professor I've ever had. The man really kills it in every aspect of teaching. Propelled me beyond what I thought I could achieve. Thank you for everything

Mackey is hilarious and really high energy. It's pretty amazing that he is the head of the math department at a school like CMU and still chooses to teach Calc 1 to give freshmen a solid foundation. It's all downhill with math professors afterwards.
great

He was the best professor I've ever had. He brings tons of energy and gets you excited about the material. If I had him for every class my life would be amazing. If you have the opportunity to take his class, do it. Also loved all the riddles.
I honestly can say that Professor Mackey is the best professor I have ever had.
He's awesome. Interesting and he's really interested in letting the student LEARN.
Take concepts with Mackey, there really no other way, and hes a brilliant proffessor.
Brilliant Professor. My favorite class by far since coming to CMU
seriously the reason i became a math major. made me want to go to class in the morning. his classes are hard, but he's amazing at explaining things. and he's attractive.
Simply awesome teacher! Fantastic class! No complaints!!!!!
If you can, take Mackey
Mackey rocks! He's very helpful and always willing to answer questions.
Funny, enthusiastic, helpful. The course material can be a bit challenging, but if you're willing to put in the work, you'll get a lot out of it.

Hilarious, engaging, and effective. A genuinely great guy who takes pleasure in his job. Teaches with the intent to be difficult, but enjoys his students' struggles in an understandable way. Memorable, a great professor, and a very interesting class.
An *excellent*, hilarious lecturer. His classes are notoriously hard but not impossible by any means. Has a great staff of TAs and provides lots of help and generous curves.
Dr. Mackey is AMAZING! I feel like he has prepared us all very well for the next level of hard proofs: 251!

Very good teacher. Very clear, very far. Very nice and caring. Mackey is well known on campus, and one of the favorite math professors. The class is hard if you've never seen the material before, but he is very balanced, and if you conceptually understand the material, you will be fine.
