Graph Theory is an amazing course if taught by the right teacher, aka Mackey. Lectures are mostly proofs of theorems in the book, Midterms are challenging but Mackey curvres a lot, and Final was harder than midterms.
Hard tests, but they're on material covered in class.

Professor Mackey is very cool, and very smart.  He's great at teaching, very friendly and helpful.  Also, he's the coolest professor here by far, because he went to the University of Hawaii! How cool is that?
Amazingly good teacher.
Wonderful teacher.  I highly recommend him to anyone wanting a great class.  A little hard, but worth it.

he's too hyper even for someone who drinks 2-liter bottle of coke each morning. Got us interested in Euclidean alg bc every mathmetician apparently jumps up and down when hearing it, but he didn't tell us what the alg is:(
Very difficult class but very funny and interesting prof.
Great Professor.  The exams were really hard, but he curved them so it was okay.
No Comments
tests are HARD... he gives concept questions, but overall he is very helpful

Tests are ridiculously hard, but only involves material he explicitly covers. Often tests technicalities and problems requiring creative thinking. Curves grades fairly at end. Really passionate about math and how "beautiful" it is; energetic lec
Not the best lecturer but nice
Not recommend.
Very good lectures and empathetic to students

Super engaging professor, and even though my class was her first-ever class she's taught, we all thought she had years of experience. Incredibly helpful and caring, and extensive OH/help sessions make the course amazing.
Very good professor
Prof makes content a lot more difficult than necessary, and his expectations for tests are very unclear during class.
The course requires you to be good at test-taking. Lenny is easy to reach and provides good feedback when asking questions during office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nice professor. I think his first test is really difficult but the next two seem to be better. Lots of credit given on tests and homework for getting close to the right answer. Provides a really good structure for the class and the notes guide you well. Gotta do most of your learning outside the class but thats cause the class is only 50 min.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Really nice professor and not bad at teaching the material at all, but the tests are unusually hard. I understood the material quite well, but the tests ask very hard questions and even with the review it felt like you couldn't really prepare for them in completion. It is not an intro chem course. Very heavy on quantum mechanics in unit on.

I love Prof Vuocolo!! He is so funny and says the most interesting things sometimes. Exams are not bad as long as you go through the practice problems and recitation content. There is a lot of material so be sure to keep up with readings and assignments.

Nice person, but overcomplicates topics in lecture. If you are taking this as an intro to chemistry class think again.. It is useful to have background knowledge from high school. You can do well and not come to lecture because he talks a lot and more than half is unnecessary. HW is fair and boosts grade + Exams are more difficult but doable.

He is great. Tests and homeworks tend to be straightforward, and he is INCREDIBLY helpful in office hours - take advantage of it! He used an experimental grading system this semester that was very nice (more forgiving than point grades), and hopefully that continues. There is a participation portion for lectures, though, so be sure to go.

King he's so funny and deserves the world I've never seen anyone look so tired and sound so vocal at the same time just knowing that I get to see my king lenny vuocolo for chem lab later in the day makes me so happy hes got this little lab coat that he trots around the room in hes not taken seriously but hes fr so caring and nice
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Vuocolo is nice and engaging, and the OLI/textbook provide great practice problems. He has a nice sense of humor.
He is caring and kind. Really great content even for non-majors.
This man is crazy hard. I took this course twice and still did bad. The material makes no sense. beware

The class is doable for those with prior exposure, but would be difficult for those without. Do exam reviews & do/understand every. assignment. Study & complete assignments in advance. If you need help, go to your TA or Dr. V, and they'll be happy to help. He seemed a bit preachy to students. Too many moving parts in the course overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Good lecturer and carefully goes through his notes in class. This class is very time consuming (10hrs/week). Watch out for tricky questions on quizzes/tests. Psets are hard-ish and often include unfamiliar questions. Online software is expensive ($40-60+) and only slightly useful. Always do the exam review! Even a chem background can't help much.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I've never been more confused about concepts I already understand. Lectures take way too long to explain things in the most confusing way possible. Not good if you don't have a strong background in chem.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lenny honestly tries to help every student as much as he can. His lectures are fast but the notes are clear if you go over them afterwards. His office hours are usually very helpful and he's pretty easy to talk to. Sometimes he can over explain things, but overall he is very thorough and puts in work to help your understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: I didn't actually do that bad in Dr.V's class, but I know from talking to people that if you weren't already pretty good at chemistry, you definetly will take a beating as all of the topics are brutal, particularly if you haven't taken a good amount of chem before. Would steer away unless very confident with chem, even then proceed with caution.

It is stated that the class is designed for people with no chem background, but if you dont have any you will likely be floundering. Vuocolo explains everything Im the most unnecessarily complicated way (ie taking an entire lecture to explain a concept I can show you in 3 min, only I will make sense) Grade relies heavily on tests. Must read book.

The lecture could use a lot of improvement, but his office hours are helpful. The class moves insanely fast, so I'd recommend coming in with chemistry experience if you want to get a good grade. At times there is too much work assigned all at once. The assessments are extremely hard for what is put forth by the professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: His lectures aren't too helpful in my opinion and doesn't explain concepts too well. The first unit is very unnecessarily quantum heavy. He makes problem sets and other assessments pretty hard, but I will say that he is pretty accessible outside of class and was, from what I've heard, pretty helpful office hours.
The first unit is just quantum physics

Although he gives a lot of details in lectures, he likes to trick you when it comes to quizzes and tests. You have to be very careful because of those tricks he throws in, and I always found that recitations were more helpful than the actual lectures. He does give an ok amount of extra credit, but I didn't like how he looked down at his students.
Good professor overall. There are quizzes almost every week and a lot of opportunity for extra credit. The lectures are kind of lacking; he gives a packet of notes and goes through it.
Worst professor I have ever had at CMU. You cannot avoid Lenny, but atleast try to take Chem 1 over the summer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. V made the lectures and materials easy to understand and provided feedback for every questions you asked. He also gave extra points if you did the reading and the corresponding sampling problems. Overall he was a good professor.

His tests (and graders) are super tough and cover things that are not in lecture.  Attendance is supposedly mandatory (you get extra credit for doing in class exercises).  Problem sets take a while and are graded for credit (sometimes the grades don't even make sense) so expect to have no idea what grade you're actually getting

Dr. V's a really nice guy. A lot of people think he's mean because of his hard tests but he does care a lot for his students. He gives so much extra credit, and is so willing to help out his students especially during office hours. Ace problem sets by going to office hours and tutoring, do all the extra credit, do the review problems--get an A!

The professor's teaching strategy is to give out packets of his notes and go through them in class. While many find this useful, he leaves out information from the textbook that he expects you to know. And even if you do read the textbook, you will always find topics that were never covered in lectures or in the textbook on the exams.

His course structure is superb. He makes you work for it, but you'll learn the material (which is conceptually hard) very well because of the sapling extra credit, weekly quiz, well-structured recitation combination. A a very enthusiastic lecturer, though meanders a bit. And is incredibly helpful if you're willing to see him. P.S. He doesn't age.

Dr.V is a tough teacher, but he genuinely cares about his students. His tests can be confusing but you get used to it. Go to his office hours, and take advantage of the MANY extra credit opportunities he provides (15 points).

Lenny is a great professor, by far my favorite of my first year. He is very personable and gives very thorough explanations. He expects a lot from his students, but I learned so much despite the fact it was an intro to chem course and I had already taken chem classes in high school. Lenny's strongest attribute is he really cares about students.

He doesn't care to help. I had never taken chemistry and had a TA who could not explain things. I'm happy I passed, and as a first year theres no way Im dropping my dream of being a doctor due to someone's inability to teach.

Quirky talkaive professor who makes his tests ridiculously hard. Epects you to keep up with a lot of work that doesnt exactly help on tests because he includes random questions that werent covered in lecture or on assignments
His class is a joke. He skips material then tests you on it. He doesn't regrade even though it's in the syllabus and is never available for office hours.

Very rude, nasty, disrepectful person. Will talk down to you, like you a child, and doesnt treat you like your an adult. Thinks he knows it all, and is always right. Your grade with him as a professor will suffer, even if you spend 40+ hours studying a week! Don't take his class.

Took Chem I with him, and as a second year engineering major, expected it to be a breeze.  Let me assure you, he doesn't make it easy.  It is MANDATORY that you do the readings, pay attention in class, take good notes if you want to do well in class.  The most challenging final I've ever taken in my life and I studied for weeks in advance.

Lenny taught Chem 1 for the first time this year, and everyone died as a result. I know of so many people who are dropping their dreams of being a doctor because of his class. He does not explain things well at all and expects everyone to be some of type of chemistry genius. He finds his quizzes and exams somewhere in hell.

Vuocolo is amazing!! He's hilarious during lecture and office hours. He tends to talk a lot, but after awhile you become genuinely interested in what he is saying. And if you really like him, you can see him in the gym at midnight every night lol.
He talks *so much*. Often to himself. Often to a student-victim who can't escape. He's nice, though (albeit a little unclear).
He basically talks to himself during lectures.  He's so unhelpful and his final was hell. He uses the same tests and homeworks every year.

Took Chem II with him. He essentially expected everyone in the class was a chem major (I needed to take three science classes to fulfill a requirement, and I picked this as one of them). It was pretty challenging as a non-major, and he didn't really do much to help.
Doesn't teach so much as say 'here is your assignment. Figure it out yourself' for chemistry prof. com. class. Professor is friendly, but also passive-aggressive so watch out!
He is a really nice person and is essentially a good professor. The class is really difficult so you do need to study a lot but if you put in the time then you can understand the material.
Keep up with practice problem sets and pay attention in class. Take good notes and go to his office hours and you'll be fine. Read before and/or after class too.
Good
Took Intro Chem Lab.  He explained everything well, and even gave extra credit for drawings in your lab notebook (of experimental setup, etc.)

Good and very friendly teacher, but a really boring class with moderately difficult quizzes/exams. Too much "busy work" as homework, and homework is due every class. Take a different science class to fill your requirements if you can.
Good class, would recommend

Lectures are dense and packed with information, but they're very insightful and high quality. Leoni will challenge you to your limits, but he's also very helpful and will help you succeed. He's also very reasonable on homework and exams. The course is very hard but very rewarding. Definitely recommend if you have the mathematical maturity

Pretty decent lecturer though he sort of tends to spaz out on the board. However he is very willing to help. Mostly I find problem with the class structure. The full grade is based on about 4 different things. Merciless grading. Biweekly quizzes were terrible -- I didn't learn anything and only crammed page after page of computation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The course is inspirational, but lectures are pretty fast-paced, and biweekly quizzes can sometimes be overwhelming if you have other deadlines/midterms coming along. I ground through the course and somehow regretted it.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretty good professor, makes you think very deeply about analysis. For this particular course, his syllabus diverged wildly from the course description, which was rather frustrating. I felt that I learned the content on his syllabus very well but that I missed out on what I expected to learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: His class is not easy. A lot new information comes in one lecture. Homework also requires deep thinking and reasoning. He sets high standard for this class and pushes the students to their limits. I learned good math in this class and spent a great time doing the biweekly homework.

He truly knows what he's talking about. His homeworks and exams are quite tough, but he teaches you to the point where the material gets very engaging. Really defines the true meaning of an honors level math class.

This class is nothing like AP Stats. The professor talks a mile a minute (barely leaving her w/ time 2 take a breath) and overcomplicates everything, so most students disengage. Her high-pitched voice can feel like nails on a chalkboard. There are 2 midterms and a final; she gives past exams that help. Homework is manageable once you learn R. 70207
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pay attention to lectures and homework, make sure to attend class as well as there's often supplemental tips that can really help with understanding the material, especially since there's no textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Good prof who teaches in a straightforward way. Attendance is 10% of the grade, and if you show up to class the exams are not difficult. The problems aren't tricky and as long as you know the concepts you'll do good on the exams. They give lots of partial credit and grade the exams pretty generously. A is achievable and the professor is very kind.
Attend classes and take advantage of the practice material within recitations!
Avoid if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Lessem was a great teacher! Her exams are easy as long as you take notes, do the homework, and do the practice exams. She's also very willing to help during office hours, if you ever have questions on any of the material.
Kosbie genuinely cares about his students. Class was great, definitely got me back into compsci after falling into the vibe code trap. Learned a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: prolly top 3 classes i have ever taken. everything kosbie does is for a reason and he is goated lecturer. class is like hard labor tho, but thats what it takes to learn programming

Professor Kosbie truly cares about his students. To him, 112 is more than a programming crash course; as he often muses, "it's a mountain," and though you're gonna climb, the view will be worth it at the top. He supports his students every step of the way, and you will exit the course having grown a lot if you care just as much. Hard and worth it.
He's an awesome professor for my first cs course at cmu. Definitely recommend. His lore drops are also insane.
Fantastic Experience. Professor Kosbie is the most engaged instructor I have ever witnessed.
He cared about his students' health
amazing. I learned so much in his class.

You'll get a lot out of lectures if you read the notes beforehand. The issue is that the class is so rigidly structured that it sometimes seems like you're trying to keep up with the trillion requirements rather than actually learning anything. I didn't think being too organized was a thing until now. You can know it all and still fail.

Disregard anyone who says stuff like “oh no he cold calls me and doesn't want me to be on my phone.” Yes, he does, pay attention in lecture. The main issue I have with his 112 is how unreasonably strict it is. He told me I could start my term project early to clear up my finals week then decided I would have to put in the 20 hours that week anyway.
Great professor, gives useful advice occasionally in lectures and previews of future courses towards the end of the semester. Gives excellent help with assignments. The GOAT of 112
W mans, 10 / 10 guy
A lot of work, grading is extremely harsh for no reason, midterms cooked me

I once walked around 20 minutes in snowy and icy conditions to get to lecture. Because of the conditions, maybe 50% of the class showed up. 

Kosbie then wasted at least half of the lecture reprimanding those who missed class and further emphasized the importance of attendance -- seems a bit ironic. A very egotistical man. Great course, though.

Probably a great teacher in a small-group setting, but something about the lecture hall seems to make him think he's a stand-up comedian and self help coach. Talks mostly to/at freshmen and has the humor of a primary schooler who just learned profanity; can't really recommend taking the class if you're any older than that.

If you're someone who suffers from anxiety, DO NOT take this class with this man. He tends to cold-call and make fun of students for not showing reaction to his cold calling. Gives a lot of work and does more talking than teaching. If your sitting close to him, get ready for more cold calling and his finger in your face.
Hands down the best professor I've had. Kosbie created this course and teaches it extremely well.

Kosbie is the GOAT. This dude cares about your learning. He is interesting, and has a lot of insight on life. I really appreciate the time I spent with him in lectures, and I wouldn't hesitate to say his class had a big positive impact on my life. Wish I could take it again.

I think if you get pass his exterior, he really cares about his students. He is passionate and I recommend his online lectures. I think my biggest criticism is in his lectures he sometimes becomes too distracted; we miss time learning. As well as towards the end with recursion he becomes too babying with the problems where before he was quicker.

Honestly this class is quite bimodal. I found it quite easy and I wouldn't say I'm a genius in CS by any means. I have a bunch of friends who found it to be easy as well just time consuming especially largely the term project. However, there are others who struggled quite a bit.use your resources, Piazza, OH and past papers to prepare for midterms.

This guy has a cult following simply because he's egotistical and always goes on tangents about his own achievements. Basically assumes that everyone cheats and is extremely paranoid about students. 

I'm not even bitter because I didn't do well. The class was an easy A because of my prior experience. But I still hated the class because of Kosbie.
A great intro to CS

Constant tangents during lecture, brash attitude, doesn't care about students. Very arrogant and jumps to conclusions without any evidence but his "experience" and basically assumes that everyone is cheating until proven innocent. It is easier to take a grade hit than waste time fighting in his ballpark with his goons. 112 and 122 are the worst.
Every time I walked by a bus on the way to school I hoped it would hit me so that I didnt make it to his class.

Kosbie's a great professor, he's knowledgable and gives humorous lectures. The majority of assignments are solo and he is tough on academic integrity, but he's always open to helping students outside of class. The class is hard, but make good use of resources available to you. Highly recommend him

Fantastic lecturer, even over Zoom. Pushed me to change how I think about problem solving, and helped me grow into a better person. Very thankful for the time I got to spend with him. Excellent person to go to for advice during OH!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The course is vigorous but he's the BEST prof.

Incredibly mean person who disrespects students almost every lecture. Constantly brags about working at Microsoft and his lawyer wife, probably to cover up the fact that he is so insecure and a horrible person.

The man is very sarcastic and a little cocky, but I think it's warranted because he's literally a genius. Though the lecture notes are online, he goes over them very well and answers questions very clearly. However, he talks about the class average/median a lot and it comes off as harsh especially to those who were below that :/
He's a bad person who disrespects students constantly.

I have to admit Kosbie is perfect as a course instructor. However, he is bad as a man. He did not show any respect and talked sarcastically when he wronged me for cheating. Though I finally showed my innocence to Mark and persuaded him and received 98 out of 100 for the course at the end, what Kosbie did hurt me badly.

Kosbie talks about his wife during class, talks about how he worked in Microsoft and Apple and got welalthy because of it. Literally, his classes are kind of useless for that reason. I hope he realizes no one cares about his professional careeer and his family. I go to classroom to learn CS, not to hear him talk about this.

David Kosbie is a great professor. I never enjoyed programming that much but with 15112 programming is different. He makes you learn a lot and work hard but it is worth it. Highly recommend taking 15-112 with Kosbie

I can't say I learned much in class- he just goes over notes that are online. Anyways, class is so much fun because of him and he really cares about his students and tells awesome stories. I benefited so much from the course and got a ton better at programming and made a great TP.

Kosbie is definitely the BEST teacher I have ever met. He cares a lot about every single student in the class. And he offered lots of resources to help students. Definitely take his 15112 if you are a CMU student.
The most fantastic professor I could have imagined. He elevates the class like it's an Eminem concert.
Amazing class, Professor Kosbie really cares about his students and gives so many chances for you to get help when you need it.
I love this professor!

Best class i have ever taken. You can see he along with TAs, have invested a tremendous amount of time in course materials. Yes, you might be stressed by the homework, but reach out for help when you need. There're a lot of TAs who are ready to help you. All you need is a heart that is eager for knowledge. Be the change you want to see in the world
Hard class, but you will learn tons. Expect at least 20 hours per week of work.
He is super awesome! I learned not only how to do programming but also how to become a great person!

He is a professional and I learned a lot from the class. But he does not really care about student's stress, does not provide enough help when it is really needed, and has unreasonable distrust on students' academic integrity.
Best professor I've ever had!

Sure, the class will make you stressed, but you know deep down that this is a good, productive stress that will turn you into a stronger and better person, which, at the end of the day, is all you could ever want from a class, isn't it?
Says this course is his child and yet class time is used to do everything but cover the material we need to know... K

Kosbie is a phenomenal professor.  He's super understanding, and really puts the students first.  The course is also really fun.  Be prepared to work a LOT, and your weekends will be absolutely shot, but you'll learn a lot and if you do the work it's a fairly easy class.
Best.

Kosbie is probably one of the best professors you will ever get. If it wasn't for him, I wouldn't have been kickstarted in programming so well. Heck, I didn't even want to do programming before I came, but after 15-112 I am considering double majoring or minoring in CS alongside ECE. Take advantage of this class and learn as much as you can.

I think his class time could definitely be used more productively. The basis of a flipped classroom is that it offers class time to reinforce material, and that has not happened as much as I would have hoped so far. Otherwise, great class, offers you every possible chance to succeed and puts his heart into it.
over rated at best, good intentions and a stand up guy with great class structure but his philosophy / inspirational lectures only cause students to be more stressed or overwhelmed
Very engaging and incredible professor. Love his lectures.
Incredible. Take this class, you won't regret it
Best professor ever
Very inspiring. Favorite professor so far

David Kosbie is among the Top 3 Professors at CMU. His lectures are legendary and will teach you everything that you need to know. The course does give extremely challenging homework and it will push you. He is not an easy grading professor but you will learn so much. 1010 Would take again.

15-112 is the most challenging course I've ever taken in my academic career, but it is also my favorite (despite being a Business major!). Professor Kosbie challenges you to excel not only as a programmer, but as a human being. His passion for living is infectious. By the end of this course, you will emerge with a new view on life beyond academia.
The best class I've taken!

A lot of people find 15-112 a very difficult class. I did not find it too difficult, and I am a non-major, but it is time consuming. However it is fun, so I rather do the homework for this class than for any other class. Honestly this is my favorite class that I have ever taken. Prof. Kosbie wants you to work hard, but definitely not to overreach.

At first, I thought the same thing as everybody else: great teacher, good sense of humor, great class, that kind of thing. Then I came to him for help. Instead of giving me helpful feedback, he just shamed me for having social anxiety, and I can't see him the same way again. I hope I never have to take one of his classes again.

David Kosbie is hands down the greatest teacher I have had in my entire education. 15-115 -- Fundamentals of Programming and Computer Science -- has been the most rewarding, enriching, inspirational, character building, and difficult course i've taken in my life. If my entire time at CMU was to take this course, it will have been worth it.
hes awesome
The most helpful professor imaginable. Makes everything about the class interesting.
He is not even a good CS professor. He is just simply the best.
He is the BEST professor I've ever met. I enjoyed every moment in his class! I even want to fail the class and take it again! Just TOO MUCH FUN!

Incredible professor, incredible person. 15-112 is a very difficult course but having such a great professor makes it very manageable. He not only knows his subject inside and out, but he is 100% committed to helping students succeed and is very helpful for students who seek his assistance. Highly recommended.

Kosbie is an amazing guy. I went in with a bit of background in programming (not python though) and still got whacked HARD. He will drive you really hard, but he'll offer you all the resources available to make sure you make it through - only if you work for it. The defining course in my CMU education so far... Carpe Diem!

Took this class a few years ago.  Kosbie is a good teacher who shows his passion for CS.  His class is hard relative to other sections but this is good preparation for upper level classes which can get very difficult.
Best professor ever! Entertaining, an amazing lecturer, and passionate about the students' learning.

Kosbie's awesome! You learn tons in his class. He does go reasonably fast and also talks about life, but it's all worth it; you learn so much in his class! Just make sure you're on top of things, and don't fall asleep in class!

By far the best professor I have this semester! The class will kick you in the butt, but Kosbie is such a great teacher it's all worth it.  Taking advantage of his and the CAs availability makes learning all the programming feasible and decently fun.  Carpe diem!!
Reasonable amount of work, but he does not go over material very thoroughly in class.  Class notes are not all that helpful

THE BEST PROFESSOR! If you pay attention in class, you will learn A LOT! All necessary notes are online. Is willing to do anything for his students and will stay at office hours until EVERY student is fully helped. I took his class a year and a half ago, but he still asks me how I am doing. Students are Professor Kosbie's top priority.

This class is simply amazing. Kosbie is a great professor and passionate teacher. He will make sure that each student is coping and keeping up good work with the course material. he fuels imagination and teaches not only CS, but also about life. A must take course in CMU. Carpe Diem!
Take 112 if you can. It's one of those courses you're missing out on if you don't. Kosbie is a fantastic lecturer and a reasonable person. But, NEVER BE LATE TO A MEETING WITH HIM. EVER.
Kosbie is awesome. The course demands a great deal of hard work, but it's ultimately very, very, very rewarding. I learned so much in such a small span of time.

Kosbie is by far the most passionate, dedicated, helpful and amazing person I've come across. Expect a lot of work, but don't fret because there's more help than you can imagine. I wish he taught all my future CS courses! Also, he's super-accessible and does everything he can to make sure we do well. Carpe Diem!!

You want this man as your professor!  He taught me so much about programming in one semester, yet I learned more about life than I had in the 18 years previous.  He will accept nothing but your best, but don't let that scare you.  Consider it a breath of fresh air.  TAKE THIS CLASS.  (Oh, and dont sleep in class).  Carpe Diem Kosbie!             42

Oops meant to choose 5s.Kosbie is the best. He is SO good at teaching, so clear in explaining, so invested in the course, so passionate about programming and even just about living life in general, so approachable and accessible outside of class, so wonderful, so amazing... oh man I wish he would teach all the rest of my future programming courses.

"If you do everything Kosbie tells you to do, you will be a genius": John Mackey.  Can't say it better myself. Amazing professor who gives 110% to his class.  He will inspire you to do things you never thought possible.  Lots of work involved (expect 20+ hours weekly), but Kosbie provides so much suppor that nothing is impossible.  Take this class.

Amazing professor, in all regards. The class difficulty and homework levels take some time to get used to straight out of high school, but you can tell Kosbie really wants you to do well. I struggled during the year, but he was willing to sit down and help me out, even with nonCS stuff. Definitely take this class.
Awesome person!!

Amazing teacher. Really has the passion for everyone to do well if they put in the effort. Class definitely kicks your butt. Stay on top of it, couldn't have done it without Kosbie. He definitely keeps you awake in class.
You want this man as your teacher.

David Kosbie is inspiring, amazing, challenging, and so much more. He's not just a professor, he's a mentor, and he teaches his students so much about life. For lecture in range(len(course)), there were hundreds of stories. I'm blessed to have been able to take his class. A most rewarding experience for any freshman--you won't regret it.
SIMPLY AMAZING

He is simply incredible. His explanations of complex ideas to complete n00bs are astounding in their simplicity and effectiveness. He assigns a LOT of work, but it is all helpful and even fun. He has an excellent rapport with the class and has time for each and every student.
He is amazing, I wish he taught my math class. He used to be a high school teacher so he relates very well. Very eccentric and amusing. DO NOT FALL ASLEEP IN HIS CLASS!
He is amazing, I wish he taught my math class. He used to be a high school teacher so he relates very well. Very eccentric and amusing. DO NOT FALL ASLEEP IN HIS CLASS!

I've only been in his class for two weeks and have fallen in love with the material. His passion for programming and computer science resonates through the room each time he gives a lecture. It is not an easy class but Kosbie is very helpful. Carpe diem Kosbie, you're an integral member of the CMU community. Keep up the good work!
He's fantastic!

An amazing teacher. He's passionate about programming. He tries to make it fun and interesting challenging those who need a challenge and reaching out to help those in neef. The class is entirely doable if you have some motivation.  The homework can be a little time consuming and challenging. Same with the midterms. The final is easy.
This man gives Introduction to programming a heart. I would recommend everybody to take his class. He demands much effort, but is extremely serious in making sure you get it.

Kosbie is a good professor who's passionate about his work and gives clear explanations of every concept covered. However, his lectures go beyond the scope of the course and his homework requires a lot of time. His midterm exams are relatively easy if you understand the material, and his final exam is extremely easy if you've survived the semester.

Kosbie teaches through experience He does indeed give some intense homeworks that demand 20+ hours of work a week, but in the end the assignments become 2nd nature. Also, he's always there to give the whizz kids challenging problems and is always there for the those struggling to keep up. Always accessible and has tons of well trained TA's.

If you're interested in programming go for Kosbie. There's no one better at explaining and he's by far one of the most helpful and accessible profs here at CMU. But he does give more homework than the other professors and he covers more material so be ready. For those taking this class for "fun" should probably reconsider taking his lecture.

I personally had zero programming experience and was nervous about the subject. But now that the course is wrapping up, I can honestly say that I know how to program. Always entertaining. Good teacher.

I have no interest in programming but am taking it because it is a requirement of CIT. Kosbie is nice and funny, generally a really chill person but seriously STAY AWAY from his class if u have no genuine interest in programming in the future. Compared to all of the other 15-110 his is SO much HW and Lab work.
Great Prof, but his class is really difficult for people who don't know any programming. In my opinion he is only suitable for CS students, other students should try to avoid him.

Don't get Kosbie under any circumstances, unless you LOOOOOVE programming, and want 30+ hour programming assignments every single week.  Kosbie is far more difficult and time consuming than any other 15-110 teacher, he teaches far beyond the difficulty of the final, and grades quite harshly.  Stay away at all costs, unless you want to have no life.

Excellent teacher, yet difficult homework. Expect to spend a lot of time doing work, but his lectures are fun and make sense. Once you finish this class, I guarantee you will know a lot about programming and will be ready for 111. One of, if not the best 15100 programming teacher.
Good teacher and very passionate. What he teaches goes way beyond the scope of the course, but teaches the information well. Tough and tedious HW but you WILL learn a lot in this class! -15100
Amazingly good professor. Superb command of language. Are you ready to freaking fly? This writer of BRID will have your ideas multiplying as she slashes and burns her own writing into the sky.

Lauren was a great professor! Very kind and caring. She values participation a lot, and she is great about providing feedback to everyone for all assignments. It's clear she genuinely wants you to succeed
Great class
It's not an easy class, but Lauren is awesome!

Manfred Paulini is literally the greatest professor I've ever had. He starts every lecture with inspirational quotes, and he really cares about teaching physics. He's one of the few professors that I think values teaching elementary aspects of his subject. The class itself is hard, and you have to put a lot of work in to get a decent grade.

Can be entertaining at times. His lectures do not demonstrate the scope of information we should know, especially when it comes to exams. There is a lot of learning you will have to do on your own time. Recitations/homework are nothing like lectures and are much more difficult than what is taught. He also grades very harshly with strict rubrics.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
THE BEST PROFESSOR!!!! 
Make me rotate and heart warm warm.
(it's actually 33104, but there's no option for 104)
Paulini is a really great guy, but he is harsh when it comes to giving assignments, pop quizzes, and exams. Extremely difficult class

He is a great person- warm, funny. But, this was the most stress-heavy class I have ever taken. Online quizzes Monday, Wednesday, Friday, 40 point pop quizzes that are impossible during class. He has 100 point exams that he himself say we deserve more time... but does not give us that extra time... impossible grader... basically my gpa is screwed

Overall, I like Paulini, but this class is very difficult for those who have no Physics background. He's also not very helpful in office hours, but he's a great lecturer. Be sure to do the readings and get a tutor early on.
Does a good job covering the material (works out derivations a bit further than the book).  Great job interjecting humor in the middle of fairly complex physics to keep student's attention.
Receives many highly critical reviews here and always has them removed, so the ranking and results are skewed.

Melissa has awesome energy and will stretch your brain. Be sure to approach her in person if you have more to say  add  ask, because she's an encyclopedia and she values student participation. Really supportive, and she cares about familiarizing herself with her students.

I have this problem where I sleep uncontrollably during lecture classes, but Melissa EFFORTLESSLY kept me engaged. I don't know how she has so much energy, but it makes you mad excited about contemporary art. Excellent organization. A gem of a professor.
Best teacher ever.
Take the class no matter what it is, it will be fun. You cant understand Melissa unless you are in her presence.
Melissa Rocks!
Take her class!
No Comments
This professor is chill. However, she gives way too much work for a class that doesn't really teach you a lot.

Maddy was a great professor and I really enjoyed my time in this mini-class. There definitely was a lot of work, but it was manageable and was not hard as long as you stayed on top of it. I would recommend her 10/10 times.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: For this course, we only had to write a proposal on a specific issue of our own choice. The instructor was really chill and does not grade harshly. Overall, I would def recommend this class.

One of the nicest professors you'll ever meet, he'll like you if you ask him about his amazing daughters (I feel like I'm part of the family). He mutters a lot and goes on tangents, but overall if you do the additional readings of the textbook (which aren't required) you will be more than okay!!!

Prof. Washburn is adorable, kind, and genuinely cares about his students a lot. He's not an incredible lecturer, but he's very nice and the class is fair. Orgo 1 was an easy class if you put in the work to do all of the practice problems (as the exams were replicates of the practice problems and problem sets). Study hard and you will be fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: The class was scheduled to be 2 hours long, but about half the time we ended early. Lectures were very dense and sometimes hard to follow. Reading the textbook helped a lot. The problem sets were not too bad. Getting an A was not too bad because an A is 85%.

Washburn is a nice guy, and he is trying very hard. But there are definitely ways he can be more effective at teaching. First off, he doesn't follow the textbook, which is frustrating. I almost always have to read the book since I come out of his lectures more confused than before. READ the book, DO the homework, ASK questions, you will survive...

Read read read read read read your textbook. It's not assigned, but read. Went from a D- in this class (lol) to a highhhh B due to my sacred textbook and matching the materials in textbook to what was taught in lecture. Not an easy class but it is definitely do-able. Don't freak out when lectures sound like foreign language to you, you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: He's an adorable, quirky guy. You can despise him at times just because of the nature of the difficulty of the material and how lectures can go way over your head in terms of understanding, but reach out to him about anything, and he's always ready to get to know you better and help you out.

Currently in his Honors Chemistry course; Washburn is a great guy, a tad absent minded at times, but a really good person to know in the department. He is always engaging and knows his stuff. My TA for the class was super awesome as well and I really felt like I learned so much in such a short time. Definitely not the easiest class though!!
Had a great TA, also has easy access to notes, since he posts them on BlackBoard every day. He's pretty easy, for the most part and likes to go off on tangents. But great guy!

He's a very good professor and extremely helpful. The problem sets are easy if the right person grades them. The tests are difficult, so studying is a must. Make flash cards early in the class, they are so helpful. Also, use the book it's amazing! Oh, and check out his nice butt lol.

Lecture is extremely tied to the textbook. I would have been better off if I figured this out sooner! Problem sets are fairly easy, but tests are extremely hard in comparison. Washburn knows what he is talking about, but I feel he gets a carried away with seemingly superfluous material.
Great teacher overall. Tests are a bit hard; problem sets are easier. As long as you go to class often and take notes, the textbook can be used just for reference and review.
No Comments
Very strict.
Hard on zoom but great teacher
I love this class
She was great. I took her class over zoom and it was great.

The material in this class is not useful at all. I worked in Finance Industry before and her material is not useful in industry at all. And the course is very poorly designed. Her slides have lots of grammar mistakes and it is very unorganized. Her teaching style is talking nonsense everyday. Please avoid taking her class if you can.
Her slides are the most useful thing in this class even it is poorly written. I suggest everyone just copy the cheat sheet during class and please don't listen to her nonsense.
Annoying. Poor slides.
She is talking nonsense in class. And constantly focus on if you are discussing with classmates or using cellphones.

Laughed when I saw how consistent the ratings were over such a long period of time. She is really not a good professor. All other reviews are accurate. In addition, she even makes really frequent grammatical mistakes in her assignments, making questions extremely ambiguous. Constantly harps on cell phone usage in class. Just... No.

This professor is absolutely a disgrace. She will fail you for tiny reasons, such as using phones and laptop during class. Meanwhile, her class is boring. She is talkative but nothing useful for true knowledge. If you can choose other session, dont pick her. Also, please rate hero zero in the final course evaluation so that she wont hurt others.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: boring class strange grading policy same materials and examples used in class for many years with no change to the material
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Have to be on-time. Have quizzes in every class. Miss two classes, you fail. Bring calculators to exams, you fail.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: There are a lot of homework and it has nothing to do with the exam. The class is boring and requires too much.
Lectures are poorly designed, grading is arbitrary
Lectures are confusing and not organized, homework is disproportionate to what is taught in class

Seems like a pretty nice person. Completely unreasonable and a bad teacher though. She just rambles off numbers and leaves a ton of ambiguity speckled throughout her instructions. When asked to clarify, she acts annoyed. Might be fun for someone with an acct background though.
I like her class. She makes theories and concepts easier to understand. Some professors just do it the opposite way.

Her class is really interesting and she gives many examples to make concepts easier and simpler to understand. She always keeps students updated with the latest financial news. Her grading is strict, but in a way also fair. She is a professor of principle, not like those who give easy marks . You reap what you sow in her class.
She does not know anything about teaching. The university will be better if she leaves. She is not organized. She is childish. She gives assignments that are way beyond what she gives in class.

I learned a lot in this class even though I didn't think I would find this very interesting. I have taken other accounting classes and this was presented in a much more practical way.  The assignments were were interesting and she was very approachable especially during her office hours.

Pastor's asinine policy of -4 off the top of everything frustrates me to no end. She is a good professor, but grading in the class often seems completely arbitrary. Her class is usually not extremely engaging, but she does have some decent stories mixed in.  All in all, I have more beef with her stupid grading policy than Pastor herself.

Gives no feedback because theres no homework only exams and spends way too much time on proofs rather than explaining why things are important or how to solve problems. Test cover concepts not covered in class.

He cares much more about his reputation than the well-being of the average student in his class. His lectures are less helpful and less engaging than reading the textbook. Class ramps up in difficulty over the course of the semester. Tests are more of a race to write things down than an evaluation of knowledge. Callous and loves trick questions.
Not great overall
Good thoughtful Professor. Though his lecture is not very clear, I like his personality. He makes me like matrix.
Spends every class going over proofs already in the book. His tests sometimes introduce concepts not previously covered.
Spends too much time going over proofs in class.
A terrible professor.

He asked if anyone has question in class, and I said I don't know how to do a hw problem.  His reply was "that's an statement, not a question" and then asked if anyone else has a question.  In his office hour, I asked 3 problems and the answer is always "it's straight foward, simple!  think about it yourself!"
good

My grandmother died on a Tuesday night.  He still made me take an exam the very next morning.  It's hard to concentrate on calculus with that going on.  I subsequently bombed the exam and thus failed the course.   Enough said.
If you EVER see this name and your not a grad Math major RUN AWAY!!!!!!
No Comments
Blah

He isn't a very good communicator, he chose to use a microphone in his lecture after a midterm faculty review. He knows all the material, its just hard to understand him some times, and he's kind of corny and goes off tangents. His TAs save his class.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

A special thank you to Professor Tanquary for making writing enjoyable and engaging! I absolutely loved taking 101 with her, she is super caring and is always willing to give extensions! The class was super easy with her, she sometimes did have high expectations but she was always clear with her rubric and gave amazing feedback!

Loved Professor Tanquary, she was super caring and considerate, I will agree that she can be a bit demanded with writing but she was very easy to get feedback from and is very flexible. 100% would take it again with her :) .

You cannot go wrong with Tanquary! She has high expectations at times but is super approachable. I asked her for extensions on two or three different assignments and she was more than willing to give them to me.

I loved having Ms. Tanquary as my professor. As long as you pay attention in class and stay up to date with the work, you will do well and have a great time in this class. She is super chill. Her lessons consist of sometimes a short lecture and then a lot of group discussions and group work. She is always there to help you.

Very interesting lectures and lecture notes and recordings made accessible through the canvas page. Tests and quizzes are difficult. Homework not graded, but really useful to study for exams. Lecture quizzes very helpful to ensure content is understood and help your grade also.

Awesome professor with great lectures. Was a bit slow and theory-heavy in the beginning but the payoff is so worth it. Many cool applications: Computer graphics, curve fitting, Markov matrices, and more. The material is very cumulative; the material at the end of the course brings everything you learn together. Emphasis on proofs. Highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very organized and informative lectures. The expectations for quizzes/tests are clear. Highly accessible and personable during office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I took 21-241 with Offner and it was probably one of the most chill classes I've had at CMU. Lectures go over basically everything you need to know, and recitation and homework problems are instructive and reflect what he'll ask you on the test.
His office hours made me feel beyond dumb and the lectures go at a super-fast pace. Exams require a lot of memorization.

As long as you watch his lectures (or at least make sure you understand the lecture notes), 21-241 with him is pretty easy. His lectures are very thorough and laid out well. He is also open to questions during lecture/office hours. Overall, a very good class and professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Great guy, fascinating class. Note that assignment rubrics are very vague—during my semester, the two TAs thus varied greatly in their grading difficulty. Always cite your sources properly; although the rubric fails to mention it, you will lose points if not. Would still take again, Oppenheimer is an A+ lecturer and cares a lot about his students.

Awesome guy. He cares so much about his students and he is truly invested in your success for his class. This class is heavily reliant on the notes you take during lecture, so you get what you put into it. His lectures are really interesting anyways so just don't skip and you'll be fine. Also he speaks fast so you should type. Insanely good prof.
His tests are way too hard, but he is a decent prof and his lectures are kinda engaging. But for what's supposed to be an easy-A type intro class is just way too OD.

This was easily my favorite class this semester, Oppenheimer made every lecture so fun and engaging! He truly cares about all of his students and is there to help anyone who needs it. If you haven't taken a psych class before, the exams may be challenging. But If you took AP psych or some psych course in hs, this class will be pretty easy.
His exams are too hard
very nice guy, not really college level class
Makes the already interesting class fun
Best psych professor ever. Loves his job, and does lots of dumb jokes and bad puns in class. All around a cool guy.
really confused how could he wrote this book

Not sure how you can write the book but can't explain the concepts that are in the book... Not a very good professor. At one time Dave might have been at the top, but that's when computers were first invented. He is why too arrogant/confident in himself to listen to new ideas. He would tell Jobs/Gates that he could do it better... but he couldn't.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He is a prolific writer as can be noted by his amazing compendium of a textbook used all across the globe. However, he falls short by a good deal in lecturing and conveying information in a clear and focused manner. I believe it is because he has only enough time to focus enough on one. Perhaps he should devote himself to solely authoring textbooks
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Wrote the 15213 book, but fails to explain concepts in lecture.
He's a nice person but not really a good prof. Does't seem to care about students' learning. Boring lectures.
Nice guy but dry and uninvolved with his students.
The textbook for this class is very good (and was written by O'Halloran and Bryant).  Dave is a really nice guy
No Comments

I took 76-390 Style for my major requirement and I loved it! Professor Oddo cares a lot about his students. He helps his students improve their style of writing in an effective way.
Very caring and helpful professor. Best I've ever worked with!
Professor Oddo is an embarrassment and anti-American to his core.

reviews.comment: Does a very good job of teaching essential writing tips & craft

I was privileged to take Professor Tonguz's class during my academic journey. I can confidently say that his impact on my growth as a student and researcher has been truly remarkable. Dr.Tonguz is approachable and patient. He deeply invested in my personal growth. I am proud to have been influenced by such an exceptional scholar.
great professor
Wildly disorganized. Periods of time when there's no homework and periods of time when there's three assignments at once. Course content is plagiarized. Professor unwilling to help students.
good

reviews.comment: Just about the easiest math class ever. I would recommend going to class, but you can just as easily get notes from someone else. Don't buy the book - we did three problems out of it the whole semester. Honestly, if you don't get an A in this class, there's something wrong with you.
Stats is pretty simple to begin with, and Oded makes it even easier. Don't bother buying the textbook, he readily admits that he doesn't use it.
Very clear, very helpful!

Amazing teacher! You will learn everything you need to know...and nothing else. Odded cares a lot about students...when I took a makeup test, he sat down and went through the entire exam, telling me exactly which questions are hard and how to attack them. Also he is unintentionally funny sometimes, especially when he makes mistakes...
he loves to drink coffee

Great class...lectures are clear, to the point, you learn what you nee to learn for exam. His practice exams are excellent,..I was ready for the midterms. He has some handouts online, but he gives practice solutions in class. You must commit to go to class, but it is definitely worth it.
Awesome.  Oded is the man.
awesome!

AWESOME!!!
He's a CokeHead!
Oded is the man. He will change your life in 36-201.
he's the man.. take notes

just seeing his handwriting on the board left over from other classes this semester puts me to sleep

Maybe if he actually played modern games he'd recognize innovation when he saw it. His lectures are basically just him talking about his ‘glory days' while we sit there learning nothing useful.

Paolo is not at CMU to teach, but instead to disrupt his students growth and careers and distract them from what could help them succeed. Paolo is not interested in student learning but only focused on projecting his cynical world view onto others. A waste of time and money.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Definitely avoid. One of those teachers who spends all of their time bullying students and has no interest in supporting them or teaching them. I wasted $100k on Paolo and I'd like it back.

What can I say? It's less about learning in these classes and more about paying tuition to sit around. CMU's program is dangerously out of date and faculty like Paolo are focused on teaching software that is useless in industry contexts. Courses like this are more suited for high school.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Although Professor Pederecini is knowledgeable, their teaching style made the material hard to follow. Lectures lacked structure, key concepts were unclear, and explanations often relied on jargon without proper context. Overall, the course was frustrating due to poor communication and disorganized instruction.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I think Paolo is a great professor who gives good and honest feedback. He teaches many tools and programs that are intuitive and useful. It is also not difficult to get a good grade as long as you do the work. The projects and lectures all align and help you get used to the material and program quickly. A very funny and amazing professor!
Awesome professor, wish I had him sooner. Definitely keeping an eye out for the classes he teaches next semester!
Good Professor! 100% recommend the class!

Paolo is a real one. He gives good feedback and made learning 3D so easy. Honestly one of my fav professors. I can't wait to keep taking more classes with him next semester! Even the GCS recommends his classes. Funny man, and he is the only reason Ik Substance Painter.

Paolo is such an amazing and chill professor. I really like the vibe of the course and it's one of the very few art courses at CMU where the 3 hour doesn't feel like a drag. He doesn't overwhelm the course with full 3 hour lectures or critiques and understands that students also need to work on their own during class time.

I don't know why there are so many negative reviews against him. I liked him. He is sarcastic and honest. If he doesn't like something, he will say it. He even went out of his way to teach me a program for a personal project outside his normal class hours. Also, in my opinion. He didn't assign too much homework. Easy to get a good grade if you try

I had Paolo for 2 semesters and I would love to take another class with him. He does a lot of mini projects and makes sure you understand the software he is teaching. He is brutally honest with his crits, but wont really mark you down a grade as long as you try your best (or close to your best.)

Not bad as a lecturer, just kinda scatterbrained. Rating is dragged down by just how long it takes for anything to be returned. Review guides were released like a week before exams and homework for the latter half of the course wasn't returned until pretty much right before finals, so I had no idea what my grade in the course was, made it stressful

Honestly, he was a good lecturer, but there was also next to no support for learning/studyingo outside of class and it felt very individualized, although this is partially due to the class structure itself too. Had some really interesting lectures, though!
Greatest professor at CMU and would love to take another course with him.
Best professor of the semester
Lectures are great, though a little fast paced sometimes
Wes is a nice instructor. I enjoyed his lectures although they were pretty hard and fast-paced.

Great professor. Sparked my interest in discrete math. I then took combinatorial games with Wes. His enthusiasm and interest for these topics would make anyone interested in the material. Brilliant lecturer. Fair homework assignments.

Funny, interesting lecturer. Lectures can get quite heavy (taking notes for the entire class). He knows what he's talking about but the material can be confusing. The homework is fair, typically has one/two more difficult problem. Exams are fair but make sure you actually understand the material. Pop quizzes are kind of annoying.

Quinn is amazing. He is a funny, eccentric guy and he knows his stuff. He gives some challenging homework problems but tests are straight-forward: if you study, you should get an A. A nice, helpful, funny, and overall awesome professor. If you want or need to take a physics course and he's teaching it, TAKE IT. You won't regret it.
His teaching is really unclear for Physics II. He rambles on and on without really teaching core concepts. Try to take Schumacher if you can if you need to take Physics II for engineers.

The course is poorly structured, and it is evident that little thought went into its design. It is clear that he does not enjoy teaching this material. The lectures lack substance, reflected by how no one goes to them. His attitude toward students is simply unsuitable for a professor.
Good luck and get ready for a semester of self-learning.

He manages to write simple concepts that took other professor 5 minutes to explain into rediculously long and weird lecture notes that doesn't makes any sense. Also, he leaves so many typos in practice problem solutions that make people all confused.
lectures have almost nothing to do with homework, which has almost nothing to do with exams. exams are pretty easy but homework and lectures make no sense at all
Very interactive class, understanding professor, good environment. Not too challenging.

Don't take if you aren't interested in writing about art. I didn't realize what the class was when I signed up for it. Kinda boring, not that hard though. I didn't find her feedback on papers to be particularly insightful and sometimes it seems like she gets too caught up in political correctness/feminist views (I say this as a liberal).

This was my favorite class during my freshman fall. We got to write papers on cool things. I wrote an essay on the movie Edward Scissorhands. The professor is always available to help and super accommodating when students have outside commitments. She gives really good feedback on all assignments and wants students to do well.

really nice prof -- super understanding when it came to deadlines and fairly easy grader. made english interesting fs (final paper was on xmen) and def cares about her students -- willing to go over papers and give feedback when asked. go to office hours if you're struggling. easy easy A

I really enjoyed the guest lectures throughout the course, as they provided helpful real-world perspectives. Overall, Professor Abbott makes the lectures very easy to understand by explaining concepts clearly and at a good pace. The organization of the lectures and examples used in class made the content more engaging.

The material was comprehensive, and she goes out of her way to give multiple examples of how Biomed can be applied. The grading always felt fair, and she was always willing to set up time if I needed extra help. Overall, she really cares about her students and makes sure the class is engaging. One of the best profs freshman year.

Flashcards are really helpful for exams. Homeworks are pretty rough, but the TAs at office hours are great. Dr. Abbott genuinely cares about her students, and overall is just great. She also makes it so the person who does the best on exams is able to get out of the final.

Professor Strickland was funny, personable, and willing to adapt to his students. There was lots of reading, and of course writing, so be sure to prioritize the reading. Participation in class is mandatory. I would take him again!

Assigns an incredible amount of meaningless work; with the class three times a week, one can expect around an incredulous amount of work. Will not be wishing this man well. First-year writing class, but I spent more time on this than 15-112. Obnoxious prick.
Time-consuming and tedious.
Unnecessarily rude, no clear direction in his classes
Unnecessarily rude, mostly focuses his class on trying to make himself seem cool
The other 2 reviews on here could not be more accurate. This was the only section of interp available and I regret not waiting til spring.

So much busywork it's ridiculous. He's such a stickler about everything despite the fact that he's horrible at using Canvas and makes unclear guidelines. He's a harsh grader and he'll take away points for really specific criticism, and if you ask him about it he'll give some super vague answer about what he's looking for.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: My experience with Professor Strickland was extremely disappointing. The homework was overwhelming and felt like busywork. Instructions were unclear, and clarification responses were often unhelpful or absent. Strickland was an excessively harsh grader, giving zero credit for completed homework and hypercritical feedback. AVOID THIS CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Not a great class by any standards and difficult to talk to Suzie. Failure of the department to address ongoing concerns
Mean and angry all the time, she made me and other students cry.  I learned editing alright and using cameras but I also hate myself because of how mean she was.

Susie values hard work, so don't take her classes if you're coasting. In my experience, she bonded more obviously with certain students and picked on the weakest link in the class, so don't be that person. If you need help with software, hardware, editing, or finding artists whose work may be of interest to you, she is a wealth of information.

Suzie is definitely tough and she might throw some devastating blows to your ego, but she knows her material. If you work hard (especially outside of class), not only will you learn a damn lot, but she might even like you. And trust me, she is a great professor to have on your side.
Good Lord he is just the greatest.
The goat. And his drip is immaculate
He is so nice and helped me a lot. Always ready to answer my questions and respond to emails really quickly.
15112 is a well run class, especially considering its size. I have learned a lot, and found I never got overwhelmed by homework if I started it early. He cares for the students a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The class was really good overall. As someone with close to no CS experience, it was a great class to learn the fundamentals of programming.

Good professor, good course.
- CMU CS Academy is used for all homework.
- There is a main site for the course. Easy to find syllabus, schedule, office hours, etc.
- MAJOR FLAW: There is a rather stupid policy that does not allow laptops during recitations.

Lectures are nice and easy to follow. Very knowledgeable and funny. His axolotl is awesome.
One of the best prof, very good lecture, a very nice guy

One of the kindest professors at CMU. Motivated me to keep going with the course even when I wanted to give up. Set up a new system to help struggling students, even came to me for input. Alerted authorities when a racist man verbally abused students leaving Mellon Inst. and showed his support for us directly. Always available for his students
Great professor, very well structured course

Overall a good professor. Based on the way 15-112 is structured, I'm not sure that lecture is even necessary. I got bored sometimes during lecture since he talks kind of slow but was definitely helpful and available outside of class
Used a new method of testing for students and the class was going fine till then. Struggled to submit the quiz and gives me a zero. So unnecessary and messed with my grade.
One of the best professors I've had in my entire life. Super kind, quite funny, and a fantastic teacher all around! I
Amazing caring professor, he's really sweet

You can still learn a bunch, even if you've seen it all before. But make sure you study anyways, or else this class will go from "easy A" to "easy aaaaaaa I got a C!" I went in super cocky because I knew python and had a bad time until I learned my lesson. The good news is Taylor is the nicest prof you'll have and super kind (and interesting too).

He. Is. So. Caring. What kind of professor meets with you on short notice on a weekend just because he doesnt want you to stress about his class? Professor Taylor, thats who. Hard class but 167282/10 would recommend. Puts so much effort into this course its incredible.
Taylor is a great CS professor! He shows photos of his axolotl in class and it's excellent:)
He's really funny and his way of talking is very captivating
At first, his sarcasm may be off-putting but he means well and he is genuinely interested in helping you. Do the readings for the class and you should be fine.
He is really a very interesting professor. His humor and teaching technique kept me intrigued.

So it's a great thing she's not teaching 24-261 in fall of 2025 because if she did, those students would suffer as she's the most unclear lecturer ever and focuses more on her research than teaching, plus, she's passive aggressive and constantly dock people's points. avoid her
group projects are fine, but she does not explain stuff well nor goes in through detail. avoid her

this mechanics class is supposed to be a hands on learning class with lecture, but she makes the teaching very unclear at times! While she's nice (generally), she can be pretty harsh on grading with the units and the midterms so watch out!
She also doesn't explain a lot of stuff, so read the textbook and don't depend on her!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: She's okay. Her teaching style can be quite unclear at points. She also doesn't post lecture notes, so beware of that. Also, sometimes homework will unfortunately cover material that hasn't been covered yet in class, but you should be able to teach yourself through the textbook. Group projects are quite reasonable.
The way he grades students is not clear. He is a very difficult person to communicate with. It can take weeks for an answer or email. He is not inspirational and spreads hate on students.
Seems a bit intimidating at first but you will get used to it. Questioned my choice of athiesm, which seems blasphemous.
He gives everyone in the class the same grade, regardless of your personal efforts.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor is very entertaining through his condescending humor; however, he plays favorites and there is no real syllabus or final.  The syllabus informs that he grades based upon how he feels about your talent and about your ability.
lectures are very hand wavy

Professor Acar is an amazing lecturer and overall great at getting the content across. Diderot is a really easy platform that complements the course. The homeworks are difficult but you learn a lot by completing them.
He is tough.
Weirdest course policies. And overall bad lecturer made everything more complicated than it had to be. Also HW only 5% but takes hours and hours to do. Hated this class.
i would not recommend this professor at all. she's a very unhelpful person and poor at explaining stuff while being impatient and dismissive.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: very tough and cold and dismissive toward her students, she docks points for a lot of reasons and even threatens to give you a 0 on the midterm if you don't call her properly. she's not a good prof
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a teaching professor who's not good at teaching.

He is very kind and caring. He cares a lot of about his students and tries his best to make sure we understand everything. The exams can sometimes be hard to understand but he is great at preparing us for them. Best prof ever!
Extremely unprecise

Could tell that he really cared about the students. I learned a lot throughout the course. Don't understand some bad reviews on here, but my experiences were very positive. Would recommend this class and this professor.

Dr. Virtue meanders about introductory concepts, runs out of lecture time, and then rushes through the rest of the material, which is the actually important part. He seems to not organize his slides ahead of time and ends up rearranging them on the fly. His assignments are visibly rushed and grading often depends on minor details or technicalities.
he is a nice person, but the class is structured poorly

Extremely poor teacher, slides are extremely dense, shows up ill-prepared  to class sometimes. Tests are an absolute blood bath since he will take a minor detail from lecture and create a whole problem about it. Grading policy is not fair since everything is graded harshly and some questions are all or nothing.
He sometimes wrongly accuses people of cheating based on weird reasons. Although he did not make a report, the experience is still traumatizing.
He doesn't care about his students and is extremely disrespectful. All he did was try to catch people cheating, traumatize them (even if they didn't do anything wrong), and make examples out of them.

He is using a course from Berkley that is alright. However, the way he teaches is really poor. What got me to write this is a week ago I had a meeting asking for help with concepts after the midterm and he seemed to frame it as me being dumb for not understanding rather than his explanation being at fault. I feel basic respect is not reciprocated.
A very caring person. The slides were very well prepared and the lectures were quite straight-forward. I learned a lot in the class and made me want to pursue AI down the road.
Easily the best professor I have had at CMU. It is obvious that there is thought put into every component of the course

I wrote the last comment and I'd like to revise it but I can't figure out how. I was very stressed out during that semester so I wasn't my best acadmic self. and I think it might have been his first semester teaching this course. I am retaking it this semester and I still find part of the course material confusing with another professor.

He is a nice person and seems very available to students. However he glosses over more difficult concept (sort of goes around the concept itself and speeds up?) as if he himself doesnt understand them, and I find it hard to concentrate in his lectures. Theres also this participation poll so you have to sit in class so I ended up hating it.
Doesn't value learning of students
Poor professor  teacher

The TAs deserve almost as much credit as Von Ahn, since they write all of the assignments, but 251 is an amazing class.  Von Ahn is a funny, informative, and brilliant lecturer, and the class goes through an enormous amount of information in a short time.  You will suffer, but you will love it.
Totally awesome class and awesome professor. I switched majors straight away.
His lectures are both very entertaining and informative. He has a lot to teach and is very interesting. This particular class has enough work to kill you.

I dropped 15-251 and switched majors entirely. While this is partly because Luis is VERY HARD, it is mostly because CS wasn't for me. Luis challenges students and comes off a bit harsh, but is a very entertaining lecturer and a fascinating individual.
No Comments

251 was HARD as hell, but Luis made it enjoyable, at least. He does lots of fun things to get students' attention, but honestly, I didn't care about this course at all and got tired of him near the end. Brilliant guy though.
Good teacher, gives clear explainations of the concepts and always has humorous material to keep everyone awake (the crush script, the marshmellow launcher etc...)

The class itself isn't bad, but the professor makes it annoying. He also seems to hold little respect for the students he has, even blowing up on one over Piazza to accuse them of cheating, of all things. Kind of weird how much he promotes his Youtube channel. If you read the lecture notes on your own time, you'll learn the material.

Quizzes every week, wouldn't tell students what was on them although he said he would in the syllabus. Just overall not a very good lecturer, makes topics unclear and more confusing than they need to be. I'd recommend just sticking to the course textbook - it was how I taught myself the topics.
