I appreciate Dr. K, he spent hours to make youtube videos for us to get supplementary instruction on topics introduced in lecture. However, I don't recommend lecture as I never got anything out of them. If you follow his study guide, you should be very well prepared for the exams! I got an A, but I studied his hour long videos religiously to get it
Neve taken again
Not a hard class, but the course is definitely annoying. He made it annoying.

He is a nice guy and knows clearly what he is teaching. The workload is below the average compared to other introductory-level math courses I've taken. The only thing I don't like is the mandatory recitations and quizzes. I'd rather have tests to be more weighted. Everything else is fine.
everyweek has quiz and enforce students to participate the lecture by unknown quiz

Funny guy. Not a tough class- never went to lecture but got an A (though I did take AP Calc in highschool). He grades fairly and consistently in my experience. Tests are exactly like the study guides and his youtube videos, so as long as you are comfortable with the study guides and practice problems you should be fine.

He does lots of example problems in class. Quiz questions are often more difficult than in class (the homework is a good gauge for difficulty). 
He also has videos covering the material; definitely watch them before tests.
Go to lectures.
It's a great class.
The learning resources provided by the professor are great. They help grasp difficult concepts beyond the classroom.

I took Dr. Katugampola's calculus course as a freshman and I'm very much satisfied with his teaching style. He expects his students to learn the subject and provides lots of opportunities for improvement. I'm not a strong man when it comes to math. However, getting a B at the end was a great achievement depending where I was before. Thanks, Dr. K.

Dr. K. was the best calculus teacher I ever had. While I knew some of the materials before I never learned it that well before. He is very passionate about his teaching and loves to explain if you want to. He has his own jokes. 16/64 = 1/4 is favorite. This is the kind of professor I would like to learn from as he knows what he is doing. Thank you.
This course is tough and get teady

Coming into this class with no calc background was rough. The quizzes were often very difficult -  sometimes the averages were 30-40%. He posts a lot of the practice questions/supplemental material on YouTube which is helpful but the videos are quite long and not the most efficient way to study. Lectures were useful.

There were a lot of tests and homework in this class. The tests were a lot more difficult than expected and the grading criteria were very strict for them. You could get the right answer on a test problem but get a 0 if you did not show enough work.

Dr. K is the best professor. I might switch my major to math thanks to Dr. K. I watch his YouTube videos daily, and everyone else should too. Make sure to subscribe and leave a like as well. While Dr. K's teaching methods may not align with conventional pedagogical approaches, his commitment to academic rigor is evident.

Worst professor Ive ever had. Used the threat of harder makeup exams and no makeup quizzes under any circumstances to force sick student to either take the exams sick, or have a failing grade. In class lectures are unhelpful, resumed to answer questions in class and instead told students two watch his YouTube videos,  overall awful teacher
He is the type of professor who doesnt understand that you are taking other classes and that calc is not the only thing you have to be worrying about.
He does not teach at all, and overcomplicates the most basic topics

He is not a good teacher, only reason I ended with an A is because I learnt it all in high school. He also makes us watch his youtube videos that are hard to understand. His quizzes are by far much harder than the material and on top of that some classes get the quiz answers while others do not.

Dr. K is probably the best professor I have had in my life. He really likes what he is doing and encourage others to do the same. Math was not my favorite subject, but after learning from him everything changes. I'm even considering switching majors and wanting to be a math major. I know it is tough, but I love to dive into it. Thanks, Professor K
Nicest professor ever, super kind, super helpful. Would take any of his classes again.
Incredibly kind and approachable professor, willing to work with students as he wants to see them succeed

Genuinely loves materials science, his lectures are fun and engaging and he often gets excited when talking about topics he particularly likes which made the class extra fun.
He's super accommodating and friendly, always happy to explain a concept after class, and really cares about his students learning the material. Great professor :)

super passionate about MSE and cares more about the students learning than grades (meaning homework and test are relatively easy). recitations were optional and labs were super helpful in learning the content
He such a great guy. He really loves what he does and he genuinely cares about his students. He also answers emails super fast.

Sokalski is phenomenal professor who is passionate about helping you learn. He's very flexible with being able to teach you in a different way/with different examples if that's what you need to learn. The material can be intimidating at first, but never be afraid to ask questions and you'll get the hang of it in time for the midterm and final
Sokalski is engaging and is invested in student success. The lectures tend to get long, but the office hours are amazing.

Prof Sokalski really cares about your success and gives great lectures (with notes that he also posts online). The homework and exams are difficult, but the lab is pretty easy and his office hours are very helpful. Just a great guy all around.

lovely person and a great teacher. i've had many harp teachers over the years including across different countries and she is by far the best. she challenges you for sure, but it really helps you grow and i've never voluntarily practiced so much in my life. she makes me love the harp.
By far, the best harp and theory teacher in the world!
Unbelievable teacher; really one of the most fine in the field.

He is very knowledgeable and passionate about calculus, which shows in how often he derives formulas in lecture to explain where they come from. His midterms are moderately difficult but match the level of the final, so they prepare you well. He is empathetic and genuinely wants his students to succeed. If you put in the work, you should do well.

Very very easy grader, gives a lot of partial credit on every question. He wants to give you points. Loves mass center of mass problems. Definitely knows the material well and explains it well. Can be a little derivation heavy but if you just memorize the equations the class is pretty easy
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is just terrible avoid him please !!!
Very nice guy, but lectures barely help.

He's super funny, keeps the class environment light. Personally, I thought he did a great job at teaching the material. His tests are also very straight forward and there is a small curve on every midterm. He is also pretty generous at partial credit. He is honest about what he is looking for on the tests and very approachable outside of class.
Lessons are pretty confusing but he's an easy grader. Nice, not a lot of homework.

His lectures are borderline useless because he works way too fast. Recitations are the only thing that got me through this class. He also makes the tests and quizzes very difficult. Very nice guy but bad teacher. He does grade kindly and puts a slight curve at least.

He cares about his students getting the help they need and is very accessible by holding office hours immediately after class which were very helpful. VERY fair grader (next to no points off exams for small algebra mistakes). Easy to catch up in the class if you read the book. You will be prepared for the final taking his class.

Prof Wojtowytsch (at Pitt now) is ok. Clearly he's new to teaching and I think he knows that and tries to improve. Would teach concepts but not really how to apply them- need to go to recitation for that. Exams are ok, similar to the practices, and he seems to want to make them as fair as possible. Felt prepared enough for the department final.
He is in Texas A&M now. If you have to take his course, be ready to study by yourselves
Be ready to study by yourself
Boring and hard to understand. Most people don't even go. Nice guy, just hard to follow.

She's a great professor and you learn a lot from her class. She grades the drafts pretty harshly, but they are worth close to nothing as you can get multiple 4/10s and easily get an A. To revise, just fix everything she wrote in the feedback, it's all there for you. Do that and you get a garunteed A.

Do the work (max 30 mins a day) = A

This professor is rude and puts me in a bad mood. She also does not post (or tell you about) assignments on canvas until the day of or two days in advance, which make it impossible to work in advance. I don't think I learned anything from her class, it felt like a waste of my time.
Gives possibly too much feedback for the papers we have to write. She is quite caring, but you must participate in discussions in class in order to receive full credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: The other reviews of Professor Wright are correct, however I'd like to give her a little more credit. She is very curt and rude to students, especially if you are shy and don't like to speak up, but if you follow her directions to at T (which is incredibly easy, as she is incredibly clear about her expectations) you will most likely receive an A.
Just the worse
Constantly challenging the low bar of being a professor
Terrible professor, no point in taking the class. Simply wasting time for no reason and spending more time on it than my core classes.
Avoid at all costs
Bruh fr tho so bad
Absolute worst professor I've ever had, ever. Marks everything unreasonably, unreasonable feedback with no basis. Brags about her family all the time, has no intentions of actually helping students.
Prof Zhang is very patient and offers exta office hour upon need. Very nice experience.

Xu Zhang is the best professor you can possibly ask for. He is sooooo patient and good at explaining things you wish he taught all your classes. Also super caring and is always looking to give students some points back on homework or exams.

Professor Zhang is the best professor for 18220. He teaches very clearly and slowly. With him lectures are very understandable and homework is much more enjoyable to do. I really liked this class because of him, I hope he is given the chance to teach 18220 alone!

Professor Xu was always nice and was willing to answer any questions, even if it meant for her to check for answers outside of class. The tests and midterms often skewed in difficulty, some were a cakewalk, others were pretty hard. The end of the course starts was cramped in, so make sure to self study if necessary for the final exam.
Such an inspirational math professor. She will explain many abstract concepts in an understandable way. You will learn a lot from her teaching.

Great professor, one of my favorites. She's super nice and very accommodating. Lectures are very good, she's quite thorough and makes sure everything is understood. A good amount of homework - it was enough to learn/practice but not too time consuming. You can tell she really cares about her students.
Super patient. Very accessible (emails/OH).

Nice professor who has very helpful office hours. The homework can be somewhat time-consuming. Her lectures can be difficult to follow (very fast, and she also has a strong accent), resulting in a lot of extra textbook reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: She really cares about her students and is happy to help. She overcomplicates concepts in lectures. In examples, she skips a lot of steps which makes them hard to follow.
The difficulty level was average—not too hard—but it could have been a bit more challenging.

If you're familiar with the material, I highly recommend him. Class will be a breeze. I didn't have to come to lectures, slides were fine for hw/exams. But, if you're new to this stuff, avoid. Literally, first lecture he wrote out a bogus proof, and he's terrible with students' questions. His and his TAs' math is lacking, bad proofs.

Professor Adamchik remains as the worst computer science professor I have taken thus far. His lecture style is erratic, he shoots down questions, and the sheer disconnect between lecture content and homework/test content in terms of difficulty is not only wildly discouraging, but also incredibly unfair. Do not take this professor.
Difficult to understand the accent initially but you get used to it. He  makes you think during his discussion lectures and explains the concepts very well.

for this online class, it didn't really feel that she cared. there was no online lecture.  just homework assignments, quizzes tests etc. tough material that she could have made easier by providing videotaped lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: great

I took this class over the summer at CMU. His accent was a little difficult to understand at first, but he gave really good lectures and I definitely knew the material after I finished the course. In the end. I didn't get a good grade in the class, but, in retrospect, it was all due to the mistakes I made.
Adamchik is amazing

Great lecturer! Discusses good questions in class and is AMAZING during OH for clearing concepts. Would strongly recommend attending his OH if you don't understand something. DO NOT expect significant help in homework problems. Has a thick Russian accent so 10/10 would recommend sitting in front.

Once you can understand his accent (which took me about a week), he's easily one of the top 10 professors at CMU. I heard that 21127 is hard class, but not with Adamchik teaching.  You'll feel like you were born knowing the stuff he teaches by the time you're done with his class, because everything just makes such perfect sense when he explains it.

Really nice class - learnt a lot. Professor himself is decently tough, not an easy A, and has a bit of a Polish accent that can be sometimes hard to understand. If you sit near the front though, you should be fine, and should end up liking him. Has a sense of humor and is generally kind.
Took 15-121 and 15-211 with him. Data structures are way more up his alley than algorithms. Has a Russian accent but is totally understandable. Great sense of humor. Very friendly.
He is VERY bad at proofs and theory. He is totally AWESOME with applications and data structures. But seriously, he sucks at theory.
Knows what he's talking about.  Other posts are correct, he has a thick accent but is understandable

Thick Russian accent, but still comprehensible if you sit near the front. His explanations are sometimes shallow or hard to understand due to the accent, but that's also the nature of the course. Genuinely helpful when you need it.

He has a Russian accent, but speaks fluent English, and it's not hard to understand him. He's a pretty good lecturer but let the annoying TA kind of run the assignments. Posts lecture notes online and keeps up with posting grades.
Very friendly, laughs almost too much. Very entertaining and presents his examples well. No tolerance for fools, good for people who actually want to be there.
Totally awsome
No Comments
He is a really good teacher, also is really funny, laughs at his own jokes.

As others have said, his accent is think but I don't think it's incredibly hard to understand. If it's a big lecture hall, sit in front and he'll notice you. He usually engages only the first few rows as he teaches. Subject matter is very interesting; I feel like the people who will give him poor ratings don't care for the course much anyway.
His accent is hard to understand sometimes
Adamchik will work you, but he's a good man, and you'll know your stuff by the end of the term. It's critical to sit in the front and get to know him, though, if it's a large class.
Ok, so sometimes it is kinda hard to follow him (accent and all), but the man knows his stuff!
yeah, sure, he can't speak english. admit it though, you were entertained.
He rides spinners and does not stop

This class was HORRENDOUS. Unfortunately I had no choice of professor: Adamchik taught the CS section and that's the way it was. If you can possibly avoid it, don't take anything he teaches. It's not the difficulty: it's the muddled style of presenting and low intelligibility.
Gave good explainations of basic concepts
Hard to understand, too focused on GUIs
Very hard to understand, makes it tough to stay awake in lecture
A little hard to understand because of the russian accent, but mostly pretty good.

Pay attention and go to class, he's an amazingly brilliant guy.  If he sees you've been in class and knows you try hard, there's no way you can get a bad grade in this class.  Talk to him if you don't understand him, he's so nice!
Quite easy of a teacher. Not always clear on the programs, but great lecturer.
Passionate about subject. funny. i loved this guy!!!!  whoever gave him the bad rating sucks!
No Comments
probably the easiest 15-200 teacher
No Comments
No Comments

His lectures are easy to follow, but you definitely need to do way more preparation outside the lectures to do well on the exams. I recommend doing textbook problems. Considering the exams are 80% of your grade and multiple choice, they are pretty difficult especially the final. He does grade on a curve if necessary which is good.
Marking was super difficult — take Matrices with an easier professor. Learn the same content, but get a better grade.

I have the impression that I had to work so much more in his class than comparatively to other students in the same class with other professors. Not too bad though, it ended up giving me a way better understanding than I would otherwise, but I wish the markschemes weren't so punishing.
Does not answer questions, just reads off of lecture notes which were from the textbook.

Class was marked incredibly poorly, midterms would lose 50% of points on a question for a simple algebraic error. Markschemes were just garbage in general, and there was no curve on the class, with the exception of a 3% curve on the final. LMAO. For a trivial class, exams were too tough. Nice guy tho.

Easy A. I already took AP Stats so this intro level course was easy, but it was definitely way easier than my AP class in high school. Tests are not tough, but you have to know the material to get it done in the time allotted. Homeworks are once a week and take anywhere from 2 to 4 hours. Overall not bad.

Professor Cai is a very thorough teacher. She expects attendance, but is willing to accommodate students who have to miss class because of illness or internships. If you do not understand the material, she is very willing to work with you in office hours or at other times. An easy class if you do the HW and take notes.
Dedicated and really willing to help.  Answers questions well.  Gives a lot of opportunities for bonus credit.

Not the most amazing pure teacher, but he gets his point across respectfully. He is very intelligent, and if you ask questions or go to OH, he will explain everything perfectly. His type of teaching might not be for everyone, but the class itself is fairly easy.
Nice guy, incomprehensible at times as he loves to talk about theoretical chemistry but usually if you ask enough questions he answers them well enough to be able to understand what's going on

He is very relaxed and chill if you like that. He explains concepts, but usually its very confusing and I have to use REALCHEM to actually learn the material. The grading is tough, but there are opportunities to get your grade up. Overall I like him, hes sort of just average. Class is easy.
Pretty decent professor. I didn't get much out of lectures but the class is structured so that many resources and practice problems are available.
Great professor that is always willing to give helpful knowledge to students during lectures. Supportive and would recognize all the work students achieved. Would definitely take again.

Prof. Yaron is a nice professor, but not the most engaging of lecturers. However, he provides so many resources  for the class if you don't get anything out of lectures and he is very accessible. Overall, super nice and patient person, but not always the most clear lecturer.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Dr Yaron is a nice guy. He's knowledgeable about chemistry, but he is a boring lecturer. His voice is quite monotone, and if you're not interested in the subject, it will be extremely hard to pay attention. However, it can be done.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Kinda boring but he knows his stuff. Very nice and answers questions well. Overall recommend. Tests are straight forward, no surprises.
Dr. Yaron can be a little dull in his lectures, but he is always more than willing to answer any question during lecture.

He is a professor that knows his topics extremely thoroughly. Explains most of the concepts pretty clearly. Homework and tests are veeeery reflective of the problems we do in class. Tests are EASY. He gives random chemistry facts related to real life situation which makes it really interesting. Seems to not like the New England Patriots.

Yaron's lectures are a little dry but he gets the point across pretty well. He gives notes sheets with basic info on them so the class is very organized. There is a lot of homework in this class, but it relates well to the concepts. There are 3 midterms worth 100 points each, and one of them can be completely dropped or you can drop half the final.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY--Would take again

reviews.comment: Honestly, I never went to lecture and I'm sitting pretty with a high B. Yaron is extremely dry and not engaging, but don't expect the class to be a breeze if you don't put in the work. A good chemistry background and a good TA is necessary to understand the concepts taught because Yaron often has difficulty explaining basic concepts.

Professor Yaron is pretty clear cut. He explains the concepts well, and lets you know what he is looking for on the homework or on an exam. I think he is very clear and willing to help you if you ask for it. Great class! 10/10 would take again.. I hope I ace this class.

Sometimes he doesn't do the best job of explaining a new concept and has trouble rearticulating it when someone asks for clarification.  The homework assignments often have seemingly nothing to do with the textbook or lectures, and you have to specifically ask the TAs to learn how to do them.  Not an awful teacher, but not always the clearest.

Professor Yaron needs to be more confident! He understands the subject matter and why it matters well, but is too sheepish teaching it.   Also, doesn't do a great job integrating math into the content.
Lectures can be a bit dry, but it's a fairly straightforward class content-wise.

Tremendous professor. If you want to learn French, take her courses. Super interesting work, and she cares about student input a lot. If there's something you want to learn about, suggest it. Could not ask for a better French professor.

Great Professor, really funny, treats her students like adults. Her grading method of cumulating points for assignments was interesting, and really took the stress off of having to do individual assignments. I'd like to see future language classes do the same. The topic we were learning wasn't very interesting, but I still learned a lot.

Such a great teacher, made Elementary French a fun class (but not a super easy pass). Was actually really interested in getting us to learn French, and was overall a great teacher with a fun personality. Heavy workload with a textbook, workbook, and classwork, so be prepared for that.
Bonnie is wonderful and engaging! She is also very personable and kind. Hers was my favorite class.

She makes learning French Culture at 9.30 am very engaging. She's over the top in a fun way, and she's very much an awesome professor but do not get into her bad books. That being said, just make sure you do her work and participate in class because attendance and participation make up a significant portion  of the grade. Highly recommended!
good teacher and real nice! Fun in class.

Yao was better at teaching in Mandarin. Just kinda chatting about content was a good way to learn Chinese and a less good way to learn history. Her grading criteria were also clearer for both 82231 and 82232, whereas 82333 was more just 'did she vibe with your presentation'. I came in with a bunch of prior knowledge, so at least it was an easy A.

Not only an excellent professor, Yao Laoshi is also an incredibly kind person. Her course was extremely challenging and all classes are entirely in Chinese, but I learned a lot from her. She supported me through some difficult times and would worry about us if we were absent. She was so proud of me after our oral exam and gave me a hug!
She expects you to participate and practice speaking. There are weekly quizzes.
More than anything, she wants to see that you're interested in the class. Pay attention, ask questions and volunteer to read and you're guaranteed an A.

She is HOT! Very good looking woman! I couldnt concentrate. She is strict though, which makes her even hotter!!! The other students should be in an English class! NOT THEIR OWN LANGUAGE! Then again, if CMU had Korean, I'd take it for an EASY A toooooo. <><
No Comments

Yao is great,just do the work and show her you are learning something..you will get what u deserve..go to class though. the material isnt so easy coming from elementary.Many presentations, essays, strict attendence. And yes, too many losers looking for easy A. Losers who should be in an English class not their own language.but dept wont do anything
Great teacher and very understanding. Maybe it is the material or how the class is set up, but was not easy to learn in this class. too many losers looking for the easy A.

Dr. Rabin laid out his expectations very clearly at the beginning of the course. Come to lecture, do the problem sets, meet with TAs for homework and exam study, and you will be fine. Tests are open book and TAs basically tell you what's going to be on the test, and so will Dr. Rabin. People who don't follow his advice are the ones whining.

Why would someone like this want to be a teacher? He obviously hates people and is completely condescending. Try to avoid this guy, or hope that karma is coming for him soon. Worst prof I've ever had at CMU. You'll work really hard for a low grade and be completely demoralized in the process.

I can only guess he is a great researcher because he is a miserable human being who thinks he's a god and looks down on everyone. CMU needs to do something to get teachers like this out of the classroom and back into a lab somewhere. Teaching is obviously not the right job for him, he is truly mean, condescending and uncaring. Avoid at all costs.

Not at all understanding. Doesn't respect students, is extremely stuck up and not at all accommodating. Doesn't teach well and is not at all coherent when he speaks. I don't know why CMU still allows him to teach

Felt it wasn't taught in a conducive environment. The various aspects of the class were not tied together at all either. While labs were engaging, homework didn't seem to apply much. In addition, tests were not reflective of learnt material from HW or Labs. Also, grading rules were absurd, and it was possible to fail with B-D avg.
Genuinely a mean guy. I feel like he is in the wrong profession.

A very strict professor which made this one of the toughest classes for the major. However, he does pay attention to who is struggling in (or skipping) his classes; he helps those that seek him for additional help. You will have a tough time, but getting through will make toughen & ready you for the final year.

Seems intent on making class incredibly hard by not giving instructions until after the fact, where he then criticizes our mistakes. Has a drill sergeant mentality-hates anyone that is late or absent to class (has given punishments for this).Extremely intimidating guy. Basically makes the class a struggle-expect one or two all nighters a week.
He is just horrible. Unfair grader. He abhors unwelcoming language. Try to avoid his class in any means necessary. At least one all-nighter a week guranteed.
Fantastic professor, though also a fantastically hard grader. Worth the pain.
The class was just unfairly graded.  I must say I learned a lot, but one of my very few Cs at CMU.
Heat transfer, the most feared MechE class, and it wasn't fun. In retrospect he did teach well, but the class wasn't a walk in the park.

12h+ per homework and only takes 30% of the score (6-8 homework),
In-class midterm takes 30% and finial 40%.
Lectures are basic, homework and exams are extremely difficult math.
Take 18698 if you are TRULY confident in math, consider take neural data analysis instead.
Byron is a good guy but he doesn't apply to ANY email.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I have never seen a professor prepare to teach a class less than Byron Yu. He believes that just because he understands what is going on that the rest of the class does as well. Be prepared to be dropped in the deep end and expected to swim. The class is poorly explained in the syllabus, and feedback is nearly non-existent.
Great in lecture, very helpful inside and outside of class. Very reasonable.

Jimmy is a very caring and nice professor, not his fault 18100 is a class broken beyond what he can fix, but he honestly isn't helping. Vague lecture slides, changing attendance/exam policy halfway through the semester, entirely disconnected from labs and administrative stuff (which the TAs do exclusively). His class could use more standardization.
Jimmy is goated
amazing professor, one of the best
Jimmy is the goat
Nice Prof

18100 has been a hard class historically and I'm so glad the stars aligned that I took the class while he was teaching it. Lowkey lectures can be learnt on your own with the slides but I attended it for Jimmy's good vibes. A big softy who genuinely cares for the students + made the class a lot easier = definitely take it if he's teaching.

He's so nice!! He's always talking about how you need to truly learn how to think and not just memorize material, and I think he delivers on teaching his students how to be creative thinkers. When I got sick, he was super accommodating too.

Not the best lecturer. Has an accent and tend to lower his voice as he gets to the end of a sentence. HOWEVER, he genuinely cares about his students learning and will go to great length to explain things to you if you go to office hours. Overall very reasonable professor and a great man.

Jimmy is a god amongst men. He preaches the light of knowledge to those left in the darkness. Jimmy could create life with his sheer will if he so pleases.  Jimmy's existence has accelerated the advancement of society tenfold. If aliens came to Earth, they would bow to him in reverence. I am a changed man after taking his class.
Excellent lectures, but labs can be time-consuming or frustrating at times, but TAs offer lots of support.
Great professor and very knowledgeable. Talks about the relevance of material!
Jimmy is my boi

Teaches the recitation for this course. Extremely good at explaining concepts and problems. 10 minutes with him will give you complete understanding of the concepts from a particular problem. Good at pep talks. Tests are very hard. (It's a weeder course.)
Best Professor Ever!
great course and not so much homework
This is the real ultimate proffessor! If you haven't had his class, you better get a life.
worst professor ever, i couldn't understand a word he said
Very solid professor.

his homework and the actual exam are not related at all. 
his lectures are not quite helpful to complete the homework as well.
during office hours, he always seem grumpy and not helpful at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class is not super difficult, however he is not a great teacher. The review content for exams is not very helpful for the exams and we were tested on something he never taught us and is not mentioned in any slides or notes.
Really cool professor, not a lot of busy work, definitely would recommend.

There are so many things wrong with his teaching style. For one, he assumes everyone is on the same level with material. He'll gloss over concepts saying "we should know this already" and doesn't go over multi-step problems. Homework questions aren't related to in-class assignments. Don't bother asking him questions - he doesn't want to help.

He is not the best professor teaching-wise
Macroecon is not even a difficult subject, he is just not great at teaching in general (especially in preparation for quizzes and tests)

I don't know anyone who is not struggling in this class. It's difficult to receive a good grade in this class since you can't recover if you do poorly on 1 exam. I have a high A average on Homeworks and Quizzes, but I am struggling to get a B for this class. He does not give partial credit and will test concepts that he covered once in class.
The course content is fine as far as it goes but this dude is a clown lol. I would only recommend taking his class if you want an NFT shill teaching you about economics LMAO

Professor Zelin-Jones' class is amazing! He prepares the lectures very carefully and always provides wonderful lecture notes. His homework instructions are also super clear. You can always learn so much from his office hours, too! He is extremely nice and knowledgeable.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Homeworks are intentionally hard to make you suffer, project is very confusing till the very end, exams are way harder than what the practice entails, sometimes even misleading from the practice. The students did give feedback on this mid semester but little to no change were given to the course
Difficult tests, tough homework grading, terrible project.
This professor tried her best to make this course hard. No lecture note, few feedback. Very unreasonable grading. exams are 10 times harder than practice test. Do not recommend

The most difficult math class I've ever had. No guidance, no help, and incredibly tough homework and midterms. There are no lecture notes, no feedback on assignments, and TAs are not given hw solutions. Midterms are 5x harder than practice midterms, and there's no guidance or direction for students
He is the best professor you could get in any engineering course.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: One of the best professors at CMU. While he is quite knowledgeable what sets him apart is his genuine care for students. He is soooooo nice and patient. If you have an option try to take 18-100 with Zajdel.

It was a cool October afternoon. The students in Doherty Hall were rushing to their next class. I approached DH 2315 ready to learn about signals and systems, Tom's expertise. As the lecture begins, and Tom begins to speak he opens with an interesting phrase. "Guys" he said "I've been to a grad party". At that moment I knew that ECE was my calling.

He is a great professor! He is respectful, funny, explains well, and is very accessible. His lectures are full of useful information and they are inspirational. He talks about the relevance of the material given. Just a great human being that cares for the education of his students.

Zapanta is hilarious and a great guy. He responds quickly to emails. There are five labs and they are not too bad. Graded pretty nicely too. There is a poster presentation on a scientific paper at the end of the semester.
Love Dr. Zapanta. If you can, take his classes. Great mentor and instructor
Great Teacher and Mentor.
Best BME teacher ever. So into the subject and knows his stuff!
Dr. Zapanta is a great professor if you can get over the fact that he's a Penn State fan.
Amazing teacher. Proactive and goes the extra step for his students and wants every to understand the basics of economics
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: His class was great!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Prof. Acquisti is one of those people, who make you feel a little bit smarter just by being in the same room as you. He makes Economics so much fun, and his lectures are very interesting. (I legit woke up on time and went happily for all his 9 am lectures - that should tell you something). His assignments really make you think critically. 10/10!
Brilliant Professor with stellar teaching skills. Highly knowledge and humble attitude. The aura of intelligence surrounds him.
One of the best professor's I've had. Great teaching style, keeps you engaged. His homeworks and tests make you think. Would definitely take another class with him.

He is an amazingly skilled professor. He brings life in a dry theoretical subject. It could have been a lot more challenging to understand the concepts of Econ if he would not have taught us.   Thanks.

Possibly the best professor I've been taught by. His classes are well structured, and the homework, while difficult, perfectly complements what he teaches. Also really helpful, prompt and a great person overall.
He is a great professor and an even better person
Great professor. Gives tough tests and quizzes. Don't expect high grades in his Econ course.
Great
Great professor. Very helpful.
he is great
Always assigns lots of readings, and quizzes and tests are challenging.  I learned a lot, though.
Fairly Good professor. He's a great guy, but his Econ class can get sort of boring. His quizzes are pretty difficult too.
Best professor at Carnegie Mellon.
Great prof, loved taking her class, 10/10 would like to have her as a prof again
Lectures do not help with quizzes and there needs to be more relevant practice.

Adam's enthusiasm always got me excited to learn about cs theory, and you can generally tell he cares a lot and puts in the effort to make the class enjoyable. The class itself is extremely difficult and homework's are killer, but the tests are considerably easier. GO TO OFFICE HOURS, you will get answers to questions you didn't even have.

Destroyed my gpa. I did better in Chem 142 than CSE 311. HW is ridiculously hard, and they're graded harshly. He talks really fast and his explanations depend on you understanding what he just sped through 5 seconds ago, which isn't good because he's not very good at explaining. However, he is very dedicated and holds a lot of extra office hours.

You could really tell Adam puts a lot of time into the class and cares that the students understand the material. He's passionate about what he teaches, which made lectures entertaining but sometimes too fast for me. The class itself is hard, especially the homework,  but there is always available help if you are struggling.
Very helpful, way more office hours than anyone else
Probably the most caring teacher I've ever had. Really sets the tone for the rest of the CS curriculum.
Super super super helpful and cares a lot about his course and making sure students understand the admittedly difficult material.
I never choose professor again
explains topics very well and love his enthusiasm, tests are never unreasonable
He is fine in general. Most of the time he just reads his notes. He makes some mistakes in lectures from time to time but he posts notes online. The homework is way too hard and time-consuming.

Pisztora is a decent professor, the course itself covers interesting material and it is taught well. The homeworks and tests are a mix of computation and proofs, and are pretty challenging but not too hard. As a teacher, he sometimes tends to ramble and loose track of what he is teaching, and his noted are sometimes difficult to understand.
Heavy accent... but good professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Pisztora is probably the best professor I have ever had. His lecture notes are beautifully clear and he provides concise and well formulated proofs for everything he shows you. He also has a great sense of humor. Homeworks are a challenge, but not unreasonable, and tests are easy if you do the homework well.
OMG This is crazy hard and the homework take forever.

I took Matrix Theory with Pisztora in Spring 2015. Lectures are kinda boring but really useful and clear. Some jokes are present in lecture. His notes are amazing. Expect hard and long homework. Midterms are easy if you could finish 10 problems in 50 minutes, usually 8 computational ones and 2 proofs (It's not hard to finish). Final is easy.
Professor Pisztora is a good mathematician who also teaches well
He uses his own handwritten lecture notes which are super clear. His lecture and homework are great to motivate students. Exams are easy!
Amazing

Took this course Fall 2012. He does read from his notes, but they are very thorough and comprehensive. Lectures are clear and amusing at times (he will crack some jokes if you're lucky). Overall very nice teacher and satisfied!

Literally one of the best people to grace the face of the earth. But seriously, he is one of the better math professors at Carnegie Mellon. He actually cares about his students and is willing to help anyone that asks for it.

Illegible handwriting, and everything except homework and tests is handwritten. Very boring lecturer with a strong (but understandable) accent, though occasionally funny if you pay close attention. Recommend any other equivalent probability class (36-225, 36-217, 15-359)
The best professsor I have ever met
Great Prof, fun class!

VERY difficult class, but the best security course I've taken. Adrian is freaking brilliant, and I *LOVED* his class. Learned more than any other class at CMU. You really need to pay close attention at all times in the class, to fully understand it. The "summaries" you have to do really suck but otherwise, this is a great professor and great class!

Bredikin is a tough professor. You must prepare (both his lectures and the textbook) to succeed. That said, he is tough but fair, and he definitely cares for both the students personally and whether they learn the class material. I feel that I got my money worth  and has built a solid foundation for moving forward and for my future.

He is a tough professor. As a customer, I expect my professor to make it clear how I can get to an A in their class and then help me do just that. Professor Bredekin is not that. In fact, he is the opposite of that. He is old school. I learned the material but I didn't enjoy the class or him.

Professor B can be described as “tough by fair”. I have taken 2 (two) classes from him, which suggests that obviously I find his lectures relevant and useful. He is a tough grader but if you show up and do the work you ll be fine. His tests are rooted in homework assignments, so don't skip them. A very interesting final group project.
I value that Prof Alex is a part-timer who is a practicing professional. Since I am going into this field, his practicality was invaluable to me.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: A practicing professional, prof. Alex is a bright star among the chiefly bookish, impractical, uncaring CMU faculty. He demands accountability and challenges you, but helps and provides constructive feedback. He obviously cares. Not a slam dunk, but a very rewarding class.

A very personable and "lead-by-example" professor with great insights into the subject matter and a wealth of professional experience that he shares freely and willingly. A game changer for me - switching majors from CE to CM.

I have encountered no problem with this class. Alex is flexible if you communicate in advance. I was given a chance to turn some homework in late for a small penalty which is fair. He provides good feedback and is always in communication. I have learned tons of new useful material.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I am a senior here & I have seen them all: good, bad, and ugly. Alex is one of the best IMO. For once he brings real world experience into the classroom. Secondly, he cares. Thirdly, the cry babies who whine about too much homework ought to be embarrased - this is CMU after all. The class was relevant and the class work was adequate. I loved it.
I am completely dissatisfied with this professor. He gave me a C. In our school, this is a no-no. I don't know what his problem is, but I suggest you don't take his classes in the future!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Alex is on of the best in this department, probably because he is a part-timer. I liked how he linked all his lessons to real projects, that was rad. What I didn't like is that he come down hard on slackers in this class. In my team, the two of us, out of 5, did all the work, but all 5 passed the class. Unfair!
This is a hard class. Lots of work. I learn a lot, but the amount of work is unreasonable. Heading for an A, but, all things considered, wouldn't take it again... Did I mention too much work?

I feel that I have made a solid choice innpicking this elective. Professor Alex is relatable, concise, and teaches from real world experience. One negative though: too much homework. Test are similar to it though, so it is not wasted.

I enjoyed his class. The information presented applied to real life and the professor teaches from real life experiences. He gave us a lot of freedom in choosing the class schedule which was good. Relevant test (they were hard). I would take a class from him again.

IMO, this professor is a good one. A h/w every other week, 2 or 3 quizzes, and 2 exams plus a term project. He is not good looking at all, but he clearly cares about students learning and grades. He is accessible by email at any time. Explains material real well. Overall, a solid choice if you want to gain knowledge and to learn.

He seems to be on top of his game. The class is not easy but very rewarding. He is answering emails within a couple of hours - unheard of at the CMU. His midterm exams are a murder. He does curve, so I got an A in the end, but several people quit mid-class. It was overall a good solid engineering class. I learned a ton, guest speakers rocked.

The barrage of homework, quiz, assignment, in-class team assignments and tests is relentless. Lectures are very engaging, all from real project experience, good examples, but he piles up many work on student. Very personable and cares about students. Curves grade, so I did fine.

The text is published on-line - SWEET! PLUSES: The professor is a practicing professional - A BIG PLUS. Some amazing high caliber guest lecturers. Relevant material. Approachable. MINUSES: He teaches for people with engineering and construction backgrounds. Assumes students know certain things. Heavy-load homework / tests. I learned a lot.

I have thoroughly enjoyed this class and professor A in its helm. He is a practicing PM at a Fed Govt shop, and it shows. He makes you work for your A but it is well worth it. The case studies are relevant and the term project was fun and fostered synergy in my group. Time well spent.

I feel that I have been inspired by this professor to switch majors; from civil engineering to engineering project management. It seems more fun and more rewarding. I kind of been burned over by the cut throat CMU Engineering approach, and this is the first professor who has acted like a human towards his students.

Will randomly call on students during lecture. Grades with the intention of taking off points, ie is very nitpicky, and there is no clear communication of grading expectations. Accessible for office hours, but otherwise is very unclear in learning expectations and fosters competition.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Rubric not clear, so awful, final super hard and will NOT CURVE any exam, and rarely respond to email and TA was so bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I really did not understand this class. There was absolutely no grading criteria and on top of that, she grades very harshly. She had no rubric on the assignments she grades and does not give good feedback on how you got the grade she gave you. There are no practice exams and barely any homework so studying for this class is impossible.
Unclear grading standards. Clearly had favorites. Wouldn't recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: concise and full of information. Not a class for slackers. Found this class totally absorbing, and went to all the lectures which were not confusing if you'd done any preparation.
Unclear standards. No practice tests. Confusing lectures. If you can, don't take a class with her.
Very very intelligent professor, but is a harsh grader. Knows her stuff so you can't bs your way through anything.

By "Skip Class? You Won't Pass" I don't mean you will miss so much material that you wouldn't be able to learn it on your own. What I mean, is that she takes personal offence to students who do not attend classes. Between her pejorative comments to and about students and her discomfiting teaching style, I would highly advise avoiding this course.

This class absolutely sucked. There were no practice exams, the material was convoluted and poorly taught. The discussion always digresses and you never know what to study for the exams. Wikipedia is the only chance of acing any tests.
Gives detailed feedback to students and provide good resources to support our understanding. Great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Amazing professor!
Alan is genuinely the nicest professor you will ever meet! You'll love his class and teaching style.
He is awesome. I would absolutely take him again. I would highly recommend him.

I love professor Kohler. He deeply cares about all students and starts EVERY CLASS off with a question about our week or some game so that we can build connections. He makes sure we all know he is always a resource to us. His class was interesting. At times the assignments could be confusing but he was always there to help.

I truly believe he is one of the most passionate, kind, and caring professors at Carnegie Mellon University. He cares more about the students and how to help them then they do, and will be there every step of the way. I really believe that no matter the subject he can make it something fun, something that anyone would want to learn. :)

Professor Lew takes his lectures very slow which is great when you are not too good at diff eq. His expectations for HW and exams are very clear and everything is pretty easy. Def the easiest math class ive taken at CMU.

Prof.Lew is a very kind person who is always willing to help students solve probelms. The course have weekly quizzes. This means you have to attend one of the recitations each week. The lecture slides are quite informative and helpful, so I mostly studied by myself in the library. Midterms and the final are not so hard.

Professor is a nice person, but is hard to understand. I never felt like I was really learning from the lectures because he is reading off of his slides. His slides are pretty informative but I would prefer not to always have to be self studying. The class midterms are not that difficult and are departmental, but the class averages are always low.

Professor Lew is a very sweet professor. He is always checking to see if anyone has questions during the lecture, and stays after for questions. His lectures aren't the most interesting though. He can speak kind of quietly and he just goes through his slideshows. If the lecture isn't helping you, you can always go to his office hours.

A good instructor in the math department. The content on the lectures at the end of the semester is a little bit hard. However, homework and office hours help improve our understanding. The exams align with the homework and the lecture. I have a good experience for this class.

He was probably one of the best profs I have had at CMU, I took 21-325 with him and his lectures were quite hard but he is a nice and understanding person, helps understand material in office hours and gives extensions when needed.

This professor was disorganized in lectures and distrusting of his students. This course was extremely difficult due to the poor quality of resources he provided, and I have taken calculus before. Exams were disproportionately difficult in comparison to lectures and homework, and he failed to provide adequate resources to prepare for his exams.

Dylan's friendly, but he's often late. He likes using the textbook and its fancy words, which can be annoying if you don't get the math terms. HWs are rly different from lectures, and since he doesn't create the questions, he has to solve them first before helping. Test averages are low, but he drops the lowest midterm & the other two are 25% each.

This was his first semester teaching, so he was still learning how to teach. The first exam was awful and was all trick questions, but the next two exams were fairly straightforward on what the material was vs what showed up on the exam. Overall, he had notes for each lecture but does not explain the topics extremely well.

New but getting better compared to start of class. Agree homework is hard but sometimes very similiar to the textbook example and sometimes not, it not impossible to solve. Exam questions are usually from lecture or homework but I do agree grading is not generous making averages super slow. He is a very nice guy and will be amazing prof in future.

I don't think he's bad as others say. He is definitely inexperienced, but I think he's improved a lot compared to the beginning of the semester. I understand why he makes certain decisions (like placing emphasis on theorems or choosing difficult homework problems), even if I may not like them.

i get what hes trying to execute in class but whatever he teaches in lecture never lines up with what the homework is, homework is 10x harder for the first few units and he isn't great at teaching.

update: failed the class so do what u want with this info, even the final wont save me
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dylan is a nice person but not a good professor. Homeworks are difficult and sometimes doesn't think about the difficulty when he assigns it. Exams always have typos. Grading is very strict and barely get any credit for wrong answers but correct work. I like how he drops lowest midterm but the averages are still low, also be ready to FAIL exams.

Lectures are from the textbook which makes it easy to follow but he makes the homeworks so so hard and the exams as well. When I ask a question he replies "just think about it." He also makes the rubric so poorly making it impossible to get partile credit. I studied 10+ hours for an exam and still got a 30% :( he is also ALWAYS late to class.
Very conceptual questions, very little emphasis on computing derivatives and integrals, more emphasis on reciting theorems.
humorous
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The homework and the exams are nothing like what we learn in lecture or recitation. I understand during lectures he has a lot of material to get through but you will always walk out being so confused about a pretty simple topic. The tests make up so much of your grade and beware to fail them even if you study.
I walk out of lecture feeling amazing and then I look at the homework and it makes absolutely no sense

Although today is my first time having his class first time, I hate this professor. His voice was like a mosquito then made me confused about what he was saying. Also, the blackboard writing is too thin and messy to be difficult to recognize and then take notes. :(

Has high expectations (it was a first-year class for most of us), but she seemed to care a lot, maybe too much. It was fun, a lot of interaction with classmates, and not really that hard if you don't mind reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: The class was more work than expected for a writing class but you can get a lot out of it if you chose a research topic that you care about, the professor cared a lot about students improving, and the readings were a lot but mostly interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Don't take prof. Alexander's class if you want to spend more time on English than your in-major classes. She doesn't seem to understand that this class is a first year English class and grades the assignments as if we are all English majors... Spent more time on this class than on my math classes...

Dunn's a delight. If you're willing/able to contribute to discussion/class energy, you'll have fun. Knowledgeable/passionate about topics and generally very understanding w assignments just like talk to him. Grading's more effort and creative willingness based than technical. Vocab can be uh robustly confusing. Chat w him. Dont listen to his haters

Loves what he talks about but if you don't know anything about philosophy and vague art history, everything will probably fly over your head. Written assignments have no set rubric and can feel like they're graded based on vibes sometimes...
lecture heavy but vaguely interesting
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Andrea seemed to really care about my work and gave very detailed and helpful feedback online and in meetings. I did a project I was proud of and didn't feel like I was just checking boxes. Highly recommended.
Very tough grader and not clear about participation expectations (often takes off unnecessary points). Only small percentages of her class receives an A.

Really nice professor who gave great feedback. She's very passionate about the subject and tries to make it interesting. Only complaints are that her grading standards are considerably higher than other 76101 instructors (not necessarily a bad thing) and she gives a lot of homework, although the assignments were typically short.

Tests are hard if you don't fully know the material, and easy if you do. Lenient grader; OH are helpful. Lectures were good enough that I, personally, never really needed the (online) textbook -- can't speak for others though ofc.

Lectures can be hard to follow, but as long as you go to good TA office hours you should make an easy B. Homework isn't too bad, one a week that is not hard to score high on if you go to office hours. Tests are incredibly fair, not made to trick you but rather to check you know the concepts. He is very nice and wants you to learn the material!
The hate for this guy some ppl have is way overblown. Not the greatest professor at CMU by any means but the exams and lectures were clear enough and there wasnt much to complain about.

So bad. I am a math major and lectures made absolutely no sense even to me (and to everyone else I spoke to). Lectures honestly made me feel dumb because I could never follow. The only positive thing is that the course isn't that hard and if you teach yourself some of it, it's not too hard to end with an A.

He taught pretty well and really cared about students.
Exams are hard but normalized in a fair way.

2 in class exams, weekly homeworks, very poor lectures. all lectures are problems straight from the book. unreasonable and not really adaptive to rescheduling exam times and grading

better off finding a substitute class
He's a really bad professor. He doesn't care about teaching you anything that doesn't come directly from the book. Do not take.

Not a great lecturer, and the content wasn't very clear. At some point I thought I didn't have the prerequisites but some students with less seemed to be doing fine. I think if you mostly self - study you should be fine. On a positive note, he has office hours and does seem to be trying his best.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Probably one of the worst lecturers I have ever seen in my entire life. He spends more time looking at his ipad and the ground than at the students even when talking but it's probably because he's literally reading out of the textbook... Don't take his class if you want to learn calculus properly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Beware, taking a class taught by Andrew Newman entails you self studying this subject for the entire semester. Why? He literally reads off the textbook verbatim during class while talking to the floor. The exams are in no way related to the practice exams he gives out (why even?).
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Going/not going to lectures have the same effect - none. Newman's lectures consisted of him reading off the textbook literally word for word, copying textbook definitions onto the 'lecture notes,' and him awkwardly shuffling around while mumbling into the mic. Tests (hard) also did not reflect the difficulty of the content or practice tests (easy).

References the free textbook we used heavily. Not necessarily a bad thing. Lectures are fast paced, but his explanations are helpful to understanding the concepts. Provided past midterms/quizzes and suggested textbook questions for exam prep. Exams did not contain any surprises. Had to spend time out of class reviewing notes/textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Andrew Newman reads off the textbook for his lectures and enjoys giving midterms that do not reflect the course material (in one instance, he made a problem not covered in class/hw worth 15% of the midterm). He provides little to no review material and thus it is very difficult to know what to study. It would be more effective to study on your own.

Extremely rude and has no respect for students. Terrible terrible person, if you expect to be treated as human, stay away from her at all costs because she will attach you for no reason and made you think you are stupid, incapable of doing anything, and a terrible student. Miserable to work with
Extremely rude and has no respect for students, whoever working with her must have a miserable life. Stay away at all costs.
Extremely extremely rude professor! Do not take her classes if you have other choices.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Good professor! Very clear with explaining concepts. Tests are not extremely difficult. Lecture slides are clear. Good place to learn intro to ML

Awful professor, she accuses students of cheating (I did not cheat and had to prove I didn't). She doesn't seem to know the material too well either, she seems like she's perplexed by what she teaches. Complete waste of time, a udemy/coursera class would have you in a much better place.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Leman is incredibly rude to her students, mocking their lack of knowledge on things that are not prerequisites for the class. She teaches at various speeds and can deep dive into a topic and explain with clarity, but mere seconds later realize her pace is unacceptable and she accelerates past the point of comprehension. I would recommend avoid.
Rude.  Hopefully she doesn't last long after she get's terrible teaching evals this semester
Exams are nothing like the homeworks or PowerPoint.   No guidance on what needs to be learned.
A thoroughly and completely wasted opportunity her class was. She teaches from theory since she has no practical experience. She is nice but clearly shouldnt be teaching this class. What a waste.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Very smart professor, great lecturer too. Assignments were not bad unless you left them until the last minute.
Highly committed and passionate about teaching. Superb person to know.
shes an awesome professor I love her!
Very good at what she teaches.
Go to the TAs for help. This instructor is useless and can't explain things clearly. Her English is not so great either. Should update those slides!!!

Grading was very harsh. Lectures did not well prepare people for weekly quizzes so the class average was quite low. She is very mean towards students by saying that she wasted a good 15 mins of her time trying to recognize my handwriting. (I have never got any points off/received any complaints from any class because of my handwriting before.)
Professor Simmons is very friendly and inspiring. Her teaching pace might be a little too slow, though.

I'm interested in learning another language so I thought NATURE OF LANGUAGE would be a good course to take (& get gen ed requirements filled) Overall I enjoyed the class but Dr. Simons can be confusing and present her lectures in a condeluded way. Easy readings. Recommend going to Office hours (both for Prof and TAs) because they're SUPER helpful.

Mandy is awesome! She is such a caring and kind professor. I didn't do well on my first paper for 80-484, and we made an appointment to go over everything I missed in my paper in great detail. I took her 80-180 last spring and have now taken her 80-484. I always love Mandy's classes.

I had difficulties while in her class, and she was so understanding and helpful in meeting my needs. She was accessible outside of class for feedback and to go over material. Professor Simons also gave me by far the best writing feedback Ive ever received from an educator. Knows her field in incredible depth and cares enough to share it with you

Daily quizzes have stuff on them she never spoke about. Very ambiguous, sometimes the quiz answers are different than the reading they're supposed to be based on. Mandatory attendance for somewhat boring lectures. Can't use tech

She is very very kind. She gives very interesting lectures, and really pays attention to class feedback. I am really enjoying her class and highly recommend. the discussions make me feel very close to my other classmates, and the learning environment is wonderful.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Her lectures and the readings are very engaging. You will learn a lot by showing up to class.

Great teacher; very enthusiastic about and interested in linguistics. She was the advisor for the major, but it changes every so often. She's clear in her explanations. Nature of Language with her is relatively simple if you get the concepts, and homework is once every 2-3 weeks. Not many exams. As a Linguistics major, I like her.

If I could live 100 lives, I would take a class with Prof. Talwalkar in every single one of them. I took the course because I wanted to learn about CS and AI, and I ended switching my major 3rd year because of how inspiring he was. To me, he is like a father figure. He built the man I am today and I aspire to be like him in the future.
Get ready to work hard! Professor Talwalkar pushes you to the limits but you will learn a ton. Inspired me to pursue a career in machine learning. You must take a class with him!

Really bad teaching. Just showing the math instead of teaching the people. Did a survey but never take the voice from the students who think the course has too much math rather than how to use big-data ideas. If you just want to know how the big data is used in the industry, stay away from him. You will get nothing from his lecture.
I really liked my class wit be Professor Apt. He is very engaging. The class helped me a lot.
A real astronaut! It was an amazing experience to take Professor Apt's class knowing he has flown on the space shuttle and been to space!!!
Professor Apt is an actual astronaut. I guess I am still a little kid at heart when it comes to meeting astronauts. His course is very interesting. He is easy to talk to.

Although Dubrawski's stories are really entertaining, this whole class was very unorganized. I feel like i wasted 12 credits on this course. I suggest taking Practical Data Science instead. The first half was okay, but the entire second half of the semester was pointless. Also, even though he's funny, he is a little full of himself sometimes.
Brilliant professor! His assignments are very tough, but worth all the time and effort. I strongly recommend this course with Professor Artur.
Excellent course .Poorly taught .If not for homeworks the subject is useless. Homeworks make you learn
Best class I have taken at the Heinz college
He takes pride in being a tough teacher. Fun stories, but, for me, not a fun class.
The homework is ridiculously time consuming and the graders (TAs) are not lenient. The professor has a great personality, but his class is poorly structured in terms of promoting learning.
95-791 is the hardest course in CMU - M.I.S.M. but it is worth all the effort and pays off. Artur Dubrawski is a genius.
Prof. Dubrawski brings together his practical business experience and acute knowledge of the subject to make the class very interesting and useful.

Prof. Dubrawski's class is REALLY hard, but it's a lot of fun. He's a great teacher who really does a good job of conveying the subject matter to his students. I've rarely learned so much in class. He also throws in a bunch of little jokes here and there, but you only notice them if you pay close attention.  Great Professor!
Amazingly inspirational and humble human being !!!!
Amazingly inspirational human being !

Unfortunately he doesn't teach this class anymore, but as the department head put it, it'd be a shame for any Chem student to go through CMU not having taken a course with him. I loved his humor and energy and for a topic that's known to be "extremely painful," he made it incredibly easy to understand, although some effort and time were required.

The best professor I've ever had. Makes everything seem simple; if you dont understand something GO TO OFFICE HOURS. THEY MAKE A WORLD OF A DIFFERENCE. He's very likable and funny and makes the content very interesting.

A very good teacher. His jokes are pretty painful, but he's good-humored and likes to engage with his students. If you go to class and pay attention, you'll do fine. The class is definitely difficult, largely just because of the subject matter, but he and the TA are very helpful.
Awesome professor, super funny and engaging. Really nice guy too! One of the best if not the best profs I've had at CMU
Armitage = Amazing  One of the most engaging professors I've had, and I learned more from his class that basically any others
He has great energy. It's incredible that he can make organic chemistry interesting at 8:30 on a Monday morning.
Great teacher
This is the most clear, organized, perfect and boring class you will ever be in. You will know exactly what to learn and how to learn it at all times - no thinking required.
i love him...amazing teacher..great person
No Comments
Great prof, explains things very well and is easily accessible.  He makes an effort and cares about his students.
Not the easiest classes, but if you can get him for a harder one, its definately made easier by him

everyone likes art when they first have him, just don't over expose yourself to him or let him have too much control over the curriculum. he doesn't actually know what he's talking about, and all of the "research" he's doing is mindlessly accepting whatever he reads, and making it law.  he doesn't actually understand much theory. super frustrating.

Well intentioned but holds strongly to personal opinions and approaches, which it sometimes seems he is trying to force students into pursuing.  Introduces some stimulating readings and discussions, but can be overwhelming to the point of stifling personal growth and development
The best ever...definitely impacted my life!
He is "Da Bomb".  Long Live "Da Art".
Art is the best teacher.
Really great guy and a great overall professor. Made content super digestible and wish that he taught higher level CS classed ;(
Genuinely one of my favorite professors at CMU, even though he's only part time.
Austin was an amazing lecturer and caring person! He made lectures really fun and also gave thoughtful life and career advice. Highly recommend taking 112 with him if you can.
Awesome professor!!!
Really great professor, truly cares about his students and faculty.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Awesome professor! Provides very helpful help for anything!
One of the most incredible professors that I have worked with. We could not have had a better learning experience as students.

Unprofessional and disorganized professor. This person will assign work last minute while happily claiming you will not sleep till 2AM as result of this last minute assignment. Very poor course structure and awful lectures. The slides look like a 2 year old made them. Avoid this professor.
Best prof I had at Tepper.
Beware of the quality of the information presented. Questions are normally far below the expectation of masters students. Professionalism is questionable at times.

Terrible. Did not have correct facts in some cases. Did not give midterm grade. Most of final grade is huge group project. Will not re-grade individual questions on an exam, only the entire exam and "your grade may go down". Final exam is on last day of class.
Aven is great. Organizational Behavior, however, is pretty boring.
Extremely good lecturer.
He is a good teacher for this class.  you should take it with him
His dad is better.
Worst teacher ever with horrible class structure
A prof that actually cares about his students! Gave us the skills to work on real-world problems. The Excel sessions rocked!
The best finance professor I have had at Pitt and one of the best profs ever. I learnt more finance in his class than my previous 4 finance classes combined.
Great professor. Very down-to-earth. Focuses on the basic, big ideas and makes sure you know them cold. Brought in great guest speakers.
Amazing prof and amazing class. Showed us how to analyze what's going on in the real world. Really cares for his students. The best elective class I've ever taken.
The best prof I have had this sem and a really nice and approachable person. The best quantitative course I have taken at the uni.
Really great prof. Very helpful and approachable. Arranged for several amazing guest speakers and even got a company to sponsor a project competition for us.

Very demanding prof. But, you learn a lot in his course. Like other posters said, don't screw around with him. He failed 10% of the class. Arranged for several excellent guest speakers. Very helpful -- gave lots of contacts for internships/jobs. If you take his courses, work hard, learn a lot and do well. Or, don't take a course with him at all.
Really tough but fair professor. Don't take his class if you are going to blow it off. He will fail you. He advised several students to drop the course. Those that did not, flunked out

Prof. very interested in the subject and a reasonable guy. Has very high expectations. His courses are hard, but you learn a lot from them. His courses are not for wimps or those with weak quantitative skills.

Incredibly knowledgeable professor. Course was very applied. Lots of cases/problems solved in Excel. Great prep for consulting interviews. Has lots of industry contacts. He's approachable and willing to help the top students land internships. He is tough but fair. Don't try to screw around with him.

This guy has THE most screwed up grading system EVER. He's a great guy on a personal level, but he's a horrible teacher. The whole course is set up for you to fail. You can spend days working on the homework and not get anything correct. In class, he does really simple examples, but the homework and tests are crazy-hard. Avoid at all costs!
The greatest acting teacher I can imagine. Absolutely study with her if you get the opportunity- she will change your life!

Very disorganized. Contradicts themselves, but does not acknowledge it. Harder on you as a person, than as a student. Repetitive & not very good at re-phrasing concepts. Created a space that was often emotionally & mentally unsafe. Yet loving, passionate, dedicated, & always puts in the effort to understand.

The best and probably most invaluable class I've taken at the School of Drama. It's hard to appreciate it fully when you're in it, but do your best to soak up every moment you'll never know the class's worth till you've said goodbye to Barbara!
Know more about theatre and acting than anyone at CMU
like fine wine
One of the finest acting teachers in the country.
This professor refuses to offer feedback, instead urging students to purchase his book.

Favorite professor. Teaches a lot, and if you listen will help you become a better designer. Wants you to succeed, but you need to put in the work, because you are an adult, and he won't do it for you.

If you want to learn how to draw and you are disciplined and committed, this is the right class. Prof Baskinger is smart and will demand the best from you but isn't this what college should be all about?
Excellent teacher. Hard but fair and if you work hard you will learn a lot. Not for people who wnt to malinger or be coddled.

Mark has a great sense of humor, often cracking jokes during class. He is a very talented and skilled drawer and knows what he's talking about. Expect to draw incessantly in your sketchbook; Mark likes clean, complete, and well-crafted drawings (and lots of them). He is an excellent source for specific feedback if solicited.

Baskinger is the coolest teacher ever. He is a good balance of professional, personable, and humorous. His relation with Matt is charming and the duo often makes the class chuckle. Mark is very skilled and knows what he is talking. He wants you to succeed but you will have to want to work for it as well.

He's a great professor, He knows what he wants and wants you to get better really bad.  He is just too cool to admit how much he cares. As long as you give an honest effort, he will respect and help you immensely.
no comment
Pretty impressed with himself but after a semester it remains unclear why.
