He's really hard at first and pretends to hate everything but he's a REALLY great teacher once you are able to talk to him about your work. Also very funny.
No Comments
No Comments

No one deserves the the cool sunglasses icon more.  The guy's awesome... but not easy.  He won't pretend to like anything you do.  And he may rip it off the wall and stomp on it.  But all in good design fun.  CUBES!
Great professor and great class. he mainly lectures about his work - he's done a lot of stuff across different fields.

Love this guy. Kind to everyone and offers extensions upon request. Definitely take his classes before he retires. Readings definitely help with tests and class participation but it isn't a big deal if you miss a reading. He also gives you the textbook so you don't have to buy it.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He is a good professor. It was very interesting course. He seems to care about his students and was approachable.
Solid professor.  He definitely has a history with the material and makes it very relevant.

Told us day 1 that if we weren't going to do the readings, we should at least skim them--honest, but not encouraging to do the work. Much discussion, with a large component of grade being discretionary for participation and attendance.

Objectively speaking, his lecture is terrible. He likes to ramble on different topics and goes on and on, on examples that makes his argument even more confusing. The quality of the content of the class is quite low, and I have the feeling that everyone is not bothering to pay attention in class just because of how bad it is.

Don't find his lecture very helpful. Sometimes he will even mock himself during class which I found funny. Makes me wonder how could someone who is clearly not knowledgable enough in the subject matter teach a class of 70+ students?

Poorly structured course. Homework does not seem to be well designed and structured. His lectures are basically reading off slides, and very wordy. Typos occur everywhere despite him teaching for years, and overall appears to be very unprofessional. Would not recommend.

Matt Bass is good at speaking with elevated authority, but in reality the content that he is speaking is very poor and does not have much meat. Sometimes I found myself able to summarize one hour of lecture in one sentence, and he can go on and on. It all boils down to it depends, and I dont find his lecture useful at all unfortunately.
You wont basically learn anything in his class. He did not care about teaching.
Easy class. He wants everyone to get an A. Nice person but makes quite a lot of mistakes.
Mad chill. Probably my favorite professor in 4 years at this drab school.

Lectures were pretty boring and were kind of hard to sit through. She gives a lot of example problems and explains the solving process well. Homework is hard but you have an assigned group to help you. The final was crazy difficult compared to the midterms.
Not too great at clearing up concerns, gives very poorly worded assignments, and didn't teach a good chunk material before the exam which led to a lot of self teaching.

The class is quite essential and teaches many things that can be applied to many aspects of engineering. However, the professor is very difficult to deal with, and sometimes her lectures confuse me, even after I've done the reading. Make sure you are an obedient student and don't think outside the box when it comes to projects—she can't handle it.
I loved Professor Beckwith's class, as I felt respected and that she truly cared about my and my classmated education. It's a tough course, but she makes it much easier to bear.
Don't take this class.

Prof. Best was accommodating and open to changing his course to suit students best and improve their learning. However, significant concepts are covered quickly, along with other content, in many slides. He is nice and listens to students, but he isn't the best lecturer or teacher figure. Most students who took Micro before skipped class. 3.5/5
I took his 73102 during fall. He is definitely one of the best professors. Strongly recommended.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was one of the best classes I have taken at CMU so far. I learned so much because the lectures were interesting and captivating. Also, the TA's were super kind and helpful.
His lectures are so nice to sit in because he sounds like a podcast

Enjoyed 76106 with Dr. Williams. Course content was heavy but made the writing feel more meaningful. (Especially after coming from Writing with Data which was not at all informed by the realities of the world.) Would take another class with him in a heartbeat!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Can be a little intense at times, but super caring and willing to help you succeed. If you show up, do the work, and genuinely try you will get an A. Much more meaningful content than the data mini. Would recommend over the other minis

Very sympathetic towards students. If you let him know in advance that you need a deadline, he will give it no questions asked. He grades homework pretty leniently, but he can be a tough grader for midterm and final papers. He always seems to find something that you can improve on.

Many positive things to say about this professor! Pretty kind and caring but very demanding when needed. They will not allow you slack off at all. This teacher works well with students who take initiative to solve their own problems. Since studying with this professor, my artistry has improved to what is pretty close to professional level.
Yes, other students need to know that he is uncaring and a bad instructor.

Great recruiter, not a good teacher. Stopped caring about me the moment I enrolled. Cannot convert what he is able to do himself into effective coaching for others. If you are seeking more than arrogance and the insipid platitude, "play louder, articulate harder", where your ability to develop as a musician and person is concerned, look elsewhere.
The professor was good and I enjoyed the class!
Best prof in the HCII. Knowledgable and inspiring.

This course has changed the way I SEE the world, in every aspect of my life. The things I learned about design and typography, about visual and verbal communication, will affect my career and my personal life forever. Karen comes across as very kind and nurturing, but she is a fierce and brilliant scholar who expects the same from her students.

I have taken 3 classes taught by Karen Kornblum Berntsen, simply to get as much wisdom and insight from her as possible. She is the best teacher I've ever had in any subject. Karen has the ability to pull the curtain back and bring her students directly in contact with their most intelligent and creative selves.
Best professor I've had at CMU, across all departments.

Karen is an amazing professor! Sometimes her head can be way up in the clouds, but she has a heart of gold and really cares about student learning. I am so happy that I had the chance to learn from her, such an amazing class and a really good experience.

Karen is a brilliant scholar and teacher. She has the ability to impart knowledge so as to light a fire in her students. I learned more in the classes Karen taught than I did in all my other CMU classes combined.
Best professor I've ever had, in any subject. She has honestly changed my life.

Prof. Berntsen puts the 'human' in human-computer interaction. She an extremely approachable and empathetic person, and has a very professional eye for design. The feedback she gives can be unorthodox, often using metaphors to illuminate her points - this kind of analogical thinking is exactly what HCI students need to balance more 'techy' profs.

Very nice and caring person. Passionate about her work.  However, super head in the clouds with a bleeding heart.  Need somewhere to go for thanksgiving? She's there. Need a concise, consistent explanation of what she expects from the assignment ? Not so much.

Not very good at giving feedback, and often focuses on minor details vs. the big picture.  You'll spend a lot of time of a project and be super proud of it, and have the entire thing discounted because a shade of purple is slightly off, or she doesn't like the text of a sub-headline.  Prone to hyperbole.
Best professor ever had.
Ridiculous workload

Good professor and a great person with concerns about students who work sincerely. The course is very heavy- challenging HWs, project and quizzes that eat up the whole weekend. However if you put in adequate work, the outcomes are worth it.

I have never seen a professor care so much about ensuring the students learn. He explains concepts very well and is very patient. Understands the difficulties faced by students and is very approachable.

Confusing slides. Usually, 30 slides to explain one super easy concept, including super complex or advanced unnecessary calculus/optimization/Machine Learning/Linear Algebra knowledge. Yes. As a student who has taken all the referred math/machine learning courses, I can understand, struggle. But not all students can.

Incredible man and genuinely nice person. Cares deeply for his students - advocates for them and spends a lot of time writing recommendations, connecting his students with both academia and industry, and revising his course content. IDL isn't easy (ton of work) but it's really enough to become an ML engineer. Got my job because of Bhiksha.

Bhiksha is a truly awesome professor who delivers engaging lectures and wants you to learn. Weekly quizzes and biweekly projects are a lot of work and ate up most of my weekends. But the course is worth it. You can get a taste of the material by watching recordings on Youtube and checking the assignments.
Brilliant class!

Bhiksha is very responsible as he is very patient in explaining things and holding extra office hours, or even classes, for students who don't understand his lecture. However, sometimes you lose the track of his talking in class. When you understand the materials and look back, you will get his talking and say "Ah! That's what he tried to explain."
Very helpful.
A great person all around who wants to make sure that you pass the course. There is a lot of homework that needs to be done, though don't let that discourage you from taking his class!
Professor Bier was super kind and accommodating! I did not like the OLI, but the class as a whole was super fun!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is a good teacher if you meet individually, easily accessible. It is a lot of work especially if you have no cs experience. He will make sure you understand the material before you leave his office, but his class is extremely boring. The real challenge in this class is trying to stay awake. He's cool though and the TA's are super helpful!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Richard is overall a very friendly and caring person. He makes every attempt to help each and every student feel safe and understand the course content. His homework is always very long and complicated but he is available outside of class to give you feedback and help if necessary.
He is a true gem! He is so accessible and really cares not just about his students' academic success but their personal lives as well.

Good ol' Richie is always a classic to have in drafting. He seems like a hardass at first but actually really cares about students work and well being, it just takes time to understand. 10/10 would hand draft again.

Amazing professor. Truly knows his stuff. You know this guy revolutionized parallelism and invented things like Scan / some of the best graph theory algorithms?(read his wiki) He's on point when he teaches. Plus, the class is an easy A. DO 75% of the Homeworks and get an 80% exam average (not hard to do) and BOOM you have an A while learning a lot!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: The professor doesn't reply to emails nor does he hold office hours.
Guy really cares about teaching, and it shows. Would recommend 210 with him even for non-majors.
Very good!
Blelloch is simply just a god. He taught me how to do things that I previously thought were impossible. One of the best professors at CMU, certainly one of the best CS professors ever, period.
Slightly boring at times, but overall Guy was a really good teacher who clearly cares about the material.  Very challenging course, but I learned a lot.
Very dedicated teacher.  Passionate about the material and covers a lot of it in a high level of rigor.  Not an easy course, but one worth taking.
No Comments

his lectures are very dense and confusing. the proofs were very hard to follow and he was hard to understand due to his accent and messy writing. the homeworks carry a small weight, and they are very hard. his tests are even harder (they are curved heavily). can't learn a lick of anything from this guy. better off taking 21-237 with conley
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very confusing class. Professor went over material too quickly in lectures and did not check for students' understanding in between. Lecture notes were not published and course material was hard to access online. Abysmal class averages on tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Overall a very confusing and disorganized class. HWs were very long (16 hrs on average) and Tests were also not reasonable. I felt like I learned nothing after every lecture. Youtube, TA's OH (not his tho, he was mad confusing), and the textbook carried me through this class. Very few other useful outside resources.
This yo-ho should not be teaching

Prof. Bukh was very passionate about the subject matter, and consistently asked if students understood what he was saying.  He was a bit idiosyncratic, if in an endearing way, and seemed to embody many of the most stereotypical traits of a professor -- somewhat disorganized, kindly, pedantic, and above all, intellectual.
The professor is an incredibly smart mathematician but he does not really know how to be a good lecturer. The notes is confusing, and the pace is too fast for someone who is not a genius.

Boris is extremely passionate about mathematics. He asks good questions on tests, and the bonus problems in his assignments are very interesting. His enthusiasm for math is evident; on occasion he hops with delight when he has shown us an interesting proof or result. He is clearly very knowledgable about linear algebra.
His intellectual arrogance can be matched by no other professor at CMU; he dismisses students' comments often. For somebody who is apparently sooooo smart, I wonder why his class is such a bore.

1-star reviews are just salty that a course that they took as a gen ed actually required some work (an entirely reasonable amount), which really should be expected of any course at CMU. Professor Bjorndahl is an outstanding educator – engaging lectures, presents material well, and truly cares about each of her students. Great prof and great class.

Christina is the best professor I met in cmu so far as a sophomore. She teaches the edgy concepts in linguistics in a very friendly way and showed care for students. The grading is tough but she has been trying to help students out. Also, here is a PSA, gened is not equivalent to an easy A. I fell in love with linguistics through her class.

If you are reading some of the 1 star reviews and thinking "this is misogynistic and rude," you are correct! More than 75% of my peers in this class received an A, thanks to how many retake opportunities Prof Bjorndahl gives. This class is not "just a gen-ed." If you think it is, get out. It's not supposed to be an easy class.

I took this class as a freshman. I'm graduating as a senior now, and I still hate this class with every fibre of my being.  Christina Bjorndhal is a menace and should not be teaching such a useless class with such useless techniques. She should take the time to better develop her course instead of memorizing everyone's names like a fun little game

I do not understand how this woman has a PhD. It's been three years since I took this class and I still remember this class as the worst one I've ever taken. If you'd like to stay sane, stay out of this class. Fresh snow peas will haunt me to the grave.

NEVER TAKE THIS CLASS. Here are the main reasons why:
1) her lectures DO NOT help. They feel like random ramblings tbh. I feel more confused after than before.
2) the new grading system is SO STRICT. More than 1 question wrong on a pset and you're stuck at a B.
3) every assignment takes 4+ weeks to grade, even if there's no feedback left by TAs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I'm in ECE with a minor in biochem and this class alone has more work than any other class. I don't have time for this bs where she prances around the classroom gurgling vowels at us. The class is a gened. Act like it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: THIS CLASS THINKS ITS THE MAIN CHARACTER OR SOMETHING. we're not even halfway through the semester and i get nightmares from this class. i'd wish she'd stop trying to guess my name and JUST TEACH BETTER. DO NOT take this class unless you've already memorized the entire IPA. TORTURE TORTURE TORTURE
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Great professor. The lectures are interesting and there's lots of class discussion (but participation is optional). I felt reasonably well prepared for quizzes/exams/homeworks, and there's retake opportunities for everything. I saw a lot of negative reviews concerning grading, but I think the TAs have probably helped a lot in that regard.

I took this class two years ago and whenever someone asks me if they should take this class I list all the bad things about the class and the professor so fast to the point where I'm practically rapping. I'd rather my tuition go into maintaining the grass on campus than going to this hell of a professor.
Worst professor ever. I've never had a class haunt me so badly
Absolute horrible. Do not take. Super uninteresting and makes content absolute hell

Probably one of my favorite courses I took last semester, but definitely not the easiest. You will probably not like this class if you don't have any interest in linguistics. The homeworks require a decent amount of effort, but office hours are very helpful.

Lectures are confusing and tangent filled. She doesn't fully explain things, then expects students to do well on midterms, homeworks, and exams. If you like Linguistics, you will like this class. If not, you will absolutely hate it. She is an overall nice woman, but would not want to take another class with her.

Everyone is giving her low ratings because the class was very difficult,especially for something taken as a gen ed. But the material was interesting, and you can tell she really wants people to learn. I guess the class serves as a weed out. Most people hate it, and the people who like it REALLY like it.

Took this during spring 2022 and now I finally have the guts to write a review. This class was GOD AWFUL. This had more work than all of my stem classes combined. Grading is very inconsistent; worked with a group of friends and yet sometimes I got 15 points lower than the people I worked with (or vise versa). Genuinely thought I was going to die
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Stupid, ridiculous class and professor. Most useless content ever. If you do take this class be prepared to put a tremendous amount of effort if you want to get an A. I literally get nightmares thinking about the IPA chart

Probably the most useless class and professor ever. Emailed her 5 times telling her that my grade on canvas is wrong due to an accepted regrade and responds back twenty min later saying that she will fix it only for it to be the same two weeks later (yes i have emailed her again during the two weeks she said she was going to fix it).

This is literally the worst class I have taken at CMU. There is simply zero structure to this course. If you happen to see this too late and are taking this class right now, do not go to lecture; I haven't and I score quite well on the tests by just cramming the night before. Also, do your wiki stuff.

All the material and her very often irrelevant lectures completely ruined my love of linguistics. 
She's a very nice person, but tries too often to act like our therapists, asking us what's stressing us out, and when we tell her that she gives us too much unnecessary work she does absolutely nothing about it.

Although Bjorndahl is a really sweet and caring person, I heavily disliked the structure of this class. OLIs, homeworks, long Psets, quizzes, canvas assessments, don't even get me started on the wikis... it was way, way too much work. Definitely don't take this class if you were looking for an easy A gened course.

There are few long assignments for this course. Procrastination not recommended. The professor is nice and caring, but her lectures can get boring. She is open to help you with any questions you have. The class was interesting in the beginning, when we learned about IPA and transcription, but learning grammar rules was less engaging.

This professor gives the most mundane work I've seen in my entire life. In an effort to "get to know" her students she cold calls them and spends her time memorizing students and calling on them when they clearly do not want to be part of the discussion. She claims that her problem sets gets easier as they go on but they get harder

Gives too much work and expects students to complete it all. Does not thoroughly review material and has unorganized slides. Never take her class unless you want to major in linguistics. Assigns work on things she hasn't taught, gives extensions then acts upset when students do not do well. Spends too much time talking about Canadian identity.
Way to fast and very disorganzied slides. Talks about irrelevant things a lot and does not provide much explanation for the relevant material
Christina is probably my favorite professor I've had. Fantastic person, very down-to-earth. Will be understanding about extensions (within reason). Made me feel heard during discussions.
Cool professor. Adapted the course amazingly to remote learning. I was a pretty terrible student in this course I really wanted an easy gen-ed, and this was NOT it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Some questions in the midterm exam also appeared (albeit slightly modified) in the final. Btw she can literally remember everyone's name. It's like magic lol

Christina is great! Her lectures are comparatively interesting, yet nothing super mind blowing. She is funny and will oftentimes poke fun at herself. While lecture isn't always mandatory in NOL 80-180, I would definitely advise attending most (if not all) of them.

Christina is a fantastic professor who is extremely passionate about the course and her personality/attitude makes the course enjoyable. The class has required lecture attendance with daily 'clicker quizzes' that are pretty easy. The textbook is recommended but not necessarily needed to do well in the class if you pay attention during lecture.

He's very nice, but not the best at explaining things. His lectures are very "definition" heavy, very non-interactive, and the examples he goes through are hard to understand sometimes. He already knows the material so he'll do the example really quickly, ask if we have questions and then most of us are so lost that we don't know what to ask.

Pretty nice guy, but lectures are a bit of a slog and are unengaging. Not very good at explaining concepts, but class is overall pretty easy, so I guess that makes up for it. Tests sometimes come with extra credit questions.

Lectures are almost entirely just writing down definitions. His homeworks and tests are extremely easy, and hes an overall funny and nice guy. Would only take his class if I knew the material beforehand (he is not very good at explaining). If I had him for anything else other than Calculus 1, this review would have been different.
Rocky start, but sailed the ship home. Good guy!

Professor Bourne has a great attitude and brings a lighthearted energy to class! His calculus class 21-120 can be hard to follow at times, it is lecture heavy with little engagement. It often feels he is moving too quickly, and the class has gotten collectively lost multiple times. Too which he responded with enthusiasm to help us.

Good professor with a fair course structure that makes an A pretty achievable. He explains things well in class (slides on Canvas too) and does practice problems on each topic. He explains the answers to the practice problems and answers all questions in class. Course is made up of 3 exams, weekly HWs (graded on completion), and a team-based essay.

His accent with the combination of his habit to look down and talk and stray off topic, sometimes made it hard to actually understand what he is saying in class. The midterms are kind of hard. Overall, he is a nice dude, but I pretty much had to learn everything on my own.
THICK Accent but he seems like a genuine guy. The class was pretty plain but that was most likely because there are not so many ways to teach a lower-level accounting class.
Good guy.
Easy class if you have any semblance of a brain, tests are light work. You can tell he really cares and he is super understanding about grades and deadlines.
Great guy, really tries to make lectures interesting. Uses real-world examples to engage participants. He's a beast.
awesome and chill guy. class was boring but easy, and I can see the value of it in other classes
Professor Routledge was an amazing teacher. He used a lot of real-world examples within his lectures which was nice.
The class is way too easy, honestly at times felt pretty unnecessary. Routledge is fun tho, so that made up for it.
Very easy professor for Business Science. Rarely ever needed to pay attention to lectures, and still did well for the class.

No one ever knew what he was really teaching but just make sure you know probability. The home works are somewhat random while the quizzes are not the difficult if you do the practice ones. The final exam was definitely harder than all the other tests. All tests are online and attendance may be taken through Menti.

This is a freshman business requirement. My section was large so I never connect with him. He seemed nice. The exam was challenging for me. The class is basically an overview of everything you will learn in Tepper.
most people didn't go to lecture and it was chill

Professor Routledge's Business Science class was incredible. Every lecture covered real world scenarios. He is an extremely well respected professor at the Tepper School of Business and he normally teaches Finance grad students but I'm SO HAPPY that he chose to teach this undergrad freshman class. He was so much fun and an easy A.

I took ESE 105 with Sinopoli. It seemed like he mostly does really high level classes so he didn't know how to teach an intro.

Hes a nice guy and is funny but he's horrible at teaching. Hell read out the textbook and copy it onto the board.

Now that Ive gone through the course, even I think I could teach the topics better and more concretely.

Professor Sinopoli is a great professor. He's dedicated and he's receptive. Lectures are great because he takes positive and negative feedback (shoutout to his controls class WHUTTTT) and he will stay extra hours and hold extra office time just to help you  out if you're stuck.
Take his class, you won't regret it.

He is the worst Biology Professor I can ever wish for. Yes, he is nice, but his class is horrible!!!! He never stated things clearly, everything is not in order, and he always go off track on our materials.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's the kindest human being in CMU! Blessed to have him as our advisor and teacher. His lectures are amazing and he makes sure everyone understands
Professor Brasier was one of my favorite instructors. Very lenient and cared about his students. I highly recommend taking any class with this professor.
Great professor, very responsive to communications and helpful with work throughout the semester. His lectures were great and the exams were easy if you went to lectures
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof Brasier is amazing! definitely one of the best professors I had in all my time at CMU. He is caring and kind and provides all the resources you need in order to succeed in his class. I would highly recommend taking one of his courses if you get the chance!

I took Neurobiology of Disease with Dr. Brasier. He is an excellent professor. The textbook "Psychopharmacology" is not necessary at all, I took the class and learned 100% of the material from his lecture and my class notes. You definitely need to show up because a lot of participation points are awarded for the class. He really is a great man!

He really cares, and is the most available professor around. He wants you to learn and will go out of his way to do it. Sometimes lectures drag on. Tests are extremely fair, straight forward. Highly recommend.

DJ is a very nice person and his lectures are high-quality; his examples can get a little awkward though. He wants students to succeed. He will answer all questions, and he holds review sessions before every exam. He also offers a lot of practice material before each exam. One exam can be half-weighted and the tests are very fair. Highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: Dr. Brasier is a very good professor. He seems to really care for all of his students. Has great funny analogies. The only problem I have would be that he tends to ramble off quite a bit, so you don't end up covering a lot of material and have to read the textbook. But he does answer all questions and engages students well.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Brasier is one of the nicest people I have met. He is willing to meet outside of class and responds to emails quickly. The class is quite easy if you go to class. He mainly teaches verbally with little writing on the board so pay attention to what he says as that is mainly what he grades on. Definitely recommend taking this class i loved it!
He is the best professor I've every met.

An excellent professor- the course I took with him is probably my favorite course I've taken so far at CMU.  He's a skilled and accessible lecturer who really cares about student learning and success.  The course felt fairly easy, but I still learned a massive amount.  Strongly recommended.
Great lecturer! Much better than other bio professors. Very nice, helpful, kind, and supportive. Also very entertaining.
A really great teacher. Always willing to help his students out with going the extra mile. Totally amazing.

He really cares about students, and is very patient with students. For the final paper, professor spent lots of time reading through the academic papers that students chose and intended to analyze and write paper on.  Course lectures are clear and helpful. Beside the textbook, outside academic papers are often used in this course to help learning.

Wonderful teacher. Let the class decide what we wanted to focus on the most. Had very interesting guest speakers that either were doctors specific to the disease we were focusing on or suffered from a disease. Consisted mostly of exams and a final paper. Lots of group class work (as attendance quizzes). The exams were easy if you went to the review

Lots of really entertaining analogies - keeps people engaged fairly well. Sometimes can get a bit technical and sometimes has to think of best way to explain something, but is always willing to help out if you email him with questions. Posts presentations online. Clicker and warm-up questions required. Strict about technology.
A great professor who is very invested in teaching and will do anything to help students. Had a really fun final exam for the class and a lot of fun activities throughout.

Very bad professor. Course is practically taught by the TAs. He was advised by the TAs that the final was too long and too hard yet he gave it anyways. Did not offer any regrade request on the final. Does not respond to his email. I highly advise you take 150 in the spring.

* lectures and lecture notes are basically useless, and make you want to sleep.
* never interact with his student, dude doesn't even have an account on piazza & instructor OH.
* impossible final, with no curve and no regrade option.

I got an A solely because I learned functional programming before, avoid taking 150 with him if you can.

He is definitely very knowledgeable about the topic, but he is definitely not good at explaining it. Notes and lectures were not helpful, he teaches with the assumption you are already good at CS(even though it is intro level). The most I learned was from recitation and office hours which was led by TAs only. Overall not a pleasant experience.

Really unpleasent experience. He teaches in the Falls, do not take his course and prefer the very good spring time 15-150 professors. Lectures ended up being entirely worthless and hw often was not well related. You are entirely reliant on the TAs
Terrible professor that writes awful exams. His lectures and lecture notes are borderline useless.

The TAs are incredible and will carry you through the class. As for Brookes, he's quite a snooze of a professor. In a class of 150 students, lecture attendence was high at first, and dropped to about 30 people(including TAs) because just about every student saw that his lectures are worthless. Horrible lecture notes, doesn't respond to emails.
Very funny professor but can not teach. What he teach has nothing to do with the homework, and you will always find yourself wondering when was this taught.
Good class. The tests are really tough and long - be prepared.

Not a bad professor. Cares a lot about the course and sometimes makes it very challenging. Lectures are boring but he puts up slides so you don't really need to go. Each hw takes about 8 hrs to complete. Challenging midterm, okay final - do past midterms and KNOW foldl and foldr. Enjoyable course if taken seriously.
He has very loud music/videos in his lecture slides

Did a good job of presenting otherwise dull and drab material - lecture slides are well designed and cover the necessary topics, and there's always music and funny pictures to amuse you (if you stay awake). Impossible midterm (avg 39), but class is curved. TAs have more effect on your 212 experience, since they write/grade the labs (rather poorly).
We love him he's such a wonderful man

Prof. Budnik is one of my favorite profs at CMU. He is a teaching professor and it shows, he is very good at breaking down concepts and making them understandable over the course of a single lecture. He 100% cares about his students, and goes the extra distance to help students with internship/job advice. Highly recommend.

Mark Budnik is the best professor that I have had here throughout my CMU career. He treats us as if we were his actually children. He makes complicated topics super simplified within a short span during lectures. Highly recommend taking this class if you love analog circuits. Labs are also super fun. The workload just about right.
Amazing prof who really cares about his students, favorite professor ive had even though i didn't enjoy the class material

Prof Budnik is an amazing guy. He really cares about his students and wants them to do great in his class. The class itself could do better but the professors offer a lot of resources to help the students succeed. Definitely one of the best professors in the department
Awesome Professor, provides great feedback and cares about improving

She is so nice and caring. Really enjoys teaching. A very friendly and approachable person too outside of lecture. However the grading is difficult. She tries to reduce the average grade whenever she can. Beware.

Professor Burke highly values first impressions--both ways. She will put on a facade for the first week. Then, she will become herself--monotone and callous. She does not care if you are struggling with mental health. If you do not look interested and excited as well as speak in class often, your will grade suffer (a lot).
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Burke is a nice, helpful teacher, but her class is full of group projects that require a ton of work. This class is not an easy A- it requires a ton of work (Although that work is easy).
Lectures aren't that great, instructions are too ambiguous, and gives AIVs for no reason. The labs also take a god awful amount of time, and don't think that you can do the labs without going to OH.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took 15346 (not 213) and I thought it was really good. We covered so many comp arch concepts and I definitely learned a lot. I'm honestly shocked at the rating here since I really enjoyed his lectures. I still have the project from this class on my resume. I do think Professor Railing can be a little disorganized tho in terms of course logistics

His own words on piazza "I have contemplated adding a "your final grade cannot be more than X% different than your final exam", but there is no such rule in 15-213/-513." 

As a remainder, for summer 2024, final: 30%, lab: 50%, wa: 20%, in case he decides to make things more difficult for others.

Worst professor ever. He changes the schedule randomly to accommodate his vacations, provides poorly taught lectures, and lacks supervision of TAs. This results in materials created by TAs being filled with errors. The schedule was crazily unrealistic, with two labs due on the same day with no grace days, which is one day after the final.
Avoid at all means.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Railing is one of the nicest professors I've had. His lectures are very engaging and he puts a lot of effort to make difficult concepts simple to understand. I'm surprised by most of the ratings here and I believe that people are rating the difficulty of the class rather than how he is as a professor.
Don't take his class.
Literally the worst prof I've ever had. Not just bad lectures but also the rude attitude.

He's been grinding with HIS thoughts and ideas all the time while the course's assignments require something else. No help with the actual coursework or stuff, and I don't understand why he is the professor of this lesson.
It makes no sense for him to assign students do all the study without answering the questions. He is supposed to be the professor after all.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: extremely aloof in person as well as on piazza and gives off an aura of arrogance
Extremely impatient and rude to me. Could at least pretend to care about students but he didn't.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever seen. He is extremely smart but fails to deliver his knowledge to students. I'm an above-average student and still need to rewind his online teaching materials to find unstated assumptions. Worse than that, he likes using long and discontinuous sentences and is somehow rude to students.

Dude's whole personality is "weird flex but okay". Loves talking about how advanced he was at CS growing up, or the saxophone, or building legos. Knows a lot about CS but isn't the best at teaching. Really cold on piazza responses as well.
He is extremely rude, and disrespectful to students.

Professor Railing has helped me multiple times when I needed an extension due to a persistent medical issue. He has greatly helped me through the course and the course itself is super interesting if you want it to be. Pay attention in lecture and when doing the homework consult the slides. Overall great class so far and a professor who helps a lot.

I think Professor Railing is an extremely clear lecturer. I am taking 15-213 on Zoom and greatly appreciate his clarity and patience with delivering the material to people who've never seen it before.

Professor Railing is the meanest and most confusing professor/teacher I have ever had. He may be an expert on his subject, but he doesn't know how to deliver his knowledge to his students at all, making all his lectures trash. My and my classmates search for past 213 videos on youtube.
He shows little interest in students' learning and never answers questions. Think carefully before you take his 213.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's the worst professor ever and doesn't care about students' learning and well-being at all. Super rude and mean to students too. Avoid him at all costs.

He is the worst professor I had. He's rude, doesn't care about the teaching crap he keeps talking about, and just wants you to get PONTS by suffering. Ask questions? No way he answers that. Don't fall for his soft voice, the guy is real trash. Like the kind, that doesn't even deserve to be near a school.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: A little rude at times.

Probably one of the people that have a lot of difference in what they say and what they do. Usually expects the students to be prepared when they take his course. If I knew everything, WTH would I take your course? Also, he will never help outside of class, because he expects you to know everything. If possible, avoid him.

She’s one of the most caring professors I’ve ever had and checked up on me many times throughout the semester (I was struggling with my mental health). Very flexible with extensions as well. Overall she puts herself on an equal level with her students, unlike other professors that love the power trip. Very sweet and kind.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: Do not take this class if you dont want a shot ton of work everyweek
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Burakowski is great! The lectures and the content are very interesting. Though there are two hw assignments a week, you can easily do them in 10 minutes. The exams consisted of short answers and a concept map which was not too bad, but they took a while. Fun and easy class!

As a technical major wanting to take an interesting course, I was disappointed not only by the lack of professionalism and subjective grading, but also by the standard of teaching.  Also more concerned with self-promotion than student learning.
No Comments
No Comments
Hopeless egomaniac is hell-bent on diguising her lecture class as a discussion class. If you disagree with her agenda, be prepared for low grades.
Very good professor

Dr. Doonan is incredible and I am so thankful for the time I got to spend with her as a student. She was always available for extra help if needed and was so supportive - she truly wants to see her students succeed.

Both 343 and 344 are two of the best classes to take at CMU. They are well organized and designed by a team of dedicated instructors led by Dr. Doonan. Dr. Doonan and her team are a force of nature -- making sure every day is full of intense experiments and students walk out with full understanding of all the core molecular bio knowledge.

Wow, the last comment left on this page was meant for Lynley Doonan, I am a little tired if you can't tell. But this Dr. Doonan was a great advisor and is 100% the reason I was able to graduate early and deserves a shoutout too so thank you to the other Dr. Doonan, haha

This class was hard but Dr. Doonan cares so much about her students and it really shows. I really respect Dr. Doonan a lot and would be a much worse scientist if not for this class. Never had a Professor care this much or work this hard to make sure students learned something. I would 100% recommend, great lectures too

She is amazing! She truly cares about her students and their success. She creates such a fun environment in lab and bakes cake and brownies all the time. Her grading criteria is very clear. If you follow her directions and do the extra credit, you should do well. She is always available for help (emails back quickly) and deserves a lot of respect.

Dr. Doonan cares deeply for her students. She's insane, but wants her students to succeed. She doesn't really show this until you get to know her though. Her lab reports are hell. But I will admit that I learned a lot. The grading is very inconsistent.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This class is a lot of work but Dr. Doonan is supportive and works with you. She forces you to think very critically and will not think highly of you if you aren't putting in the effort.

She will like you if you go to class and participate. Make a good first impression and an effort to understand material and you will get good grades. LOTS of homework but I'm pretty sure 76-108 is just like that. Would recommend 76-107/76-106 if you are undecided on which minis to take for FYW. Expect to have to reach out to get specific feedback.

It's clear she cares a lot about her students however, she does not give great feedback on assignments so it is difficult to know where you need to improve. The class was more homework heavy in the first half of the semester and more student-guided in the second half. Overall, a very enjoyable FYW course and I would recommend it.

The sweetest professor ever. She really cares about her students and wants them all to do well. Class was genuinely pretty fun too. She is a very fair grader a long as you put in effort. Do your assignments and go to class and you will most likely walk out with the grade you want.
So sweet and understanding. An easy grader as long as you put in effort and genuinely makes an effort to get to know her student. Love her!

She is an easy grader and all you really need to do to get A's on assignments and papers is to turn something in with some semblance of effort. If you have experience with essay writing, you'll be fine. The first half of the semester is very structured, there is a lot of reading and small assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: As long as you make effort when writing essays, you will get a good grade. Do the homeworks to receive credit. Participation in class is also encouraged.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: She seemed to be very interested in the course content and I found her to be very approachable. Class is not hard as long as you do the work for it, which admittedly there was quite a bit of.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I literally want to throw up whenever I work on her assignments and midterms. This course if ridiculously tedious. 6 midterms, 4 papers, 2 chapter assignments per week, I have never had a course that took me this much time. Material gets retested and questions re-asked over and over again. Not much learnt from the course, very disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Dr. Carver remains one of my favorite professors at CMU. She puts an extraordinary amount of thought into her teaching methods (makes sense, considering her field!) and makes a huge effort to get to know her students even in a big lecture course. If you have a chance to take one of her higher-level courses, I also highly recommend!

Dr. Carver is by far one of the sweetest and nicest professors at CMU. Her class is extremely manageable and the workload is light, provided you stay on top of your work. She gives so many opportunities for extra credit and her final is optional. Willing to work with students AND she's a very fair grader.

Professor teaches lectures very well in class.  Is very easy to understand.  I feel as if test and assignment questions focus too much on course framework, that it detracts from the learning in the course.  It feels unfocused.  She knows the subject material really well, though.
Taking this class takes up more of my time than all four of my other classes.
Child Development - best class ever
makes class mind-bendingly monotonous
She uses mostly slide shows to teach but what she adds to the class is also interesting. The papers she gives aren't very fun but that is not her fault, it's the classes general requirements.
Very friendly and helpful.

Amazing professor, definitely cares about his students. More than willing to help students after class if you don't understand anything from lecture. Exams are 80% of the grade which is somewhat unfortunate but the material is very learnable if you put in the time. Would 100% take another class with Prof. Campbell.
My favorite class this semester! Professor Campbell gave very engaging lectures, and the class content was very interesting

Campbell is a great professor who made me look forward to the class even when it was super challenging. Three midterms each making up 20% of your grade. The final is another 20%. The lectures are very dense. Grading on exams and homework is annoying because they are looking for specific wording and phrases. 10/10 would take again

Easily my favorite class this semester. Lectures are engaging and he tells funny stories. Homework is weekly, but I found it interesting and fairly easy to get done. Attendance isn't mandatory, but there are quizzes every Monday. Flashcards are helpful for the class. Definitely possible to get an A, just put in the effort.

Dr. Campbell is an amazing lecturer and is incredibly engaging. Easily one of my favorite professors in the entire university. Caters to each student in the course, across all colleges and majors. Starts everyone out on an even playing field and assumes no prior knowledge. Always curves grades and expects you to really put your best effort forward.
Campbell is the man. Tough class, great stories. Read the textbook, go to SI.

He literally puts stuff on the tests that was never talked about. Going to SI is a MUST or you wont pass. You can hope and pray he uses the same questions as past exams but if he doesn't, its really hard to interpret what hes asking in his questions. Tests are unfair. Oh yeah, and lectures suck, he never finishes a thought he just rambles on
Participation in class isn't required, nor is note taking, but without it, you will fail. Study a lot for this class!! He was enthusiastic and made it interesting.
Really solid teacher. Would definitely recommend him as a teacher. Not the easiest Interp section, but it's worth it because you will learn a lot.
Great teacher. Class is fun and makes you think. I cannot recommend his class enough. You will have to work for a good grade, but it's worth it because you will learn a lot.
Awesome class. Far superior to other 76-101 sections. He's funny, too.
really cool, really awesome, just truly glorious.
Great Class.
Interesting course, tough but fair grader, cool style.  Encourages interesting discussions.
No Comments

Respectable, professional, and sweet guy but MAN OH MAN are his lectures kinda insane for an intro macro course. He does tend to overcomplicate things, and made micro seem like a breeze which usually is the opposite. Tolerable though- if you attend office hours and really understand the material, you're good to go. Plus, his curve is pretty great!

He's an okay professor: very smart and clearly knows his stuff, but struggles to explain it to students who have little to no background in the subject. The material itself isn't too hard. It's the way he formats his tests that makes things confusing. Study hard and go to office hours if you need help, and you'll be fine.
overcomplicates some things, but is better than the alternative professor options

This man is infuriating. I am sure he is very intelligent, but he is horrible at teaching. He just talks AT YOU, and half the time doesn't make any sense. He uses the most complex ways to explain the most basic topics, and it makes it so confusing. I am good at Econ. I am literally an Econ major and I would NEVER recommend anyone EVER take him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I really loved Professor Sleet's class! He took a lot of time explaining difficult topics and was always available to help even if it was not his office hours. I came into the course not knowing what to expect but ended up loving the course mainly because of Professor Sleets great teaching style!
Exams were very difficult, and this class was heavily test dependent. Had to take some substances that I was not proud of just to do well in this class. Would not recommend..
Real good lectures
Exams are extremely difficult, borderline impossible. He is so nice and such an incredible lecturer however. But his exams are unfair.

This is absolutely a professor that is one of his kind. Ridiculously unrelated and unhelpful lectures, more quizzes than hw, while the structure for grading is not systematically designed. The worst thing about this class is that it does not really teach anything useful about "macroeconomics", I for sure feel like I learned more in my AP econ class

Chris Sleet is an amazing professor, in terms of teaching abilities and clarifying questions. His tests however are EXTREMELY difficult, because they are designed in the British format. Getting an A is very hard.

This class was tough and Prof Sleet seems very strict at first. But if you go to lectures, review all of his notes, and make sure you understand the material, you'll be fine. If you need more help, he is more than willing to meet with students during office hours and at other times. He is very passionate about the subject, which can be refreshing.
Lectures unrelated to textbook, but he has a British accent!
Clear and commanding and he knows what he is doing.

Grad-level in name only. Tons of busywork, Thanksgiving-week homework due 2 days before the final (happy holidays). Reading homework was not helpful especially when you already need to read a lot for your research. Grads: take Neural Signal Processing instead. This one won't help your research but burden it. Undergrads: take this if you want pain.

This class consists of plenty of work outside of class, including asynchronous lectures, mini participation assignments, and extra credit assignments. He makes the lecture interesting, which is where plenty of professors miss the mark. He doesn't prioritize this class very much, but it is a pretty simple class taught very well.

While he seems to have his priorities elsewhere, Professor Chase is a captivating lecturer. His homeworks were a bit frustrating, but doable, and there were only 5 of them total. I would only wish that I had paid more attention, as I think I could have gotten an A.

His priorities are kinda elsewhere, but fortunately it translates into the class being relatively easy and chill. He posts everything in lecture before class, so you don't even have to show up. The problem sets can be confusing, but going to TA office hours helps. The tests are non-cumulative, memory-based, and based off only the lecture notes.

Professor Chase is an extremely smart person. If you read about his research and publication history you will be inspired by the work he has done. His teaching is quite the opposite. You have no idea what you are expected to know in the class. Never holds office hours. Takes forever to return grades or provide solutions to past assignments.

Guys his lectures are all based on textbook readings, our class had to end up paying 100 for the textbook even if we got it another way so some people paid double. Attendance is mandatory and he rambles during lectures, tests are hyperspecific and ask questions that rlly don't relate much to what we learn. PMO!

Loved Professor Chin for this class. His jokes were quite interesting and kept us entertained. The only assignments you submit are graded, most of which are at the end of the semester. Would take one of his classes again! Make sure to exactly follow instructions of assignments or you'll be graded down.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He rambles on the entire lecture while expecting you to copy down the slide show behind him. usually doesn't even give time to copy down notes. He continuously goes off on random tangents, and honestly doesn't make clear what he is trying to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of my few complaints about Professor Chin is that oftentimes, specific facts mentioned in the textbook only once are able to be tested. However, if you study the textbook and lecture slides well, you should be ok. Tough paper grader, so go to office hours. He offers up to 5 percentage points of extra credit though, which is kind of unheard of.

To be completely fair and honest, Professor Chin is a somewhat nice guy and all but he's an awful lecturer. He then expected you to know all this random stuff about political theory even though he didn't even teach it. The university should do everyone a favor and make Professor Chin just do research instead of teach because he is awful. 0/10!

if you want an A or B you probably should not take John Chin's classes. very very harsh grading. he is a nice guy until he grades your papers and quizzes. you can do all the readings thoroughly like i did and still end up with 60s and 70s for grades.

Chin is just a boring dude, cares more about his research than his students' success. Highly recommended to stay away from Chin if you want an A... he gives out C's to his students because he grades harshly.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Ok so I am only writing this because Professor Chin doesn't deserve a 1-star rating. He is definitely a hard professor and sometimes his quiz questions are a little unfair. But I think overall, he cares about the students and is easy to work with if you're having trouble.

His class is extremely hard to get a good grade in, I had a 4.0 GPA until I took his course in which I received a C. He grades essays unfairly, and his quizzes are specific and based off of sometimes over 100 pages of reading. He's also very inflexible to improvement, stay away from his class!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Pretty caring for his students, his lectures are easy to follow
CG

Great lecturer, very knowledgable of the area. If you take his class you will learn a lot. That said, his grading criteria is absurd. He assigns more than 20 hours of work every week, but punishes extra work (the more code you write, the more places to deduct points from you). Hard work in his class do not pay off grade-wise. Not very empathetic.

An amazing professor! I had only mild interest in Irish history prior to the class, but it's now my favorite subject. A good slideshow. One class weekly devoted to class discussion. Opens class with fun roll calls so you get to know your classmates. And you can't beat his Irish accent.
He's a completely unfair grader.
Really great personality, interesting lectures, good use of media... I like the class format a lot and enjoy being there.
Awesome personality.  Will go the extra mile to help you.  SMART profesor.
Best professor that I had for histor here at CMU.  Very helpful and willing to go around your schedule to help you.  Great personality.  Nice person  Thanks so much Mr. McMahon

A super nice person.  Beyond excellent - great personality, caring and nice.  If all professors were as kind as he is - or was in my class - I would be a straight A student.  He makes class very interesting.   Thank you

Lectures are dry and dont help you understand the concepts. Homework and exams are also extremely difficult for no reason. Only good thing is that he curves, so as long as you can keep up with the content you can easily get a good grade.
Awful dont take him. Hopefully the curve is hefty because thats the only thing that can save me.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This professor is awful. Lectures are uninspiring, his slides are useless and exams/HW are unjustifiably hard. The material he tests on are much more difficult than what he discusses in class and even unrelated. He also explains things too quickly, then gets annoyed at students who ask clarifying questions. TLDR: DO NOT TAKE HIS COURSE.

His lectures are extremely difficult to understand and useless. He doesn't answer many questions from students, such as some math problems because "Calculus I is a prerequisite", even if the concepts aren't even covered in Calc 1. Oftentimes the homework is too difficult, and the tests are impossible to complete (avg score for midterm 1 was a 59).
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Clementi's lectures go fast and can be hard to understand, but it is reasonable given the amount of content he has to cover. The first week of lectures and homework are the hardestso don't be too afraid if the material seems overwhelming at first. Definitely a significant weekly time commitment and the tests are hard but everything is curved.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Might be the worst professor at NYU. You dont know your grade until the last minute, the criteria is so unclear that you would just end up with an B. Absolutly no one listens to his class - I would rather sit and do nothign at Washington Square Park than do go to one of his lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Terrible professor, terrible lectures. Everybody falls asleep during his classes and his grades are also extremely subjective.
Very intelligent Italian professor.  No problem with accent.  Nice guy.  Very into his research and not as much into clear teaching.
The man is a genuine genius--one of the professors you just have to take if you want to be exposed to the best a brain can ever offer.
Arrogant know it all.
This guy is hilarious and I loved going to class
Knows more about philosophy in his pinkie finger that the dumb people in his class who call him a simpleton.
Arrogant bumbling simpleton. Easy A, though.
Masterly, charming, old guy.
this man is retired but he was the worst person i have ever had this misfortune of meeting
A kind man with a lot of wisdom to share. Teaches as much about life as about poetry.
The smartest man I know!
Talks a lot, but is pretty awesome.
No Comments
Took his intro class. Awesome.
Hardcore.

very smart, occasionally cranky but always affectionate. if his comments seem cryptic, it means he respects you. it means he wants you to figure it out on your own but is still willing to give you a hint. i took three levels of workshop with him, and was sad there weren't more to take.
AWESOME.
One of the best teachers you can have if you come to class all the time.
This man is a genius.  I mean, he kep his job this long!  (I mean in this so not liberal school)

Overall, Professor Jahel is a good professor. It might be a little difficult to follow his pace in lectures because he explains pretty fast and his handwriting is sometimes not very clear, but he is very friendly and approachable when you talk to him and ask him questions. He will also give us curves on exams and extensions on homework.

Prof. Jahel may be hard to follow in lectures because of his accent/handwriting, but he actually tries to explain as clearly as possible and genuinely cares about students' learning experiences. He also gives extensions and curves pretty leniently.
great professor!

Professor Jahel's lectures were slightly hard to follow, but since this review is being written during his first semester teaching, this isn't a valid criticism for the long term.

Homework load is light, but very open ended and sometimes confusing. I recommend reading the textbook (even though its not required) to fully understand key topics
This class is great!

Professor Compeau is an incredible professor, His lectures are really engaging and he clearly puts in a lot of work into his classes and really cares about his student. I highly recommend taking his classes even if you didn't like biology in high school.
worst unprofessional tutor i have ever seen

Took lot of effort to prepare lectures and notes. Amazing work in inspiring wonder in subject as well as creating a narrative connecting all topics. However, went in a little too much detail sometimes and sometimes didn't cover some topics very well.
Great professor

I've taken 3 drawing classes from Cooper and saw tremendous improvement in not only mine, but everyone else's work. However great a professor he is, he is NOT an easy A at all. He's super critical of drawings and knows what he's talking about. Super talented.
He is probably one of the most interesting professors you will ever have. He tends to tell a lot of stories that seem pointless but they alway have a meaning...plus he is a great drawing teacher!
The best professor in architecture ever.

Doug Cooper is so pro. You'll learn a lot from him, as long as you're willing to work hard and improve yourself. You don't have to be a good drawer to do well in his class; he appreciates your effort, and always loves improvement. Don't fall too behind on portfolios, because they coincide with Studio deadlines all the time.
Best professor in Carnegie Mellon's school of architecture, hands down.
Such a harsh grader, never got an A on an assignment. The most boring lectures in my life. Just horrible!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Such a harsh grader. If you want a decent GPA you should stay away. She is very nice and caring but just grade unfairly.
Really good teacher.  Includes students, makes class interesting, knows history very well.  Have had some trouble getting in touch with her via email, but generally very good prof.
Really amazing professor: kind, helpful, and has interesting classes.

Amazing teacher who really cares about her students.  Decent amount of reading, but all of the grades in the course come from essays written at home about the texts, so if you keep up with the readings and put effort into the papers, it should be a fairly easy A.
Nice enough person, but the workload is crazy! 10 books and 7 papers plus a final exam. Plus, her lectures are boring. Bring something to read to keep you entertained.

I took the Grand Challenge Seminar with Professor Cox-Boyd she is a great professor. I learned a lot from her and she is so nice and funny. Her lectures were very engaging and interesting. The class itself is easy, write your essays, show up to class, do the group project, you will get an A. Will miss her so much and would love to have her again.

Kind of a bird course. Exam might contain weird stuff not really related to the course, e.g. the content of a movie, so be prepared. Otherwise quite an easy course, especially if you learnt AP psych before.
Class was fun!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: NO NEED TO GO TO CLASS. Read the textbook if you're an overachiever. OR just go to the review session day before an exam, and fill out the study guide (then study that). grading policies are DESIGNED to give you an A, so if you're not getting an A in this??? what are you doing??? worth going to class for quality bantz tho boyd is legit the best
If you know everything on the study guide you are guaranteed a high A on the test. The study guides with answers are available online and she reuses the study guides.
She was really into teaching the topic. I took her course more than ten years ago and I remember her enthusiasm, pretty fair
She's awesome! She's super funny and makes the class super interesting. Her Social Psychology class was by far my favorite class I've taken so far.

She is a great professor. She is energetic and uses many real life examples to help you understand the material. She makes the class very interesting. The tests are pretty fair but your grade heavily depends on the TAs.

Very interesting class. Few Papers and a little bit of homework. Easy to do well. Do study guide for test and you will do fine. Review sessions before test with TAs are great and make test easy/ Checks attendance by having people sign in (so if your running late a friend can do it for you). Shes not to helpful in office hours
Excellent professor. Class is really interesting with lots of personal examples. Easier if you have some background in psych. checks attendance, but only used if you're on the boarder of grades.
Best professor ever!
Very entertaining stories that she tells during lectures which relate to the topic at hand. Still remember the story of how she told us how she met her husband.
Very good lecturer, but goes off on tangents very often. Exams often do not reflect the class, and she has very poor organizations skills.

Lectures are interesting, but not necessarily clear in their relationship to the assigned material. Exams are straight forward, but require study of exam review sheets -- there may be material tested that is NOT in the textbook. TAs and organization needs work. Completely possible A if you do the work assigned and reach out for help.
teaching is good. Organization and TAs are poor.

TAs grade completely arbitrarily, no consistency in grading system. Professor chooses tricky questions for tests just to make them harder--can have complete understanding of material and still not get an A. Only talks about her personal experiences during lectures, making them pointless.
No Comments
No Comments
not a difficult prof, but you do learn something and she is helpful
don't bother going to class...it's straight from the books and her lecture outlines (verbatim) are online
No Comments
Prof. Meyers is great! Super helpful during office hours.
It's great that he wrote the textbook for M&I, but about 20% of worked problems and examples are wrong.
felt like Mr. Rogers was my professor, super nice man and very invested in his students success

Professor Meyer is amazing! He has a nice sense of humor and is engaging to listen to. He is super easy to meet with outside of class and clearly puts a lot of effort into the Canvas page to support our learning.

I enjoyed Curtis Meyers class, although sometimes he could do a better job of explaining why we need to know things or being specific with his worked examples during class. Definitely don't be afraid to ask questions. Super understanding (let me take an online quiz after due date bc I forgot) & easy to schedule private meetings to review material.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Group projects

reviews.comment: Great Guy. Lectures are kinda dry but overall good. You don't really have to go to class since the textbook just has the exact same notes but more in depth, but he makes attendance a grade. The tests and homework are pretty chill once you get the hang of the types of problems. Final project was chill. Overall great

M&I is a great physics class. Nothing (other than some quantum and relativity stuff) stated without proof. Course project is optional and replaces one exam grade. Curtis wrote the textbook for the course and so it contains better versions of the lecture notes (more in-depth derivations, often). Homework is very helpful for learning the content.
Excellent teacher

fair tests, good teacher and I never felt overwhelmed by the course work. Can be dry at times during lectures but otherwise conducts informational, interactive lectures. A great teacher to have if your interested in physics.
Meyer is the man.  He's an awesome teacher.  Very fair, excellent fashion sense.  I know of no one who dislikes this man.

TBH: Cuonzo is not your average math teacher; he's an extraordinary maestro in the world of matrix algebra. With an infectious passion for the subject and a knack for making complex concepts understandable, he stands as a beacon of inspiration and knowledge in the classroom. Rate: 11/10
Cuonzo really knows his stuff and is very caring; however, sometimes I get lost in his eyes because he is so attractive.
yikes
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: Class was a joke.  Dude showed random video clips from movies that were only very weakly connected to the lecture in order to win over the younger crowd.  I learned nothing other than that I don't want anything to do with this guy.

Professor more interested in making friends than making a meaningful lesson. Culbertson's course is essentially just him reciting stories and giving students gifts in an attempt win favor. All in all, the class gave me little help when I started my own company the following year. Go find another entrepreneurship class, you will be glad you did.
To be honest I found him biased towards some people. He talks about unrelated things. Will not take any of his classes again.
Great guy, hilarious, easy-going. Talk up and he'll love you.
He knows how to teach outside of classrooms and textbooks. Recommend taking follow-up courses after Intro, because he really drills on important skills you should have when starting up a venture.
Intro to Entrep. was the best course I've taken at CMU thanks to R.F.  Incredible professor, and a great guy who really cares about his students.

Probably the first genius level professor I've taken a class under. But be absolutely sure to go to his lectures consistently or you will fall behind fast, and it is very hard to get up to pace. Nevertheless, he is very approachable and kindhearted.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Cumming's lectures are clear and easy to follow, although I hope he could go a little faster. He is very articulate and explains subtle things well. He is very willing to help, offers hints/comments on proofs if you email him. Midterm and Final are take-home, and the problems are pretty standard. Definitely recommend.
At first you might think Prof Cummings is too theoretical, but when you start to keep up with the proofs, it makes Linear Algebra more understandable and interesting. Great professor.

His teaching style is very difficult to follow and often times his lectures had nothing to do with the problem sets assigned. Teaches in a very abstract way and hardly provides examples during his lecture.
He never provides context; rather, he teaches in the most abstract way possible; proofs can be symbolically slick and appealing, but few people seem to understand what is really going on
He is simply one of the best mathematics teacher in the world. A true mathematician, his class is so intriguing and proof so inspiring that you do not want to blink...

I am a recent graduate who had Discrete Mathematics with Cummings.  The grading was not clear, and at times his explanations were very unclear.  He was not accessible at all and never returned a single email.
Echoing the student who took Math Studies with Professor Cummings, his teaching of Commutative Algebra was just amazingly effective and thought-provoking.  Excellent.
No Comments

Professor Cummings is a wonderful teacher and is one  of the most approachable teachers in the math department.  He likes to do a lot of his lessons on the fly, but this is not a problem because he can prove almost anything off the top of his head.  He loves to help and is very easy going.

Very poor lectures—he will ask vague questions with an expected answer and become frustrated and rude when students don't immediately guess the point he wants to make. Essays had very few comments on them that offered little insight, and he is very unresponsive outside of class. Material he offers during lectures is easily found in any textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Often wrong about the material. Students had to point to context and teach him in class because it seemed as if he had not deeply understood many of his own readings. I would recommend taking someone more knowledgable if possible.

His approach was heavy on pretension but light on substance, giving the impression of rigor without delivering real educational value. The structure of the course often felt like a hollow approximation of what a college class should be, but without meaningful content to back it up.

I genuinely can't comprehend why he's put in charge of so many mandatory classes. 
I swear, this guy gets a power trip out of pulling the most obscure detail out of readings for final exams. Participation is mandatory but don't expect to engage in any meaningful dialect either. Feels like taking a high school class if the teacher had a massive ego.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Horrible professor who gives really rude comments on your papers, very little feedback, and his class is so boring. He will ask specific questions in class, but no way you will get it right and he gets annoyed afterward. Hardest grader I have ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The film analysis is more "learn by doing", or more accurately "learn by watching him do" for large portions of class. This may work for many but if you are looking for a systemic approach you will be disappointed. He will ask very broad questions and expect a specific answer (and sometimes be annoyed by the class not guessing the answer).
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This is a standard class. He has good slides and posts them on canvas. That said, I had a problem with how he treated me as a student. I needed more time to finish a HW due to my grandpa's funeral. I asked him for more time and to drop the late penalty, but he refused. I was very disappointed, especially since he presents himself as a family man.

I found Handron frustrating for several reasons. First off, he removes much of the more mature mathematical notation from his notes and homework, making theorems and problems overly complicated and wordy. In addition, his tests focus not on deep understanding, but on your ability to do elementary calculus and simple algebra extremely quickly.
