He tries his best, and is very kind, but manages to overcomplicate topics to such a degree that his lectures often take away from my learning rather than help. His lectures are more focused on mindless algebraic manipulations rather than intuition or practice problems.

I had to miss an exam due to a family emergency, which I told him about as soon as I knew. When I came later that week to take my make-up exam, he told me that he had "forgotten I scheduled the make-up exam until 20 minutes ago" and that he "hadn't even printed the test out yet." I was offered no extensions on that week's assignments either.
Demonstrates Newton's Law of Cooling by putting a brick in a freezer. Doesn't deserve the hate tbh
Absolutely awful at lecturing, cannot pay attention to him for my life. Frankly a bizarre experience. Overcomplicates a frankly easy subject to comical levels. Just terrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's the singular worst lecturer I've had at CMU. Every single lecture consists of 18 pages of handwritten notes, of which only 2 at most contain useful material, if at all. People who are taking diffeqs already know how to do elementary algebra, but he focuses all his attention on that instead of actually teaching the core concepts of the course.

Uses slides from previous years, and goes relatively fast through them. It's more important to follow them rather than writing everything down. Very clear and a lot of good outside help is available. HWs can be challenging but are very reasonable. Plenty of review problems for tests and no surprises on them. Highly recommend.
Third class I've taken with Handron. Very reasonable guy. Funny and interesting lectures.
Kind of unstable guy, doesn't know what he's talking about and babbles on about stuff that is unrelated to course content. Also not funny at all.
not reasonable

He's very funny (put a brick in the freezer to demonstrate Newton's Law of Cooling), and I liked his lectures quite a bit. It was clear he went out of his way to adapt to online class (and did so well). It's diffeq, so don't expect a cakewalk, but show up to lectures and study a bit and you'll be fine. HW is sometimes a bit long.
Amazing and interesting teaching. Easy quizzes and exams. Very friendly professor.
Honestly probably my favorite math professor, he always makes an effort to explain things within the context you're likely to see them, which is very helpful
Good at explanation materials. His office hours is very helpful!

Imagine getting all perfect scoreS on everything but the final and one quiz and still get a bad grade, this is how this guy treat his student. He intentionally give you very bad grade if you are way too ahead of the average score to drag you down. I dont know why someone will be such mentally ill to do this. Shouldnt even be a professor at first.

Professor Handron gives clear, descriptive lectures and often pauses to ensure that the students are keeping up. Take advantage of his office hours! It's obvious that it really does matter to him that his students learn, and I've never gone to his office hours and left with unanswered questions

Great person but has no logic AT ALL. His logic goes the opposite of all my other math professor. Go to lecture and you'll be scratching you hair off. Read the online notes written by another professor and you'll be fine. VERY boring lecturer too. Worst course I've taken.

Good person, but this man just does not know how to explain. I am honestly considering stop going to lecture and read textbook. I took Calc BC in High School and I cannot understand what Handron explains. This man needs to change the way he teaches.

Pretty good overall. Goes through material at a reasonable pace, but sometimes hard to understand his definitions until he does an example. Very good at answering questions and really cares about his students.

He was a good lecturer, exams were more difficult, seemed to think students actually cared about course material but most were just their to get their gen ed credit so he would be frustrated at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Really cool guy. Deserno tries hard to explain everything clearly. His problem sets are really inspirational. They're not easy but when you figure them out you do feel you learn a lot.

One of the best professors in the department in terms of teaching abilities. Explains everything clearly, keeps things interesting in classes yet always in context as well. Not always easy homework sets but they tend to give you an "AHA" moment once you figure out the problems.

Excellent professor. Very engaging and clearly interested in what he teaches. He is willing to explain concepts multiple ways to make sure everyone understands. Highly recommend his course Physics for Future Presidents.

Amazing prof and is able to really explain things really well. The course is reallly realllllllly fast paced and hard to keep up but as long as you go to class, pay attention, and do the homeworks, should be a solid A. Best professor and definitely the most fair I have ever had.
Really cool teacher. Knows his stuff and is very willing to help if you need anything. Is not the easiest grader, but he is very fair.
He is not easy, but a really good prof.
Made Intro class fun with cool experiments and grade curve
As a Mechanical engineer taking this class, I had NO idea what was going on.
Great class, great teacher.
No Comments
No Comments
Very fun class!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor, made my experience as a first year!
Lots of problems to do ... she makes you work for it but ok if you stick with it
She is a nice person but not the best professor.
She's so sweet and the lectures are awesome

Fully lecture-based. There is no interaction with students, which may make the lecture very boring and difficult to concentrate on. There were only three exams and no other assignments, which I personally think is not encouraging the learning process at all.

It was unbelievable to see such a positive vibe in the classroom, thanks to her. You can clearly see her enthusiasm and excitement of all the material. She is so interested in everything and takes time to get to know you. Would 11/10 recommend.
She is very passionate with her lectures, that makes interesting and entertainment.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really passionate, really engages you with each building

Diane Shaw inspired me continuously throughout my architectural education @ Carnegie Mellon. By far my favorite processor throughout my college experience. She has a way of making buildings come to life in a rich, passionate, funny way & challenged us to view architecture as a story. I took every class of hers that I could :)

Professor Shaw is an absolutely astounding professor, easily my favorite one from my six years at Carnegie Mellon.  I took every architecture class of hers that I could, and loved them all.  She makes it about more than just what's in the textbook: you learn about culture, the other arts, and history as well.  So much admiration for her!

Really funny, and really interested in what she teaches--and by virtue of that, makes it interesting to learn. Was only mildly curious about architectural history before I took the class, but the class turned out really interesting and even kind of fun. Don't miss a lecture if it can be helped.

Professor Lal has been by far my best professor at CMU. He is very charismatic and energetic when teaching the class. He provides very thorough feedback and truly cares about his students. He is very responsive to his email and always makes time for his students when they need help. Would definitely recommend classes he teaches.

Although some of the material was hard I always enjoyed going to lecture. He is a very caring professor, one time I submitted a big project late and he came up to me after class and asked if I needed any help and if everything was okay. Not just that but he is very charismatic. I really enjoyed having taken this core IS class with him.
He's really nice and understanding. He's not the most organized but he really takes time to help each student if they need help.

Professor is so nice and understanding. He takes time to know his students and is willing to meet with anyone for any reason at any time, even late at night and during the weekends if needed. He gives good feedback and is very clear about project criteria.

Difficult to understand him and syllabus structure is ambiguous. Feedback is subjective (to his experience). Dismisses an entire section of a submitted assignment if he deems the beginning unworthy of being read. Suggestions are often disregarded. Have to squeeze clear info about exams/homework out of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Very good intro to calculus course. Should be super simple if you've had any calc background. Even if you haven't, prof Brandon is wonderful and begs the students to ask questions. She makes herself available if you need any help and is very thoughtful. If you get an A on all three exams you don't have to take the final, which is super nice.
Deb's class is amazing! I learned so much, and she is very understanding and helpful with all her students.  Take her if you can. Will not disappoint
Deb is the BEST professor i've had in college. She is ALWAYS willing to help and pushes you beyond what you think your ability is. She is also a fun and loving character!

I graduated a couple years ago, and it was thanks to professor brandon. I was a humanities major who HAD to take a calc class (as you can infer math is not my strongest subject) and she took the time to help me conquer calc. Many thanks to professor brandon for being so understanding and helpful.
Best professor I've ever had. Sometimes I would even go to her office hours just to talk, extremely cool person and really understanding of students.

One of my favorite professors. Goes to great lengths to make sure our enormous lecture does not have any questions about topics before moving on. She is always willing to answer questions a million ways until you understand. Brandon keeps Calculus light and occasionally fun--a great accomplishment. Go to lecture/recitation, ask questions, get an A.
I had her for Diff EQ in 2000 and at that time she didnt seem very interested in the class or the students very much at all.

Best calc teacher I ever had.  Encyclopedic coverage of topics, but always willing to walk you through stuff.  Expects you to learn, and works to support that.  It's an easy class if you work hard, and a hard class if you treat it like it's easy.
She was a pretty good teacher.  A LOT of quizzes, too many in fact... exams were decent... very understandable and good at explaining things.

One of the best calc professors I've had. Excellent at lecturing, and would always be able to clear up any confusion you had on concepts. Had very realistic expectations of students, and was fun and energetic in class. Exams were fairly easy.
Very very hard, and goes blazingly fast during lecture...like 20 boards within an hour!
challenging class, but she is VERY good.
I got striaght 76%'s by no fault of hers. But I've heard that Matrix can be taught MUCH better.  REALLY friendly person!
she's the reason I just graduated with a math degree.  wonderful person.

Excellent professor. The course can be very hard, but the way she teaches it makes it much easier. Her notes are VERY helpful. This is definitely one of those few courses where you don't want to skip the lectures. Review sessions for exams are HELPFUL!
This teacher is mean, and nasty.  I didn't like her, and she sucked.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Dilsun is a nice lady who seems to actually care about her students. I rarely went to lecture because of how amazing the course notes were, but every time I went she was able to explain things very clear and took time to answer the many questions I had during and after lecture.

Dilsun is very kind and always answers student questions without making them feel dumb. The assignments and midterms are the same as Iliano's so the class is still really tough, but Dilsun makes lectures a little better
She's very nice, trying to remember students' names, and very organized in her lecture. I enjoyed attending her lectures which made everything clear and easy to learn.
Cares so much, but is extremely boring in lecture(fall asleep snooze).

Great professor and lectures are very clear. The class itself is interesting, although the homeworks, particularly the writtens, are very mundane. But that's not so much a problem with Dilsun but rather with the class as a whole. Would recommend choosing this instructor if possible for the class.
Dilsun is a really kind professor and her lectures are very well-delivered.

Kaynar is such a wonderful professor. She truly cares about her students which is a rarity. Despite the class being incredibly difficult, Kaynar goes the extra mile to ensure all her students have the resources necessary to being successful
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Dilsun is one of the most caring professors at CMU. She prepares her lectures really well and ensures that everyone could fully understand her. So grateful to have her for 15122.

I really enjoyed this class. Prof Kaynar made this class worthwhile. She loves the material and makes every effort to help the students find a passion for it as well. She is the sweetest professor and always wants to answer questions.

Dr. Diabes is great, I highly recommend his class. Every other class session is an in-class negotiation where you can practice course concepts. The other sessions are lectures where concepts from the negotiations are reviewed. The class isn't too difficult with only a few major assignments.
Attendance is REALLY important on negotiation days.
Great Prof. very passionate and it shows. His assignments and lectures are fun and enthralling.
An absolutely wonderful professor... highly would recommend taking any course he teaches in the future!
Very theoretical, but very rigorous
Very well structured and very helpful during the tutorials.

One of, if not, the most brilliant professors I've ever had at CMU. There was never a question he couldn't answer. For Continuous-Time Finance, the lectures are very theoretical but you don't necessarily have to understand of all of them to do well. HWs, exams, and grading are all very fair.
Brilliant professor, very rigorous instruction.
Great prof

Really good lecturer and inspiring to have as a professor. I really enjoyed having Prof. Downs as a professor. However, the exams are pretty difficult, and if you do poorly or average, it can sentence your grade to being an overall B.

Fairly difficult lecture-based class, but she gives a TON of extra credit to offset that. If you do all the extra credit, you get 3 points added to your final grade (so an 87 becomes a 90). There are 4 exams and you can drop one grade entirely, if you have a good enough grade you can skip the final. Got an A without getting above an 85 on an exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The exams are really hard, and the daily quizzes are almost impossible to get points which pulls down the grade a lot

I really love this class! If you understand the concepts in the lecture, you'll understand the exam question well and know what to answer. The exam content is all covered in the lecture and is of average difficulty. Also, Prof Downs is a really good lecturer, I enjoy every minute of her lecture!

lectures are really fun and interesting. It's a pleasure to listen to her lectures. HOWEVER, tests are incredibly hard. All tests are fill-in blanks or short answer, which makes it harder than ever. You'll have to figure out what to put in the blanks. Very confusing, super super hard.

The lectures are fun and informative, HOWEVER, the exams are awful. There are many midterms, and there are many questions based on readings that she never mentioned in class or in homework so students have no chance to practice those completely unexpected questions. Most questions from the exams are fill-in-blank questions that are super confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Worst gened course I've take at cmu. As a gened course, the exam is too hard. All we want is an easy A so stop being so demanding on the exam.

Lectures are really interesting but her tests are very unclear. They can be hard to study for and her test format takes awhile to get used to but the class is definitely manageable. A great class to take if you're wondering what decision science is about; you learn a lot of generally applicable information.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Teaches a great class (Reason, Passion, and Cognition) and her lectures are very interesting. Her readings are long and sometimes boring.  She gives some pop quizzes, but they're pretty easy. Her exams can get confusing sometimes... not extremely clear exactly what she's asking for. Overall though, a great class w/ interesting topics!

I was in his fluids class. He's a nice guy but the worst professor I have ever had. He just reads off of power point slides he printed out, doesn't go through the math written out on the board. He types out all of the math on the power point slides and talks about it. His power point slides are extremely unorganized also.

Had Domach for Spring Intro to Chem Engineering. Homework is challenging but minimal, tests are a bit harder. Goes through book examples on the board, to clarify if you don't understand the method. Likes to put in real-world applications, such as biotechnology and using distillation to make moonshine.

open book/open notes exams, 2 open laptop exams (geez), but he's not an exciting or great professor, uses same transparency, bad writing,..easy grader, I guess, but learn stuff..ehh...he gives no practice exams and he wrote our iffy text book.....

He says you didn't follow instructions on his assignments even when you follow them perfectly. He doesn't know much beyond the things he teaches in class - If you know some stuff beyond the readings he assigns, he will tell you you are wrong and to follow the readings. You really have to fight and argue your way through this class.

He is a very self-aware professor, eager to improve his teaching style to suit the students. He wants students to get something out of his class and if they put in the required effort they will. His classes will require thought and effort but he is willing to help and change his teaching to suit the students.
No Comments
Great professor! Definitely my favorite that semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Lots of reading (a reading journal entry or an argument outline sketch due every class) but very detailed, lots of interesting debatesdiscussion, and pretty thought-provoking. Pay attention in class! Work hard and do the readings and it will be rewarding.
Lectures are discussion format, so don't take the class if you're not ready to contribute at least a little. Great coverage of very interesting topics, and not a ton of work overall.

He knows his material, and is willing to teach it. His lectures often take discussion formats and he facilitates student discussion with him guiding it; if this appeal to you, the class itself will be enjoyable. However, grading is done by TA, while he is willing and accommodating to help students, the result is that he has little impact on grades.
Obvious that he has a passion for teaching and philosophy. Really wants students to learn and understand concepts. But too much work... TAs do pretty much all of the grading.

Terrible professor. Does not handle any aspect of grades nor does he care about any aspect of grades and delegates everything to TAs. Lectures are mildly interesting, but assigns an absurd amount of work.

Awesome class, as long as you put in the effort. Participate in discussions, and read all of the assignments closely and you will get a lot out of this class. I was not his biggest fan in the beginning, but looking back his class was one of the best experiences of freshman year. Nice guy too.

He's a pretty good professor in general. Knows his stuff and is somewhat interesting. He's not very helpful and directs you to the TAs and grading always depends on the TA. Depending on the TA you'll either get an easy A or you'll get a C.
sucks
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Her homework write-up was so clear that I want all the instructions that I receive in my whole life to be like that. The workload is heavy but since the instructions and TAs are helpful it's a fruitful and satisfying process (if you start working early, otherwise it is hell). Her lecture is also one of the best at Heinz, clear and easy to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If you don't get a correct output of your code, you will almost get 0, no credits for the other parts of the code. TA will not spend time reading your code, if you don't get it right, then you die. Every week has a quiz and a time limit lab. No excuse for absence, if you miss any quiz or lab, you will have a 0, no makeup exam.
Worst professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Neelam is a great professor,one could actually build strong fundamentals in java using this course. She gives very interesting questions for lab and practice. Exams are a bit tough but can be easily solved if you do all the labs and practice problems. The only complaint I have is attendance. A very good course, I would highly recommend this.

The exams and labs are challenging with time limit but the contents are fair. If you understand the class materials very well, there will be no surprises on the exams or labs. Quizzes are given at the end of the class based on self-assessment and lectures. I highly recommend to take Neelam's class if you want to get a solid foundation in JAVA.

Just had the first test of the semester tonight. You have an hour to code a high level task. It was awful, you can't use anything to help (no googling!). The class is run opposite of how you'd want a programming class to run. You "learn" outside of class and she answers questions inside class about what you learned.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: 350 characters is too short to describe how bad Neelam is. She is the worst student anyone has had by far & looks to punish students. She may be smart in Java but is unable to communicate concepts, is very rude, refuses to explain things looks to fail students and gives 2 quizzes per class via labs and reading quizzes. Run away as fast as you can.
She seemed to be unprepared and winged it most of the course. Never posted up grades so you could see your progress, but the content was easy.
No Comments
Very interesting class, tons to read, and a lot of essays but worth it...she's real chill
Tests are based on what you know or can quickly find inspiration for, not what you can learn.
Not Bad.
If you don't take OS, you don't know what you're missing. ;-)

Professor Barr taught the best class I have had on professional communication thus far. He does a great job of getting students to understand how people communicate by employing real-life examples.  He also is committed to students outside the classroom – making professional introductions that further career aspirations. All round great guy!

It was a great pleasure taking the Strategic Writing class with Ed. I sincerely loved his way of teaching which emphasized more on simpler ways of effective communicating. To sum it up, the best class to refine your professional knowledge.

Probably the best class I've ever had on communication. Ed is a real master. He got everyone engaged as I've never seen before in my life. Everyone was happy and participative in the class. Hopefully, his course will become a core course for everyone at CMU. I would recommend his services to anyone who really cares about good communication!

His class is very practical and helpful for me.  I had many wrong understandings of professional writing before and I had been using them in writing all the time before. He corrected many of my wrong concepts although the class is only 6 weeks long.

Ed Barr is such a great professor. I used his tips from class to solve a delicate sponsorship issue with one simple and direct email. I would have missed out on the scholarship if it weren't for his class. His tips works like magic!!
This guy is a back-stabbing SOB.He cannot be trusted. His persona belies the evil within.
Excellent course
Ed is truely sensational
Great fellow, a lot to learn from Ed.
HIGHLY recommended.  This class was fun and educational.  Plus, he will be a reference for you after school.

Prof. Barr is one of the best Professors I've ever had. I came into the class thinking I wouldn't really learn much, but afterwards, I'm so glad I took it. He does a great job of giving students "real-world"-type writing assignments. He is a fascinating guy, with a great insight into life - it'd be worth going to class just to listen to him speak.

Straightforward and socially awkward, which can come across as rude. Very nitpicky over student's writing, but lenient with grading. I've written several papers that she's torn to pieces in her comments but still got an 8/10 on. Don't you casual language, because she takes every word that you write very seriously and literally.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Has relatively balanced views on an inflammatory topic- the Middle East. That is really valuable.

Professor Eisenberg is a wonderful professor with a wealth of knowledge to share with her students.  She is certainly a tough grader, but not out of spite.  Prof. Eisenberg is particular, but polite; encouraging, but realistic.  I highly recommend this professor to my peers.
Very great teacher, communicates the material very well

Prof. Eisenberg is fantastic. I don't particularly care about the Arab-Israeli conflict but she makes it fun and interesting, but there is a lot of work that goes into taking her class so be prepared! If you want to learn, take this class if you're looking to get an easy A, take something else.
Demands high quality assignments - you can't just write bs down and get credit for it.  Encourages interesting discussions and debates and teaches the material masterfully.

I didn't like Prof. Eisenberg at first, she is very direct and often somewhat curt with students.  However, in the long run this is really a benifit to her as a professor as she provides very clear and percise feedback on papers and other class work.  I came to apprecitate her as a great professor!
i love him !

Brilliant, interesting professor. Some say lectures can be dull, but I find that his enthusiasm for Anthropology and the topics enliven the discussions significantly compared to other Anthro courses. Probably my favorite in the history department.
brilliant
discussion is relaxed, which works well for my style
he knows so much but his classes are so very dull.
Great professor.  Not the most assertive lecturer, but great non the less.
great professor, knows so much. good class.
totally confusing and ditzy. pretentious and unprofessional.

Ugh. Dropped it 3/4 of the way through even though my grades were good. Its TERRIBLE. She's extremely boring and her tests are only (extremely) hard because she is incapable of making any sense or even writing in gramatically-understandable sentences.
Elaine Baby, you are too fabulous for words. You put the "mod" in Postmodernism.
elaine is really nice and silly and her class is super easy.
Oh Elaine, Im sure you are still trying to figure out how to use a zip disc, too bad those are obsolete now!

If this hadn't been a required class for my major I would have dropped it on the first day. She reads slavishly from her notes in a very boring, monotonous way and provides superficial, repetitive commentary on her innumerable slides as she plows thru the material. Very uninspired, no classroom discussion. I also suspect she doesn't know her stuff.
No Comments
Elaine Baby, you're fabulous.

One of the best professors I've ever had. A lot of the papers and projects are open-ended, she really wants to see how you think rather than how well you can follow an arbitrary prompt which I very much respect.

An incredible and engaging professor, she works with you to make sure you do well if you need help and helps you make up for any missed classes by attending other events. Most classes are discussion based so make sure to do the readings and attendance is a big part of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've ever taken hands down

Dr. Grama was a great professor. Her lectures are very discussion based, so be prepared to read outside of class and obtain required reading material on your own. She was extremely caring and understanding and is very knowledgeable about anthropology.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Grama is extremely kind and very knowledgeable. She really warms up once you get to know her. She is really excited about the material for her courses, so she is very enthusiastic. Sometimes I left class a little more confused than I was when it started, but if you do the reading consistently and participate you'll be fine.
Garrett is the kindest person I know. He would help even with material outside of his class and outside of his OH. At times, he would also provide emotional support to get thru the grind.
best prof for concepts period.
great professor!

Great professor, really clear in lecture. Also he does a great job of providing additional help in office hours. Like I do not like math but loved this class so much. The class is easy to get an A in as long as you do the homeworks.

The best professor to take Concepts with. I had to retake the class for Concepts credit after failing 21-128 and my ride with 21-127 was really smooth with Ervin because he is the best at clarifying anything that's confusing and he won't stop until you get it.

Highly recommend Ervin for Concepts! He's an amazing lecturer, goes at a pretty good pace, and is a very kind person. It should be relatively easy to get an A in this class as long as you go to office hours for homeworks and do relatively well on assessments. I had an A up until the final which I didn't study for because of various other reasons.

The best professor I have ever had. While you can find the specific examples he uses in class in the textbook he provides, the way he lectures is significantly more digestible than any textbook out there. Made 21127 probably the best introduction to discrete math anywhere on the planet.

I had Professor Garrett for both 21122 and 21127 . He introduces topics in math gently and takes the time needed to understand the material, instead of just rushing into difficult problems. I'm so glad I took him for concepts, and the subject itself is quite challenging, but he did an excellent job of teaching it. I would gladly have him again.

Difficult, but thats because the course material is difficult Ervin makes the material as easy and understandable as possible. I ended up with a B in his class, but could quite easily get an A if I went to class or watched the recordings. Office hours are very helpful, and reading the textbook before lecture is the best preparation, and helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I had a fantastic experience with him for Concepts, and I really enjoyed learning from him. He's really good with lectures and the class is challenging but very fair. He was always willing to help out and teach when we asked, and he was pretty accommodating too, within reason. Very happy to have taken Concepts with him :)
Garrett Ervin is perfect
Garrett is super nice and accommodating for students. Genuinely to help everyone succeed instead of making us suffer for the sake of learning.

I had an extremely enjoyable time in his class. I also took 21122 with him. He explains content extremely clearly and thoroughly. He makes sure you understand everything both in lecture and office hours as he introduces content at an extremely reasonable pace. He is very approachable and open.

Garret is great! he's covering the materials at a pace that's easy to digest even for people who have no experience with mathematical proofs before. Perhaps the only concern for me is that maybe we're covering fewer materials than other lectures taught. If you are not a math geek, try to get in his class! If you are, you should take 128 instead :P

Math is not my best subject, but Ervin is a good professor. Lectures are standard, but he's good at explaining things if you ask questions. HW is 10ish problems a week due at the end of class. Weekly quizzes. Worksheets from recitation can be really hard. Exams aren't too bad. He's really nice. Gave a friend makeup exam without a note. Cool guy

He teaches cog psych as if it's a research methods class. Quizzes are difficult for no reason and homeworks are lengthy. Too much emphasis on reading research articles; he would be better off teaching a research methods class where that stuff is actually useful.

The weekly quizzes are pretty standard and are an easy 16/20 at minimum. The homeworks are pretty easy but lengthy As, and the exams are open note so you just have to understand what happened in class. He drops the lowest grade for the homework and the exam, and the lowest 3 quizzes. You can get by with just the slides and not going to class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Really good lecturer, makes it interesting and engaging. Weekly quizzes are annoying. Highly recommend this class.
He is one of my favorite professors, really funny, and is soooooooo helpful. If you're in the psych department you have to talk to him because he has a lot of great advice on how to navigate cmu
Great professor
No Comments
Fun, clear, and with a clear understanding of the material. Oh, and he is so sweet you just want to eat him.
He is very understanding and is always willing to help. He keeps the class interesting.

He is such a funny guy, always telling jokes right in the middle of lecture.  It's a tough class but he tries to keep it interesting.  The good thing is he is always trying to help and is quite approachable.  It's not easy, but don't be afraid to ask him for help.  He is very understanding!
has a great accent, always willing to help, doesn't give bad grades unless you're a jerk
First time coming to the U, I thought the class was a basic introduction. Everything I expected from an average class. Good teacher though.

his class is a joke. After a semester of class with 50 students he has to see every other day still possibly only knows half of our names. He is vague about project goals, his feed back is terrible, unhelpful and generic, he has bad communication skills, and he lacks technical skill.
Amazing! Is willing to stay after class and use office hours to help.
He's great! I would take his class again.
he's okay
Beats around the bush answering questions and needs better feedback.  Generally provides feedback late or in a confusing way.
Yay Eric
great guy, but maybe was still getting the hang of teaching.
Eric's a really great teacher. He's willing to help students outside of class.

I took both Development and Advanced Development with him. He's very clear on the concepts and delivers good lectures. He's also good at drawing lol I asked him a lot of questions and he was patient with me. Even if he couldn't answer some of the questions right away, he would check them after class and send us emails.

Ettensohn is an excellent teacher, he makes things very clear. You really have to go to class though or else you'll miss a lot. The exams are not difficult if you study the material and go to class, very fair.

Although the lectures aren't mandatory, missing one will have you far behind.  He is the perfect teacher for students just entering college! He breaks everything down so you understand it. His test can be difficult just make sure you study your notes and know them well.

Class consists of writing copious notes, both from the chalkboard and from PowerPoint slides, so I would recommend sitting in the front, taking great notes. He assigns readings, but make sure to get the big picture by matching the notes with the readings. Tests are not too hard; just make sure to study.

the lectures are pretty good. my notes for this class came out pretty well and easy to study from, and he draws plenty of pictures that are easy to follow. the tests are hard...need to know concepts pretty well. no recitation, but plenty of test help. GO TO SI
Tests are hard. Overall I did not find the lectures helpful.

Tests are hard; multiple choice questions often have 6 or 7 answers so study hard and do the readings! Quizzes aren't hard and they are useful for studing. In lecture he is pretty good, but sit at the front so you can hear him.

Clear, helpful teacher. Tests are challenging and grading is tough/inconsistent -- check you're exams! That said, I found him to be on of the best, most straight forward professors I've had in the sciences.

Such a funny professor hes always making jokes during lecture and seems so excited about what hes teaching. Very patient when answering questions and overall just a good dude. Kinda strict on grading but u get a dropped test

Professor Fernando is a great professor. Even though he makes Calculus I a really hard class, it is still an enjoyable class. He cares about his students and makes jokes in class to make them more entertaining. He is a great person and very accessible outside of class. Overall, hard class where you will learn a lot and have fun with his lectures!

Professor Fernando is an amazing lecturer. His jokes make people laugh! As long as you show up in class, finish your homework, and go to office hours if you don't understand something, you can get an A (He drops two lowest quizzes). He is patient to his students and approachable even not during office hours. Good at explaining!

I did pretty well in the class, but I know a lot of my peers were struggling. He went over the material pretty quickly but spent too much time going over all the minor proofs that we ended up being behind schedule for most of the semester. I think if he could be better if he spent more time actually focusing on the course material instead.

He's a cool guy but he made the class so unnecessarily hard. The grading is not forgiving at all either, even if it's obvious you understand the concepts but you make a small mistake you get a lot of points off. The grade I got was definitely not worth all the work I put in.

I expected calc 1 to be my easiest class of the semester, but I quickly realized that it would be much more difficult than I initially anticipated. Fernando has very fast paced lectures and likes to focus on the proofs behind each concept. His tests and quizzes are extremely difficult, and you really have to work for an A.

Easily the worst professor of this semester. He is a visiting professor from Northeastern university and he has turned a regular introductory class into an abnormally difficult class where you need to put in 2 hours/day studying in order to just pass the class. What he teaches in class and what we are assessed on in exams/quizzes are not the same.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: My truly inspiring professor Dave wrote that witty poem The Square Root of Three; but you still dare ask, how stunning this gentleman could really be?  Professor Feinberg is a caring, knowledgeable teacher with past experience in the software industry. I could not have asked for a better person to excite my interests in introduction to programming.
He wrote The Square Root of Three.  How much more awesomeness do you need?
Dave is just amazing and he is really cute in a nerdy way. He is the kind of guy you just want to go laser tagging with (?). Anyways he's awesome.
Kept the class very entertaining and very down to earth. The homeworks were challenging, but besides that the rest is pretty easy.
Best teacher to get for 15-110. Helps you through assignments by setting up the methods you need to write for you so you're not totally lost. kinda boring if you already have programming knowledge.
Dave is great.  Everyone in the class likes him.
Coolest Professor. He teaches you by having you program games and computer robots. Like for my last few HW assignments I programmed Tetris, King's Up, and that Snake game in JAVA :D

Fantastic professor; he was an AP Comp Sci teacher at a high school before, so he easily relates to students.  His lectures are always exciting, fun, and enjoyable.  I learned a lot of skills in that class.
15-111 I personally found the class too fast-paced for me and struggled on the assignments, but Feinberg is reasonable and fair, and a pretty cool guy.

Horrendous lecture organization. Material taught was at the 5th grade level but averages on tests were still C's since they weren't representative of what was taught in class. I hated this course mainly because of her.

Solid teacher and course (Regression) - very easy material to learn if you do the homework and study. She was very nice and available for extra help (i.e. anonymous note card questions after class, office hours, and exam review sessions). Accent is hard to understand at first, but you get used to it.

Professor had no interest in helping students.  Continued to state that students were really stupid if they "super failed" by having a 0 on a rubric that was completely arbitrary and not shown prior to the first test. The rubric jumped from 5 to 4 to 0 with basically no partial credit.  Strong emphasis on vocab and unclear in requirements for tests

Teaches the material quite well, but often appears to advocate extreme ideas that make the class seem like propaganda. Tests and homework are difficult not for the content, but because it's extremely difficult to tell what exactly she wants written. Grades are based on a rubric placing significant emphasis on economic vocabulary.
Great professor. Very inspiring. She really takes time to know her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This was a very useful class.  Prof Finger made it interesting and helped me when I needed guidance.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This is an amazing professor. I learned a lot in this class.  It was really interesting also.
This was an amazing class.  Prof Finger is an inspirational teacher.  Overall it was a great experience.
Best professor I have ever had. She genuinely cares about her students and will fight against the administration to make sure you have a great time inside and outside of her class.
rapid proto was a great class, pretty easy
Fantastic teacher.
No Comments
sfinger was always around to help, even at 2 AM.  she was a great advisor, even when she wasn't supposed to be advising you.  she went out of her way to make sure you know what was going on.
No Comments

Very engaging teacher.  Friendly, concerned with every student's performance, and has great class projects and interesting homework.  Keeps late hours and is always available to give help on homework.  More like a student than any other professor I've had

do not take organizational behavior with Fichman. His tests (multiple choice) are extremely vague as is his grading for homework. His lectures are helpful but slightly rude in his behavior to students. He means well but his personality is not suited for most. If you have the option, I strongly encourage you take 70-311 with different professor.
Rude. Very difficult tests. Lots and lots of reading and memorization.

He was fairly rude to several people who were trying their best to participate this semester. He says the tests aren't graded on keywords, but that's exactly how they're graded. It's good that he drop one test, because you'll study very differently after the first one.
inconsistent

I took Organizational Behavior with Professor Fichman. He is a great teacher, and seems to knows his subject material better than anyone else. I highly recommend him. However, just as a note of caution, one must be really careful while writing emails to him, as the replies are often harsh, and public rather than private.

Grading was tough, because it literally meant memorizing pages and pages worth of reading, IMO. The readings were long, and participation was graded harshly. Exams were based on 150 points. So a single mistake == minus 5 (deviation from what they expected(!)) for 3+ questions basically means B. Not satisfied with the outcome. Learnt lots though.

Not very good at giving feedback: the course content (for OB) seemed like common sense, but did not do well on exams or cases - and I don't know why. Very demanding of attendance and participation, cold calls for hard questions sometimes and looks for very specific answers. Has strong opinions, but you learn to just take them with a grain of salt.
Tries to press personal opinions on students.
Smart, fun class.  Difficult though.
No Comments
the guy's got his faults but all-in-all not a bad guy
No Comments
Candidly, constructively critical and helps with revision towards an ideal grade
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Sincere, but very hard to understand sometimes.
Great class!

His lectures are really confusing. You can tell he's a nice person and cares about the material but I stopped attending lectures because they were basically useless. The textbook got me through the course.

As someone who only took MODERN BIO because it's mandatory in MCS, I hated every second. Professor Lanni is a kind soul but I learned nothing from his lectures and stopped going. Getting together a STUDY GROUP and a PEER TUTOR is the only thing that barely kept me afloat. I survived on the lecture notes and google. Good Luck.

He's pretty nice and definitely cares about the material, but the lectures can get convoluted and he goes off on tangents sometimes. Tests are most of your grade. The homeworks he gave us were easier than the tests which sometimes got really specific. He's a nice guy and a decent teacher.

(Taken with Lanni and Jarvik) Prof Lanni knows what he's talking about, but it can go too in-depth and convoluted. I recommend going to lecture and exam reviews. His exams are pretty application based which make them harder than typical biology exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He is really passionate about Biology and he knows what he's talking about but he goes off on a tangent almost the entire class. So basically I would say forget about going to lectures. His tests are pretty hard but besides exam there's barely any work.
He is an absolutely awful professor, terrible communicator. Avoid him if you can, although i've heard that all the bio professors are bad
I love him!!!  He is a great professor and I have learned a lot from him. I will remember Dr. Lanni forever.
Kinda just complicates things. just read the text book and don't bother with class.

He's very interesting in Biology, and teaches the class accordingly. If he mentions something, even in passing, he will probably talk about it for 15 minutes, even if it means coming back the next day to do so. He's a good professor though, and fair.

is a nice guy but does goes off on crazy tangents that will probably just confuse you and won't even be on the test. homeworks are really hard and tests are fairly easy, just read the book and your fine. curves are nice too
Goes off on crazy tangents...
No Comments
Honest work = Honest grade.
Great professor. Very purposeful and detailed lecture. Homework is reasonable and well graded. Straightforward and clear explanations to questions.
Great professor with passion for this subject. Exams and assignments are challenging but very purposeful and are extremely helpful.
Classes are boring, but lectures make sense and go along with book. Tests are easy if you pay attention
Very good at teaching graduate classes. Very approachable, and knowledgeable on the subject matter. Highly recommend as an advisor for graduate work.
No Comments
No Comments
Higgs is fairly confusing sometimes, he tends to repeat himself, and calls on the same 4 people every class. He likes football players, easy tests tho.

Very caring professor, who is super accessable out of class as well if you need any extra help or clarifications. Super passionate about teaching, and really wants everyone to get involved in learning the material and participating. If you do that, he will do so much to help you succeed, in his class and outside of it

This professor operates daily on ego. I recommend all students to avoid taking Mr. Gasper's classes. I can't believe CMU still allows Mr. Gasper's presence on campus based off feedback from nearly any colleague I've spoken to.

Not for me, hated econ. We didn't even learn micro economics, half the time we were learning macro! Plus there was a huge cheating scandal, and he didn't even curve the grades of the people who didn't cheat.
I took Mircoecon with him. It was hard. I struggled. Econ is not for me. But I did pass. I would not take again as this class was just hard for me.

I've never rated a professor before, but wanted to make sure everyone knows how great Professor Gasper is! He was super accommodating during the pandemic, and actually managed to make zoom lectures interesting.

Gasper is a great lecturer, and clearly has passion for what he does. Material was difficult for me because I haven't taken econ before. Recitation was also really helpful for me, but it depends on your TA. Didn't do too great on the last midterm, so my grade took a hit. But overall, great class!
very good professor. be healthy!
Easy A even if you only study old exams prior to the exam
Great class. Prof. Garrison is always willing to help.
great prof. nothing bad to say
Some of the most random stories you'll ever hear.  Watch out!  He's going to pass out!
So nice and understands that the language is difficult. Definitely going to take a class with him again.
I really enjoyed his class.  He loves teaching and he treats his students well.  Going to miss his class!
He would like it if everyone could learn Chinese and got an A. If you let him he will help you.
Liu is amazing as a teacher. he is patient and will take the time to help you learn the language. I love this class and hope to have him again next semester

The professor provides good notes of the textbook and assigns homework at a faster pace than lectures, so it's helpful to read extra materials on top of the lectures. He responds to emails consistently and is flexible with extensions. Recitations and office hours are pretty useful for homework support.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: Took it during the summer. Homework due every day except for exams but very lenient on due dates/extensions. Professor is friendly, but is against releasing solutions to exams and homework sets. Basically have to figure out your own mistakes and try to do better next time, which was frustrating for a subject like physics.
Amazing professor, he's been teaching for quite a while so he has a grasp on how to communicate the concepts effectively.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: He laughs a lot at his own jokes.
The class is sorta boring and you really need to pay attention to grasp whats going on. Tests are almost identical to practice but the final was pretty tough.

Lectures are entertaining, but you don't learn much. He's a nice, funny guy, and he's enthusiastic about the material he teaches, but you learn most of the material from reading the textbook and trying to do the homework. Exams are exactly like the practice exams.
Amazing teacher. Would 100% recommend him as a teacher and this class. Very into what he teaches which make the class fun.

Absolutely hated this class.  He is horribly inefficient and unorganized and he babbles to himself in lecture.  He has you complete these little quizzes in lecture to check attendance, but he barely covers half of the stuff on the page.  His antics in lecture aren't funny either; it is just plain annoying.
babbles to himself, lectures worse than useless
The class is hard and boring. He murmurs to himself and you cant hear what he's saying.

Very entertaining! Uses a fire extinguisher in class to demonstrate flux, totally awesome.  He's friendly, cool accent and tests are challenging but fair.  He also TA's for other classes, so is able to teach on a personal level too (in office hours).
This guy is funny! Go in to his office hours, he's very nice on the personal level, and is willing to do as much as he can to help
Hilarious. Kept class interesting. Tests weren't easy, but they weren't hard either. They were fair. If you study, do the homework, and go to class, you'll do fine.

be sure to attend the classes to avoid a failure. he is very strict about grades. also, be prepared for a low grade if that matters for u. the course content itself is wonderful, and he is obviously an expert in BE domain.

A pretty engaging professor (more than most people who've spent their life in economics). He's reduced the amount of reading for this year's class and the workload is manageable. He's extremely clear in his lectures, which makes the material easier to learn.

Clear and fairly engaging lecturer, but his own work appears in class a lot, so he can seem self-centered. The class' case studies also involved sexual questions to a potentially uncomfortable extent.
Genuinely one of the worst professors I have ever had at explaining course material and expectations.

he's really bad at explaining topics and will leave you really confused. the lectures don't really make sense and it's a struggle to do any recitation or homeworks because literally nothing makes sense. frustrating class, not worth your time

If you're not interested in physics, Prof Garoff will be hard to learn from. He often jumps straight into topics in lecture, so reading the textbook ahead of time is crucial. He does not offer zoom classes, so if you miss a class you miss a lot. Exams are heavily based on lecture and is more conceptual, while homework is heavily based off the book.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Makes getting to class at 8am worth your time. Really good guy who teaches the material well, but you will have to work for it. He gives you every opportunity to succeed, including being very accessible outside of class should you need him.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: As a person, he is very knowledgeable and extremely caring and sweet. His lectures are interesting in concept but were too early in the morning to attend. Also office hours were limited to the TAs . ATTEND COURSE CENTER!!! IT WILL SAVE YOUR GRADE

Professor Garoff is not great at explaining things. This will have little impact on your grades early in the semester, but will completely screw up your final. Half the test was on something neither Garoff nor the book explained . Only one person left early.  He was forced to give extra time since no one understood the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Really bad at teaching what you need to learn, doesn't really explain anything just shows power points. If you also get a bad TA you're done for.

If you have the opportunity to take this class in the spring, do yourself the largest favor and do so. This professor and his class are horrible. He does not post grades online, he does not hold office hours, his class is taught using quizzes and poorly explained power points, and lectures are rushed and no examples of problems are ever given.
The class was not easy, but Dr. Garoff was very willing to explain things to try to help me understand what was going on.

Dr. Garoff may very well be a good physicist, but he is not a very good teacher. His explanations do not clear up misunderstandings, and often cause students to be even more confused. He often gives the impression that he doesn't really know what he's talking about but would rather make up an answer or obfuscate the issue than admit to not knowing.

Difficult class, requires a lot of effort. All classes are mandatory and constant test, quiz, hw, etc. Had a hw and reading due, online assignment, and quiz in class all on the same day. Not abnormal.

He is earnest in teaching, but earnesty is not equivalent to imparting actual physics knowledge. He has passion for physics, but his explanations are not very straightforward, and at times, confusing. I didn't have much of a physics foundation, so this class was hard. If you had a good physics teacher in high school, this class might be easier.

He means well, I think.....But he is probably much better suited doing research somewhere, not trying to teach freshman physics.  If you have credit, or can place out, DO IT.  He posts old exams online, but they don't reflect the actual exams.  His class is painfully boring, and unnecessarily difficult.  Maybe do something besides show powerpoints?

Hope that you have a good TA for Physics 1, since you will learn everything in recitation.  As for lectures, showing the solution to a problem on Powerpoint slides is not teaching physics.  Neither is vaguely explaining concepts without making the connection between the math and the physics.
MPL explanations were helpful.
This was a fairly difficult class, but it wasn't nearly as bad as Physics II with Swendsen.  I didn't mind it too much.
Tries very hard and is quite approachable, although the intensity with which he approaches the material  could be viewed as a negative quality if the student was not interested.
Reaallly hard class in general. He doesn't really explain well though. Good intentions, but hes sort of difficult to talk to. Kind of awkward in terms of communicating. Seems like a nice guy though.
Very difficult class. Garoff tries hard, but is not successful in explaining the material.
Teacher does not explain everything that well, however recitation is very useful.  Overall material  of the course is difficult, hence the teacher will get a bad rating in most cases.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
Terrible class.  Not clear or understandable at all.  Pop quizzes constantly.  Not a good class.
Really awesome teacher. Good lecturer, content is interesting, and all of the course expectations are very clear. Do the readings and take good notes, you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He's a really great professor. He makes you work hard but it's extremely fulfilling. He gives great feedback on papers and is very informed and enthusiastic about the subject matter. Definitely would recommend taking his class. It's a lot of work but definitely worth it.

Geoff is a fantastic professor. He really knows his stuff and is passionate about the material, which shows through in lecture. His classes are demanding in terms of the reading load, but he gives great feedback on assignments and exams. Definitely take his course - if you stick with it, you'll learn a lot.

I took 33-106 with him over the summer. Great man that really cares about his students, if you ever need help he'll find a way to help you. The class was a lot of work but nothing impossible, he teaches the material well. If you care you're going to learn because he cares to teach the stuff.

He is one of the best teachers I've had. Incredibly enthusiastic and makes an effort to meet all students on a personal level and help them excel. Always available for help with physics or just plain advice. Just try to answer a question every now and then, and he'll love you. P.S. I took him summer class, which was smaller. Take it!

He was the worst professor I have ever had. If you can, do not take his class. He was not clear whenever he lectured and kept getting on tangents. The tests? Literally nothing you have ever seen before on homeworks or quizzes.  It was an "introduction class" that barely introduced physics to you. Just a bunch of derivations not even needed.
Energetic, sometimes hard to understand, very passionate. If I was majoring in physics (and didn't suck at it), I'd love to have him as an advisor.

Incredibly energetic and uses hilarious examples to illustrate physical points. Sometimes difficult to follow in lecture, but a great guy who is genuinely happy to teach. Material is hard, but worthwhile.
Very interesting professor. I'm not sure if he can actually teach since so many people failed or dropped out of the class but the lectures are interesting.

Dr. Ghosh is the only man I know who runs across the room with a meter stick when talking about vectors. It is just about impossible to fall asleep in his class, which says a lot about a physics professor. He is the undergrad physics advisor and one of CMU's best assets. Not many people care about students as much as he does. He is there to help.

He is so awesome. You couldn't fall asleep in his class if you tried. He is very friendly in person. He is an advisor to many, so he is busy. When he is not busy he is great to talk to. I wish he would teach all my classes.

Class is "easy", but he expects CRAZYINESS from you. Hes a nice and funny guy, and teaches you things in class, but they probably won't have anything to do with the class. Nice guy, difficult to explain things to.

He's definetly the most energetic professor I've ever had. Passionate about physics, and will get you passionate too (even if you hate the subject and are just taking the class for the lab credit). Good at explaining experiments and their background information, and has realistic expectations. Highly recommended.
I like his method of teaching (compared to the first professor). Also has a sense of humor and we laugh a lot in his class. The exams were a bit harder than the one the first professor gave.
Don't expect to learn anything in lectures.
The only class where you can take the derivative of an elephant I wanted to fail just to have him as a prof again
excellent prof, learned a lot
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
his name is spelled Kunal Ghosh
No Comments

Good lecturer, but passive aggressive when answering questions. When students are seeking understanding and asking questions, it's harmful to act this way towards those questions.
Lecture slides are posted but a lot of important lecture content happens verbally and on the blackboard. Attendance is tracked with location on a 16$ app subscription.

Course itself is pretty organized. I didn't find her lectures helpful at all which sucked because attendance was taken by tracking your literal location. She's kind of harsh and not very understanding in her class. In person she's pretty nice though.

Professor Ryan is a great lecturer. Attendance is mandatory but going to class would be worth it regardless. The class is a lot of work and can be difficult if you don't have a good grasp of all the topics. She can also be a little blunt when it comes to answering questions, but she's generally pretty nice.

She is reachable outside of class time, the only thing is she can come off as rude sometimes or very cold/short. The lectures are really good, and the concepts are explained very clearly. The syllabus is extremely easy to navigate, and there are no surprises when it comes to her.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is very hard-headed. Hard to work with; and packs his students with loads of work with assignments and writings while not being aware that the course only has 9 units. I do not recommend anyone taking this course if you want to continue liking the English language and Carnegie Mellon.

Professor Gillette gives pretty good feedback on your writing, especially if you go to office hours or request a personal meeting. Although I did learn a lot, I personally think he grades harshly on major assignments. Just get 100s on the minor assignments and I think it's a solid A.

Interp is supposed to be an easy A, but not with this professor. Overall, his grading criteria is really weird because you will get points off for random things on major assignments even if you follow all the steps. He teaches advanced English too, which is why I think he's such a harsh grader. Overall, nice guy but bad prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I took matrices and linear transforms with professor Ginster. Having a textbook is optional but I chose to get one for myself since his lectures were too hard to follow. The homework is not hard and addresses topics discussed in class (get ready to do a lot of proofs). The midterms and final are proof and definition heavy. Overall a fun class. :)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lectures were clear, and questions were answered well.  Lectures are thorough enough that there is no need to buy the textbook. Homework prepares you very well for the tests.
Fair grader, very forgiving, easy to reach outside of class. The pace in class is fast but the tests aren't too difficult if you've done the homework.
Good class overall. Easy but got a lot out of it.

Freshman seminar. Over 90% of classes followed format of collecting questions students had from previously assigned readings, then answering them. Very little off-book teaching. Awkward teaching style. I knew a lot about the subject but found myself in trouble due to unusual grading on tests.

Golman is a young professor - I think he is mainly at CMU for research - but he is good and passionate about what he teaches.  He is the department of social and decision sciences.  He hosted a bring your friends to class day and served coldstone ice cream! For not being very interested in game theory, he made the class enjoyable enough.

Every semester this class is far, far behind schedule. She refuses to admit that there's too much homework, gives terrible feedback, grades harshly, writes confusing guidelines, doesn't understand how to answer questions, inaccessible outside class, bullies and looks down on students, and doesn't care about anything but proving how smart she is.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Sherwood is a great lecturer, but she makes the Lab 1 environment incredibly stressful. She's very blunt and sometimes just mean, and due to Lab 1's already very heavy workload, her attitude sometimes makes the class even harder. That said, she's very smart and I've learned so much from her. It's a crazy grind of a class, but it's rewarding, too.
Do not like her, she singles random people out each class and comes at them to answer a question very 'passionately.'

BLATANT favorites. if you're not her favorite she will cut you off mid sentence and make you feel stupid for asking questions. she prides herself as “honest” and “real” but she's actually just mean and unwilling to do any degree of self reflection.

lectures for such a long time that we have no time to do our labs yet blames us for being behind schedule. instructions for assignments often contradict themselves. canvas page is disorganized. will shout out letter grades at students' answers to her questions (usually “C!” when she doesn't like your answer) entirely unwilling to listen to feedback

Super knowledgable professor, cares about her students. Don't get put off by her blunt attitude, she is a great person and professor and she really does care. She is blunt to help you and if you show up to class and participate you will learn a lot!!

She can come off as blunt but if you actually take the time to get to know her and speak to her she is very kind and incredibly intelligent. Her lectures are very engaging and she clearly has passion for chemistry and for her students. She is a tough professor and many people seem to not like this but it is all the marks of a good professor.

Ms. Sherwood is NOT respectful towards her student's time. Students are busy individuals too; I don't want to spend hours searching upon her disorganized canvas class set-up because she couldn't bother to make a reasonable interface for the students. In addition, she grades WAY too harshly, always giving the lowest end of the rubric when possible.

Professor Sherwood is by far, indubitably, the worst professor I have ever had. She is the most unreliable person I have ever met. She never answers her emails. She laughs at you for not knowing what to do, and she is completely unprofessional. DO NOT take a class with her ever!
She is not the most approachable and is not always receptive to feedback. However, she seems passionate about the material and is a relatively engaging lecturer, though intimidating at times.

she basically makes you feel dumb for asking questions in class and makes the learning environment very stressful if you do not understand the material. she often goes around and patrols the class looking for students who aren't paying 100% attention and will comment or call them out. this class is difficult enough without her belittling us

horrid organization made this class hell. TAs would give bad advice and then we'd be asked why we listened to them. Last minute assignments / feedback posted but yet very quick turnaround expected from us. unclear guidelines and incredibly inconsistent grading on major assignments. it completely depended which ta was assigned to grade you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Terrible communication, unnecessary aggressiveness, and unprofessional. I think she is probably very knowledgeable, but the way she taught/led this course was not enjoyable at all. Most of the time the students were just complaining about professors and the course.

I understand that she’s a professor and she’s worked hard for it, but every one of her students is working hard too. She belittles her students in a hostile manner, often yelling when unnecessary, honestly makes asking questions terrifying, we’re all learning at different paces. I’ve never had a professor that makes me this uncomfortable.

Nice as a person but not great as a professor. She makes it obvious that she thinks you're dumb and will use any chance she has to tell you so. If she likes you then she's a lot more helpful. Very unorganized notes and lesson plans
terrible at explaining things, and overly demanding

You can tell she knows a lot about her subject, but not much about people. She makes asking questions terrifying, making fun of students for asking questions she thinks are "dumb questions". I have never had a bad professor, but this one clearly has issues. Her classroom environment is harsh, and she is just straight up mean. Avoid if possible.

She is a very good lecturer and explains topics in a way that makes them easy to understand. However, she was not very understanding with virtual and time difference troubles even when factors were out of my control. Many students were scared of asking questions due to her sometimes overly aggressive and rude responses.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Even though the class very intensive and requires a lot of time and attention, Sherwood is intelligent and an excellent teacher. She is also very considerate when we have midterms in other classes. A lot of people complain about her direct, brutal attitude. Tbh I prefer that as she ended up providing insightful, specific feedback on everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She is clearly knowledgeable with what she teaches and an organized and clear lecturer. However, she is known to have a bad attitude with students. She is difficult to approach and often condescending if you try to ask her questions after class. Overall, she's a competent teacher but creates a hostile learning environment.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Oftentimes If you ask her a question that she thinks is dumb, she will mock you for it instead of just telling you the answer.

Belittles students for small mistakes. Rude and honestly just downright mean. Has told her classes that she knows people don't like her and justifies herself by saying that she's a powerful woman and that she won't yield to anyone.. but you can be a strong woman and still be gracious. Genuinely shocked at how high of a score she has here.
She cares a lot about her students and the material she is teaching. Definitely worthy of a lot of respect

Absolutely amazing teacher! She truly, honestly cares about her students, and she does everything in her power to make sure all of her students pass. She has actually answered emails at 2 am before and showed up to teach at 8:30 am the following day. One of the MOST demanding professors here at CMU, though. Definitely go to class if you want an A!

She is amazing! She may come off as mean, but it's mainly because she wants you to succeed. Don't take it so hard, she only cares and doesn't want to baby her. She is more than qualified for this course, but does it for her love to see students succeed. Listen to her advice and you will do well.
He is so sweet and really cares about his students. His lecture slides are nice and detailed which can be a bit much in lecture but is very helpful when studying.
The best 03-121 professor by far. Lectures are sometimes on mundane and boring topics but he does his best to explain in an interesting way. Super nice and considerate if you need help.

Biochem 1 was the easiest class I've taken at CMU. It wasn't super useful to me bc I put in no effort & wasn't interested in the content, but if you're actually a good student, it's a good class! Dr. Rule is a rare gem who genuinely cares a ton about his students :)  I felt bad obnoxious premeds still always bugged him abt grades, y'all gotta chill

this class and dr. rule were both quite lovely! he is a lovely teacher who really cares about his students, the only issue with this class was our TA graders graded quite low on the problem set, making the overall class average be well below an A. the lectures can be slightly boring but he tries his best to make it engaging and cover everything.

Professor Rule cares deeply about the success of his students. He very clearly lays out content that students must learn for an exam, and even gives study tips and hints on what to focus on. He provides expectations for every lecture that are helpful for test prep. Professor Rule provides many resources for students to succeed. Strongly recommend.
My favorite class this semester! Dr. Rule always made sure to explain concepts clearly and thoroughly and was always available outside of class to answer questions/go over confusing material.
An amazing teacher dedicated to his students. Make himself available to help out. Always willing to explain things a second time.
Such a nice and clear professor. Apparently does some amazing research too. He is extremely fair and will almost always work with you to help you. He even let me submit things late a bunch of times
Super nice, but class can be pretty dull. If you're confused though, he's patient and able to explain things clearly. Be sure to review the material, and you'll be fine for exams.
Good teacher, class sometimes boring.
EXCELLENT prof...really cares about his students, makes himself available and explains things clearly.  He's the best!

He is a hardworking professor.  But what makes him special is that he cares about his students.  I was struggling to find my passion one summer, and Dr. Rule gave me a very thoughtful advice that got me on track.
every nice and helpful
mr. antibody!
BEST professor!  Dedication to teaching shows.
Wonderful Biochemistry professor!
My favorite professor in 4 years at CMU! Clearly cares a lot about the material and her students. Can be tough but is never unfair.
Very difficult. Tough grader.
Substantial lectures, not overwhelming, nice person, cares about her student.
Wonderful. Gives interesting lectures, interesting reading, really talks to the students.
Great teacher. Lots of reading and discussion, but not much written work. She makes class fun and easily understandable.

Carol should really take a good look in the mirror and re-evaluate her teaching career. Carol does not care about the well-being of her students and quite honestly deserves to be in the deepest depths of hell for the way she has handled the Economics program at this prestigious university. She is a disgrace and leaves a stain on this institution.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Goldburg is the worst professor ever! SO rude and tries to threaten students with academic integrity. She infiltrates every econ class by adding herself to the Canvas courses and then contaminates the syllabus. STAY AWAY!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
She is the absolute worst professor at Carnegie Mellon. She gets involved in econ classes just to ruin student's grades. She doesn't like college aged kids for some reason.
Shes quite possibly the worse professor at CMU. Utter dissapointment. Hasnt gotten better at all. She does not deserve a teaching position at this school.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: just noticed all the bad reviews and i agree completely and say that the professor quality for this professor is horrible from my experience
HORRIBLE!!!!! STAY AWAY SHE IS A TERRIBLE PROFESSOR SO BEWARE

So i actually transferred out of cmu, but i had Goldberg for intermediate micro my sophomore year. Although she didn't teach it, she is in charge of econ advising. My experience at cmu was positive except for her. she is the worst adviser out there and i wouldn't wish for even my enemy to have her for an adviser
This woman is awful. She's rude and will literally make you feel dumb on purpose. Extremely unfair grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader
