reviews.comment: Carol Goldburg is by far the worst professor at CMU. There is a reason that she has the lowest rating on here, and it's backed up by so many student testimonies. She cannot teach, makes INTRO LEVEL CLASSES difficult than it has to be, and does not care about her students. She doesn't have the decency to even show up to SCHEDULED office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Cannot tell you how awful she is. Knows nothing about even basic economics. Answer keys were often wrong/not released before exams - useless for studying. Arrogant and blames her own incompetence on students. Loses homework assignments/forgets to grade but never tells you. I don't know how she is still here. Really deserves a 0 rating.

Could not care less about the students. Inconsistent. Told students not to worry about writing down the ressitation section then threatened to take off points because of it. Grades don't reflect understand. Will not give any grace. If there's a multi part question and you get one part wrong, everything after that will be counted wrong as well.
Hard grading for this most basic econ course! Not responding the emails! Horrible!
full of lies. she wrote that 73-100 is a curved class in the syllabus and no curve was implemented. how bad can she really get?

Carol Goldburg is quite possibly the worst professor you'll ever meet. The tests are only half-relevant to the subject, the course is very poorly run, and she doesn't respond to emails or even show up to her own office hours. The fact that she's still employed is a disgrace to Carnegie Mellon. Prepare for an extremely hard first midterm.
Giving her even 1.0 is being nice. She should not be a professor here, plain out terrible, disorganized, etc. It's funny how she got all four degrees of her's at CMU and yet she is so bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--SO MANY PAPERS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Worst professor ever! The seminar courses should have been an easy A since we don't have tests. But this professor make things incredibly difficult. She is so disorganized during class, may be due to the fact that she doesn't understand materials herself. I feel so unfair to receive a C, given that I showed up everytime and did all the homework
Very harsh grader, docks points for simple math that no one needs to use, except her. Does not know econ, and does not respond to emails. Horrible overall.
Horrible professor. Not willing to help students.
She didn't even know the material. She just shadowed other teachers and tried to BS the material, but she often didn't understand.

Took over for another professor while she had a baby.  No interest in helping students, poor lectures to the point that she did not even know her own lectures.  Tests were made to have less room after they had been extended by previous professor.  Professor caused the class to be more confusing that with the previous professor.
supercalifragilisticexpiadocious!
This man is just as intelligent as he is good-looking! He just takes my heart away!
I really love this sweet enthusiastic young man who makes quantum mechanics so much fun.

He is the greatest human being I've ever met. He saves me from a subhuman savage who almost made me quit physics. Extremely gentle and sweet when interacting with students. Also instills students with the true love of theoretical physics.
Very respectable teacher who is probably too nice. Taking this class with Professor Gregory was the best decision of my life.
Just go to lectures. He makes it fun.
Great professor who makes a challenging subject fun and interesting. His grading is tough but fair.

Required course in Fluid Mechanics.  Kelvin makes this dry (ahem) subject fun.  His grading is tough but fair.  He makes time for students and wants us to do well.  Don't skip lectures!  Some exam questions come directly from lectures.
She is incredibly caring and truly an awesome prof! Her attitude towards students is so refreshing and really conducive to understanding the material!

She's very nice and very smart, but a hard grader. Be sure to follow her report-writing instructions to the letter, no matter how trivial they may seem. Lecture can get pretty boring, but definitely take good notes! She covers everything you'll need to write your lab reports and get a good grade.

Whether or not you're really interested in the material, or even being in lab, she makes things understandable and somewhat interesting. Always really helpful when anyone had questions or wanted help with a lab report. I was sad to switch professors when I finally finished Lab II and III.

I think she is very clear on what she wants and she goes over exactly how she wants her lab reports set up. You can't make a lab II class much easier. She is also always available for help. She may sounds unsure of herself but I think that's just how she talks.
She is so confused all the time just on her own - she should not be allowed to teach! She goes back on her word and lies to professors. Don't go to her with a problem.
Shes easy/self-explanatory, but Lab2 means HELL, as spending 10-15 hours on lab reports. o.chem was EASY.
She explains things relatively well and there are no surprises in her class.  If you put the time into it, you'll do fine.
Recommend everyone who do not have to take his course avoid taking his course.
Kai is an energetic teacher and always looking to have a discussion  about architecture or just whatever. He's a great guy and a committed pedagogue
Good job scaring away half of the class professor :)))))
He puts in the effort but he's still a pretty pointless presence.

kai is the best kept secret of second year and you should take all of his history classes. don't be fooled by the art-vs-kai attitude of the 2nd year. kai seems a lot more rigid compared to art but to understand where he's coming from you really need to take his history course.  definitely one of the best profs in cmu arch, worth your tuition.

Extremely well read and invested in giving students a strong foundation and understanding of the material being covered.  Always willing to offer extra help, even if inconvenient.  Basically, you get what you put in with Kai-you'll love him if you're a hard worker and will find him less appealing if you aren't.
Good course. Lots of great material.
Does a great job as coordinator considering all he has to deal with.
No Comments
This was a great course. I learned a lot
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: good prof. would take again

I hate to rate every thing as excellent because it appears like I just picked the highest marks but this is exactly how I feel, there isn't anything that I would change. The class is truly excellent and I learned a lot.

Overall, I enjoyed the class and will definitely go deeper into programming. The TA was really nice as well. I liked how Professor Guna incorporated class participation; that was a clever way to keep people engaged.
Worst teacher in the world. No one attend class

I was very happy to be able to be taught by Professor G. He has a great teaching style that makes class discussion both informative and fun. I look forward to learning more from him if I am admitted here.
i like his lectures

The absolute worst professor. If you have him, drop the class immediately. Unfortunately, this guy does not know how to teach a class. Very disorganized, lectures are unreasonably useless and inconsistent.
Avoid this professor at all costs. Although he seems to try his hardest, he is utterly inept at explaining concepts which renders his lectures useless. Thank god for the pdfs from last year's course.
You have to learn how to teach
good class
Great lectures
He did nothing while PhD student Dan Licata taught the class. Speaking of, Licata is one of the best and most engaging professors I've ever had!
Amazing.

He is a respected professor at CMU, with multiple awards. 
However, his lecture style is probably not the best for an undergraduate student taking a class taught in an obsolete language. 
The concepts are there, but he never gives review guides or learning materials for new languages. 
Has an overly intruding style to personal space when teaching.

I took two classes with Prof H, 272 last spring, and 443 this fall. I recognize that the class is hard, and it's a lot of material to learn, and I ended up getting a high 79% with a C. But I would still recommend him for any future students. He's a really genuine person and he really cares about his students. He will help you if you come to him.

To future students: go to office hours as early as possible, as you need to learn EVERYTHING on your own. Does not provide any material to study for the exams, so be prepared to get ~70 for your exams even if you reviewed everything you can think of. Homework are due every Sunday, all I can say is good luck and find a friend group to collaborate.

The problem with this teacher is that he uses a Mac computer and then only gives mac-based software to use in the class. It was never mentioned that it's practically required to have a Mac beforehand and I suffered miserably due to that. Also, the exams are incredibly hard without, and you are given absolutely no clue on what they will include.
barely provides any material, really hard to study for, except students to magically learn the content
mysterious homework and problematic lecture-hw-test relationship
Terrible professor, unclear criteria, unrealistic expectations, terrible lectures, unorganized curriculum, and tries to be rude/intimidating during lecture.

This guy seldom gives clear instructions in assessments that have a large weight in the final grade. Complex and obscure writeup with lots of typos, which is unfriendly for ESL students. Points deducted for not understanding poor writeup. Lecture pace too fast but HWs and exams even faster. Expects students to understand magically by themselves.

This class is super lecture-dependent. That being said, the homework makes a lot more sense if you've paid attention to the examples from class. He's extremely helpful in office hours if you have the time for it.

The first assign had a class average of 55%. That is when is knew this class would be bad. Indeed, I was correct. I think this class can be summarized in the following way: whatever grade you think you deserve, subtract 20 points (at least for anything that is graded manually). Terrible class.

In general this course is tough, however if you truly pay attention to his lectures, read the comments in his code, you will understand what he is talking about. In this class, paying attention to the Lecture matters just as much as attending it. Also, this guy LOVES Monty Python. 

In general, DO NOT start the work for this class last minute.
Horrible. Please avoid
Awful professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Worst professor I have ever had in my LIFE. Does not know how to teach lectures, and constantly makes uncomfortable jokes about students' names, it is ridiculous.  Does not actually teach you anything, he just expects you to understand and implement codes magically and finish assignments. Is not understanding towards students.

In retrospect, a lot of the stuff he taught was very useful. However, I believe the course could've been structured in a way better manner. His Canvas page is extremely disorganized with a lot of random information, which is similar to his lecture style, where he talks with no slides and walks through codes expecting you to remember every word.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He often criticizes other professors for their curriculum while not paying attention to his own. He also tends to berate students when they go and ask for help. It is almost impossible to reach out to him outside of class. From personal experience, he does not seem to care about the students' wellbeing.

The Prof tends not to teach the material correctly and is very unspecific as to how he wants certain assignments done. A lot of credit is taken off for this. He also has strict policies regarding help and makes it hard to receive help directly from him.

Professor unfairly cold calls on students and is decently rude/intimidating during class. His expectations for our assignments are also incredibly unfair and unclear at all, which results in all students getting terrible grades (for example, the average for phase 1 of our semester-long project was a 54%).

Dr. Heise was an amazing teacher. I took her seminar in comp at Pitt and the class was sometimes boring (many easy/long readings) but overall great. It is a grade-less class, so assignments are marked for completion and you are given supportive feedback. 2 times a year you conference with her for an official grade. I could not recommend her more!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Heise is an excellent professor; I took her for Seminar in Composition at Pitt and she was great. She gives encouraging feedback and is super supportive. Also, she does not grade assignments, rather, she just gives you feedback and at the end of the semester, you fill out a self evaluation sheet where you determine the grade that you get.

Dr. Heise is super nice, she will help you out if you have any issues with deadlines or figuring out next steps on papers. Attendance is mandatory and there is a discussion board post and reading due every class but I never did the readings and was fine and the discussion posts are easy. Her lectures are fun and she's a great person and teacher.

Prof Heise is amazing!! You can tell she is passionate about what she does and cares about her students. Take her class if you have the chance, it's a great intro course especially if you haven't been very exposed to writing in the past. She is extremely accommodating and I would take another class of hers in a heartbeat.
Passionate about MatSci, helpful and always willing to explain subjects, very forgiving grading (lots of HW/test drops)

The hw is frustrating in Essentials because directions are not clear and he doesn't proofread his answers to the code. Usually it is pretty time-consuming. That being said, he was really helpful in the Mat Sci intro, and I've heard he's a good teacher in the course he teaches for juniors. He just doesn't like & isn't very good at teaching MATLAB.
Heard is probably the best prof I know and he was super helpful when I was having problems with my lab group.
Great professor and great lab teacher. Funny, clear with directions, and helpful. One of the best professors I've had
Easy class, impossibly boring lecturer, has a heightened sense of self, teaches problems. didn't learn anything. If you can do Physics 1, you will easily get an A in this class.
Interesting presentations, low workload, and generous grader. Cares a lot about the students' learning.
He is soooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo nice!

Super interesting class, would recommend to everyone! Lectures are accessible, Vicki uses loads of real life examples to explain concepts. Midterms are 25 short answer questions so can be difficult if you don't study. However, it is easy to do well if you show up to lectures and study the lecture notes. Read the textbook for a little extra boost.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Test questions are very very specific. Need to memorise exact wording and definitions of all concepts covered, and recite examples given in class.

Dr. Helgeson is amazing. Her Psychology of Gender course is the summation of all of her research; the textbook, written by her is such an easy read. The course relies on student interaction and personal experiences and it's the safest space I've ever had to explore my own thoughts in academia. Easily the best course I've ever taken at CMU.
Tests are hard.

Vicki is an awesome lecturer and provides excellent examples to illustrate class concepts, many from her personal experience. She's also my psychology major advisor, so that's an added bonus when help is needed; however, she has no concrete office hours and encourages students to meet with her whenever, for the student's flexibility.
Great professor, very knowledgeable and holds great discussions in class. Very clear about her expectations. Really really recommend Psych of Gender

Social Psych  Easy class.  Good lecture notes.  Gives good anecdotes.  Need to pay attention in class and give examples of the concepts we learn in class for the test.  Test is composed of many short answer questions.
No Comments
Very challenging, but incredibly knowledgable and interesting.
No Comments
boring lectures
His lectures are mid and he's bad at explaining things.

Kesden has a teaching style that may not be suitable for everyone. Perhaps if you can self-study before the lecture even starts and already knows most of the stuff, you might get something out of it, otherwise you can get lost easily and get cooked.

I'd assume Kesden is good since he taught for so many years. Somehow it's not reflected in the lecture. I stopped going by the 3rd lecture because it has little content and I didn't gain much from attending, I ended up watching past year lectures from 2015. At least 90% of the students did the same, the lecture hall is basically empty.

My only class with B grade this semester, this is unlikely my fault. Looking at the rating of the class 3.17/5, pretty sure the other half who didn't even bother to rate has an even worse opinion of the teaching quality.
just awful, make me sleepy and disinterested in class, when I go into his class I feel stupid not because I  don't understand the lecture but I have no idea what the heck is going on

He talks a lot in non-stop fashion except it's all shallow and superficial. I didn't learn anything from him honestly.  I mean I know two people cannot use toilets at the same time, but I expect CMU prof to be knowledgeable and tell us more about concurrency in-depth, instead of useless stories and not funny jokes.

Love the lecture he gave! Very interesting and the professor is an awesome hilarious person.
Would like to take more courses

worst prof in terms of both teaching and behaving. teaching wise, he is unprepared and disorganized, likes to walk around the lecture hall just to check which students are sleeping, shows no respect for student time (start late without explanation and finish late without considering some students need to go to next class)

not attending his lecture is just a waste of money, attending his lecture is both a waste of time and money, honestly had no clue what he is talking about, he talks for 30 mins on a blank page with some drawing then skip 20-30 slides.. basically have no idea why i am going to the lecture and with nothing learned.. it's just beyond horrible..

Everything negative about him that other students have mentioned is true, i experienced firsthand.
He cannot really teach, he likes to talk about irrelevant stuff, and jump between slides, actually he doesn't even teach materials in slides, it's more like a story-telling comedy show. He is also late for classes. I would avoid taking his classes
Mid professor, great person. He is not the best at lecturing, it can get confusion sometimes. If you're willing to speak up in lecture he will clarify and help, but otherwise you're cooked.

The lectures feel very dispersed and confusing, in a way that doesn't really support intuition. The style kind of feels like skipping from one place to the next with the expectation that we bridge the gap. But with gaps and problems to solve, this approach becomes too dispersed, we don't know where to start, and the depth is limited.

He's ok. But he yaps all the time. I never know what he's getting at and walk out of lectures so confused. His slides are pretty much like his lectures too. He might be good but he's really not good at teaching

He is a really nice guy and a caring person who wants his students to succeed. However they usually don't succeed because he is not very good at teaching. The lectures he gives are so unorganized and he goes all over the place- you get confused within a minute because he goes on a random tangent to something unrelated every 2 seconds.
One of the worst lecturers I have ever experienced in my life. Goes all over the place
Extremely boring and useless lectures.
Boring and doesnt know how to teach
Worst!
He doesn't know how to teach. He keeps jumping in class in name of a joke but isn't funny in anyways!!

Greg is a terrible lecturer, one of the worst. That being said, he cares a lot about his students and is always available for extra help. Although I'm not too sure if that's helpful due to his inability to explain anything. The exam was curved quite heavily when I took the course, so that's nice I guess

Lectures are engaging, but sometimes not very relevant to what you actually need to know to pass the class. However, he really cares about his students and offers plenty of support with office hours, TA office hours, and small group meetings with TAs.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is an okay lecturer but not so great prof overall. May have been the class structure + covid, but I really do NOT recommend taking 18100 with him. (or at all, really)
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Caring professor, always (and really, always) available to help. Reasonable grading schemes, very focused on students learning. Great lectures but fast, you'll get lost very quickly if you don't pay attention.

Kesden is extremely energetic. Some people like that, some people don't. But no matter your opinion on his teaching style, there's no denying that he cares about his students. Kesden has kept his office hours open throughout the night (I've seen him on at 5am) helping students. He insists that you can call him any time, any day. He cares a ton.

Very hard to pay attention teaching style.  Lecture style not very good for learning.  Can't really expect students to learn if you ramble on and on with examples that don't necessarily explain the concept you're trying to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Puts effort into using metaphors, analogies and storytelling in his lectures. Good teacher. The labs were very fair; I loved that he gave us so much freedom to do whatever. The frivolous complaints are from people who needed excessive handholding or had little hands-on programming experience. He was so lenient and easy on his first class at UCSD.
Avoid this professor like the plague. New to UCSD, but is unorganized and has no idea how to manage large scale classes.

I'll give him slack because it was his first quarter teaching at UCSD (Fall '15). He was very energetic and knew the material. Tests were actually the practice exams that were provided, and the group projects were graded extremely leniently.  Passing the class with a good grade was not an issue because I didn't need to learn or retain anything.
Shows up late to class. Admits that his lecture slides are incorrect but doesn't correct them before he posts them online. Unclear instructions for group project. Doesn't prepare you for the final
Okay, I was thinking he's a good professor until I saw his final. Every single instruction is very vague. Would not recommend.
If you want to raise your stress level for some reason, I highly recommend taking his class. Of course, you wont be learning anything except the fact that he's the worst professor at UCSD.

He's late to class and doesn't seem to teach. Rather, he shares stories and tries to engage the class by hitting other students and jumping on their desks. I would not recommend this professor. He also seems to have temperamental issues (ie hitting students). Worst and most annoying professor I've ever had at UCSD .

I agree with what the 3.9 GPA student is saying. I definitely don't think he is a good instructor, because he is always late and unprepared. I have never taken a class in which students wait for the professor for 20 - 30 min each class. The project starts from week 5 with almost no preparation. I don't really learn anything from this class.

This guy is by far the most annoying professor I've ever taken. He has no sense and I think he's he snorts cocaine before coming to class. He is useless, very unclear about the grading scheme, extremely disorganized and a massive waste of your time. Believe me when I say this, I'm a straight A student with a 3.9 GPA. I know what I'm talking about.
Great class.

Energetic and tells fairly engaging stories, but I can remember one time when a student-worker came in to try to fix a faulty projector and when the student couldn't fix it he started ripping on the kid. Not a class act

Class lectures are superb.  He keeps the class interesting and really knows the subject matter. Sometimes assumes you know too much and isn't thorough enough during office hours.  Seems confused when you don't get it.  Class is the hardest one i've taken in my life.

Moves fast, spends more time telling funny stories, climbing on desks, and throwing things at his students than covering lecture material, but is helpful outside of class. Keep on top of your work and lecture is hilarious.

He's such a little boy. Annoying. Too busy taking care of tons of workload. Assumes students already know most of the materials probably because there are many graduate students in the lecture. If you are advanced and want to know much more, he is great.
Awesome class and professor :)
Great teacher.  Thoroughly enjoyed his class.  If you have him, ask him about the Chinese textbook that used some of his code.  He makes some good exceptions.

I came into the class not knowing C but by the end of the semester I knew what was going on. Most of the stuff you know are learned from his extremely hard labs and exams. Don't take him if you want an easy A

Very hard class. Tells students they're "drinking from a fire hose" and it's apt because he expects a lot of learning, quickly. The class is sink or swim, but if you manage you'll learn a ton. I didn't realize how much work was required initially, but once I put in the hours I started getting As on tests.
Doesn't teach anything, expects you to know C already. Does not introduce you to C.
HAWT<3!
Kesden is awesome! I <3 him!!!

Took 15-111 (called 15-121 nowadays) with him. Very energetic lecturer. Hates it if you sleep/goof off in class. Can be frustrating if you're not in CS or not taking courses past what he's teaching because he will sometimes stretch the material and teach beyond the scope of the class.
Awesome. Flat out.
Excellent. Entertaining and informative.

Be warned that 15-100 is not adequate preparation for Kesden's 15-111.  Lectures primarily consist of "humorous" stories and Java esoterica.  Learn the programming yourself the summer/winter before the class and you should be fine.  Exams are straightforward, yet tend to cover the lecture notes more than the class itself.

Very, very tough teacher compared to colleagues teaching the same course.  However, he knows very well what he's talking about, and every student who has gone through him gets the last laugh when the final comes.
He's really nice as well as helpful! Kesden time is always a little later than real time, but if you really want to learn programming, it's Kesden all the way.

I loved Kesden! He is an excellent lecturer, but not very organized. His TAs keep him in line. He is a lot of fun in class and does not tolerate laziness so if you want an easy A, take another class. If you want to work hard and learn C, take Kesden. TAs very knowledgeable and helpful and Kesden always available for help.

Professor believe in the power of the TA. Course taught through example. Examples usually good, but jump around a lot. In love with floodfill. Lives 2.0 miles from campus, but can't get here on time because "neighbors are moving and blocking driveway".

Perpetually late, Arrived 45 minutes late only to be told by student that class started at 9:00 not 9:45. Terrible excuses for absences (ie: bus wedged into intersection, A tree fell on my friend's garage and oh sorry I don't have an excuse today)  Teaches class well if and ONLY if you know what he's talking about. Prior knowledge REQUIRED!!!!!!!!
He gives out really hard tests.

great professor. really tough class, really high demanding but if you wanna learn he is your man;) really no downsides, I loved his class and the way he teached and learned a ton the hard way (ie sleepless nights..). Highly recommended for passionate students not for the rest.
He was an amazing professor, period.  Not for the feint of heart

Not a great 15-100 teacher.  Horrible at explaining simple concepts make you more confused.  Swears, insults, throws things, and jumps up onto desks.  However, he REALLY knows his stuff.  He is available 24-7 for help on labs (mostly easy), I don't think he sleeps, since he replies to emails at 4:00AM within 90 seconds.

He's HARD and he fails 1/2 the class. If U R looking for an easy A, U R in the wrong class. But if U R looking for a gd learning exp., then pay attention. He's v. gd at explaining CS concepts and data structures. Assg. are fairly hard but the exams are the killers. U can't simply BS on the exams and expect him to give U partial credits.

A great professor. Even though the course load was heavy, he always was reasonable. He cared a lot on students doing their best. Always available for help with projects, and course material. (even at night). He is funny and has a great way of making the course more enjoyable.

Kesden is my favorite professor by far, for the reasons that most of the 100 kids hate him.  He swears a lot, and throws stuff at people who are sleeping.  He takes a more ADULT approach to a class of adults.  Also, despite what those say below, he is exceptionally proficient in Java.  Yall need to grow up.
Boooooooooooo
He doesn't know how to properly explain java.
he's a funny guy who keeps class amusing.  oftentimes i'd find stuff on the homework that he didn't go over / went over very quickly in class, but he's very helpful if u go talk to him.
Perpetually late, profane, unprofessional.  Throws things at students and walks on desks.  My diagnosis: Napoleon complex.  Not very good at teaching Java, either.

Doesn't let you take notes on the computer to follow along but he talks to fast to handwrite them. It's close the the end of the semester and I still haven't received grades for the first several projects. How am I supposed to learn from my mistakes if I don't get any feedback?
The professor has a nice attitude but he loses the class.  I have no idea whats going on.
available, but not very helpful, watch out for that truckload of work!  confusing and unclear and very disorganized-ask the TA's for help, not him!
disorganized, unreliable, always late. but, *relatively* easy on the eyes for a cs professor
No Comments
this mofo throws pens at his students =P  he is short and he talks a lot
I found Professor Masters highly intelligent, very, very helpful, and a pleasure.Very good experience!

Hilary knows the craft very well and will be frank and honest with you about your writing ability.  Some people can't handle the criticism, but also some people shouldn't really be writing professionally and should find that out sooner than later. Unfortunately, he isn't always effective in communicating with students as to what he expects of them.
I enjoyed this class and his style of teaching.
No Comments

Honesty and serious effort, not sentimentality, are what matters in Hilary's courses.  These values derive from the respect that he demands that students give each other (and indeed that he fosters).  Not to seriously consider a writer's work and to offer genuine criticism would be belittlement.  Hilary is serious about the craft and shows it.
No Comments

If he doesn't like you, he will encourage the class to join him in mocking you.  He holds his students to his own standards of morality and taste.  He does not care about helping students find their own voice, but instead tries to mold them to his.
Hilary's fair but only if you show a serious commitment to the course and craft.  Otherwise, you might as well drop.
Hilary's the archetypal sage American writer.

Hilary is a fantastic raconteur and professor of writing.  At first, you might think he's a jerk in workshops, but that's because he's very serious about what he does and expects you to be too.  Expect no coddling, but he is generous with his time--just ask.  Very funny.
participate, don't make excuses, and work hard
He takes no crap, but genuinely cares about writing and his students. One of the good guys.

Maybe my biggest complaint about Howie is his absolute lack of flexibility, whether that's delivering 10 min screeds any time a student is late to lecture or completely refusing late work under essentially any circumstances. I missed 40% of a HW because I needed another 15 min to run the code on the hardware and that just wasn't an option.

Intro to Robo would be super cool if it was half the work. Labs are exhausting, but interesting and hands on, but also graded super unforgivingly: the difference between a 100 and a 35 is narrow. Lectures & HW are often only barely related to labs. This course could be 2-3 courses. Also, 'mid semester' grade was returned 2 weeks before end of term.

Awful organization! Weekly home works take up to 2 months to grade, regrade requests take up to a month to handle... The professor literally does nothing outside lecture. All gradings, labs, and even exam-writing are done by overworked and underpaid TAs. 
Lecture and course content wise it's actually pretty good.
Professor lectures about his research and gives homework about material uncovered in lectures. Course staff are really disorganized and confused.

Most of these reviews are old, or for when he taught intro to MechE, but I took his Intro to Robo class and had a great time. He is an engaging lecturer and very approachable. He has high expectations from his students, as all good professors should, and provides the proper resources for his students to succeed.

this professor does not seem to have any clear idea of what he is teaching. i dont think he is happy with his job, because he is depromoted or something. i mean, he is in robotics department, but hes teaching intro to mechE? what's up with that??? DO NOT TAKE THIS GUYS CLASS!!!!

I took his General Robotics class, and wow he is one arrogant guy (no wonder why he didn't get tenure from the MechE dept). I did learn quite a bit, did well in the class, but found it "strenuous"... Lectures are so-so, but kept interesting by his (good/bad) personality.

i thought he was funny at first but i soon realized that he's just an annoying person who can't really teach well.  u don't really have to go to class though, b/c his lectures are crap and the book is really helpful.  i imagine wickert is a better intro to mechE teacher
Not very forgiving or very helpful, don't sweat not taking notes, just read the book and go to recitation.  f you like robots though ths is your man.

He's a robotics freak, so that's all you'll ever hear about. Doesn't so much teach the material as copies Wickert's notes on it onto the board (luckily it's all easy, and the book is pretty good). Funny, but pretty arrogant and annoying at times.

I don't know what the guy who posted before me was smoking. Choset is very impolite and makes fun of students in class. He brags about his course, but it is definitely no big deal. His personality is just very arrogant and irritating. Fun class though.
This guy is awesome! This class is better than sex. (If you don't believe me, do a google search on  howie choset  sex god).

I took this just to fill a requirement and now I am considering majoring in neuroscience because of this class! I found it so interesting and looked forward to lectures. Professor Holt explains things so well and is funny which makes lectures interesting. There are no midterms or finals, just weekly quizzes and projects which was a plus!!!
Super easy course aaawesome professor 10/10
This was an interesting class.  I enjoyed hearing Dr. Holt's insights.
Dr. Holt is the best. I took BFOB with her and there was no exams, just weekly quizzes with two PowerPoints worth of material and 4 problem sets. Great class and I still talk to her even a year later
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Class is rather boring and the material isn't exactly the most interesting for students who have never really been interested in science. It's technically a psych course but the majority of the material is biology-based (hence it's name). Can be challenging if you don't attend lecture because material is usually based on add'l notes from lectures.
The class was fascinating, and she teaches it very well. There was a lot of reading, but it was mostly manageable. She's an excellent teacher in general.
Interesting class, fair grading system. She explained the material clearly and was always available for questions after class.
I am baffled that anyone would find this Professor physically attractive or interesting. I have found her to be abrasive and cold in and outside of class.
No Comments
No Comments
excellent teacher-very knowledgeable, and helpful

I took 15-123 (Effective Programming in C and Unix) with Professor Hoffman in 2010, and I must say that I consider him a truly great teaching professor. The students with an opposite opinion about this man are either completely disinterested in learning technical skills directly applicable to the industry, or just despise programming altogether.

This was a fairly easy introduction to Java from a pretty cool professor. He has a lot of industry experience and often tells interesting stories.  No mandatory attendance for class or recitation and a cool guy...take a class with him.

forgot it was a java class sometimes and would start teaching C, which was kinda humorous. pretty funny guy, mostly nice, although can be rather blunt when you meet with him. pretty decent professor overall, which i guess says something since i hate computer science

He is an outstanding professor.  He really took the time to explain a lot about how Java works under the hood and provide details far beyond syntax.  He used great relevant examples and good analogies to explain everything.  His class was also well organized with a lot of interesting programs to learn how to write Java code.  Take professor Hoffman
He gives a lot of extra credit. The homework and exams are pretty easy if you pay attention.
Hoffman is awesome.  He has some great stories and is just an all around good guy.

Good guy, funny, helpful w/little things that might prop up. 15-113 is pretty basic stuff, but C can get a little tricky for those without a CS background. Posts all the notes online, assignments are basic (and are basic variations of the same thing each semester). Final exam is cake, midterm somewhat harder. 1/2 pay attention, do the HW, get an A.
I was well prepared for the class in general; he's hilarious.  I don't even like computer science!
Not particularly well prepared for classes and doesn't make them particularly interesting (I've had him twice now).
well..i didnt fall asleep in this class..so i guess it was ok..but he gives the hardest midterm ever...but after mine he said it is too hard so he gives easier ones now supposedly
amazing
Howard is awesome.He knows everyone's name and really cares about his students. The best way to succeed in this class is to go to his office hours-he gives great help on hw.
Cares so much about students, is always willing to help, in any way that he can. Only wish he taught more classes.

Howard is a phenomenal teacher.  He cares deeply about his students (memorized all 100+ names in the first week!). He also is committed to teaching (he works hard to make the material accessible, and is constantly seeking to improve).  Great person. Great teacher.  I definitely recommend any class he's teaching.
Nice guy, very accessible. Cares about his students' progress.
He knows how to teach. Well-designed assignments, people come out the class happy and educated. Great guy in person too!
Great teacher. Always puts aside the time to meet with you and answer any questions.
very helpful guy-not a hard course and he's always available to answer questions

Lectures are kinda slow but are interesting. Lots of fun demos. Homework relies heavily on textbook problems (average difficulty). Tests are fair but slightly difficult. Makes you work hard for an A but doable.

My favorite professor here at CMU.  Yes, the class is really hard (Physics 1) but he is THE BEST guy to take it with.  He's super accessible and gets really excited when students come to office hours.  Go to office hours and get to know him - he's fantastic and will work with you until you understand it.  LOVE HIM!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: He's a really great professor who really enjoys what he teaches, and he loves explaining concepts even if they're not a part of the actual class. He references the textbook a lot, and if you're taking Physics for Future Presidents, you'll want to use the textbook to do well. Quizzes are sometimes difficult, but not if you pay attention in class..

Professor Vogel is hands down my favorite professor. Not only is he very clear and explains the concepts well, he is willing to sit down with you and go through everything! I always go to him (even though now i'm in physics 2) and ask him for help. He is more than willing!  I love him. He is a great and caring professor and friend!
awesome teacher. one of the best i ever had. his tests are really not that hard. his quizzes are hard. GO TO CLASS. i didn't and i regret it so much
One of the best professors at CMU! He's friendly, and will always be ready to help any student!

Best professor ever.  He really cares about all of the students, and if you go see him in his office he will instigate conversations with you on a personal level. Incredibly nice and understanding, and incredibly accomplished.

Best physics professor a physics-phobic student could ask for! By the second week of class, my friends and I started to refer to him as our "german grandpa" He is super nice and makes it a point to get to know all of his students! Pro-tip: hes pretty generous at giving back points on exams if you go see him personally (office hours)
He's really good, helpful and extremely accomplished - he works on the LHC!
Take this class if you bad at Physics. It is very similar to high school physics.

Professor Vogel is my absolute favorite professor at CMU.  Amazing, perfect, wonderful personality (go to office hours just to talk!).  I absolutely love him :) Class not too difficult (I had trouble with final though)... good luck!
Very good at explaining, interesting, inspiring, funny, and just awesome in general.
Nice and helpful.
A wonderful Prof

Generally a very good teacher, interesting too.  The only thing that might take getting used to is "Vogel Math."  You cannot use calculators on exams so you sometimes you have to make 'approximations' during the exam.  Only in Vogel's class does 3*3 = 10 :-)
Just splendid and wonderful.  Very enthusiastic, willing to help, and provide further challenging material.
Pretty good teacher, but rather difficult.
Professor Vogel is one of the best teachers I've hd this year.  His clas is funny, interesting and his demos are fantastic.  Absolutely a brilliant man and a brilliant teacher.

Vogel is an amazing teacher. He is good at explaining difficult concepts and always adds humor to lectures which keeps you paying atention. He tries hard to make sure that everyone understands the material. True, the class moves at an exceptionally fast pace and the tests are impossible, but it is still an amazing class which I enjoyed immensely.
Prof. Vogel is the man.. his class experiments are awesome and he helps so that each student does well
No Comments
nirvana.physics.cmu.edu
He taught the class very well.  Vogel has a good sense of humor which helps to liven the class and keep students entertained.  I would recommend his class to anyone able to take it.
Best prof at CMU i've ever had.
Vogel is an amazing professor. He explains concepts very thoroughly in class, provides realistic examples, and is extremely caring about his students. I definately enjoyed taking the course with him.

I'm not a huge physics fan, but his class is ok.  Uses some humor in lecture, don't expect standup.  Assigns a lot of homework, which is checked and graded.  Tests are challenging, but not impossible.  Awkward to talk to outside of lecture, makes me a little uncomforable to ask questions.

What can I say -- he's a great teacher. Although he's starting to get older and slightly more forgetful, Vogel is a wonderful first-year instructor. His methods are always clear and easy to understand, he is open to questions at any point during a lecture, and manages to interject humor into even the most boring of topics.
his tests are really hard, but i don't think Physics 1 people have a choice w/ the teacher...
Vogel math is awesome. Class was wonderful my favorite physics class to date
ok
Vogel is Awesome!!!
i love him!

Vogel is a very unaccomodating teacher.  I once woke up the morning of the exam with the flu.  I couldn't even walk to the bathroom without throwing up.  He wouldn't let me take the exam the next day, after I had been to the doctor.
Made me consider switching my major until I realized engineering classes aren't nearly as good as his.
This dude meditates about the universe in his free time.
his tests are hard! look at old tests on his website
Dr. Vogel is a god.
Vogel is the man
great teacher, funny jokes, hard hard class
one of the best teachers to ever walk the face of the earth.
handwriting is horrible but his jokes are funny.  class is hard get tutoring
notorious for pressuring students to drop out if their work doesn't match her style, often dismisses students' work as trash, and her project advice changes daily, leaving her expectations unclear

you will never understand what she is saying, she'll take you on a long journey that seems like you'll end up with helpful points by the end of it, but it always ends in nonsense. if your style or work doesn't align with your tastes, i wish you luck. extremely micro-aggressive as well.

Horrible. Don't take her class, take a semester abroad if you u can. Does not know how to give real feedback and has no real world experience. Her projects change every year and are a hit or miss. If you get a bad project then be prepared to lose your sanity and a lot of sleep. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS

Known to make students cry and encourage them to drop out of the program. If you are a gifted designer but aren't repackaging the kind of design she likes, you're in for a rough time. Openly prejudiced towards other disciplines and tracks.

Lives up to the The famous rumor she knocked over a freshmans project from the table to the ground. She left saying its probably better than it was before shes rude and cant tell the difference between asian girls nor does she try. I cant say she is not a good designer but def does not have the personality quality of a professor.

Be prepared to answer the question, "So how would you happen upon this?" or "Where does this product live?" literally every class. She will give feedback on your project to you one day, and say the exact opposite the next day.
Disrespectful, unkind, and unpleasant. Gives unconstructive criticism and meaningless feedback. Discourages passion for design.

she has her favorites, also won't give you feedback until the very end, when its just too late to do anything .... also a direct quote from freshman yr "make sure you drink lots of water", this was her only advice before the entire class pulled an all-nighter before the project deadline
she has a serious attitude problem, and needs to get a real design job before she continues forcing students to do pointless projects.

she means well, but she's really hard to understand, and is more interested in the experimental than the practical. she also has a hard time giving constraints to a project and sometimes they are unreasonable, for instance we were assigned a video project to be displayed in a gallery, but she get a gallery or see if we had access to video cams. :(

You better agree with her.  Not interested in the practical aspects of design, Kristin is more into the theoretical crap.  Not hard to understand - she went straight from university education to university faculty - never had a real-world job.
the semester was very structured but her focus was way out there and she seemed extremely interested in the meaning of projects. she is a 'driven' person and expects you to be too. creative.
A world expert in what he teaches: makes you learn things you won't find on textbooks or the Internet. Makes you think during lectures. Heavy load (at least 18-756).
In and out, doesn't care.
Doesn't provide help AT ALL.
non-transparent grading, bad teaching, even if there are multiple ways of doing, he only allows his way
The Final comprehensive exam is tough and needs a lot of attention. Even though the hours/week is less you need to put in a lot of time to understand and learn the subject
Best Professor!

reviews.comment: Worst professor had in CMU
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Brilliant professor. The lectures are delivered to pin-point precision. While, he is extremely strict about certain things (No acceptance of homework if late by 1 minute), he is extremely approachable and cares about students' learning.
Best prof ever! Disciplined and great teacher.
Best professor ever
Prof talks very clearly and he is very supportive.
His lectures are well organized and clearly delivered.
excellent prof
He's extremely good at explaining concepts.
A very good teacher
quizzes almost every class, heavy courseload, tons of readings and writing assignments.

reviews.comment: quizzes almost every class, heavy courseload, tons of readings and writing assignments
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Jarbo's lectures are always very engaging and interesting. He is very fair and the class should be easy if you keep up with the readings and do your weekly reflections in a timely manner. Both him and Dr. Manke-Miller are super understanding and accessible, and their class is amazing. Hope to take another class with them sometime.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very helpful both in class and for career development
Amazing Professor

Prof. Hrusa is one of the best professors I have ever had! Not only does he teach things brilliantly, his class will help you grow your professional aspirations tremendously. Also, he is an extremely caring person, something that you may not find that easily!

Overall, great professor and person! Definitely recommend

Really nice guy. He's extremely passionate about math and puts in so much work for his students. Schedules office hours regularly, posts lecture notes, and loves working with students on open-ended homework. If you get the option, take his 3 unit mini 21-101 (discrete-time dynamical systems). Fairly easy but very interesting and rewarding.
You cant graduate from CMU without going through this man's class. Amazing.

He's the best professor out there. Incredibly dedicated to the course and hilarious. He can be intimidating sometimes but it's because he really cares about student learning and tries to make sure you don't consistently make the same errors. He provides the course text online for people so many people don't tend to come to the lecture.

Hands down the best professor I've had at CMU. Not only is he an amazing lecturer but he truly cares about students and would go far and beyond to help them. He is also extremely committed to his course, going out of his way to make extra office hours and sending us tons of emails regarding updates. CMU is truly lucky to have a professor like him.
LOVE this professor. Teach well, hold a lot of office hours, give great advice to students... He always tries his best to help students. I am taking classes just because he is teaching it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Undoubtedly the best instructor at this institution. His commitment to the course and to students is unparalleled.

Best professor I've ever had at CMU. He is a very reasonable person with respect to homework, exams and grades. He is very dedicated to his job, even holds office hour until 11pm when needed. He almost knows every student's name in his class and cares about students a lot.
Amazing teacher.

He was one of the best professors I had at CMU, really personable guy who cares a lot about the students. He even sent me an e-mail at the end of the semester congratulating me for getting an A in the class. Wish I had interacted with him more when I was there.
BEST PROFESSOR EVER. He knows every student's name even though it is a huge class with 150 students. He offers great help in academics outside of the class. EXTREMELY WONDERFUL PROFESSOR!

I had Professor Hrusa his first year at CMU, and felt he was engaging, funny, and a very good teacher. He genuinely cared about his students and had a way of reaching them. I would recommend him w/o reservation.

Best Math Teacher I've ever had, easily available to talk outside class. Even if the material is difficult, he'll find a way to teach it that makes it easy. Patient with answering questions, and a kind/understanding person in general.

I never would have realized that math finance was so interesting.  Very helpful both in and outside of class.  If it's hard, it's because you need to understand both the math and the finance; you can be a perfect mathematician, and if you don't get the "economic" interpretation, you can throw your math out the window.

Cares about his students and is an entertaining lecturer.  Amazing at teaching the material.  However, his class is difficult and he said "I would fail my own mother to protect the integrity of this university"

I am a recent graduate and found Hrusa to be the best professor I've had by leaps and bounds.  He is effective in delivering difficult material and is also accessible in aiding students and answering questions.  Phenomenal teacher that I highly recommend!

I've taken 6 courses with Prof. Hrusa and have had the privilege to TA for him once.  I find him to be one of the most dedicated, fair, and clear teachers that I've seen in undergraduate and graduate school.
Prof Hrusa is a great math teacher. Best math prof I had at CMU. Very easy to understand, engaging, and interesting. I loved his class on engineering math, and would wholeheartedly recommend him!
Best math Prof by far I had at CMU
No Comments
Best prof I ever had, excellent teaching ability. Didn't think Calc in 3D would be so straightforward when taught correctly.
wonderful, wonderful, wonderful
Not only is he a great lecturer, but he will also go out of his way to help his students like none other! Stop me if you've hear this one before: this is the best math teacher I've ever had!
Really, excellently clear in lectures.
Explains things very well and is always available for extra help.
Hrusa is the best math teacher I've ever had.  He is very intellegent, and knows how to make calculus enjoyable with his subtle humor.  You'd be a fool to take calc in 3d from any other teacher.
Hrusa is the best math teacher ever. If you can, take a class with him; the class may suck but he makes it good.
No Comments
Good professor

He is a great professor. Very passionate about the subject, and will do anything to help his students succeed. He always was giving us contact information of various ChemE people who came in to speak to us about research and jobs in the field. Also, his office hours were extremely helpful, as the weekly homework could be extremely challenging.
good man
best professor
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Prof. Schneider is really really good. Warning: his intro to chemE class is hard. Yes. But if you go to his office hours he will do anything he can to help you. Take advantage of the TA office hours to make them help you understand and his office hours. This class requires alot of studying. It is a class where if you want to pass, its up to you, no

Very interesting teacher, and I like his teaching style. However, the class material itself is pretty dry and by the end of lecture I'm always half-asleep. Not because he's boring, but because I find material balances to be the most boring topic I've studied since Latin.
Great teacher
He's interested in his work but isn't exactly a good teacher. Presentation of materials is poor and he's unapproachable after class. Doesn't return emails either.
Stars Galaxies and the Universe is very interesting and entertaining.  Low stress, lots of extra credit, I have learned a lot about astrophysics/ cosmology, and he is genuinely interested.

Don't expect to get much out of lecture. Copies and pastes problem sets from other professors without even checking the dates on them, uses wikipedia as a textbook. Seemed unprepared for class most of the time, often late, didn't seem to take class very seriously.
Quite probably the single worst physics professor at CMU. Don't expect to learn much in lecture. Frequently cancels class or ends class early just because he doesn't care.

Seemed disinterested in teaching the lessons.  Never did examples (and often got lost in a problem when he attempted them).  Homework was confusing at times (first textbook was terrible).  Regular exams were pretty straight forward, final exam was pretty hard.

Lectures were pretty disorganised-they were moderately interesting, but i'd realize that I don't actually know anything well when I sat down to do the HW. I have a lot of complaints about both books, so if youre in Phys 3 go to the library pick ANY book off the phys shelf, and ii will be better than the textbook. And ask questions.

Doesn't teach much in lectures. Exams are not hard though. After teaching half a semester when the other professor takes over, said he's going to the South Pole to search for a site to install an astrophysics device of some sort. I think he was for real!
Meh. Wouldn't take again, but also didn't kill me or my GPA.
super sweet guy that is understanding of extenuating circumstances and wants to see nothing but success from his students. absolute GOAT
Bearable first year writing instructor. He makes it clear what he wants. Just follow directions and don't be stupid.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: He can't effectively teach, when he grades, he writes comments without giving feedback on how to revise it. There is also an unreasonable amount of work that is not necessary for class, it creates a lot more stress than necessary when this is only a gen ed requirement. I have better things to focus on that's not your petty and unuseful comments
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Jim Daniels, while he gets off topic occasionally, is contagiously passionate about the craft of poetry. And as a person with anxiety, he was very lenient with grading participation after one exchange with him on the subject.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: One of the few professors I've had that I can actually talk to for more than just office hour time.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Most of class is spent discussing what students thought of vague, literary short stories. Pretty much zero time is spent on the actual craft of fiction. The major grade is participation; talking about "how you feel" is the main grading metric. Only worth taking to get to the workshops after it.
thinks he's brilliant and gorgeous but really a solipsistic jerk
he's a good guy. teaches well, doesnt push too hard in any one direction - not sure though if thats good or bad...
No Comments
No Comments
Great class.  I did not find him to be stifling to creativity in the slightest.
No Comments

Really great approach to teaching and reading poetry; was extremely supportive of me when I was trying to figure out whether or not to go into writing.  Highly recommended if you want to work hard and take writing seriously!
conceited, arrogant bore. his poetry is stale bread and he thinks he's better than blake, but he sucks****. make him get a real job.

Lousy teacher. Too conceited to praise anyone's poetry but his own -- but only gives good grades to students who write HIS way. Forget about being creative or experimental if you want to pass. You even have to buy HIS book for the class.
Daniels is a fantastic poet, and his tradition of industrial Detroit really resonates with Pittsburgh readers. Just being around the guy is a pleasure.
He tends to go off-topic a bit much, and his introductory courses are boring and crappy, but his actual workshops are quite useful and he gives very good written critiques of poems.
No Comments
Enjoyed the course overall, however a few of the movies were a little dullunenjoyable (55 days in peking, gate of heavenly peace)

I thought the class discussions were very interesting and helpful. The readings were small enough to not be over-whelming, but still incredibly informative and framed discussions well. I love that I learned more about both China and film, both in an enjoyable manner.

She is such a hard ass when it comes to giving points. She's the government and your grades are tax money. No thats not right, at least there are tax returns. She does not give you a break.  A classic feminist chinese teacher that tries to make her class seem important by grading it as if it is a graduate course. My rec? Would not take.
Moves very fast. Did not respond to my emails. During lecture went on rants about unrelated topics

Very nice guy, probably the nicest professor I've had.  I took him at Pitt and took CS cause it's a good skill to have.  He moves VERY quickly and if not for seeking lots of help, I would've done poorly.  CS in general is based on you working REALLY REALLY hard to understand it (especially if you're a non-major like me)

I have never programmed before and find it very confusing, but there was so much help available in this class that i did very well. Hes a great teacher and I am sad that they are canceling this class. It was a lot of work, but they made it manageable. A+ professor

Jim is experienced and good at what he does. He makes the class interesting, and also REALLY easy if you care. It took me little effort to get an A in this class, and I had a good time when I went. He's a great computer scientist and really cares about the success of his students.
15100 is required for my major, and before being in Jim's class, I thought of it as a just get it done kind of thing....Now, I really do have a strong interest in CS and may minor in it!
No Comments
I probably wouldn't have survived freshman year without this guy and Stehlik.
Jim is the man. Right up there with Stehlik, seriously.  He also taught Fencing, which rocked.  One of my favorite people at CMU as a person and professor.

Jim is a really nice guy and a really helpful teacher.  When you don't understand something, he will be happy to explain it to you again and again until you understand.  His lectures can be alittle hard to follow.  I was sick with the flu and he let me reschedule the test until the next week.
HE IS THE BEST!!!! AND THE BEST MOOSE ;)
No Comments
an amazing teacher
this guy knows his #@$%...take his class

The course content was interesting but the way the professor taught it was kind of boring unfortunately. The class was easy though, just show up and complete the assignments with a normal amount of effort and you're set.
Prof Soluri was kind and understanding. His lectures were really interesting too!
He's the best prof I've had at CMU so nice
John Soluri is a great guy and a good professor; if you put the work into the classes (and it's not a whole lot, usually about 50-100 pages/week) and participate in class, you'll get an A for sure.

Really cool professor. I will admit, his voice is a little difficult to remain focused on during lecture and he occasionally moves very fast, but his courses are interesting and he is always willing to put notes on blackboard or explain concepts further.
A pretty monotonous voice, it becomes pretty easy to fall asleep in class

Bravo Professor Soluri for actually making an introductory class interesting. For once, we learned about something other than the Crusades; the quizzes, papers and exam were all really straightforward.
Great professor, very interesting discussions.

His lectures and recitations are not that helpful for me to be honest. The workload for this class is rather light, all we need to do for every week are two readings with five questions each and one FRQ.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Prof Smith is a great guy! Each week is 2 readings, reading questions, and a short essay. Lectures are interesting and discussion-based.
A real gem at Carnegie Mellon University! You are lucky to have this man as your professor, he will make you see the world in new and exciting ways.

Like the others said, Prof Smith is really receptive to student feedback--which is great--but the first quarter of 80-100 was brutal. Long readings due Monday and Wednesday. The grading criteria isn't clear as well and the comments the TAs/Smith leaves are ambiguous. Lecture was essentially useless in my POV but others really enjoyed his lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Incredible guy. Super interesting while lecturing or when talking with. Gives two questions on a reading as homework and then discusses them in class. Then the class breaks into small group discussions about the material. He'll change the course's format based on student input too. Contender for best prof at CMU.
Great professor! Incredibly smart, and will take students' concerns and opinions into consideration. Is approachable. Do the readings and homework and show up to class and you will get an A
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Amazing! So genuinely nice and caring to his students, and he WANTS to give you an A so don't give him a reason not to? Just do the (short) readings and write (short) responses to 2 questions, 2x a week + show up to class and you'll get an A for sure. (You could probably do less and get an A, but why? It's easy and interesting, respect the guy)
I love this man. He is genuinely a wonderful Prof that just cares about having his students learn. The most intelligent person I've ever met.

The professor cares too much about her lectures; if you multitask, she will get mad and pull you aside. Her rubric is unclear however, she grades unnecessarily harshly, and her presentations are incredibly superficial and dramatic.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Wish I could go back in time before I met her

Professional Julie is kind and can give feedback but I wouldn't completely suggest taking the course with her. She does give a large chunk of reading the first couple of weeks (30+ pages before each class).The work is not too bad but she does expect far too much for a first year writing class. Most classes are group based and participation required
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Mediocre but nice enough, does give a large chunk of reading the first couple of weeks. Majority of work and class spent in group work, somewhat easy grader. Don't expect to learn much but is friendly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Took Concepts and Graph Theory from him... If you've already had a normal class in or have some background in whatever Schaeffer is teaching, and want to gain a more formal, rigorous understanding of the material, his class is for you. Otherwise, wait for someone less pedantic.
He is the man. All you do is write one paper and his standards for writing quality are low. Pretty easy if you show up and listen.
Fun class, really easy. About a month of cool stories about math, and a single large paper about like any topic.

I love him, but he's a horrible teacher. Most all students aren't as smart as him, but he doesn't compensate. He likes abstraction too much to help most students. It's possible to get a good grade in his classes with little work if he trusts that you're bright, however that doesn't help learning either. But try to get him off-topic; he's hilarious.
I took this guy 30 years ago, and he could not teach then.  Seems some things never change. I am a college math professor in spite of the fact I got a "D" in his class my senior year.

Good luck. I came to every class, diligently took notes, read the book, tried the HW... and got nowhere. Dropped the class, took Matrix the next semester, and suddenly everything actually made sense. Maybe it's b/c I prefer numbers and closed solutions to random garbled letters, but maybe it's because no one understood what he was trying to teach.
He is the awesomest man alive. And his wife makes great breakfast.
No Comments

I thought I was the only one that was completely out there. He sounded so smart, looks like albert einstein, and most of the class appeared to follow him. It makes me feel so much better to know that i'm not alone. It was much easier to retake this one in the summertime. I feel bad for wasting my parents money.
By far the worst teacher I've ever had, and that's saying a lot considering this is CMU.  A classic example of a guy who has no business being in a classroom.

I was a sophomore in 1970 in a class with a bunch of grad students.  I don't know that anyone had any idea what was going on, but if you didn't drop the class you got an A.  It was pretty much way above anyone in the class at that time.
Very challenging, and his style is not for everyone. If you love math (and can learn from proofs), he's your guy.  If you need more structure, look elsewhere.
He didn't seem too bad...unfortuneately I have had worse teachers...
Horrible teacher.
Really looks like he enjoys teaching the class. I have no idea what's going on.

I concentrated almost entirely on getting a high grade in the class, but still struggled to get an A. I didn't realize before taking the class that almost more than half of the people in the class were PhD students, and the standard of assessment was completely based on the doctoral model of doing research
One of the best professors Ive had at CMU. Really good in class with sarcastic humor. tough but fair grading, people like to complain when they dont do well. You have to work for it.
OK Lecturer. Assessments in this class are horrible. Takes off points for irrelevant mistakes. The grade you get in this class does not reflect your understanding of the material.
Kelly Rivers is so nice, excellent lecturer! Class was so clear. She is so kind. Like her Halloween look!

Shes a great teacher. I came in dreading programming because of a bad experience, and I came out wanting to take more programming classes. She is probably the best lecturer I've had this school year, and she explains super crazy concepts clearly. The tests can be hard but you get a cheat sheet, and they even provide one that is usefull.
Taking this class can be torture for students with no prior knowledge.

Professor Kelly is a fantastic lecturer, even if the class is a bit fast paced. She makes it clear that she values student feedback. Office hours is really usefultake full advantage of them! I like how she takes time during lectures to answer students' questions, but this does sometimes result in her not finishing her lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: v good lectures, pace of the class is pretty fast if you don't have previous cs experience but still very doable. rivers is a great prof and is super helpful during office hours. quizzes are not too bad and hw takes some time but you learn a lot. if you have cs experience it'll be an easy A, if you don't you'll at least get a B if you do the hw.

Professor Rivers is an awesome professor and will go out of her way to help her students; her lectures were fun and engaging. Just like any other class, you need to put in the work and start assignments early.

Kelly Rivers is a good teacher for those that already know how to code, which goes against the class itself which should be an introductory class to coding. She goes too quickly during lectures, and accepts too many individual students' questions which cuts into valuable class time
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Her lectures are fairly simple to understand if you pay attention. She's really good at answering questions from students and also explains using examples that are easy to understand. Homework is on the harder/time-consuming side, so be sure to work with other students. Midterm averages are around 80%. Lenient on grading and an enthusiastic prof.

Professor Rivers is one of the most prepared and knowledgeable professors I've had! She is really helpful in lecture but you still need to work hard outside of lecture. Some homeworks can be really hard but the quizzes and midterms weren't all that bad.

Really amazing lecturer - if she's teaching go to 112 lecture! She is straight and to the point and makes examples very clear. The class is hard though (I'm a CS major so it wasn't as bad for me) because it's so fast paced but you do learn a lot. Lots of h.w that take a long time, though the h.w and midterms/quizzes load isn't dependent on her.
great teacher, learnt how to not be a terrible coder

Prof Rivers is an amazing lecturer; You will never be sleepy during her lectures. She ensures that her lectures are fun and interactive, and you could certainly feel the enthusiasm within her. However, sometimes she devotes slightly too much time for taking questions from students, which may result in the last part of lecture being too rushed.

Definitely one of my favorite professors. Prof. Haworth's lectures are fun and inspirational. His assignments are fun, and his feedback on them is very helpful. He is not a tough grader and is always willing to help you learn more.

Lovvveddd Professor Haworth. In the writing class you can really incorporate any of your own personal interests for the big writing projects which is super nice. The class is super easy, enjoyable, and Professor Haworth is just generally a super interesting person who is clearly passionate about what he does. Definitely recommend taking this class.

Took a half semester freshman writing course on graphic literature with him. Interesting in lecture, an easy grader, open minded. Generally will let you get away with a lot as long as you have good intentions. Definitely a pretty good professor and an even better person. Thoroughly enjoyed my time with him. Would recommend.

The only negative comment I have is about the course. The section Prof. Haworth teaches was the only one available at my registration, so I was stuck in it - it also wasn't that interesting to me. Nothing against him as a teacher though. He made the class very interesting for everyone. Would take another class with him, but in a different topic.
Great prof
Bless this man

Such a good professor. Office hours are very helpful and he really cares about helping a student succeed in his class. The exams are difficult, but he gives out the layout of all of the reactions needed, so study and your set.
He is a really good professor. He's funny and cares about the students success. Plus, many of the students have crushes on him. Go to his office hours!
Lectures are not helpful, so either you know how to study or you fail.
Got the idea, Got the flavor

Very easy grading, but doesn't like people not attending regularly. You are a failure if you get a C, consider that a fail with him. He's not really clear but the courses he teaches are easy and he makes them easier,  but you need to self-study, you can't rely much on lectures.
Very good professor. She really cares. Probably the smartest person at CMU honestly.

For anyone interested in political science, this class couldn't get better. Skinner's lectures are fantastic and full of current applications, and the tests are fair (although they do cover a lot of content). She's extremely approachable and goes out of her way to engage her class.

Fantastic teacher who always keeps you interested and on your toes. Not afraid to put her students on the spot and makes everyone contribute during class. Have a quiz every class on a reading but its worth it for what I got out of the class

Not too hard a class; attendance doesn't seem to matter all that much. One or two readings a week. No real homework - just exams (5 total). Discussions of readings in class for the most part. A few extra credit assignments.

very nice person, and without a doubt one of the best professors and top researchers at carnegie mellon. difficult to get in contact with at times, but definitely worth taking the class. and good luck, her classes fill up real quick.

I have him right now for business, society and ethics. Class material isn't always the most exciting, but that was to be expected and not the professor's fault due to the material of the course. Really enjoy the professor though. Always very funny and does a great job at keeping the class engaged. My most enjoyable course as of this semester.

He is literally the most amazing professor. Probably the best business ethics professor in the world. He cares about student and also cares about the subject he teaches. He poses questions for you to think which really helps you learn the subject. Almost always available in his office to answer questions.
What I learned in this class was actually much more useful than I had expected. He also cares about your learning. He apparently held office hours during spring break..
Amazing professor to sit down and have a conversation with about business ethics. He really cares about your learning.
He approaches Business Ethics with an interesting sense of humour. Pretty awesome, overall

DON'T take 10601 with Professor Kim. Extremely unclear lecturer. Lecture slides are  full of mistakes and confusing notations. Homework assignments were prepared by the TAs, who gave ambiguous explanations/questions and were slow to clarify. Final project was done in groups of two, and little guidance/clarification was given. Take the other section
