Professor Kim is an absolutely terrible professor. She is very, very poor at explaining concepts; even those that are relatively simple. She also is very slow to respond to emails and Piazza posts, and her homeworks and exams were all riddled with careless mistakes (which again, she was very slow to correct on Piazza).
First time she taught this class. Super theoretical and unhelpful. Take the class in section A instead.

This class was one where the professor was tough but supported students. She offered us extra credit, and we were able to turn in test corrections for the midterm. She also provided study guides with a short list of practice questions for each test (some were on the final), and dropped some of our attendance and discussion board assignments.

did not provide clear expectations or guidelines in preparation for the midterms and final. expects us to know lots of material and was not clear on which material will be on exams. most students take this class as a gen ed, but this class was made unnecessarily hard. attendance was taken too seriously and was not forgiven even when sick
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Kliewer class might be a bit boring at times (Lecture), but she's incredibly caring and the readings are  lengthy at times. Attendance is done based off in class assignment and mostly discussion posts, along a midterm and final. You can tell she is very dedicated and loves to talk about the subject, and she is very reasonable with grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: If you value peace don't even attempt this course... Overly critical, difficult to reach out to, and is nearly impossible to score a good grade in. Please do NOT take her courses.

This professor is a harsh grader with vague, overly critical feedback. Assignments labeled as “15-minute” tasks take hours, and grading is slow. Expectations are unclear, effort often goes unrecognized, and the workload is far beyond what's advertised. Avoid this class if you want clarity or fair feedback, or simply a good grade (impossible).
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Weak class material given in a Zoom format that requires about 15 hours of non-interactive attention over a single weekend and grades harshly despite the obvious limitations of the format and cramming of lectures. You won't receive any grades or feedback until over half of your grade is already submitted, and the grading does not catch up later on.

Taking class with her was the worst choice I've ever made. Extremely harsh grading, extremely aggressive comments. The class I took with her was only a 3-unit writing/seminar class at CMU, and she gave me a C. I stayed up a whole night writing a 2000-word paper and received a 84/150. Avoid her at all cost. She's a nightmare.

Avoid Korryn's seminar at all costs. Although you may learn something about storyboarding and prototyping for a VR experience, you will regret taking her class all semester long. Extremely harsh grader, very passive-aggressive, and makes horrible jokes. Last person you'd want teaching your first-year seminar course.

Professor Ada recommended this class. I was excited to learn about complexity theory and this class didn't disappoint. The material was great (though hard) but Kothari gave well-prepared lectures and had clear lecture notes. His office hours were particularly helpful. I recommend taking this class with Kothari if it's offered again.
Prof Kothari's lectures were really fun and engaging despite being about sometimes hard math.
If you are taking 455, you must take it with Prof. Kothari.
Teaching is not important for him, only himself, avoid by all means!
Amazing! Prof. Kothari made an online class feel more interesting than in-person classes. Would 100% take it again.

Just lower your expectation for the class. Despite teaching at CMU for 10+ years, the class is still one of the least structured I have ever taken. Don't even bother trying to do well in this class. The grading scheme will make you get either B, B+ or A- no matter how hard you try. And of course you learn nothing LOL.
No structure. Lecture and homework have no correlation and quite boring. Fake smile and bad grades
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Expectations are vague. Course content is not very helpful or meaningful.

Sorry to say, but this course provides little if not none value to software engineers. The fact that this class is a requirement of the MSE program makes the program arguably a bad way to spend your money if you focus on learning. And yea he gives bad grades on really easy stuff

Not a very responsible professor and it seems like he doesn't respect students. The Master of Software Engineering program at CMU aims to focus on industry knowledge, but in this class you will learn nothing while getting bad grade. Definitely worth $5000 USD for the class, and it is a core class you can't drop it/switch out.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Communications professor that doesn't respond to emails. Do not respect students and he is really mean. Gives really rude comments on homework and do not care about students' success. Yes these are true and good luck if you take his class

Great class. She provides the reading material and instruction videos. There are 6 assignments and 7 quizzes. All assignments are doable and very helpful in better understanding the material. Would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: One of the best classes I have ever taken at CMU. A wonderful professor who cares about her students.
Great
none except that's she's one of the most knowledgeable person on the subject.
A little bit unsure in the beginning about the relevancy of this course with public policy major but then she unfold the experience through the assignment. Best course that I've ever taken at CMU.
friendly knowledgeable and helpful
Good Teacher, Assignments are right on.
I found her class very difficult but very rewarding. If you really work hard and take the time to sit with her, you'll succeed. She's very sweet and an excellent listener.
reviews.rating_tags: Inspirational--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Bluntly said, if you do not write in a specific style, you will be unfairly torn apart by her. She allows for no personal voice and makes backhanded comments in class. While she is incredibly intelligent, she comes across as harsh and clearly thinks there is one way to do things: her way.
Just perfect
No Comments
No Comments

Really cares about her students, but her lectures are really confusing and all over the place which makes the homework and exams difficult. She grades exams leniently though. Three midterms and the final is optional.
Deeply invested in her students. Her enthusiasm for the class and her students can be easily seen. It also helps that she's very funny.
Very nice teacher very caring.
sweet, personable, knowledgeable , intelligent, helpful, wants you to learn not turn you into a machine
Overall a pretty good professor!

Dahl was the worst professor I've ever had.  She was never willing to answer questions, didn't make herself very available, and overall was incredibly rude.  She treats students like they are beneath her and not worthy of her time.

Dahl was difficult, but fair. calss was interesting and she was funny. always extremely helpful and willing to answer questions. I had to work hard to earn my grade, but she definately wanted everyone to succeed.

Prof. Dahl was incredible!  Her class was very difficult and took the most time/effort during the semester by far, but I learned so much and have really come to love ChemE.  She's very funny and makes the material very approachable and understandable.  She's also very helpful and wants you to do well in her class.
Awesome teacher! She teaches you everything that you need to know about basic Chemical Engineering. IF you read the book before coming to class, you will be fine.
Students learn a lot from her class, but her class is quite hard.

One word: Horrid. A teacher from the 70s, with zero grasp of how companies and world work today. If you want to listen to stories of what work for baby-boomers, treat yourself to his fantasies. If not - get an internship anywhere else and you'll learn more in 2 days there than in a semester with David.
Amazing Professor, his lectures are the best

Professor Krackhardt is an AMAZING lecturer. Every week he picks at least 1 group (usually 2) to present a case, but the cases are usually very interesting to read and straightforward. He teaches by putting his students in the position of managers and needing to make critical decisions. Very interesting topic taught by an amazing professor.

By far the best instructor I have had so far at CMU (and the class was outside my major).  I really enjoyed his teaching methods.  He engages the entire class in discussion, to the point where most want to contribute.  Structure of class very well planned out.
writes for hours on this chalkboard, but this is very useful if you manage to follow her.
tough, tough, tough, but you learn a lot! :)
Just awful.  Notes were all over the place and were not useful when doing the homework.

Terrible teacher. Got smart with you if you tried to ask questions, didn't explain what variables in formulas meant, and was just downright disrespectful to students. I hope they never let her teach another class again.

Ass't professor. Lectures from prof's notes, seemingly without going over them before class. Drags out simple derivations over several days, putting us behind schedule. Seemingly unfamiliar with material (told us that the current between two bulbs in series is zero). Slavishly follows prof's (Swendsen, which see) absurd grading system.

Completely uninterested in the class.  Not sure why he was there.  The topics chosen seem interesting enough, but you must learn on them yourself.  I guess he is a new teacher, maybe he will be better later on.
not prepared usually, learns how to do things along with students
No Comments
homework a lot. But a fun and important class. I would recommend it!
Get ready to learn. You need to know the material inside and out for this class but that's a good thing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Tough class

Business majors get their panties in a bunch about this class, but its the only real class they actually take. Any math, cs, engineering person will find this is one of the easiest classes in their freshman schedule.

Klepper is one of the best professors at cmu. If you are a smart and dedicated student you will get an amazing amount out of the class, as he not only will teach you econ but how to apply it in a variety of current issues. I highly recommend having lunch with him (something he offers. He's the reason I wanna minor in econ.

I'm not gonna lie, I sometimes lost focus staring at how hot he is. Definitely a teacher for looks and knowledge too. But seriously if you're stupid. Leave before you fail with a W. But then again his hotness is worth it. #cheetah. Also the t.a's really make the class so get a good one
Klepper is one of the hottest professors you will ever have. #cheetah. also if you are dumb, dont take this class, you will fail

Klepper is one of the best teachers at CMU, and he's taught for 30 years so he already knows how you're going to mess up.  I've found that smarter students love the class, and less bright ones hate it.  I feel no pity, this class is one of the few which isn't dulled down.

I came to CMU with AP credit in economics but I decided to take this class anyway. Biggest mistake of my life. I dropped it after the second week and took the credit. True or false. Black or white. This or that. How can you teach economics like that? AP Macro - 5. AP Micro - 5. 2 weeks of Klepper - C.
He's an economist so he knows what he's doing you just got to listen and study everything he says....its definitely not an easy A.
I was TA for Klepper for two years back in 2003 and 2004. I think he is one of the best professors at CMU. It is not easy class but you learn a lot

My hardest class and also the most interesting. He measures your understanding by taking what we learn and applying them to new situations and sometimes adds extra variables to questions we've had on minitests. You LEARN.
Class is actually interesting

He always teaches materials not related to his textbook. So it is sucks. On monday and Wednesday he does his lecture and on Friday (only for one day!!) we and TA go through textbook homework and quiz never related to his lecture. Beside, his final test is really funny. 100 hundred questions without explanation only T/F !!! it just measures luck
He is really an interesting guy. Hard class but I've learned a lot.
From what I have heard, it seems like you do better in this class without knowing anything about econ before having klepper

I didn't know that Economics could be this enjoyable, interesting, and HARD. Klepper is by far the BEST lecturer and teacher I've ever had in my life. He is on top of his game in EVERYTHING, including schedules, homeworks, exams, and grading.  btw, FINAL SUCKED. it was the hardest exam ive ever taken in my life.

His "win every argument" attitude makes him sound dictatorial and makes some students feel defeated during class discussions. Other than that, he's pretty knowledgeable and very effective in his teaching.

Klepper's tough and demanding, and he gives a good amount of homework each week.  His exams are definitely no walk in the park.  That said, he's an excellent teacher and you will truly understand microeconomics after having him.
No Comments
HARDEST CLASS I'VE EVER TAKEN!!! Wait until the spring to take this class...Klepper does not mess.
hard class but klepper is an excellent brilliant teacher. Not your intro to econ class but worth taking
intro to econ was harder than any other econ class

He is awesome, stimulating students to think for themselves and understand concepts on their own, but in this doesn't lose sight of actually needing to teach and does an amazing job at it, conveying concepts with great clarity and fun.

Very good lecturer. His tests are difficult at first, then get easier. I had a mid A until the final. The final is worth 40% (maybe more if you do well) and is VERY, VERY HARD. It's T/F but the hardest T/F test you'll take EVER.

his lectures are fun...esp. the experiments, but alllll the hw and quizzes(which weighted tooooo much) are not related from the lectures...i leanred more from my TA.. tests are damn hard but TAs are always nice when they grade those ;)

Very hard class but doable without too much effort. He curves a lot because he has to. Take the class in the spring with the easier prof if your looking for an easy A but you will learn a lot w/ Klepper.
Pay attention lectures or you're screwed.
harddddd classsssssssssssssss
Klepper is the man.

Very aggresive lecturer, demands attention and participation by arguing with the class over sensitive political issues.  In-lecture experiments are fun and profitable, and are the back bone of his exams, so attendance is basically mandatory.  Good TAs too
Clear, concise, and fun.
Klepper was the best teacher I've ever had!
kleppernomics is the best class i took my freshman year... it's tought and the final sucks but its a great class
No Comments
No Comments
klepper's final sucks hard
not such a good intro course
Teaches econ in a radical and probablt the best way. I learned alot and loved it
very difficult class but he is willing to help you understand econ
No Comments
No Comments
Kleppernomics
No Comments
Klepper Rocks
No Comments
Klepper's econ classes're stella.His classes are well structured and  refreshing

Very unprepared and disorganized. Missed lecture at least once a week. Seems as if she does not know the material well. She is very hard to follow in class and a horrible lecturer. It seems as if she tries to throw information at her students and hopes they pick it up.
Very nice, easy to talk to, but doesn't teach very well. Class was confusing.
Consistently late to class and unprepared. Doesn't teach material well. Posts homework and solutions very late and has major syntax errors that impede on your ability to learn.
No Comments
Great for Interp. Topic (pop culture and media) is interesting and easy to contribute/relate to. His readings are interesting, and he will help you if you are willing to put in effort.

Kurt was a great teacher. He truly cared about his subject and the students who attended class. He was very approachable and a fair grader. His readings are interesting and relevant to the subject. Definitely a must have prof.

Kurt is informative, and the assignments are all purposeful (although there are a lot of reading assignments). He's fair when he grades and reasonable with absences/tardies. Also, it helps that he's approachable and helpful. The topic (pop culture) is relevant and interesting enough to be engaged in class.

One of the best professors I encountered in my time at Heinz. He is smart and funny, and despite being a little repetitive, he is patient with students when they make mistakes. My presentation skills improved tremendously in just 8 weeks in his class.

Professor Labash was arrogant and repetitive. I would not classify myself as a great speaker, but he was always rude and made me more embarrassed than I was before. I would have appreciated private feedback, but he called out flaws in front of the whole class. Would not recommend (although I think it is a required course and he is the sole prof).

I remember he yelled at me once when I asked him a question. We were talking about how to speak in an interview and he gave me feedback on something I do unconsciously that sends a bad message. So I was brave enough to ask what do you think I should do, and he yelled at me in front of the whole class "DONT DO IT" and everyone laughed too

For smne who teaches communication this guy is repetitive to a fault and boring. Likes to think there isn't anything he doesn't know about body language and presenting. Will tell u the story of a car dealer who tried to mimic his body language and how he called him out.  Bugs you with his political leanings. I'm a liberal, but don't care if u are!

He loves to talk about how smart he is and namedrop.  He's basically that guy at the party that loves himself and thinks everyone else wants to hear him talk about how smart he is.  For someone who teaches a communications course, he is surprisingly insufferable.

Prof. Labash's industry expertise is indispensable.  Taking any of his courses will make you a better communicator, a better listener and, as such, a better leader.  I highly recommend any course with Prof. Labash.
He is not clear
Great teacher, made class interesting and was very knowledgeable.
Very bad experience. He made easy things complicated. Grading   was awful with no explanations given later.

I took this course in the Spring of 2022 and I really enjoyed the class. The lectures were interesting and actually made the material fun to learn. As a BME student, I think it was really important to understand the innerworkings of a cell on a mechanical level.  Also only one exam! Overall the class was great and exceeded all expectations.

Prof. LeDuc was a great professor and going to class was an enjoyable experience, even with remote learning. I was definitely the most engaged in this class compared to other classes I took that fall. Would definitely recommend this class to anyone interested in these topics!

I took the class in fall 2020 and Phil made the class enjoyable even in a remote learning scenario. Phil clearly cares about and respects his students. This class on leadership and communication was fun and challenges you to leave your comfort zone. He values participation. If you engage in the dialogue you'll learn a lot and do well in the class.

I enjoyed taking Prof. LeDuc's Cellular Biomechanics class. He is a great person and makes the class fun. The assignments and exams were challenging, but interesting and helped me understand the course material better. When the classes had to be transitioned online due to the pandemic, he was very accommodating!

I thoroughly enjoyed Professor Leduc's class. Along with presentations, he had a lot of real life examples and situations to share that made the class unique and interesting. I particularly liked the sections that covered negotiation and conflict resolution, communication skills, organization and planning techniques.

Prof. LeDuc really cares about his students. He's very approachable and works in your favor. He wants everyone to succeed but if you don't put in the work, he'll call you out. He has so much knowledge about academia and industry which makes him a valuable prof and connection. Overall, great prof and would take again.

I highly recommend taking Leadership and Comm. with Dr. LeDuc. His experience in academia, start-ups, and consulting are invaluable for this class. He gives his students a glimpse of his life in these areas and not "text book" examples of leadership. Sandy, co teacher for the class, was extremely helpful in developing our communication skills .

With considerable experience in academia and industry, Prof. Leduc's class is amazing if you are getting job-ready. Amazing lectures with good feedback. Participation in class helps you. Mock interviews, guest lecturers with considerable experience. Also deals with conflict resolution as a class exercise. Really useful for life. Great class overall

Dr. LeDuc has been one of my favorite professors to date. Very kind and caring but also has an extensive knowledge of biomechanics. I always looked forward to going to his lectures, he was one of the few professors that could tell a story and not just read off of the slides. He engages the class with questions and expects you to participate.
Good guy but not a great lecturer. You will have to learn his class for yourself using the textbook.

Very nice professor and person, would recommend taking his fundamentals class. You have a semester long group project as part of the class that is fun but a decent amount of work. Lectures are not too good for learning, but the textbook is very good for the course and he is clear on what will be on the test.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: He's actually a pretty cool guy.  The material is hard and he doesn't explain it very well, but if you work with him directly he will help you.
Easy class, confusing teaching. Always questions about guest lectures and videos in midterms.

Lots of videos (generally pretty old) and guest lecturers (some interesting, some not so much). Doesn't fully cover material in class that is necessary for homework assignments and quizzes - so if you don't know it, go to recitation or read the textbook. Not that hard to pass - not very strict. Have to do CAE and mousetrap car (group) projects.

Rating system on the site asks interest level PRIOR to class, but during/after? I both got a B and went into ECE b/c he made me lose interest in MechE. Instead of teaching material, always had people coming to class to talk about different programs. Also a jerk - called me out during lec for asking about a non-existent final (registrar not syll).

I had LeDuc back in '03 right after he joined CMU. Despite being new to the program, his advanced mechanics class (cant remember the #) was fantastic. He brought us cookies every class, conveyed the material well, and made class fun. The class also involved a fun team project where I got to analyze the stresses on a human tibia...

Professor Li's strengths lie in his honesty. I really enjoyed hearing his stories and
thoughts throughout the class, which also made his teaching more memorable. He did a good
job of engaging students and having us engage with each other. He is humorous and knowledgable
The professor really does care for student learning. Lectures were engaging and the professor made honest efforts to reach out and adjust the course according to student needs.
Professor Li is a very engaging lecturer and very knowledgeable as well. I really enjoyed his lectures and the homework was very manageable.

Out of all the teachers I have ever had, Li Laoshi is by far the most committed to his students! His teaching style is very organized. I could tell through his actions and persona that he genuinely wanted all of us to succeed. His feedback is always very clear, so I knew exactly what I have to change to improve.

His lecturing was surprisingly very entertaining. His humor is amazing and made everything fun to listen to. He was very knowledgeable, and made good use of the class time. He also seemed to relate to us and care about our experience. A+

Professor Li's teaching method was very effective. In general, he is also a very funny person,making any class that he teaches instantly more fun and engaging. He always prepares his lesson thoroughly before class, and everything is very organized.

The class was fantastic. Professor Li is the greatest strength of the class in being able to foster such a truly great learning environment. He picked great pieces of literature, journals, documentaries, and films to better understand the environmental crisis in China. As a senior, this has been one of my favorite classes.

Tests are harder than necessary and account for vast majority of the grade, but in class quizzes are quite easy. Beware homework is group based, so you can be adversely impacted if your homework group is bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The exams are really hard, and the daily quizzes are almost impossible to get points which pulls down the grade a lot

I took RPC with her and loved it. The lectures are fast-paced, so it could be stressful to follow and take notes as she lectured, but I would just look over the lecture slides afterward. I found the material very interesting. There are quizzes every class but half of them get dropped so it's just to motivate you to come to class.
really great professor who cares a lot about his students and the courses he teaches!!

Professor Law is one of the best speakers I have heard, and keeps you engaged the entire lecture every time. The grading is all TA dependent however, so if possible, ask friends about what section to take.
good guy

The TA I had was the hardest grader I've ever come across, particularly for participation. One time I participated 3 times in an hour long recitation and got a 91% that class for participation, which is ridiculous. Be ready to participate in recitation. Professor Law, however, is a great lecturer and teaches fun topics.

Professor Law is a super engaging lecturer and his lectures contain a ton of super interesting information. Depending on your TA, in my experience, the feedback wasn't that insightful. He won't check the readings as there aren't any reading checks but your big paper grades may suffer. The class is very doable and it isn't difficult.
Engaging, enthusiastic lectures. Best history professor I ever had.

Love Ricky Law, his lectures are funny and genuinely interesting. It is clear he is very passionate about what he teaches and he tries his best to make the content fun. I would take this class again to listen to his lectures. Great stuff.
So much reading. Insane amounts of reading. You'll want to off yourself from all the reading
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Great professor, lectures are required, lots of reading, but overall if you do the reading on time you should get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Took during the remote pandemic era so might be different for you, but Ricky was a very no-nonsense lecturer that kept on track of the material and was engaging to listen to. Much of the class experience however is up to the TA that you choose with some being lenient and understanding and some not. Participate in recitation & write good essays  A
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Best history teacher I've had, really engaging lectures

He's super nice and really cares about the material. He's always helpful and makes class interesting, even though it's a subject I don't love. Recitation attendance is mandatory and lecture attendance is optional, but if you skip lectures you won't know what's going on. The assignments aren't too bad and my TA graded fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Law is an amazing professor. Extremely sweet and kind, but the best thing about him is his actual passion for what he teaches. His genuine love for history comes across in all his lectures, which makes even somewhat boring topics interesting.
Ricky is such a good professor he truly cares about the CMU community. Lectures were actually very interesting. They made sense through his essays. Only class I've taken at CMU that I've enjoyed.

I recommend taking Law's genocide and weapons of mass destruction course as it's very interesting and engaging. The only issue I have is that, given that classes are currently online, he only makes recordings available for 36 hours, so if you don't live in a time zone where you can attend synchronously WATCH THE RECORDINGS ASAP!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is, by far, the best professor I've had so far. He seems to know everything about everything he is teaching. Truly an awesome professor.
Professor Law is simply incredible. The lectures are extremely helpful, insightful, and entertaining.

Decent class, certainly very good lectures. Recitation is kind of miserable though and participation grades can be pretty annoying. If you hate writing try not to take this class even though it's fairly easy. Also if you had any history classes in high school, take a different class because you won't learn anything new from this one.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Amazing lectures--So many papers

reviews.comment: He gives great lectures. The class is very notes-heavy though, so be ready to write a lot if you don't want to miss anything.

He is really, really good. Gives inspiring perspectives to look at historical incidents. Very caring towards students. Gave a genuine speech on the meaning of the lecture at the end of the semester, and it was very touching. Lots of students came down to the front of the lecture hall to thank him at the last semester lecture. Strongly recommended.

His lectures are funny and interesting, but are useless. Your grade is heavily dependent on your TA. Assigns a decent/large amount of reading per week and papers are about once a month. Papers are weighted more towards the end of the semesters, which can be good/bad. Overall a good professor and really makes you think about history.

Dr Law delivers the best history lectures! You gotta experience them for yourself. Before attending this class, I was dreading about history and how history classes were usually conducted. These preconceived notions of mine were completely shattered and Dr Law demonstrated what all history lessons should have been.
Very interactive, fun, passionate instructor. The TA grades all papers and quizes. It won't be a hard A if you read the materials and take notes in class. Strongly recommend.

He loves history and it shows, very enthusiastic. You can't take notes on a device in his class. You're not required to attend lectures, you can miss a few and still pass with an A. I took the Film Class b/c genocide isn't my cup of tea. I recommend it if you like films, history, or Tom Cruise (you'll understand if you take it).
He is very general in teaching the intro global history class. Your grades depend on the TAs, who will grade all your papers.
Good class to take.

Really great lecturer. Makes history very engaging, and fun. There's a lot of reading he assigns, but you don't necessarily need to do all of it to get a good grade. Getting a good TA is important because they grade the essays.
Super engaging lecturer, made well-know history fun and interesting
NOT BAD RICKY
Tricky Ricky Wai-Kei Law makes this class ridiculous. He names it death and destruction but by the time I was half way through his class I was wishing for death and destruction on myself.
Super engaging lecturer, makes history really interesting :)
Putnam Seminar was a ton of fun because of Prof Loh. He is awesome. Very funny, caring guy, and clearly enthusiastic about the subject.
Perfect professor and perfect man. A humble genius with a true passion for math and teaching. Made difficult concepts more approachable for people like me who are allergic to math
best ever, the putnam god.

Professor Po-Shen Loh is such an engaging and caring professor. He makes you love maths. His online classes are very engaging, he does ask us the right questions to encourage us to reflect and engage with the content in the lectures. The homework can be hard and do take a lot of time to finish but they are worth it.

Professor Loh is one of the most engaging professors I have ever had. He explains everything with such a level of enthusiasm and joy that it is difficult not to be interested in his lectures. He goes over the content diligently and is very good at getting the students to be engaged/participate in his lectures. Would recommend anyone take his class.
the absolute best

Very passionate about math. Homework can pile up quickly if you don't do it, but it's very easy and not graded for accuracy as long as you show that you try. Go to extra sessions (Putnam problems 1-3) if you can, they will help you get better at math in the long run.
I LOVE HIM. THE BEST MATH PROF IN THE WORLD.

I have never had a professor that is more involving and entertaining, he's the best teacher I've ever had and also outside of class one of the most genuine and caring and really wants to help you. I really hope more professors could be like him.
a math god
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He will make math your favorite subject - I dare you to take his class and say you don't love math. Even the kids who got Cs in the course stood up and clapped for a couple of his lectures. He will blow your socks off. Plus, tests are pretty easy to do well on if you go to class (open-book and 60+ was an A for us) hws are hard but u learn .. A LOT
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Po-Shen is amazing. In 21295 he doesnt teach very much per say, but makes it very open discussion (hence the name putnam SEMINAR). He is very intelligent and respected by everyone.
Great Professor, amazing lectures.
very good at explaining things
Amazingly dedicated to students' learning. Does extremely good job of teaching via insight and intuition!
Very entertaining and animated.

Amazing professor! Perfect teaching skills! He asks appropriate questions to inspire every student and facilitate discussions. With such an amazing guide, everyone gains a lot even in this 3-unit mini-course!
Truly an amazing teacher. He really knows what he is talking about and is very passionate. The class is test heavy but make sure you go to recitation and do your homework
best professor I've ever had.
PhD Microeconomics

Awesome Professor.  Funny.  Had him for the PHD Micro.  Class was very hard, but his teaching was excellent.  If you have to start the day with microeconomic theory, this is how you'd want to do it.  LOTS of homework.
This man is wicked smart. Makes econ fun and relevant. Proves you don't have to be an arrogant jerk to be a brilliant success. I hear the intermediate class is hard, the intro class was easy.
One of the best profs I've ever had.......
No Comments
Great examples.  Smartest member of the Clinton administration.

I think he is fair, and he tries hard to get the information out. He loves numbers (I guess that's why he's in this line of work) and has an excellent insight when it comes engineering issues. I understood he was a really successful businessman but gave it all up in favor of teaching.

Can't believe CMU does not fire this guy. He is unfair and rude with students. I've seen him pubicly humiliate one of his grad student then laugh about it when telling others about it. I also heard he does not read a word of what his grad students write. He jus piggy backs on the work of his colleagues. Unbelievable!
Thinks he is smarter than everyone else and loves to publicly humiliate others. This guy is not CMU-caliber. He just THINKS he is great.
He thinks class is a comedy show. Should be more serious in class and focus on teaching and not trying to be funny. This guys is a total fake!
No Comments
Amazing person, eager to help students.  Very engaging for a computer-oriented course.  Stayed very focused.  I love Lucio!
I think she's a great professor who truly cares about her students!
One of the worst professors at CMU. Incredibly disorganized, disrespectful, and overall incompetent. She does not know how to teach.

Very disorganized, and very very unclear about class / assignment expectations. Expects (and typically grades in accordance with) very specific things from assignments, but fails to communicate what these are
No Comments
she's a nice person, and available and helpful, but not good at putting her thoughts into words-very unclear and confusing, her grading scheme is a complete mystery!
Deadly boring and helpless.

I loved Dr. Lynch, and I was shocked when I saw she had such a low rating! She's hilarious in a really subtle way, and I enjoyed her class (France During WWII) a lot. The exams and papers were difficult; she's a tough grader. But she is also always willing to talk about any assignment.
absolutely terrible

Obviously you must be of higher intellect to appreciate this professor. While difficult and not necessarily endowed with the most diplomatic of social skills, she is brilliant and keeps the academic bar raised high. You can only benefit from her tutelage.
clearly bi-polar, offering perfectly normal and perfectly beastly days; avoid if possible
No Comments
Boring...*really* boring...

Current CMU voice student here seconding all of the criticisms here of Prof. Spacagna. In the context of all of these reviews calling out her abusive and manipulative nature, I couldn't help but laugh reading the two shallow positive student reviews, which clearly read as having been submitted at Prof. Spacagna's pressured and coercive insistence.

She uses criticism and intimidation instead of support in her classes. The pianist who used to work with her was so afraid of her comments. SHE INTIMIDATES her students for not doing whatever she wants. If the student When a student stands up for themselves, she dismisses them by saying they should leave because they are not happy. AVOID CMU.

Former CMU parent here who spent $250,000 for my daughter to get bilateral nodules after being assigned dramatic repertoire for 2 years only to be gaslit and told it was all her fault. "Maria Spacagna" (her legal name is Susan Fowler) is a very clever con artist. She snaked her way into CMU tenure using glowing letters from new students! Avoid CMU!

She abuses her students. It takes hours to start the lesson because she needs to gossip about other professors or students. She uses her students as her friends for not denouncing the way she teaches in CMU

Respectfully, MS has no serious understanding of pedagogy. You will not learn a safe technique from her. She basically just chases after a "free sound" for 4 years; in practice, though, she creates an artificial, always open cord position and has you pass excess air. Sounds nice and big in short term, but leads to cord problems and early damage.

Respectfully, MS has no serious understanding of pedagogy. You will not learn a safe technique from her. She basically just chases after a "free sound" for 4 years; in practice, though, she teaches an artificial, always open cord position and has you pass excess air. Sounds nice and big in short term, but leads to cord problems and early damage.

Professor Spacagna is a wonderful and very knowledgeable teacher who cares deeply about her students. She goes above and beyond! She also has lots of on stage experience in the world of opera that makes her almost overqualified for her position. I would highly recommend studying with her, especially for those very serious about opera.

Maria cares deeply about her students and will jump through hoops to help them. She is tough and will let you know if you are making the same mistakes but she is also extremely understanding and will pick you up when you're down. If you're not getting a concept she'll find new ways to explain it until it makes sense, she's helped me grow immensely!

Prof Spacagna uses her opera career as grounds for authority at CMU. However she is late to lessons, has students decide their own exercises, but belittles those she feels take their lessons more seriously than she does. She has the least success in sending students to prestigious graduate programs compared to the other two fuller CMU studios.
Love this man. Straight up god
One of the best professors I have had.  Easy to understand, good at explaining, extremely helpful, nice. Can be funny at times.
No Comments
Nice guy, terrible teacher. Most of the content covered in class came directly from wikipedia. However, as long as you are able to stay awake for his classes hos exams and tests are pretty easy.
He was very odd and didn't explain things well but gave you all the information you would need for the test. The tests weren't hard but you have NO TIME to do them.

Definitely one of the worst teachers I've ever had.  Lectures are completely incoherent and bizarre, this guy has to be on something.  And don't bother asking him for help with anything, he is utterly useless and extremely rude.

McElfresh left me completely unprepared for the 121 final, where other professors had prepared their students completely. If you take 15-121, do not take it with McElfresh. He is extremely impolite to students and doesn't really seem to care whether or not you're getting anything out of the course.
let's just say that i can't help to fall asleep in his classes. i hope he could explain the CS concepts clearer so that students like me can understand.
So-so techer, seems to dislike some students and be indifferent to the rest.

The lecturers are not responsible. The lab materials are of poor quality and poorly designed. It is hard to read the instructions and know what they expect of us. The starter code is written poorly with a bunch of errors and malfunctions.

To be honest, it was hard. Since it's hard, the professor is really important but he would not actually teach you real stuff and he was really code blooded to treat his students. I feel bad for his children. Good luck for you (whoever is reading and wishing to take the class... don't if you have a choice)

This guys is a joke. His lecture is boring and useless, even this class is called distributed system, he did not teach a single thing about distributed system. Please avoid this guy if you can. His lectures are boring and his homework/project has nothing related to his lecture and you have to learn on Google. Such a bad professor !
I literally want to die because of him. He is awful even though you look at him and feel he would be a nice teacher. He isn't. tough, hard and you just don't understand the materials.
Awful, they don't even allow students to submit lab earlier???? I mean is this still a teacher??? Making fun of their students? If you enjoy these, take with him.
The exam is insane. 60 minutes 40 question and you cannot go back. The thing that you thought you learn will never be tested..

You would never learn because it's so hard to learn. He simply assumes that we know some basics, but we don't. The quiz is random and it's not even fair for students to have different types of quiz problems which some covers the lab and some covers the project. Technically, you couldn't learn because there is so much left to learn.

Professor Mertz is really nice and is a great teacher. My problem was that I firmly believe I did not need the course. There was an exam before the course started and if you get 70/100 or above then you are exempted. I scored 67. I know its below, but its not that much below. I just didn't want to be in that class because I know that I dont need it

Distributed Systems is an excellent course combinedly taken by Prof. Merts and Prof. McCarthy. The classes are a lot of fun and the concepts are clearly understood with the super fun and challenging projects that we are given.

Great class and professor. She makes the material interesting. Not too challenging. Readings are helpful, but you could skim if you needed too. She gives really friendly, detailed feedback on rough drafts so that you can do a quick revision. Understanding, always willing to grant an extension. I can't say enough good things about this professor.
Great professor.  Engaging and very helpful.
Taught me 76-101. Awesome professor! She makes having Interp at 8.30 am bearable. And she's always so nice and helpful.

Mark is both enthusiastic about design and an exceptionally talented artist and designer in his own right. You will not regret taking a class with him, he knows what he is talking about, and will give you any assistance you need (you just have to be assertive, and ask for help). A top-notch professor keeping the CMU Design department afloat!
Great teacher. I had him in team with Baskinger for Design Drawing I and they were the perfect team. Mentzer is so helpful when you're stuck and he'll help you refine your ideas really well.

Mentzer knows his stuff, and he'll tell you if your drawings are crap. The difference is that he believes in your potential and will inspire you to become both a better designer and a better person. He tends to talk for a long time, but his stories are always interesting. Sometimes it's hard to keep his focus. But overall, I really liked him.
This man is both insane and a genious.  He's the most enthusiastic teacher I've ever had.
fabulous prof
Awesome, awesome, awesome. Everything a professor should be. Passionate, helpful, nurturing, encouraging... passionate. One of the best mentors I ever had the privilege of knowing.
Ditto to what people have said: a really nice guy, a helpful teacher, but dang disorganized.
He was the nicest prof in the design dept.

Mark cares about his students. He can be frustrating at times as he is not as focused as students would expect. He loves design, color, and drawing and this passion shows. To students who might rate this prof poorly for disorganized classes, remember he i
inspiring teacher
Really knows how to draw and help people understand drawing better!

I love him, my favorite professor. He explains concepts extremely clearly, and he loves questions, so if you are confused just ask him. He is very inviting, and makes the class interesting. His passion is very clear which makes the subject easier to learn. Also sometimes the grades are strange because of the TAs but other than that hes great.

Great Professor! Lectures are engaging, although fast-paced and he covers a lot of content in a short amount of time so come prepared to take notes! There were a lot of exams (like 5) and a final so every other week we would have a test. If taking cell biology/biochemistry/genetics in the future, highly recommend taking this class.

Minden is a really good prof. He knows a lot about his subject and makes lectures interesting with interactive examples and real world examples. If you don't read the book, you miss out on crucial information, because he doesn't mention everything in the book, and vice versa.

Professor Minden is an incredible professor. He truly cares about students and wants them to succeed. If students are confused, he takes time to answer their questions, and sometime he'll even redo a lecture to ensure students understand the content. His lectures are a bit difficult to follow, but office hours are very helpful.

Dr. Minden is the best. He really cares about his students. I was struggling with the course initially then he reached out and offered to tutor me every week. He also invited me to work at his research lab. take honors modern bio!!

Minden is a very nice, caring professor. The material for the class is very lecture heavy and dense, which could be a hit or miss for some people, but if you have any problems, he will be there for you. He's super accomodating and passionate about his field. If you can, please take honors bio so you can have him as your prof!

Professor Minden is one of the best professors I have ever had. He really cares for his students and wants you to learn the material. His classes are engaging and he teaches the material very well. Some of his tests are a little difficult, but if you put in the work you can get an A. Definitely would take his class again.
He was fantastic and always wanted to engage the class.

Such a great person. Honors Bio is absolutely worth it and made the following bio classes easier. His research is super cool and he is willing to answer any question. His lectures are easy to pay attention to, but make sure to take good notes, you'll need them. Test are not just information regurgitation, mainly application problems.

Professor Minden is a great lecturer and is always willing/eager to answer questions or further explain the material. He also worked with and knows many of the scientists whose findings are discussed so that makes it really interesting.

I highly recommend to take honors modern biology. Professor Minden gives amazing lectures and explains extremely complex topics in a way that makes them understandable without "watering them down." He has high expectations for students, in a good way. You'll be a better scientist and have a renewed passion for bio if you take this class.
The tests are something else. Good lectures though.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you're looking for an easy class this might not be it, but Minden is such a good teacher that you'll feel good just taking the class. He's open to questions, loves explaining things that are still unclear, and is just a good teacher overall. It's rare to come by teachers who are truly all about the learning, but Minden is.

If you have questions, ASK THEM. Recitation is rather useless; it's just another lecture without a  set goal of getting anything done. Minden is an awesome guy and a great lecturer, but prone to going off on tangents. Additionally, he knows the guy who wrote the textbook and he goes by it concretely. BEWARE: Hard tests, great class, awesome prof.

Very enthusiastic engaging lecturer, and a very nice guy to boot. TAKE MINDEN'S HONORS MODERN BIOLOGY AND NOT REGULAR MODERN BIOLOGY IF YOU REALLY LOVE BIOLOGY! It's far more interesting than regular modern biology (which is an AP Bio rehash). If not a true bio lover, be warned that the material is hard and the class full of pre-meds.  Great prof
Decent prof. His class can be difficult but he knows his subject well and is willing to help the students succeed. Recommended.
Took the Mod Bio Honors section. Very enthusiastic about his work and Biology as a whole. Kind of hard class, takes work and time. Overall a professor that knows what he's doing and cares about it.

All in All, he is a very fair professor. Pay very close attention in his lectures and take very good notes. Work together with your classmates. You must put in the time in order to get the grade. Review the material daily.He is very approachable and helpful
Cold calling and participation points? It's giving middle school...
Quizzes are really annoying
I hate it when he cold calls it's not cool at all

Tough class. Homeworks took really long and if you don't pay attention in class then you're kinda screwed. Exams claim to only take 24 hours but definitely not doable unless you spend the entire 24 hours working on it. Would not recommend this class with Trujillo

he's literally a professor and doesn't know the class material. the grad TA is teaching the whole course. gave 3 assignments before the exam and didn't even grade the third one before we had to take the test. course is disorganized, insists on making participation extra credit but forces students to participate by cold calling on them.

Absolutely unfair in grading. Show no respect to students' work. The grading is hard and very subjective. Be prepared to see an absurdly low grade for your well-prepared presentation. Highly un-recommended.

Unclear grading scheme. Doesn't handle change well (especially due to COVID). Passed on the uncertainty over to students, hence they suffer. Unempathetic, pompous and undeserving. Needs more sensitivity towards student goals. He should be more caring towards individual needs and must show respect back to students in all forms of communication.

Great professor. Lots of dad jokes and very upbeat. I knew most of the material already from prior experience so it was really easy for me, but there are others who struggled (though they likely didn't show up to class, because tests are centered around what is discussed in class at length). Overall great intro business tech course. Good guy.

This was a great class! He covered a lot of information, but made it interesting. A lot on the slides, but all interesting information. Weekly two page essays were the main portion of our grade. Also, he accepted papers slightly late and was good about checking his email.
great teacher, really funny, makes really boring stuff actually interesting.  And on top of that, totally hot!  I love history now...well at least one historian!
Hot ****
I found the class pretty easy (never got anything other than an A), but some people found him hard. Regardless his FCEs are awesome. Go check them.
Highly energetic, very dedicated
not very meaningful class
Attendance matters
Interesting lectures
You can never imagine the grade until it comes out
The grading rubric is not clear. Grade received was far lower than expected.

He can come sometimes come off as rude, but I've found him to be a great resource in 18220, even though he is not officially the prof. He was always willing to sit down with students for 1on1s to go over specific questions and example problems, and I really appreciated that while I was in 220.
Not good at giving feedback

Worst amongst all professors at CMU. His idea of "teaching" is to just sit there and insult you without providing any useful information. A horrible professor and an even worse human being, I don't even know what he has to do with the courses. He just stops by the TA and Prof office hours and irritates the students. No help whatsoever.

Ok he's not really a prof for 18220, he used to teach 18220 but now just helps out with office hours and labs. The guy is great!!! He knows his stuff and he comes off as blunt but he just wants his students to really understand the material. He's also hilarious when there are just some random conversations.

This guy is super rude. We use piazza in the course and he is unhelpful and replies aggressively to questions/comments. Doesn't even teach the lecture, recitation, or lab so I'm not sure why he talks to us or what his connection to 220 even is.

While Professor Muller seems to know his stuff for econ. Unfortunately he should've been a researcher or public speaker instead of a professor. He doesn't provide feedback on presentations nor assignments and provides absolutely no clarification on the grade received. The slides used in the class are hilariously bad and are no even released ahead.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great class.  Prof Muller is very energetic and engaged.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Just an unfair professor. He is just trying to get tenure but still has not. Clearly for a reason. Is an awful lecturer and his exams are IMPOSSIBLE. Should be banned from interacting with undergraduate students.
